› which project is here?


• This workspace is the drug-api project (/Users/nick/project/endurantdevs/drug-api).

  It’s a Python/Sanic US Drugs API (OpenFDA + RxNorm data) that uses PostgreSQL + Redis, with Docker support via the repo’s Dockerfile.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Mostly yes, but with one caveat.

  - rxnorm_ids is in the Product model at db/models.py:36.
  - NDC import creates a new table from that model (product_<date>) at process/ndc_product.py:143 and process/ndc_product.py:145, then swaps it into rx_data.product at process/ndc_product.py:210 and process/
    ndc_product.py:211.
  - For existing environments, the intended path is still the Alembic migration (alembic/versions/3a9f2f5e4c1b_add_product_rxnorm_ids.py:35) which adds the column/index on current rx_data.product.

  So: a successful full import cycle will produce product with rxnorm_ids, but you should still run python main.py db migrate first.


› I ran the import in production - but that column doesnt exist


• Explored
  └ Read __init__.py, ndc_product.py

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, in this code it should be there.

  - rxnorm_ids is part of the Product model at db/models.py:36.
  - NDC import creates product_<date> from that model (process/ndc_product.py:145) and swaps it into rx_data.product (process/ndc_product.py:211).
  - There is also an Alembic migration that adds it if missing: alembic/versions/3a9f2f5e4c1b_add_product_rxnorm_ids.py:35.

  If it is missing in prod, most likely:

  - prod is running an older image, or
  - import did not finish/swap (abort path at process/ndc_product.py:218), or
  - you checked a different schema/table.

  Quick recovery:

  docker exec <drug_api_container> /bin/sh -lc 'source venv313/bin/activate && python main.py db migrate'

  Verify:

  SELECT column_name
  FROM information_schema.columns
  WHERE table_schema='rx_data' AND table_name='product' AND column_name='rxnorm_ids';

  Then repopulate values:

  docker exec <drug_api_container> /bin/sh -lc 'source venv313/bin/activate && python main.py start ndc && python main.py worker process.NDC --burst'


› there should be no migration as tables must be created and replaces during import with proper schema in drugs-api


• Correct. In this codebase, rx_data.product is replaced by the import job, so rxnorm_ids should come from model-based table creation, not from migration.

  - Table includes rxnorm_ids: db/models.py:36
  - Import creates product_<date> from that model: process/ndc_product.py:143, process/ndc_product.py:145
  - Then swaps it into rx_data.product: process/ndc_product.py:210, process/ndc_product.py:211

  If the column is missing in prod, the likely problem is import execution, not schema design:

  1. Old image/code running in prod.
  2. Worker did not complete shutdown/swap (abort path in process/ndc_product.py:218).
  3. Import was not run as full start ndc + worker ... --burst cycle.

  Quick checks:

  docker exec <container> /bin/sh -lc "grep -n 'rxnorm_ids' /opt/db/models.py /opt/process/ndc_product.py"

  SELECT table_name
  FROM information_schema.tables
  WHERE table_schema='rx_data' AND table_name LIKE 'product_%'
  ORDER BY table_name DESC;

  SELECT table_name, column_name
  FROM information_schema.columns
  WHERE table_schema='rx_data'
    AND table_name IN ('product', '<latest_product_yyyymmdd>')
    AND column_name='rxnorm_ids';

  If you want, I can give you a no-migration production runbook that enforces this import-only path end-to-end.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes. DailyMed has an official source for this.

  - Use GET /dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json and read data.media[].url.
  - Those URLs are direct image.cfm?... links (exactly what the “View Package Photos” UI uses).
  - For your set ID (6660b616-a82d-9109-19ce-2bdf9747acea), the page’s photo link resolves to an image.cfm URL, so this is machine-retrievable.
  - If you start from NDC instead of setid, use /dailymed/services/v2/spls.json?ndc=<NDC> first to get the setid, then call /media.

  Caveat: not every SPL has media files.

  Sources:

  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_setid_media_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/splimage-guidelines.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=6660b616-a82d-9109-19ce-2bdf9747acea

• Yes, that screenshot is exactly the DailyMed “package photos” widget.

  Use this API for those image URLs:

  curl -s "https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/services/v2/spls/6660b616-a82d-9109-19ce-2bdf9747acea/media.json" \
  | jq -r '.data.media[]?.url'

  If you only have NDC, first resolve SPL/setid, then call /media.json.
  If media is empty, DailyMed has no uploaded photos for that label.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes. Those photos come from SPLIMAGE media files attached to SPL labels (submitted to FDA, surfaced by DailyMed), not from a separate private image DB.

  You can fetch by NDC, but it is a 2-step lookup:

  1. Resolve NDC -> setid
     GET https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/services/v2/spls.json?ndc=<NDC>
  2. Resolve setid -> media URLs
     GET https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/services/v2/spls/<SETID>/media.json

  For your example, this works today (February 17, 2026). I verified both 0178-0500-01 (package NDC) and 0178-0500 (product code) return the same setid, then /media.json returns image URLs.
  Practical note: hyphenated NDC format works; zero-padded plain 11-digit may not.

  Sources:

  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_setid_media_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/splimage-guidelines.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_setid_ndcs_api.cfm


› what is SPL Label?


• An SPL label is a drug’s official FDA-regulated labeling document in Structured Product Labeling (SPL) format.

  - It is an XML document (HL7 standard) containing things like indications, dosage, warnings, ingredients, NDCs, package details, and sometimes images.
  - DailyMed publishes this SPL content for public access.
  - A setid identifies the label document set across versions.

  So when you query DailyMed by setid, you’re querying that product’s official SPL label record.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, and your repo already does part of it in bulk.

  - process/label.py:157 to process/label.py:169 pulls https://api.fda.gov/download.json, reads results.drug.label.partitions, and enqueues all label files (bulk import, not per-request).
  - process/label.py:59 to process/label.py:83 loads label rows into DB, including NDC arrays from openfda.
  - db/models.py:87 has set_id, which is the key you need for SPL-linked enrichment.

  What is missing from openFDA bulk label data is package photo URLs.
  For photos, add a second import phase using DailyMed:

  1. Take unique set_id values from rx_data.label.
  2. Fetch GET /dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json.
  3. Store media URLs in a local table (e.g., label_media) and join at API time.

  If you want actual image files (not just URLs), DailyMed ZIP download by setId includes XML + image files.

  Sources:

  - https://api.fda.gov/download.json
  - https://api.fda.gov/drug/label.json
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_setid_media_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/webservices-help/v2/spls_api.cfm
  - https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/app-support-web-services.cfm


› so we have set_ids already in db?


• Yes, if you ran the label import.

  - set_id is stored in rx_data.label.set_id (db/models.py:87).
  - You also have spl_id in rx_data.product.spl_id (db/models.py:26), which is often the join key used to find label rows.

  Quick check:

  SELECT COUNT(*) AS with_setid
  FROM rx_data.label
  WHERE set_id IS NOT NULL;

  SELECT set_id, id
  FROM rx_data.label
  WHERE set_id IS NOT NULL
  LIMIT 10;


› do we return it in drugs-api responses ?


• I’ll verify the exact response payloads in the endpoint code to confirm where set_id is exposed and where it isn’t.

• Explored
  └ Read drug.py, json_mixin.py, models.py

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, but only on the label endpoints.

  - set_id exists on the Label model (db/models.py:87).
  - Label endpoints return label via to_json_dict() (api/endpoint/drug.py:92, api/endpoint/drug.py:108), and to_json_dict() includes all model columns (db/json_mixin.py:41 to db/json_mixin.py:47).

  So you get it as response.label.set_id on:

  - /api/v1/drug/label/package/{package_ndc}
  - /api/v1/drug/label/product/{product_ndc}

  Regular NDC endpoints don’t include label; they return product/package data (product has spl_id, not set_id).


› show me some example url for 8087 - so I will see it in response

  http://localhost:8087/api/v1/drug/label/package/0178-0500-01

  Or product-level:

  http://localhost:8087/api/v1/drug/label/product/0178-0500

  Quick check in terminal:

  curl -s "http://localhost:8087/api/v1/drug/label/package/0178-0500-01" | jq '.label | {id,set_id,version}'

• Added AGENTS.md (+42 -0)
     1 +# AGENTS.md
     2 +
     3 +## Project Snapshot
     4 +- Service: `drug-api` (Python/Sanic + GINO/PostgreSQL + ARQ/Redis).
     5 +- Primary datasets: OpenFDA `drug/ndc` and `drug/label` partition files.
     6 +- Runtime entrypoint in container: `/usr/local/bin/start_api.sh`.
     7 +
     8 +## Import Architecture (Source of Truth)
     9 +- Imports are table-rebuild + swap, not row-by-row in-place updates.
    10 +- NDC import creates `rx_data.product_<YYYYMMDD>` and `rx_data.package_<YYYYMMDD>`, builds indexes, then renames/switches to `rx_data.product` and `rx_data.package`.
    11 +- Label import creates `rx_data.label_<YYYYMMDD>`, builds indexes, then renames/switches to `rx_data.label`.
    12 +- Because tables are recreated from current models during import, schema changes present in models are expected to appear after a successful full import cycle.
    13 +
    14 +## RxNorm Notes
    15 +- `Product.rxnorm_ids` exists in model (`db/models.py`) and is populated during NDC import from `openfda.rxcui`.
    16 +- RxNorm API endpoints:
    17 +  - `GET /api/v1/drug/rxnorm/{rxnorm_id}/products`
    18 +  - `GET /api/v1/drug/rxnorm/{rxnorm_id}/packages`
    19 +- If RxNorm lookups are empty after rollout, verify a full NDC import cycle completed (`start ndc` + `worker process.NDC --burst`).
    20 +
    21 +## SPL / Label Notes
    22 +- `Label.set_id` is imported from OpenFDA label data and stored in `rx_data.label.set_id`.
    23 +- Product rows expose `spl_id`; label lookup logic first checks `Label.id == spl_id`, then fallback `Label.set_id == spl_id`.
    24 +- `set_id` is returned in API responses only when a nested `label` object is returned (label endpoints), not on plain product/package endpoints.
    25 +
    26 +## DailyMed Package Photo Data
    27 +- OpenFDA label payloads do not provide package photo URLs.
    28 +- DailyMed provides media URLs by SPL set id:
    29 +  - `/dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json`
    30 +- NDC to media lookup path:
    31 +  1. NDC -> SETID: `/dailymed/services/v2/spls.json?ndc={NDC}`
    32 +  2. SETID -> media URLs: `/dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json`
    33 +
    34 +## Recommended Implementation Strategy (No Per-Request External Calls)
    35 +- Keep request handlers read-only against local DB.
    36 +- Add an offline enrichment phase after label import:
    37 +  1. Read distinct `rx_data.label.set_id`.
    38 +  2. Fetch DailyMed media for each `set_id`.
    39 +  3. Store results in a local table (for example `rx_data.label_media` with `set_id`, `name`, `mime_type`, `url`, `synced_at`).
    40 +  4. Join/enrich label responses from local DB only.
    41 +- Continue treating external APIs as ingest-time dependencies, not request-time dependencies.
    42 +

    48 +## SPL IDs in current API responses
    49 +- `label.set_id` is stored in DB during label import and returned in response payloads that include `label`.
    50 +- Example endpoints returning `label`:
    51 +  - `GET /api/v1/drug/label/package/{package_ndc}`
    52 +  - `GET /api/v1/drug/label/product/{product_ndc}`
    53 +- Product rows expose `spl_id`; label lookup attempts `label.id == spl_id` then `label.set_id == spl_id`.
    54 +
    55 +## DailyMed package photos: source and bulk implementation
    56 +DailyMed package photos are not provided as URLs in OpenFDA partition files. They are available via DailyMed web services keyed by SPL `set_id`.
    57 +
    58 +DailyMed APIs:
    59 +- `GET /dailymed/services/v2/spls.json?ndc={NDC}` -> resolves NDC to `setid`.
    60 +- `GET /dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json` -> returns media URLs (`image.cfm?...`).
    61 +
    62 +Recommended implementation for this project:
    63 +1. Keep API request handlers DB-only (no per-request external HTTP calls).
    64 +2. Add post-label-import enrichment job:
    65 +   - Read distinct `label.set_id`.
    66 +   - Fetch `/spls/{SETID}/media.json`.
    67 +   - Store in local table (for example: `label_media(set_id, name, mime_type, url, synced_at)`).
    68 +3. Join local media records into label responses.
    69 +
    70 +Quick validation example:
    71 +```shell
    72 +curl -s "https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/services/v2/spls/6660b616-a82d-9109-19ce-2bdf9747acea/media.json" | jq '.data.media'
    73 +```
    74

› yes, explain


• Plan

  1. Create a new table rx_data.label_media keyed by set_id to cache DailyMed media.
  2. Add a post-label-import sync job that bulk-fetches media URLs for all current label.set_id values.
  3. Enrich existing label endpoints with cached media from local DB only.

  Table Shape

  - set_id text not null
  - name text not null
  - mime_type text null
  - url text not null
  - synced_at timestamptz not null default now()
  - Unique key: (set_id, url)
  - Index: (set_id)

  Import Flow

  1. Label import finishes and swaps rx_data.label.
  2. Media sync reads SELECT DISTINCT set_id FROM rx_data.label WHERE set_id IS NOT NULL.
  3. For each set_id, call https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/services/v2/spls/{SETID}/media.json.
  4. Upsert returned media rows into label_media.
  5. Delete stale rows for set_id not present in current label snapshot.

  API Behavior

  - Keep current responses unchanged except optional new field:
  - label.media: [{name, mime_type, url}]
  - Add only on:
      - /api/v1/drug/label/package/{package_ndc}
      - /api/v1/drug/label/product/{product_ndc}

  Why This Fits Your Constraint

  - No per-request external DailyMed calls.
  - Data is preloaded during import workflow.
  - Consistent with current import-first architecture.

  If you want, I’ll implement this now in code (db/models.py, new process/label_media.py, import wiring in process/__init__.py and service/run_import.sh, endpoint enrichment, and openapi.yaml update).

─ Worked for 1m 02s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I confirmed the failure mode: long index names plus _old rename can collide after PostgreSQL identifier truncation. I’m applying a deterministic archived-name helper in process/npi.py and switching
  the shutdown rename calls to use it (drop archived target first, then rename).

• Edited healthcare-mrf-api/process/npi.py (+11 -0)
     5  import glob
     6 +import hashlib
     7  import os
       ⋮
    40  DEFAULT_NPI_MAX_PENDING_SAVE_TASKS = 4
    41 +POSTGRES_IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH = 63
    42
       ⋮
    54
    55 +def _archived_identifier(name: str, suffix: str = "_old") -> str:
    56 +    candidate = f"{name}{suffix}"
    57 +    if len(candidate) <= POSTGRES_IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH:
    58 +        return candidate
    59 +    digest = hashlib.sha1(name.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:8]
    60 +    trim_to = max(1, POSTGRES_IDENTIFIER_MAX_LENGTH - len(suffix) - len(digest) - 1)
    61 +    return f"{name[:trim_to]}_{digest}{suffix}"
    62 +
    63 +
    64  async def _ensure_required_extensions() -> None:

• Edited healthcare-mrf-api/process/npi.py (+10 -6)
    639
    640 +    async def archive_index(index_name: str) -> str:
    641 +        archived_name = _archived_identifier(index_name)

• Patched. The NPI finalization now handles long-index rename collisions safely.

  What I changed

  - Added deterministic archived index naming with 63-char PostgreSQL limit handling in process/npi.py:55.
  - Added a shutdown helper that:
      1. drops the archive target name if it already exists,
      2. renames the live index to that archive name.
         (process/npi.py:640)
  - Switched both primary and additional index renames to this safe helper:
      - process/npi.py:800
      - process/npi.py:814

  This fixes the DuplicateTableError caused by _old rename collisions from identifier truncation.

  Validation

  - Syntax check passed: python3 -m py_compile process/npi.py.

  Rerun
  Use your normal NPI pipeline again (same commands).
  If you want the common flow:

  python main.py start npi
  parallel -u 'python main.py worker process.NPI {}' ::: '--burst' '--burst' '--burst' '--burst' '--burst'
  python main.py worker process.NPI_finish --burst

  - In publish rename step, added archive_index(...) helper that:
      1. DROP INDEX IF EXISTS <archived_name>

      - primary index rename
      - all additional index renames

• Short answer: not with bullseye.

  I checked remote tags directly:

  - public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.14-slim-bullseye → does not exist (no such manifest)
  - public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.14-slim-bookworm → exists

  So for 3.14 you should use slim bookworm, e.g. update Dockerfile:1 to:

  FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.14-slim-bookworm

  Compatibility check in this repo:

  - requirements-dev.txt dry-run on Python 3.14: resolves
  - requirements.txt dry-run on Python 3.14: fails on hiredis==2.1.0 (No matching distribution found)

  So:

  - If you build like current Dockerfile (uses requirements-dev.txt), 3.14 is likely feasible.
  - If any pipeline uses requirements.txt, you need to update pins (at least hiredis).

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• This error is exactly because gdal-config is missing for pyogrio source build on Python 3.14.

  I fixed your Dockerfile by adding required GDAL/toolchain packages:

  - gdal-bin
  - libgdal-dev
  - pkg-config
  - g++ (kept gcc too)

  So the install line now is:

  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gcc g++ pkg-config gdal-bin libgdal-dev nginx git curl parallel "${LIBAIO_PKG}"

  Rebuild now; that specific pyogrio metadata failure should be resolved.

  If it still fails later, the next likely point would be another strict pin in requirements.txt/requirements-dev.txt not compatible with 3.14, and we can pin-adjust quickly.


› there pyogrio 0.12.1 - will it be selected?


• No.

  With current files, pip will install pyogrio==0.11.1 because it is hard-pinned:

  - requirements-dev.txt:33
  - requirements.txt:45

  So 0.12.1 will not be selected unless you change those pins (or remove the strict == pin).

• Edited healthcare-mrf-api/AGENTS.md (+51 -17)
     3  ## Project Structure & Module Organization
     4 -Sanic endpoints live in `api/`, with request handlers under `api/endpoint/` expecting an async SQLAlchemy session on `request.ctx.session`. Import pipelines and ARQ workers are in `process/`, w
        ith helpers under `process/ext/` and CSV assets in `data/`. Database models, core async engine setup, and Alembic revisions sit in `db/` and `alembic/`. Shell scripts, nginx configs, and large
        CSV exports belong in `service/`. Keep `restore/` as frozen legacy snapshots—do not port them unless explicitly asked. Tests and quick experiments currently reside in `__test__/`, and environme
        nt defaults live in `.env.example` alongside `logging.yaml`.
     4 +Sanic endpoints live in `api/`, with request handlers under `api/endpoint/` using async SQLAlchemy sessions from request context. Import pipelines and ARQ workers are in `process/`, with shared
         helpers under `process/ext/`. Database models and engine setup are in `db/`; schema migrations are in `alembic/` (but claims/drug imports bootstrap their own canonical tables). Specs are split
         between `doc/specs/` (feature execution specs) and `specs/` (cross-cutting operational policy). Keep `restore/` as frozen legacy snapshots unless explicitly requested.
     5
     6  ## Build, Test, and Development Commands
     7 -Run these from the repository root inside an activated virtualenv:
     8 -- `python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate` – create and activate a Python 3.11+ environment.
     9 -- `pip install -r requirements-dev.txt` – install API, worker, and tooling dependencies.
    10 -- `python main.py server start --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080` – launch the Sanic API (Postgres/Redis must match `.env`).
    11 -- `python main.py start mrf` – enqueue the CMS MRF import, then `python main.py worker process.MRF --burst` to drain the queue.
    12 -- Append `--test` to any importer command (MRF, plan_attributes, npi, nucc) to run the smoke-sized workload against a shadow database named `"<base>_test"`; workers pick this up automatically,
        so production data stays untouched. Set `HLTHPRT_TEST_DATABASE_SUFFIX` if you prefer a different suffix.
    13 -- `python main.py db migrate -r head` – apply Alembic migrations; use `python main.py db generate -m "message"` to autogenerate.
    14 -- `pytest __test__ -x` – run the current test suite; pass `-k pattern` for targeted checks.
    15 -- Set `HLTHPRT_DB_DRIVER` in `.env` to choose the SQLAlchemy backend (`postgresql+asyncpg` keeps bulk import fast; switch to `postgresql+psycopg` if asyncpg isn’t available).
    16 -- Override `HLTHPRT_QUEUE_<IMPORTER>` (for example `HLTHPRT_QUEUE_NPI`) when you need a dedicated Redis stream per importer; defaults are pre-scoped so jobs never collide.
     7 +Run from repo root inside an activated virtualenv:
     8 +- `python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate`
     9 +- `pip install -r requirements-dev.txt`
    10 +- `python main.py server start --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8080`
    11 +- `pytest __test__ -x` (or `pytest -k <pattern>`)
    12 +
    13 +Importer commands:

    15 +- `python main.py start drug-claims`
    16 +- `python main.py start npi`

    19 +- `python main.py worker process.DrugClaims --burst`
    20 +- `python main.py worker process.DrugClaims_finish --burst`
    21 +- `python main.py worker process.NPI --burst`
    22 +- `python main.py worker process.NPI_finish --burst`
    23 +
    24 +Operational rule:
    25 +- Do **not** run `ClaimsPricing_finish` and `DrugClaims_finish` in parallel. Both touch shared `code_catalog`/`code_crosswalk`.
    26 +
    27 +Test mode:
    28 +- Append `--test` to importer commands (`claims-pricing`, `drug-claims`, `npi`, etc.).
    29 +- Test mode uses deterministic partial imports in `*_test` DB (suffix configurable via `HLTHPRT_TEST_DATABASE_SUFFIX`).
    30 +
    31 +## Runtime & Docker Notes
    32 +- Current Docker base: `python:3.14-slim-trixie`.
    33 +- Trixie package compatibility:
    34 +  - `libaio1` is replaced by `libaio1t64` (Dockerfile handles fallback).
    35 +  - `pyogrio` source build on 3.14 requires `gdal-config` (`gdal-bin`, `libgdal-dev`, `pkg-config`, `g++` installed in image).
    36 +- If using pinned `requirements.txt`, verify `hiredis` pin compatibility on Python 3.14.
    37 +
    38 +## Claims/Drug/NPI Integration Notes
    39 +- Procedures and prescriptions are separated:
    40 +  - Procedures/services: `pricing_provider*`, `pricing_procedure*`, cost profile + peer stats + geo benchmark.
    41 +  - Prescriptions: `pricing_prescription`, `pricing_provider_prescription`.
    42 +- Claims and drug imports are migration-independent for canonical table creation (`checkfirst` create + post-load indexing).
    43 +- `npi_address.procedures_array` and `npi_address.medications_array` are populated in the **NPI import shutdown phase**, sourced from canonical pricing tables.
    44 +
    45 +## Publish/Swap & Idempotency
    46 +- Import publish strategy is transactional rename (`live -> _old`, `staging -> live`) with index renames.
    47 +- Index archive renames are truncation-safe and idempotent for PostgreSQL 63-char identifier limits (pre-drop archive target + deterministic archive naming).
    48 +- `_old` tables/indexes are rollback assets and should not be auto-purged.
    49
    50  ## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
    19 -Follow PEP 8 with 4-space indentation; `setup.cfg` raises `flake8`'s max line length to 120 and ignores generated Alembic versions. All database access must use SQLAlchemy 2's async API—wrap wo
        rk in `async with AsyncSession()` and issue `await session.execute(...)` instead of legacy gino helpers. Use descriptive snake_case for functions, columns, and Alembic revision filenames. Prefe
        r type hints on public call boundaries and keep response models explicit. Run `flake8 api process db` before opening a PR, and format SQLAlchemy queries for readability.
    51 +Follow PEP 8 with 4-space indentation and `setup.cfg` lint rules. Use SQLAlchemy 2 async API (`await session.execute(...)`) for all DB access. Prefer explicit snake_case names and typed public
        function boundaries. Keep API contracts explicit and backward-compatible where legacy fields/aliases are intentionally supported.
    52
    53  ## Testing Guidelines
    22 -Name tests `test_<feature>.py` and colocate fixtures in `__test__/` until a richer suite emerges. Mock outbound HTTP and geocode calls to keep runs deterministic, and use disposable schemas or
        Dockerized Postgres for async integration tests. Target high-risk flows (NPI imports, JSON index parsing, async session lifecycle) before touching production jobs. Execute `pytest --maxfail=1 -
        -disable-warnings` plus any importer smoke commands prior to review.
    23 -
    24 -## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
    25 -Commit subjects should stay short and imperative (the log favors lines like `Add NPI async session factory`). Collapse intermediate WIP commits locally. PRs must enumerate schema changes, new e
        nvironment variables, and follow-up migration steps; include links to tickets and screenshots or sample payloads for API tweaks. Document which jobs or tests you ran (server start, importer bur
        sts, pytest) in the PR body, and highlight any remaining GINO dependencies that still need conversion.
    54 +Prioritize regression tests for:
    55 +- importer chunk orchestration + finalize gating,
    56 +- publish swap and index rename safety,
    57 +- API compatibility fields/aliases,
    58 +- heavy query endpoints (`/npi/all`, `/npi/near`, pricing search endpoints).
    59 +Run importer smoke commands with `--test` before merging importer changes.
    60
    61  ## Security & Configuration Tips
    28 -Never commit real credentials—copy `.env.example`, inject secrets locally, and reference them via `HLTHPRT_` prefixed variables. Ensure `HLTHPRT_LOG_CFG` points to `logging.yaml` so structured
        logs load in workers. Redact PHI from logs and exports before sharing. Treat `restore/` datasets as sensitive; keep archives encrypted or out of version control, and rotate API keys used for ge
        ocoding or CMS access.
    62 +Do not commit credentials. Keep secrets in runtime env vars (`HLTHPRT_*`). Ensure `HLTHPRT_LOG_CFG` points to `logging.yaml`. Redact sensitive payloads in logs, especially import traces and API
         error dumps.

• Edited healthcare-mrf-api/specs/import_swap_backup_policy.md (+12 -0)
    22
    23 +## Index Rename Safety Rules
    24 +
    25 +- Index archive rename must be idempotent:
    26 +  - drop existing archive index name before renaming current live index;
    27 +  - then rename staged index to live index name.
    28 +- Archive index names must be PostgreSQL-identifier-safe (63-char max).
    29 +  - For long names, use deterministic truncated+hash archive naming to avoid collisions.
    30 +- `ALTER INDEX ... RENAME TO ..._old` without target pre-drop is not allowed in finalize paths.
    31 +
    32  ## NPI Import Reference
       ⋮
    38  - rename indexes accordingly
    39 +- archive index naming is truncation-safe and collision-tolerant
    40 +
    41 +Claims/drug finalize in `process/claims_pricing.py` and `process/drug_claims.py` follows the same guardrail.

  - Documented claims vs drug import split and canonical ownership.
  - Clarified active year policy (operational default 2023 via env for both claims and drug imports).

  - Added index rename safety policy (idempotent archive rename + Postgres 63-char truncation-safe archive naming).

    3  ## Project Structure & Module Organization
    4 -Sanic endpoints live in `api/`, with request handlers under `api/endpoint/` using async SQLAlchemy sessions from request context. Import pipelines and ARQ workers are in `process/`, with shared
       helpers under `process/ext/`. Database models and engine setup are in `db/`; schema migrations are in `alembic/` (but claims/drug imports bootstrap their own canonical tables). Specs are split b
       etween `doc/specs/` (feature execution specs) and `specs/` (cross-cutting operational policy). Keep `restore/` as frozen legacy snapshots unless explicitly requested.
    4 +Sanic endpoints live in `api/`, with request handlers under `api/endpoint/` using async SQLAlchemy sessions from request context. Import pipelines and ARQ workers are in `process/`, with shared
       helpers under `process/ext/`. Database models and engine setup are in `db/`; schema migrations are in `alembic/` (but claims/drug imports bootstrap their own canonical tables). Specs live in `sp
       ecs/`. Keep `restore/` as frozen legacy snapshots unless explicitly requested.

##########Improve the speed of work of MCP server (logic / scenarios).
Then the session continues with research for possible client contacts (found 3000 potential companies, some emails/contacts)


  › so, maybe we can do better and improve from recommendations (better scenarios for, so mcp-client would work much faster)

  Top 10 Real Patient Search Scenarios (US)
  1) “I need a doctor near me right now” — Acute symptoms

  Trigger: new or scary symptom
  Search intent: urgency + triage + proximity

  Examples:

  chest pain, fever, vomiting

  rash on child

  painful urination

  migraine

  flu symptoms

  COVID/RSV/strep suspicion

  What users actually search

  urgent care near me
  doctor open now
  is this serious symptom
  ER vs urgent care
  telehealth doctor today

  MCP opportunity

  symptom triage engine

  ER vs urgent care decision

  appointment availability

  cost estimation

  This is the highest frequency healthcare search category in the US.

  2) “My kid is sick” — Pediatric episodic care

  Trigger: child symptoms (high anxiety multiplier)

  Examples:

  fever at night

  ear infection

  rash

  cough

  pink eye

  Behavior pattern
  Parents don’t want ER unless necessary — they want confidence.

  MCP opportunity

  pediatric triage pathways

  reassurance content

  same-day booking

  pharmacy coordination

  3) “I need a primary care doctor” — Moving / new insurance

  Trigger: life admin change

  Examples:

  moved cities

  new job

  first adult doctor

  aging out of pediatrician

  Actual searches

  primary care doctor accepting new patients
  in network PCP
  annual physical doctor

  Pain point
  Insurance networks are confusing → 60% abandonment rate.

  MCP opportunity

  insurance parsing

  network matching

  availability ranking

  onboarding workflow

  4) Mental health help

  Trigger: ongoing distress event

  Examples:

  anxiety

  depression

  ADHD

  therapy after breakup/job loss

  burnout

  Search behavior
  Long research sessions, late night, high bounce rate.

  Actual searches

  therapist that takes my insurance
  psychiatrist online prescription
  ADHD evaluation adult

  MCP opportunity

  availability filtering (critical!)

  modality preference (virtual vs in person)

  medication vs therapy routing

  This is the fastest growing healthcare search category.

  5) Specialist referral after diagnosis

  Trigger: PCP told them to see a specialist

  Examples:

  dermatologist for mole

  cardiologist after abnormal EKG

  GI after stomach pain

  orthopedist after injury

  User mindset
  They don’t know how to choose — they want “the right one”.

  Actual searches

  best [specialist] near me
  who treats [condition]
  second opinion doctor

  MCP opportunity

  condition → specialist mapping

  quality ranking explanation

  referral management

  6) Women’s health & reproductive care

  Trigger: predictable lifecycle events

  Examples:

  pregnancy

  birth control

  irregular periods

  menopause

  PCOS/endometriosis

  Behavior
  High education need + provider trust

  Actual searches

  OB accepting new patients
  prenatal visit cost
  IUD insertion near me

  MCP opportunity

  lifecycle-aware care plans

  episode bundles

  visit sequencing

  7) Medication & prescription issues

  Trigger: friction at pharmacy

  Examples:

  refill expired

  side effects

  prior authorization

  cost too high

  Actual searches

  refill prescription online
  cheaper alternative medication
  doctor to prescribe

  MCP opportunity

  refill routing

  telehealth Rx visits

  formulary intelligence

  pharmacy coordination

  8) Chronic condition management

  Trigger: ongoing disease

  Examples:

  diabetes

  hypertension

  asthma

  thyroid disease

  migraines

  Behavior
  Not urgent → recurring navigation problem.

  Actual searches

  diabetes doctor near me
  monitor condition
  follow up appointment

  MCP opportunity

  recurring care cadence

  monitoring reminders

  care team orchestration

  9) Cost & insurance questions (extremely common)

  Trigger: fear of surprise bills

  Examples:

  MRI cost

  lab test cost

  out of network bill

  deductible confusion

  Actual searches

  how much is X without insurance
  in network doctor
  copay vs deductible

  MCP opportunity
  This is HUGE — often bigger than provider search itself.

  cost prediction

  eligibility check

  benefit explanation

  10) Preventive care & screenings

  Trigger: age milestone / employer reminder

  Examples:

  annual physical

  colonoscopy at 45

  mammogram

  vaccines

  STD testing

  Behavior
  Low urgency → high procrastination

  MCP opportunity

  nudges

  eligibility detection

  bundled booking

  The Core Insight (Important for your MCP architecture)

  Users don’t navigate healthcare like:

  specialty → provider → appointment

  They navigate like:

  situation → reassurance → next step → logistics → booking


• This is a strong direction. I’ll encode these 10 “situation-first” patient scenarios directly into api-mcp metadata/instructions so the MCP client routes in fewer turns (scenario → first tool/batch path),
  then run tests.

  └ Read mcp_server.py

  so MCP routes situation-first.

• Yes, your screenshots show two specific MCP-routing failures:

  1. resolve_health_mapping had no catalog match and the client drifted.
  2. It incorrectly called call_operation with operation_id="call_api_read_batch" (tool-id used as OpenAPI op-id), then fell back to Google.

  I fixed this in api-mcp:

  - call_operation is now explicitly OpenAPI-only in instructions: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:798
  - Batch/tool-id usage is clarified: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:818
  - Strong no-web-search rule for RxNorm/plan/pricing: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:806
  - Unknown op-id fix-it now detects MCP tool names and tells client to call the tool directly: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:4470
  - Mapping fallback now routes to internal autocompletes (autocompleteServiceClaims/autocompleteDrugClaims) instead of discovery drift: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1995
  - Added explicit plan cost-sharing recipe to keep benefit/cost flows internal: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:381
  - Added regression tests for all of this: api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1305

  All tests pass.

     263 +fn is_medicare_field_name(name: &str) -> bool {
     264 +    let normalized = name.trim().to_ascii_lowercase();
     265 +    if MEDICARE_FIELD_NAMES.contains(&normalized.as_str()) {
     266 +        return true;
     267 +    }
     268 +
     269 +    normalized.starts_with("medicare_")
     270 +        || normalized.contains("_medicare_")
     271 +        || normalized.starts_with("avg_mdcr")

     279 +fn strip_medicare_fields(payload: &mut serde_json::Value) {
     280 +    match payload {
     281 +        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
     282 +            let keys_to_remove = map
     283 +                .keys()
     284 +                .filter(|key| is_medicare_field_name(key))
     285 +                .cloned()
     286 +                .collect::<Vec<_>>();
     287 +
     288 +            for key in keys_to_remove {
     289 +                map.remove(&key);
     290 +            }
     291 +
     292 +            for value in map.values_mut() {
     293 +                strip_medicare_fields(value);
     294 +            }
     295 +        }
     296 +        serde_json::Value::Array(items) => {
     297 +            for item in items {
     298 +                strip_medicare_fields(item);
     299 +            }
     300 +        }
     301 +        _ => {}
     302 +    }
     303 +}
     304 +
     305 +fn extract_include_medicare(query: Vec<(String, String)>) -> (bool, Vec<(String, String)>) {
     306 +    let mut include_medicare = false;
     307 +    let mut upstream_query = Vec::with_capacity(query.len());
     308 +    for (key, value) in query {
     309 +        if key == "include_medicare" {
     310 +            include_medicare = value.eq_ignore_ascii_case("true");
     311 +            continue;
     312 +        }
     313 +        upstream_query.push((key, value));
     314 +    }
     315 +    (include_medicare, upstream_query)
     316 +}

• Implemented.

  ### What changed

  - Added a default Medicare-field filter in api-layer claims/ratings proxy responses:
      - Medicare raw fields are now removed unless include_medicare=true.
      - The flag is consumed by api-layer and not forwarded upstream (avoids upstream validation breakage).
      - Code: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:29, api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:263, api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:305, api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:460.
  - include_medicare is now accepted as a validated boolean query param on all claims/ratings routes in this module (including legacy aliases).
      - Query normalization update: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:318.
  - OpenAPI updated with reusable param component + wired into claims/ratings/legacy endpoints:
      - Component: api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:3352
      - Example route refs: api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:1257, api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:1577, api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:1972.
  - Tests added:
      - Route-unit tests for flag handling + stripping logic: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:1326.
      - E2E contract check for default-vs-include_medicare=true behavior: api-layer/tests_e2e/test_claims_ratings_and_npi_contracts.py:69.

  ### Validation run

  - cd api-layer && cargo test -q -> 88 passed.
  - Targeted e2e test ran but skipped in current env due preconditions/rate-limit.

  Usage now:

  - Default: /api/v1/pricing/providers/search-by-procedure?... (Medicare raw fields hidden)
  - Opt-in: /api/v1/pricing/providers/search-by-procedure?...&include_medicare=true

    4159 +def _normalize_phone(value: Any) -> str | None:
    4160 +    raw = str(value or "").strip()
    4161 +    if not raw:
    4162 +        return None
    4163 +    digits = "".join(ch for ch in raw if ch.isdigit())
    4164 +    if len(digits) == 11 and digits.startswith("1"):
    4165 +        digits = digits[1:]
    4166 +    if len(digits) == 10:
    4167 +        return f"({digits[:3]}) {digits[3:6]}-{digits[6:]}"
    4168 +    return raw
    4169 +
    4170 +
    4171 +def _compose_provider_address(
    4172 +    *,
    4173 +    formatted_address: Any,
    4174 +    address_line_1: Any,
    4175 +    address_line_2: Any,
    4176 +    city: Any,
    4177 +    state: Any,
    4178 +    zip_code: str | None,
    4179 +) -> str | None:
    4180 +    formatted = str(formatted_address or "").strip()
    4181 +    if formatted:
    4182 +        return formatted
    4183 +
    4184 +    line_1 = str(address_line_1 or "").strip()
    4185 +    line_2 = str(address_line_2 or "").strip()
    4186 +    city_text = str(city or "").strip()
    4187 +    state_text = str(state or "").strip()
    4188 +
    4189 +    locality = ", ".join(part for part in [city_text, state_text] if part)
    4190 +    if locality and zip_code:
    4191 +        locality = f"{locality} {zip_code}"
    4192 +    elif zip_code and not locality:
    4193 +        locality = zip_code
    4194 +
    4195 +    parts = [part for part in [line_1, line_2, locality] if part]
    4196 +    if not parts:
    4197 +        return None
    4198 +    return ", ".join(parts)
    4199 +
    4200 +
    4201 +def _build_google_maps_url(
    4202 +    *,
    4203 +    lat: float | None,
    4204 +    long: float | None,
    4205 +    address: str | None,
    4206 +    city: Any,
    4207 +    state: Any,
    4208 +    zip_code: str | None,
    4209 +) -> str | None:
    4210 +    query_value = None
    4211 +    if lat is not None and long is not None:
    4212 +        query_value = f"{lat:.7f},{long:.7f}"
    4213 +    else:
    4214 +        address_text = str(address or "").strip()
    4215 +        if address_text:
    4216 +            query_value = address_text
    4217 +        else:
    4218 +            city_text = str(city or "").strip()
    4219 +            state_text = str(state or "").strip()
    4220 +            locality = ", ".join(part for part in [city_text, state_text] if part)
    4221 +            if locality and zip_code:
    4222 +                query_value = f"{locality} {zip_code}"
    4223 +            elif locality:
    4224 +                query_value = locality
    4225 +            elif zip_code:
    4226 +                query_value = zip_code
    4227 +    if not query_value:
    4228 +        return None
    4229 +    return f"https://www.google.com/maps/search/?{urlencode({'api': 1, 'query': query_value})}"

• Proposed Plan


  ## MCP Response Compaction: Global Empty-Value Pruning

  ### Summary

  Implement a global response-pruning layer in api-mcp so MCP client responses remove token-wasting fields (null, blank strings, empty arrays/objects) across all MCP outputs, while preserving a stable error
  contract.
  Chosen preferences:

  - Scope: All MCP outputs
  - Error payloads: Keep error contract keys
  - Strings: Trim + drop blank

  ### Goals and Success Criteria

  - Reduce MCP token usage without losing meaningful data.
  - Keep existing behavior/functionality intact (same decisions/results, smaller payloads).
  - Preserve debugging/error usability with stable error field presence.
  - Ensure compatibility for call_operation, call_api, call_api_batch, call_api_read_batch, and orchestrator tools.

  Success = responses are semantically unchanged but smaller, and tests prove:

  1. Empty-value keys are pruned in normal success responses.
  2. Required error envelope keys remain present even when empty.
  3. Existing helper/instruction/recipe tests still pass.

  ———

  ## Public Interface / Behavior Changes

  ### 1) Response payload normalization (all tools)

  For all tool returns, apply recursive prune rules:

  - Drop key if value is:
      - null
      - empty list []
      - empty dict {}
      - blank string after trim ("", "   ", "\n")
  - Keep non-empty scalars (0, false, numbers, non-blank strings).

  ### 2) Error contract stability

  For payloads with ok=false, do not prune these contract keys even if empty:

  - ok, error_type, error
  - operation_id, method, path
  - status_code, status
  - query, path_params
  - warnings, errors
  - fix_it, summary, request_id
    (keep whichever are currently used by that payload type)

  ### 3) No endpoint/tool name changes

  No operation IDs, tool names, or input schemas change.

  ———

  ## Implementation Design (decision-complete)

  ### A) Add central pruning utility in healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py

  Create:

  - _is_blank_string(value: Any) -> bool
  - _prune_empty_values(value: Any, *, preserve_error_contract: bool, error_contract_keys: set[str]) -> Any
  - _compact_mcp_response(value: Any) -> Any (single entrypoint)

  Rules:

  - Recursive prune for dict/list.
  - Trim strings during evaluation.
  - For dicts:
      - Apply prune to child values first.
      - Drop key if child becomes empty by rules.
      - If preserve_error_contract=True, reinsert required error keys if missing (default empty value based on key type: {}, [], None, or keep explicit boolean/strings where required).
  - For lists:
      - prune each item
      - drop emptied items
      - if resulting list empty => caller drops parent key.
  - For non-container scalars:
      - return as-is except blank-string => sentinel/drop.

  ### B) Add one output boundary for all tool results

  At the MCP registration layer, wrap every tool function return through _compact_mcp_response(...) before returning to FastMCP.

  Implementation approach:

  - Introduce internal helper/decorator:
      - _with_compaction(tool_fn) that awaits result and returns compacted result.
  - Apply to all registered tools in build_mcp_server when assigning handlers (including mcp_read_batch_tool_handlers for consistency).

  ### C) Apply compaction in batch helpers

  Ensure batch result arrays are compacted item-wise:

  - After _finalize_batch_results(results), apply _compact_mcp_response.
  - Keep error items contract-stable per rule above.

  ### D) Keep metadata resources compact as well

  For metadata://... resource JSON string builders:

  - Compact payload before json.dumps(...) to reduce instruction/metadata token overhead.
  - Preserve readability fields that are intentionally non-empty.

  ### E) Configurable kill switch (operational safeguard)

  Add env setting in AppSettings:

  - HP_MCP_PRUNE_EMPTY_FIELDS=true (default true)
    If false, bypass compaction completely.
    This allows instant rollback without code revert.

  ———

  ## Edge Cases / Failure Modes

  - 0 and false must never be pruned.
  - Nested structures with all-empty children collapse correctly.
  - Error payloads keep required keys even if empty.
  - String fields with meaningful whitespace inside (e.g. "A B") remain.
  - Non-dict/list tool responses (string/list) remain valid and compacted safely.

  ———

  ## Test Plan

  ### Unit tests (tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py)

  Add:

  1. test_prune_removes_null_blank_empty_containers
  2. test_prune_keeps_zero_false_and_nonblank
  3. test_prune_nested_structures_recursively
  4. test_error_payload_preserves_contract_keys_when_empty
  5. test_batch_results_compacted_but_error_shape_stable
  6. test_compaction_respects_disable_flag

  ### Regression tests

  - Re-run existing suites:
      - tests/test_http_server_helpers.py
      - tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py
      - tests/test_mapping_catalog.py
  - Confirm existing provider-card tests still pass with new compaction layer.

  ### Acceptance scenarios

  1. call_operation(..., summary="compact") returns smaller payload with no null/blank/empty fields.
  2. call_operation(..., summary="full") also compacted (per chosen scope).
  3. Tool input/runtime errors still contain stable key set expected by clients.
  4. call_api_read_batch mixed success/error payload remains parseable with stable error envelopes.

  ———

  ## Rollout / Monitoring

  - Deploy with default HP_MCP_PRUNE_EMPTY_FIELDS=true.
  - Monitor:
      - MCP token usage per request (expect decrease).
      - Error parsing failures in MCP clients (should remain zero).
  - If compatibility issue appears, flip env var to false immediately.

  ———

  ## Assumptions and Defaults

  - Default behavior should prioritize token efficiency globally.
  - Error envelope consistency is more important than max compaction.
  - Trimming and dropping blank strings is acceptable for all outputs.
  - No consumer requires presence of null/empty success fields.


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  regressions.

    3285 +def _compact_response_value(value: Any) -> tuple[Any, bool]:
    3286 +    if value is None:
    3287 +        return None, False
    3288 +
    3289 +    if isinstance(value, str):
    3290 +        compact = value.strip()
    3291 +        return compact, bool(compact)
    3292 +
    3293 +    if isinstance(value, dict):
    3294 +        is_error_payload = value.get("ok") is False
    3295 +        compact_dict: dict[str, Any] = {}
    3296 +        for key, item in value.items():
    3297 +            compact_item, keep_item = _compact_response_value(item)
    3298 +            if keep_item:
    3299 +                compact_dict[key] = compact_item
    3300 +                continue
    3301 +            if is_error_payload and key in _ERROR_PAYLOAD_REQUIRED_KEYS:
    3302 +                compact_dict[key] = item
    3303 +        return compact_dict, bool(compact_dict)
    3304 +
    3305 +    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
    3306 +        compact_list: list[Any] = []
    3307 +        for item in value:
    3308 +            compact_item, keep_item = _compact_response_value(item)
    3309 +            if keep_item:
    3310 +                compact_list.append(compact_item)
    3311 +        return compact_list, bool(compact_list)
    3312 +
    3313 +    return value, True

    3320 +        return fn

    3323 +
    3324 +        @functools.wraps(fn)

    3326 +            payload = await fn(*args, **kwargs)

    674 +        "ok": True,
    675 +        "count": 0,

    678 +    }

    683 +    assert out["items"][0]["in_network"] is False

    687 +    payload = {"ok": True, "empty": "", "rows": []}

─ Worked for 1m 46s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Implemented.

  - Added global MCP payload compaction in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:982 and api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:3290:
      - Removes null, blank strings, empty arrays, and empty objects recursively.
      - Keeps valid scalar values like 0 and false.
      - Applied automatically to all tool responses via wrapped server.add_tool.
  - Applied same compaction to internal batch MCP-tool execution path in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:5342 so call_api_read_batch / call_api_batch MCP-tool items are also pruned.
  - Applied compaction to JSON metadata resources in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1826 (and other metadata resource returns).
  - Added config flag prune_empty_fields with env alias HP_MCP_PRUNE_EMPTY_FIELDS in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/config.py:76.
  - Added env/docs entries:
      - api-mcp/.env.example:15
      - api-mcp/README.md:158
  - Added unit tests for compaction behavior and batch integration in api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:644 and api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1245.

  Validation run:

  - python3 -m pytest -q (in api-mcp) passed.

  If you want to compare old/new behavior quickly, set HP_MCP_PRUNE_EMPTY_FIELDS=false and restart MCP server.

    5661 +def _provider_service_claim_not_found_hint(*, payload: dict[str, Any], request: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    5662 +    if payload.get("ok") is not False:
    5663 +        return None
    5664 +    if _coerce_int(payload.get("status")) != 404:
    5665 +        return None
    5666 +
    5667 +    operation_id = str(request.get("operation_id") or "").strip()
    5668 +    path = str(request.get("path") or "").strip()
    5669 +    if operation_id not in {
    5670 +        "getProviderServiceClaimForProvider",
    5671 +        "listProviderServiceClaimLocationsForProvider",
    5672 +    } and "/api/v1/provider-services-claims/providers/" not in path:
    5673 +        return None
    5674 +
    5675 +    match = re.match(
    5676 +        r"^/api/v1/provider-services-claims/providers/(?P<npi>[^/]+)/services/(?P<code_system>[^/]+)/(?P<code>[^/]+)",
    5677 +        path,
    5678 +        flags=re.IGNORECASE,
    5679 +    )
    5680 +    if not match:
    5681 +        return None
    5682 +
    5683 +    code_system = str(match.group("code_system") or "").strip().upper()
    5684 +    code = str(match.group("code") or "").strip().upper()
    5685 +    query = request.get("query") if isinstance(request.get("query"), dict) else {}
    5686 +    year = _coerce_int(query.get("year")) if isinstance(query, dict) else None
    5687 +    search_query: dict[str, Any] = {
    5688 +        "code": code,
    5689 +        "code_system": code_system,
    5690 +        "limit": 10,
    5691 +    }
    5692 +    if year is not None:
    5693 +        search_query["year"] = year
    5694 +    if isinstance(query, dict):
    5695 +        for key in ("zip5", "state", "city"):
    5696 +            value = query.get(key)
    5697 +            if value not in (None, ""):
    5698 +                search_query[key] = value
    5699 +
    5700 +    notes: list[str] = []
    5701 +    if code_system != "CPT" and len(code) == 5 and code.isdigit() and code.startswith("99"):
    5702 +        notes.append(f"{code} is typically a CPT code; retry with code_system=CPT.")
    5703 +
    5704 +    return {
    5705 +        "hint_code": "provider_service_claim_not_found",
    5706 +        "hint": "No claim row found for this provider + code + year combination.",
    5707 +        "fix_it": {
    5708 +            "reason": (
    5709 +                "Provider-level detail endpoint returned 404. "
    5710 +                "Find providers with matching claims first, then use one returned NPI."
    5711 +            ),
    5712 +            "corrected_call": {
    5713 +                "tool": "call_operation",
    5714 +                "payload": {
    5715 +                    "operation_id": "searchProvidersByProcedure",
    5716 +                    "query": search_query,
    5717 +                    "summary": "compact",
    5718 +                },
    5719 +            },
    5720 +        },
    5721 +        "notes": notes,
    5722 +    }
    5723 +
    5724 +
    5725  def _batch_missing_auth_payload(settings: AppSettings, request: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

─ Worked for 1m 26s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• This 404 is expected for that request shape, not an MCP transport error.

  What’s happening:

  - The NPI in your screenshot (1831353697) has no matching provider-service claim row for that code/year in upstream data.
  - I verified upstream directly:
      - http://127.0.0.1:8085/api/v1/pricing/physicians/1831353697/services?year=2023&limit=10 -> empty items.
      - So .../services/{code_system}/{code} returns 404 for that NPI.
  - For a valid NPI from procedure search (example 1922097971), .../services/cpt/99204?year=2023 returns 200.

  I added MCP-side guardrails so this is clearer next time:

  - Batch 404s for provider service detail now include:
      - hint_code: provider_service_claim_not_found
      - hint
      - fix_it suggesting searchProvidersByProcedure first
      - CPT note for 99xxx codes when needed
  - Code: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:5650, api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:5661
  - Added instruction guardrails for this flow: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1004 (string section)
  - Tests: api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1115
  - Full test suite passes.

  Use this sequence for reliable results:

  1. GET /api/v1/provider-services-claims/services/cpt/99204/providers?zip5=60601&year=2023&limit=10
  2. Pick returned NPI.
  3. GET /api/v1/provider-services-claims/providers/{npi}/services/cpt/99204?year=2023

  If you want, I can also add an MCP tool that performs this 3-step flow in one call.

    5265 +def _is_truthy(value: Any) -> bool:
    5266 +    if isinstance(value, bool):
    5267 +        return value
    5268 +    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
    5269 +        return bool(value)
    5270 +    if isinstance(value, str):
    5271 +        return value.strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on", "y"}
    5272 +    return False
    5273 +
    5274 +
    5275 +def _is_claims_context(*, operation_id: str | None, path: str | None) -> bool:
    5276 +    op = str(operation_id or "").strip()
    5277 +    if "claim" in op.lower() or op in {
    5278 +        "searchProvidersByProcedure",
    5279 +        "searchProvidersByDrug",
    5280 +        "getProviderRating",
    5281 +        "listProviderRatings",
    5282 +    }:
    5283 +        return True
    5284 +    route = str(path or "").strip().lower()
    5285 +    return any(route.startswith(prefix) for prefix in _CLAIMS_PATH_PREFIXES)
    5286 +
    5287 +
    5288 +def _should_include_medicare_fields(query: dict[str, Any] | None) -> bool:
    5289 +    if not isinstance(query, dict):
    5290 +        return False
    5291 +    return _is_truthy(query.get("include_medicare"))
    5292 +
    5293 +
    5294 +def _strip_medicare_reference_fields(value: Any) -> Any:
    5295 +    if isinstance(value, list):
    5296 +        return [_strip_medicare_reference_fields(item) for item in value]
    5297 +    if not isinstance(value, dict):
    5298 +        return value
    5299 +    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
    5300 +    for key, item in value.items():
    5301 +        key_text = str(key).strip()
    5302 +        key_lower = key_text.lower()
    5303 +        if key_text in _MEDICARE_REFERENCE_FIELDS or key_lower.startswith("medicare_"):
    5304 +            continue
    5305 +        out[key] = _strip_medicare_reference_fields(item)
    5306 +    return out
    5307 +
    5308 +
    5309 +def _sanitize_claims_payload(
    5310 +    payload: Any,
    5311 +    *,

    5313 +    path: str | None,
    5314 +    query: dict[str, Any] | None,

    5317 +        return payload
    5318 +    if _should_include_medicare_fields(query):
    5319 +        return payload
    5320 +    return _strip_medicare_reference_fields(payload)

    312 +fn include_legacy_fields_requested(query: &[(String, String)]) -> bool {
    313 +    query
    314 +        .iter()
    315 +        .any(|(key, value)| key == "include_legacy_fields" && value.eq_ignore_ascii_case("true"))
    316 +}
    317 +
    318 +fn canonicalize_service_price_aliases(payload: &mut serde_json::Value, include_legacy_fields: bool) {
    319 +    match payload {
    320 +        serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
    321 +            if !include_legacy_fields {
    322 +                if map.get("avg_submitted_charge").is_none() {
    323 +                    let source = map
    324 +                        .get("charge_per_service_avg")
    325 +                        .or_else(|| map.get("medicare_avg_submitted_charge_per_service"))
    326 +                        .cloned();
    327 +                    if let Some(value) = source {
    328 +                        map.insert("avg_submitted_charge".to_string(), value);
    329 +                    }
    330 +                }
    331 +
    332 +                if map.get("avg_allowed_amount").is_none() {
    333 +                    let source = map
    334 +                        .get("average_price")
    335 +                        .or_else(|| map.get("medicare_avg_allowed_amount_per_service"))
    336 +                        .or_else(|| map.get("medicare_average_price_per_service"))
    337 +                        .cloned();
    338 +                    if let Some(value) = source {
    339 +                        map.insert("avg_allowed_amount".to_string(), value);
    340 +                    }
    341 +                }
    342 +
    343 +                for key in SERVICE_PRICE_ALIAS_FIELDS {
    344 +                    map.remove(key);
    345 +                }
    346 +            }
    347 +
    348 +            for value in map.values_mut() {
    349 +                canonicalize_service_price_aliases(value, include_legacy_fields);
    350 +            }
    351 +        }
    352 +        serde_json::Value::Array(items) => {
    353 +            for item in items {
    354 +                canonicalize_service_price_aliases(item, include_legacy_fields);

─ Worked for 1m 04s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, and they are not all the same metric.

  - avg_submitted_charge: average submitted/billed charge per service.
  - charge_per_service_avg: legacy alias of avg_submitted_charge.
  - average_price: legacy alias of avg_allowed_amount (different metric from submitted charge).

  Also important: avg_allowed_amount is Medicare-claims based in this dataset, not an Aetna negotiated rate.

  I implemented unification in api-layer:

  - Added canonicalization for claims payloads in api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:318.
  - Wired into proxy flow in api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:516 and api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:548.
  - Behavior now:
      - include_legacy_fields=false (default): output canonical fields only, remove aliases.
      - include_legacy_fields=true: keep legacy alias fields for backward compatibility.
  - Added tests in api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:1416 and related tests nearby.

  Validation run:

  - cargo test routes::claims_ratings::tests:: -- --nocapture passed (10/10).

• Implemented.

  Default behavior is now “full submitted charge first”, not Medicare discounted amounts.

  What changed:

  - api-layer now canonicalizes service-price aliases and keeps canonical fields by default:
      - keeps avg_submitted_charge (full submitted charge)
      - keeps total_submitted_charges
      - removes aliases like charge_per_service_avg / average_price unless include_legacy_fields=true
      - file: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:314
  - api-layer Medicare stripping now removes discounted benchmark fields only:
      - removes avg_allowed_amount, total_allowed_amount, avg_payment_amount, avg_standardized_amount, medicare_*, etc.
      - no longer removes submitted/full-charge fields
      - file: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:269
  - Applied in proxy flow for all claims/rating routes:
      - file: api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:513
  - OpenAPI description updated:
      - include_medicare=false => submitted/full-charge default, discounted Medicare fields omitted
      - file: api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:3353
  - api-mcp sanitizer aligned with same policy:
      - keeps submitted/full-charge fields by default

    449 +async def _enrich_provider_service_cost_indices(
    450 +    session,
    451 +    items: list[dict[str, Any]],
    452 +    *,
    453 +    year: int,
    454 +    internal_codes: list[int],
    455 +    fallback_state: str | None = None,
    456 +    fallback_city: str | None = None,
    457 +    fallback_zip5: str | None = None,
    458 +) -> None:
    459 +    if not items or not internal_codes:
    460 +        return
    461 +    if not await _table_exists(session, provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.name):
    462 +        return
    463 +    if not await _table_exists(session, procedure_peer_stats_table.name):
    464 +        return
    465 +
    466 +    npi_candidates: dict[int, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    467 +    geography_values_by_scope: dict[str, set[str]] = {"national": {"US"}}
    468 +    for item in items:
    469 +        npi_raw = item.get("npi")
    470 +        if npi_raw is None:
    471 +            continue
    472 +        try:
    473 +            npi = int(str(npi_raw).strip())
    474 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    475 +            continue
    476 +        if npi <= 0:
    477 +            continue
    478 +
    479 +        state = str(item.get("state") or fallback_state or "").strip().upper()
    480 +        city = str(item.get("city") or fallback_city or "").strip().lower()
    481 +        zip5 = str(item.get("zip5") or fallback_zip5 or "").strip()
    482 +        geography_candidates = _geography_candidates(state_raw=state, city_raw=city, zip5_raw=zip5)
    483 +        if not geography_candidates:
    484 +            continue
    485 +
    486 +        npi_candidates[npi] = geography_candidates
    487 +        for scope, value in geography_candidates:
    488 +            geography_values_by_scope.setdefault(scope, set()).add(value)
    489 +
    490 +    if not npi_candidates:
    491 +        return
    492 +
    493 +    geography_clauses = []
    494 +    for scope, values in geography_values_by_scope.items():
    495 +        sanitized_values = sorted({str(value).strip() for value in values if str(value).strip()})
    496 +        if not sanitized_values:
    497 +            continue
    498 +        geography_clauses.append(
    499 +            and_(
    500 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.geography_scope == scope,
    501 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.geography_value.in_(sanitized_values),
    502 +            )
    503 +        )
    504 +    if not geography_clauses:
    505 +        return
    506 +
    507 +    profile_result = await session.execute(
    508 +        select(provider_procedure_cost_profile_table).where(
    509 +            and_(
    510 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.year == year,
    511 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.npi.in_(sorted(npi_candidates.keys())),
    512 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.procedure_code.in_(internal_codes),
    513 +                provider_procedure_cost_profile_table.c.setting_key == "all",
    514 +                or_(*geography_clauses),
    515 +            )
    516 +        )
    517 +    )
    518 +    profile_rows = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in profile_result]
    519 +    if not profile_rows:
    520 +        return
    521 +
    522 +    profiles_by_npi_and_geo: dict[tuple[int, str, str], list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
    523 +    for row in profile_rows:
    524 +        npi_raw = row.get("npi")
    525 +        if npi_raw is None:
    526 +            continue
    527 +        try:
    528 +            npi = int(npi_raw)
    529 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    530 +            continue
    531 +        geography_scope = str(row.get("geography_scope") or "").strip()
    532 +        geography_value = str(row.get("geography_value") or "").strip()
    533 +        if not geography_scope or not geography_value:
    534 +            continue
    535 +        profiles_by_npi_and_geo.setdefault((npi, geography_scope, geography_value), []).append(row)
    536 +
    537 +    selected_profiles: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    538 +    for npi, candidates in npi_candidates.items():
    539 +        selected: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    540 +        for geography_scope, geography_value in candidates:
    541 +            candidates_for_geo = profiles_by_npi_and_geo.get((npi, geography_scope, geography_value), [])
    542 +            if not candidates_for_geo:
    543 +                continue
    544 +            selected = max(
    545 +                candidates_for_geo,
    546 +                key=lambda payload: (
    547 +                    _as_float(payload.get("claim_count")) or 0.0,
    548 +                    _as_float(payload.get("avg_submitted_charge")) or 0.0,
    549 +                ),
    550 +            )
    551 +            break
    552 +        if selected is not None:
    553 +            selected_profiles[npi] = selected
    554 +
    555 +    if not selected_profiles:
    556 +        return
    557 +
    558 +    peer_key_candidates: set[tuple[int, int, str, str, str, str]] = set()
    559 +    for profile in selected_profiles.values():
    560 +        procedure_code_raw = profile.get("procedure_code")
    561 +        year_raw = profile.get("year")
    562 +        geography_scope = str(profile.get("geography_scope") or "").strip()
    563 +        geography_value = str(profile.get("geography_value") or "").strip()
    564 +        specialty_key = str(profile.get("specialty_key") or "").strip().lower()
    565 +        setting_key = str(profile.get("setting_key") or "all").strip().lower() or "all"
    566 +        if not geography_scope or not geography_value:
    567 +            continue
    568 +        try:
    569 +            procedure_code = int(procedure_code_raw)
    570 +            profile_year = int(year_raw)
    571 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    572 +            continue
    573 +        specialty_candidates = [specialty_key] if specialty_key else []
    574 +        specialty_candidates.append("__all__")
    575 +        for peer_specialty in specialty_candidates:
    576 +            peer_key_candidates.add(
    577 +                (
    578 +                    procedure_code,
    579 +                    profile_year,
    580 +                    geography_scope,
    581 +                    geography_value,
    582 +                    peer_specialty,
    583 +                    setting_key,
    584 +                )
    585 +            )
    586 +
    587 +    if not peer_key_candidates:
    588 +        return
    589 +
    590 +    peer_clauses = [
    591 +        and_(
    592 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.procedure_code == procedure_code,
    593 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.year == profile_year,
    594 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.geography_scope == geography_scope,
    595 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.geography_value == geography_value,
    596 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.specialty_key == peer_specialty,
    597 +            procedure_peer_stats_table.c.setting_key == setting_key,
    598 +        )
    599 +        for (
    600 +            procedure_code,
    601 +            profile_year,
    602 +            geography_scope,
    603 +            geography_value,
    604 +            peer_specialty,
    605 +            setting_key,
    606 +        ) in peer_key_candidates
    607 +    ]
    608 +    peer_result = await session.execute(select(procedure_peer_stats_table).where(or_(*peer_clauses)))
    609 +    peer_rows = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in peer_result]
    610 +    if not peer_rows:
    611 +        return
    612 +
    613 +    peers_by_key: dict[tuple[int, int, str, str, str, str], dict[str, Any]] = {}
    614 +    for row in peer_rows:
    615 +        try:
    616 +            key = (
    617 +                int(row.get("procedure_code")),
    618 +                int(row.get("year")),
    619 +                str(row.get("geography_scope") or "").strip(),
    620 +                str(row.get("geography_value") or "").strip(),
    621 +                str(row.get("specialty_key") or "").strip().lower(),
    622 +                str(row.get("setting_key") or "").strip().lower(),
    623 +            )
    624 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    625 +            continue
    626 +        peers_by_key[key] = row
    627 +
    628 +    for item in items:
    629 +        npi_raw = item.get("npi")
    630 +        if npi_raw is None:
    631 +            continue
    632 +        try:
    633 +            npi = int(str(npi_raw).strip())
    634 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    635 +            continue
    636 +
    637 +        profile = selected_profiles.get(npi)
    638 +        if profile is None:
    639 +            continue
    640 +
    641 +        try:
    642 +            procedure_code = int(profile.get("procedure_code"))
    643 +            profile_year = int(profile.get("year"))
    644 +        except (TypeError, ValueError):
    645 +            continue
    646 +
    647 +        geography_scope = str(profile.get("geography_scope") or "").strip()
    648 +        geography_value = str(profile.get("geography_value") or "").strip()
    649 +        specialty_key = str(profile.get("specialty_key") or "").strip().lower()
    650 +        setting_key = str(profile.get("setting_key") or "all").strip().lower() or "all"
    651 +
    652 +        peer_row = peers_by_key.get(
    653 +            (
    654 +                procedure_code,
    655 +                profile_year,
    656 +                geography_scope,
    657 +                geography_value,
    658 +                specialty_key,
    659 +                setting_key,
    660 +            )
    661 +        )
    662 +        if peer_row is None:
    663 +            peer_row = peers_by_key.get(
    664 +                (
    665 +                    procedure_code,
    666 +                    profile_year,
    667 +                    geography_scope,
    668 +                    geography_value,
    669 +                    "__all__",
    670 +                    setting_key,
    671 +                )
    672 +            )
    673 +        if peer_row is None:
    674 +            continue
    675 +
    676 +        estimated_cost_level = _cost_level_from_thresholds(
    677 +            _as_float(profile.get("avg_submitted_charge")),
    678 +            _as_float(peer_row.get("p20")),
    679 +            _as_float(peer_row.get("p40")),
    680 +            _as_float(peer_row.get("p60")),
    681 +            _as_float(peer_row.get("p80")),
    682 +        )
    683 +        if estimated_cost_level is None:
    684 +            continue
    685 +        cost_index = _cost_level_to_index(estimated_cost_level)
    686 +        if cost_index is not None:
    687 +            item["cost_index"] = cost_index
    688 +        item["estimated_cost_level"] = estimated_cost_level

• Implemented.

  ### What changed

  - cost_index is now emitted as symbolic buckets ($, $$, $$$, $$$$, $$$$$) when peer benchmark enrichment is available, instead of 1.0..5.0.
  - estimated_cost_level remains in sync with that same value.
  - If peer enrichment is not available for a row, behavior still falls back to existing numeric cost_index (legacy compatibility).

  - Enrichment logic: healthcare-mrf-api/api/endpoint/pricing.py:435

     1 +use axum::{
     2 +    http::{header, HeaderValue, StatusCode},
     3 +    response::{IntoResponse, Response},
     4 +};
     5 +
     6 +const OPENAPI_YAML: &str = include_str!(concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/openapi/openapi.yaml"));
     7 +const CACHE_CONTROL_VALUE: &str = "public, max-age=300";
     8 +
     9 +pub async fn get_openapi_yaml() -> Response {
    10 +    let mut response = (StatusCode::OK, OPENAPI_YAML).into_response();
    11 +    let headers = response.headers_mut();
    12 +    headers.insert(
    13 +        header::CONTENT_TYPE,
    14 +        HeaderValue::from_static("application/yaml; charset=utf-8"),
    15 +    );
    16 +    headers.insert(
    17 +        header::CACHE_CONTROL,
    18 +        HeaderValue::from_static(CACHE_CONTROL_VALUE),
    19 +    );
    20 +    headers.insert(
    21 +        header::ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_ORIGIN,
    22 +        HeaderValue::from_static("*"),
    23 +    );
    24 +    response
    25 +}
