• Added api-app/app/api/openapi/api-layer/route.ts (+61 -0)
     1 +import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2 +
     3 +export const runtime = "nodejs";
     4 +export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
     5 +
     6 +const DEFAULT_OPENAPI_UPSTREAM_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:8080/openapi.yaml";
     7 +
     8 +function resolveOpenApiUpstreamUrl(): string {
     9 +  const candidates = [
    10 +    process.env.HP_API_APP_DOCS_OPENAPI_UPSTREAM_URL,
    11 +    process.env.HP_API_LAYER_OPENAPI_URL,
    12 +    DEFAULT_OPENAPI_UPSTREAM_URL,
    13 +  ];
    14 +  for (const candidate of candidates) {
    15 +    const trimmed = String(candidate || "").trim();
    16 +    if (trimmed.length > 0) {
    17 +      return trimmed;
    18 +    }
    19 +  }
    20 +  return DEFAULT_OPENAPI_UPSTREAM_URL;
    21 +}
    22 +
    23 +export async function GET(): Promise<NextResponse> {
    24 +  const upstreamUrl = resolveOpenApiUpstreamUrl();
    25 +  try {
    26 +    const upstreamResponse = await fetch(upstreamUrl, {
    27 +      method: "GET",
    28 +      cache: "no-store",
    29 +      redirect: "follow",
    30 +    });
    31 +    if (!upstreamResponse.ok) {
    32 +      return NextResponse.json(
    33 +        {
    34 +          error: "OpenAPI upstream returned non-success status",
    35 +          status: upstreamResponse.status,
    36 +          upstream_url: upstreamUrl,
    37 +        },
    38 +        { status: 502 },
    39 +      );
    40 +    }
    41 +
    42 +    const contentType =
    43 +      upstreamResponse.headers.get("content-type") || "application/yaml; charset=utf-8";
    44 +    const body = await upstreamResponse.text();
    45 +    return new NextResponse(body, {
    46 +      status: 200,
    47 +      headers: {
    48 +        "content-type": contentType,
    49 +        "cache-control": "public, max-age=300",
    50 +      },
    51 +    });
    52 +  } catch {
    53 +    return NextResponse.json(
    54 +      {
    55 +        error: "OpenAPI upstream is unavailable",
    56 +        upstream_url: upstreamUrl,
    57 +      },
    58 +      { status: 502 },
    59 +    );
    60 +  }
    61 +}

    34 +        let headers = response.headers();
    35 +        assert_eq!(
    36 +            headers
    37 +                .get(header::CONTENT_TYPE)

    39 +            Some("application/yaml; charset=utf-8")
    40 +        );
    41 +        assert_eq!(
    42 +            headers
    43 +                .get(header::CACHE_CONTROL)
    44 +                .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()),
    45 +            Some(CACHE_CONTROL_VALUE)
    46 +        );
    47 +        assert_eq!(
    48 +            headers
    49 +                .get(header::ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_ORIGIN)
    50 +                .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()),

    52 +        );

    63 +        assert!(text.contains("paths:"));
    64 +    }
    65 +}

─ Worked for 1m 28s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I found the cause: docs context can still return an old OpenAPI URL, so the UI tries it first and logs a 404 before falling back. I’m adding compatibility normalization plus a redirect from /openapi/api-
  layer.yaml to /api/openapi/api-layer to eliminate this.

─ Worked for 1m 19s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Root cause confirmed: listProviders (GET /api/v1/providers) did not allow npi in query validation, so MCP call {"operation_id":"listProviders","query":{"npi":"1831353697"}} returned 400 invalid_request.

  Implemented fix in api-layer:

  - Added npi as accepted filter for provider list validation in api-layer/src/routes/providers.rs:388.
  - Added compatibility behavior: if npi is present on list route, resolve exact provider via upstream /api/v1/npi/id/{npi} and return list-style response (items max 1) in api-layer/src/routes/
    providers.rs:1167.
  - Added unit tests for this behavior in api-layer/src/routes/providers.rs:2216.
  - Added e2e regression test in api-layer/tests_e2e/test_provider_endpoints.py:91.
  - Updated OpenAPI so MCP discovers npi as valid list param:
      - route param ref in api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:880
      - parameter schema NpiFilterQuery in api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:2494

  Validation:

  - cargo test -q passed (97 tests).
  - OpenAPI YAML parse check passed.

  After deploy, your MCP call with operation_id=listProviders + query.npi will stop failing.
  Preferred call is still getProviderByNpi for exact lookup, but now both paths work.

• Yes. For treatment cost planning, these are the CMS sources I’d use first:

  1. Claims-based provider/service cost benchmarks (Medicare, latest release includes 2023 service year)

  - https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-physician-other-practitioners/medicare-physician-other-practitioners-by-provider-and-service
  - https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-physician-other-practitioners/medicare-physician-other-practitioners-by-geography-and-service
  - https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-outpatient-hospitals/medicare-outpatient-hospitals-by-provider-and-service

  2. Drug utilization/spending benchmarks (Medicare Part D, includes 2023 in current release)

  - https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-part-d-prescribers/medicare-part-d-prescribers-by-provider-and-drug
  - https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-part-d-prescribers/medicare-part-d-prescribers-by-geography-and-drug
  - https://data.cms.gov/summary-statistics-on-use-and-payments/medicare-medicaid-spending-by-drug/medicare-part-d-spending-by-drug
  - https://data.cms.gov/summary-statistics-on-use-and-payments/medicare-medicaid-spending-by-drug/medicare-part-b-spending-by-drug

  3. Coding/pricing references (for normalization and mapping)

  - https://www.cms.gov/medicare/payment/fee-schedules/physician/pfs-relative-value-files
  - https://www.cms.gov/medicare/coding-billing/healthcare-common-procedure-system/quarterly-update
  - https://www.cms.gov/medicare/coding-billing/icd-10-codes

  4. Commercial/out-of-pocket context (non-Medicare)

  - https://www.cms.gov/priorities/key-initiatives/hospital-price-transparency
  - https://download.cms.gov/marketplace-puf/2026/plan-attributes-puf.zip
  - https://download.cms.gov/marketplace-puf/2026/rate-puf.zip
  - https://download.cms.gov/marketplace-puf/2026/benefits-and-cost-sharing-puf.zip
  - https://www.cms.gov/medical-bill-rights/help/guides/good-faith-estimate

  5. Master CMS dataset catalog

  - https://data.cms.gov/

  If you want, I can also turn this into a short “source policy” section for mrf-api and api-layer docs (including “claims freshest year currently 2023”).

• Proposed Plan


  # Doctor Page: Top 10 Procedures + Cost + ZIP5 Cost-Index

  ## Summary

  Add a new card/table on doctor detail pages that shows the doctor’s top 10 procedures (by volume), each with:

  1. Procedure name/code
  2. Procedure cost (full provider charge proxy)
  3. ZIP5 cost-index level ($ to $$$$$) for that procedure

  Data will come directly from healthcare-mrf-api (8085) which the pharmacy project already uses.

  ## Scope

  In scope:

  1. pharmacy/site doctor page backend data-loading
  2. doctor page template rendering
  3. unit tests for loading/formatting/fallback
  4. graceful timeout and no-regression behavior

  Out of scope:

  1. changing mrf-api endpoint contracts
  2. changing api-layer/mcp behavior
  3. adding new external APIs

  ## Implementation Plan

  ### 1. Add a dedicated pricing client wrapper

  Create root/portal/clients/pricing.py with a thin async client for mrf pricing endpoints.

  Methods:

  1. provider_top_procedures(npi: int, year: int = 2023, limit: int = 10)
     Calls: /pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures with order_by=total_services&order=desc
  2. provider_procedure_cost_index(npi: int, code_system: str, code: str, zip5: str, year: int = 2023)
     Calls: /pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures/{code_system}/{code}/estimated-cost-level?zip5=...

  Use existing make_api_request(..., api_type='npi') so it continues using current mrf base URL and cache behavior.

  Update root/portal/clients/__init__.py exports to include PricingClient.

  ### 2. Build doctor-procedure aggregation service logic

  In root/portal/doctor/services.py, add a new function:
  load_doctor_top_procedures(pricing_client, npi_data, year=2023, limit=10) -> dict

  Behavior:

  1. Read doctor npi and primary zip_code from npi_data['addresses'][0].
  2. Fetch top procedures from /pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures.
  3. Keep first 10 rows (already ordered by total_services desc).
  4. For each row, resolve cost-index by calling estimated-cost-level endpoint using:
      - reported_code_system + reported_code when available
      - fallback to service_code_system + service_code (HP code) if needed
  5. Normalize each row into UI shape:
      - procedure_name
      - procedure_code
      - code_system
      - total_services
      - avg_cost (prefer provider.avg_submitted_charge from cost-index call; fallback total_submitted_charges / total_services)
      - cost_index_level ($...$$$$$, no numeric conversion)
      - cost_index_scope (zip5)
      - cost_index_zip5
  6. Add robust fallback:
      - If no procedures: return items=[] with reason no_claims_data
      - If cost-index lookup fails per row: still include row with cost_index_level=None
  7. Apply timeout guardrails:
      - top list fetch timeout: 2.5s
      - per cost-index fetch timeout: 1.5s
      - bounded concurrency for per-row calls: 4

  Return payload:

  1. items
  2. meta (year_used, zip5, source, partial-failure counts)

  ### 3. Wire into doctor page view

  Update root/portal/doctor/views.py (stream_doctor_api flow):

  1. Instantiate PricingClient once near existing clients.
  2. Add async task for top procedures after prepare_doctor_detail(...) succeeds.
  3. Await and pass normalized payload into template context as:
      - doctor_top_procedures
  4. Ensure this task never fails the page render:
      - exceptions are swallowed into safe empty payload + warning log.

  ### 4. Render new table on doctor page

  Update root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:

  1. Insert a new card (near “Health insurance networks accepted” section).
  2. Title example: Top Procedures and Cost Levels (ZIP {{ zip }})
  3. Table columns:
      - Procedure
      - Code
      - Avg Procedure Cost
      - Cost Index (ZIP5)
      - Volume (Services)
  4. Display cost-index as symbols only ($..$$$$$).
  5. Show empty state when no data:
      - “No recent procedure-claims pricing available for this provider.”
  6. Footer/source line:
      - Source: CMS physician/provider claims via HealthPorta MRF API (freshest complete year: 2023).

  ### 5. Logging and observability

  Add structured warning logs for:

  1. list fetch timeout/failure
  2. per-procedure cost-index failures
  3. missing code/zip cases

  No hard failure path for page response.

  ## Public Interfaces / Type Changes

  Internal app interface additions:

  1. New client class: PricingClient
  2. New template context key: doctor_top_procedures
  3. New normalized row contract in Python dict:
      - procedure_name, procedure_code, code_system, avg_cost, cost_index_level, total_services, cost_index_zip5

  No external URL contract changes for pharmacy routes.

  ## Test Plan

  ### Unit tests

  Add/update tests in tests/python/test_doctor_services.py:

  1. load_doctor_top_procedures returns top 10 sorted rows.
  2. Uses doctor primary ZIP for cost-index lookup.
  3. Preserves rows when cost-index lookup fails for subset.
  4. Empty list behavior when provider has no claims.
  5. Cost fallback logic when avg_submitted_charge missing.

  ### View/template tests

  Add test(s) for doctor detail render:

  1. Table renders when doctor_top_procedures.items present.
  2. Empty-state renders when list is empty.
  3. Page still returns 200 when pricing calls fail.

  ### Manual verification scenarios

  1. Doctor with claims data: table populated with 10 rows.
  2. Doctor without claims data: empty state only.
  3. Slow mrf endpoint: page still loads; section degrades gracefully.
  4. Confirm displayed cost-index symbols are $..$$$$$ (not numeric).

  ## Assumptions and Defaults

  1. Sort: top procedures by total_services desc.
  2. Cost column: full-price proxy via avg_submitted_charge.
  3. Year default: 2023 (freshest complete claims year).
  4. ZIP scope: doctor primary ZIP only.
  5. Load mode: server-rendered with timeout/fallback.
  6. If ZIP missing/invalid, show costs but leave cost-index blank.


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


› use all operations async, try to await on template side(check how we do it) to avoid additional delays, so initating tasks on the start and try to finish inside template rendeding

  critical-path delay. I’ll confirm the current filter behavior and then provide the updated execution spec.

• Proposed Plan


  # Doctor Page Procedures Table (Async-First Rendering)

  ## Summary

  Implement a new doctor-page section with top 10 procedures, full procedure cost, and ZIP5 cost-index, using mrf-api directly.
  Execution model: start tasks early in stream_doctor_api, pass futures into template, resolve near render point via |return_if_async.

  ## Async Execution Design

  ### 1. Start tasks immediately in view

  In root/portal/doctor/views.py:

  1. After prepare_doctor_detail(...) and npi_data is available, immediately create:
      - tasks['doctor_top_procedures_async'] = asyncio.create_task(load_doctor_top_procedures(...))
  2. Keep existing gather tasks as-is.
  3. Do not await doctor_top_procedures_async in view for HTML path.
  4. For ?json=1 path, explicitly await it and include payload in JSON.

  ### 2. Async inside service, but one await boundary

  In root/portal/doctor/services.py:

  1. Add load_doctor_top_procedures(...) that:
      - fetches top procedures (/pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures, order_by=total_services, order=desc, limit=10, year=2023)
      - concurrently fetches per-row ZIP5 cost-index (/estimated-cost-level) with bounded concurrency
  2. Return normalized payload only once complete.
  3. Keep strict timeout/fallback so task never raises to caller (returns empty/fallback object instead).

  ### 3. Template-side await pattern (single resolution)

  In root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:

  1. Resolve once near section:
      - {% set procedures_data = doctor_top_procedures_async|return_if_async %}
  2. Render from procedures_data.items.
  3. Avoid repeated |return_if_async on the same object in many places.

  ## Data Contract for Template

  procedures_data:

  1. items: list of up to 10 rows:
      - procedure_name
      - procedure_code
      - code_system
      - total_services
      - avg_cost (full cost proxy)
      - cost_index_level ($..$$$$$)
      - zip5
  2. meta:
      - year_used (default 2023)
      - zip5
      - partial_failures
      - source

  ## Cost/Index Rules

  1. Sort: total_services desc.
  2. Cost shown: full-price proxy (avg_submitted_charge, fallback total_submitted_charges / total_services).
  3. Cost-index shown: symbol level from ZIP5 estimated-cost endpoint.
  4. If cost-index unavailable for a row: render N/A, keep row.

  ## Files to Change

  1. root/portal/clients/pricing.py (new)
  2. root/portal/clients/__init__.py
  3. root/portal/doctor/services.py
  4. root/portal/doctor/views.py
  5. root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html
  6. tests/python/test_doctor_services.py
  7. doctor view/template tests file (existing test module used for doctor page rendering)

  ## Tests

  1. Service: returns ordered top-10, ZIP5 index enrichment, and fallback on per-row failures.
  2. View: page renders 200 when pricing task times out/fails.
  3. Template: section renders populated rows and empty-state correctly.
  4. JSON mode: includes resolved procedures payload (not Task object).

  ## Assumptions / Defaults

  1. Year default = 2023.
  2. ZIP scope = doctor primary ZIP.
  3. Server-render flow with template await (no client-side fetch).
  4. No API contract changes in mrf-api.



› Implement the plan.

  tests.

    11 +    async def provider_top_procedures(
    12 +        self,
    13 +        npi: int,
    14 +        *,
    15 +        year: int = 2023,
    16 +        limit: int = 10,
    17 +    ) -> Optional[dict]:
    18 +        params = {
    19 +            'year': year,

    21 +            'order_by': 'total_services',
    22 +            'order': 'desc',
    23 +        }

    25 +            f'/pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures',

    29 +        )

     27  _BASE_URL = 'https://pharmacy-near-me.com'
     28 +_DEFAULT_PROCEDURE_YEAR = 2023
     29 +_DEFAULT_PROCEDURE_LIMIT = 10
     30 +_PROCEDURE_LIST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 2.5
     31 +_PROCEDURE_COST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 1.5
     32 +_PROCEDURE_COST_CONCURRENCY = 4
     33 +_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
     34 +
     35 +
     36 +def _as_float(value: Any) -> Optional[float]:
     37 +    if value in (None, ''):
     38 +        return None
     39 +    try:
     40 +        return float(value)
     41 +    except (TypeError, ValueError):
     42 +        return None
     43 +
     44 +
     45 +def _clean_zip5(value: Any) -> str:
     46 +    digits = ''.join(ch for ch in str(value or '') if ch.isdigit())
     47 +    return digits[:5]
     48 +
     49 +
     50 +def _resolve_procedure_lookup(row: dict) -> tuple[Optional[str], Optional[str]]:
     51 +    reported_code = str(row.get('reported_code') or '').strip()
     52 +    reported_code_system = str(row.get('reported_code_system') or '').strip().upper()
     53 +    if reported_code:
     54 +        if not reported_code_system:
     55 +            reported_code_system = 'CPT' if reported_code.isdigit() and len(reported_code) == 5 else 'HCPCS'
     56 +        return reported_code_system, reported_code
     57 +
     58 +    service_code = str(row.get('service_code') or '').strip()
     59 +    service_code_system = str(row.get('service_code_system') or '').strip().upper()
     60 +    if service_code:
     61 +        if not service_code_system:
     62 +            service_code_system = 'HP_PROCEDURE_CODE'
     63 +        return service_code_system, service_code
     64 +
     65 +    internal_code = row.get('procedure_code')
     66 +    if internal_code is not None:
     67 +        return 'HP_PROCEDURE_CODE', str(internal_code).strip()
     68 +    return None, None
     69 +
     70 +
     71 +def _base_procedure_item(row: dict) -> dict:
     72 +    total_services = _as_float(row.get('total_services'))
     73 +    total_submitted_charges = _as_float(row.get('total_submitted_charges'))
     74 +    avg_cost = _as_float(row.get('avg_submitted_charge'))
     75 +    if avg_cost is None and total_services and total_services > 0 and total_submitted_charges is not None:
     76 +        avg_cost = total_submitted_charges / total_services
     77 +
     78 +    code_system, code = _resolve_procedure_lookup(row)
     79 +    return {
     80 +        'procedure_name': row.get('service_name') or row.get('service_description') or 'Procedure',
     81 +        'procedure_code': code or str(row.get('procedure_code') or ''),
     82 +        'code_system': code_system or '',
     83 +        'total_services': total_services or 0.0,
     84 +        'avg_cost': avg_cost,
     85 +        'cost_index_level': None,
     86 +        'zip5': None,
     87 +    }
     88 +
     89 +
     90 +async def load_doctor_top_procedures(
     91 +    pricing_client: PricingClient,
     92 +    npi_data: dict,
     93 +    *,
     94 +    year: int = _DEFAULT_PROCEDURE_YEAR,
     95 +    limit: int = _DEFAULT_PROCEDURE_LIMIT,
     96 +) -> dict:
     97 +    npi = npi_data.get('npi')
     98 +    addresses = npi_data.get('addresses') or []
     99 +    primary = addresses[0] if addresses else {}
    100 +    zip5 = _clean_zip5(primary.get('zip_code')) if isinstance(primary, dict) else ''
    101 +    response_payload = {
    102 +        'items': [],
    103 +        'meta': {
    104 +            'year_used': year,
    105 +            'zip5': zip5,
    106 +            'source': 'healthcare-mrf-api/provider-services-claims',
    107 +            'partial_failures': 0,
    108 +        },
    109 +    }
    110 +    if not npi:
    111 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'missing_npi'
    112 +        return response_payload
    113 +
    114 +    try:
    115 +        top_rows_payload = await asyncio.wait_for(
    116 +            pricing_client.provider_top_procedures(int(npi), year=year, limit=limit),
    117 +            timeout=_PROCEDURE_LIST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    118 +        )
    119 +    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    120 +        _logger.warning("Doctor procedure list timed out for npi=%s", npi)
    121 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'upstream_timeout'
    122 +        return response_payload
    123 +    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive logging path
    124 +        _logger.exception("Doctor procedure list failed for npi=%s", npi)
    125 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'upstream_error'
    126 +        return response_payload
    127 +
    128 +    rows = top_rows_payload.get('items') if isinstance(top_rows_payload, dict) else None
    129 +    if not rows:
    130 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'no_claims_data'
    131 +        return response_payload
    132 +
    133 +    filtered_rows = [row for row in rows if isinstance(row, dict)]
    134 +    filtered_rows.sort(key=lambda row: _as_float(row.get('total_services')) or 0.0, reverse=True)
    135 +    top_rows = filtered_rows[:limit]
    136 +
    137 +    query_meta = top_rows_payload.get('query') if isinstance(top_rows_payload, dict) else None
    138 +    if isinstance(query_meta, dict):
    139 +        response_payload['meta']['year_used'] = query_meta.get('year_used') or query_meta.get('year') or year
    140 +
    141 +    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(_PROCEDURE_COST_CONCURRENCY)
    142 +    partial_failures = 0
    143 +
    144 +    async def enrich(row: dict) -> dict:
    145 +        nonlocal partial_failures
    146 +        item = _base_procedure_item(row)
    147 +        if not zip5:
    148 +            return item
    149 +
    150 +        code_system, code = _resolve_procedure_lookup(row)
    151 +        if not code_system or not code:
    152 +            return item
    153 +
    154 +        try:
    155 +            async with semaphore:
    156 +                cost_payload = await asyncio.wait_for(
    157 +                    pricing_client.provider_procedure_cost_index(
    158 +                        int(npi),
    159 +                        code_system,
    160 +                        code,
    161 +                        zip5=zip5,
    162 +                        year=year,
    163 +                    ),
    164 +                    timeout=_PROCEDURE_COST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    165 +                )
    166 +        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    167 +            partial_failures += 1
    168 +            _logger.warning(
    169 +                "Doctor procedure cost-index timed out for npi=%s code_system=%s code=%s",
    170 +                npi,
    171 +                code_system,
    172 +                code,
    173 +            )
    174 +            return item
    175 +        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive logging path
    176 +            partial_failures += 1
    177 +            _logger.exception(
    178 +                "Doctor procedure cost-index failed for npi=%s code_system=%s code=%s",
    179 +                npi,
    180 +                code_system,
    181 +                code,
    182 +            )
    183 +            return item
    184 +
    185 +        if not isinstance(cost_payload, dict):
    186 +            partial_failures += 1
    187 +            return item
    188 +
    189 +        item['cost_index_level'] = cost_payload.get('estimated_cost_level')
    190 +        provider_payload = cost_payload.get('provider') if isinstance(cost_payload.get('provider'), dict) else {}
    191 +        avg_submitted_charge = _as_float(provider_payload.get('avg_submitted_charge'))
    192 +        if avg_submitted_charge is not None:
    193 +            item['avg_cost'] = avg_submitted_charge
    194 +        item['zip5'] = zip5
    195 +        return item
    196 +
    197 +    response_payload['items'] = await asyncio.gather(*(enrich(row) for row in top_rows))
    198 +    response_payload['meta']['partial_failures'] = partial_failures
    199 +    return response_payload
    200

     6 +class _Recorder:
     7 +    def __init__(self, payload):
     8 +        self.calls = []
     9 +        self.payload = payload
    10 +
    11 +    async def __call__(self, url, **kwargs):
    12 +        self.calls.append((url, kwargs))
    13 +        return self.payload
    14 +

› Is it possible to output HPCS code instead of CPT where possible?


• Yes, and I can make it automatic. I’m updating the doctor-procedure formatter to prefer HCPCS codes from mrf response mappings when available, while keeping CPT fallback.

• Edited ~/project/pharmacy/site/root/portal/doctor/services.py (+28 -0)
     89
     90 +def _prefer_hcpcs_code(cost_payload: dict, current_code: str, current_system: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
     91 +    query_payload = cost_payload.get('query') if isinstance(cost_payload, dict) else None
     92 +    if isinstance(query_payload, dict):
     93 +        resolved_codes = query_payload.get('resolved_codes')
     94 +        if isinstance(resolved_codes, list):
     95 +            for resolved in resolved_codes:
     96 +                if not isinstance(resolved, dict):
     97 +                    continue
     98 +                code_system = str(resolved.get('code_system') or '').strip().upper()
     99 +                code = str(resolved.get('code') or '').strip()
    100 +                if code_system == 'HCPCS' and code:
    101 +                    return code, code_system
    102 +
    103 +    procedure_payload = cost_payload.get('procedure') if isinstance(cost_payload, dict) else None
    104 +    if isinstance(procedure_payload, dict):
    105 +        reported_code_system = str(procedure_payload.get('reported_code_system') or '').strip().upper()
    106 +        reported_code = str(procedure_payload.get('reported_code') or '').strip()
    107 +        if reported_code_system == 'HCPCS' and reported_code:
    108 +            return reported_code, reported_code_system
    109 +
    110 +    return current_code, current_system
    111 +
    112 +

        ⋮

› What is the logic now.. If doctor has claims data, but not all cost indexes for its zip code - how will it work?


• I’ll quickly inspect the exact current branch logic and summarize precisely how partial ZIP cost-index gaps are handled.

• Explored
  └ Read services.py, doctor_details.html

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Current behavior is:

  1. It always loads top procedures first from claims (/pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures) in load_doctor_top_procedures (root/portal/doctor/services.py:113).
  2. Then for each row it tries ZIP5 cost-index lookup (.../estimated-cost-level) (root/portal/doctor/services.py:182).
  3. If a lookup fails (timeout/error/not found), that row is kept:
      - cost_index_level stays None
      - procedure cost still shows from claims (avg_submitted_charge fallback logic) (root/portal/doctor/services.py:71).
  4. In UI, missing cost index renders as N/A (root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:554).
  5. The whole block is shown whenever there are procedure rows (root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:508), even if all cost indexes are missing.
  6. Count of failed per-row cost lookups is tracked in meta.partial_failures (root/portal/doctor/services.py:231) but not currently displayed on page.

  If you want, I can change it so the block hides when all rows have cost_index_level = None.

• total_benes / total_beneficiaries is the count of unique Medicare Part D beneficiaries (patients) tied to that provider+drug row for the year. It is not visits; it is patient count.

• Questions 1/1 answered
  • Include beneficiaries as a separate column in Top Prescribed Drugs?
    answer: Yes, include it (Recommended)

• Proposed Plan


  # Doctor Page: Top Prescribed Drugs (Async, Medicare-Part-D Labeled)

  ## Summary

  Add a new doctor-page section for Top Prescribed Drugs using mrf claims data, following the same async pattern as procedures:

  - task starts early in stream_doctor_api
  - template resolves via |return_if_async
  - section is hidden when no rows

  Decisions locked:

  - Columns: drug + total prescriptions + beneficiaries + total cost + avg cost per prescription
  - No-data behavior: hide section entirely
  - Year: force 2023

  ## Implementation Details

  ### 1. Data access layer

  Update root/portal/clients/pricing.py with:

  1. provider_top_prescriptions(npi: int, year: int = 2023, limit: int = 10) -> Optional[dict]
      - Calls /pricing/providers/{npi}/prescriptions
      - Params:
          - year=2023
          - limit=10
          - order_by=total_claims
          - order=desc

  No external API changes; this is internal client wiring.

  ### 2. Doctor service aggregation

  In root/portal/doctor/services.py, add:

  1. load_doctor_top_prescriptions(pricing_client: PricingClient, npi_data: dict, year: int = 2023, limit: int = 10) -> dict
  2. Behavior:
      - Validate npi
      - Fetch top prescriptions once (single upstream call)
      - Normalize each item:
          - drug_name: prescription_name fallback rx_name/generic_name/brand_name
          - drug_code: prescription_code fallback rx_code
          - code_system: prescription_code_system fallback rx_code_system
          - total_prescriptions: numeric (total_prescriptions fallback total_claims)
          - total_beneficiaries: numeric (total_beneficiaries fallback total_benes)
          - total_drug_cost: numeric
          - avg_cost_per_prescription: total_drug_cost / total_prescriptions when possible
      - Keep robust fallback on timeout/error:
          - return {items: [], meta: {reason: ...}}
  3. Timeout:
      - one upstream timeout (same pattern used in procedures service)

  ### 3. View wiring (async-first)

  In root/portal/doctor/views.py:

  1. Start early task in tasks map:
      - doctor_top_prescriptions_async = asyncio.create_task(load_doctor_top_prescriptions(...))
  2. JSON response path includes resolved top_prescriptions.
  3. HTML context passes the task object, not awaited value:
      - doctor_top_prescriptions_async: tasks['doctor_top_prescriptions_async']

  ### 4. Template rendering

  In root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:

  1. Resolve once:
      - prescriptions_data = doctor_top_prescriptions_async|return_if_async
      - prescription_rows = prescriptions_data.get('items', [])
  2. Render block only if rows exist:
      - {% if prescription_rows %} ... {% endif %}
  3. Table columns:
      - Drug
      - Code
      - Total Prescriptions
      - Beneficiaries
      - Total Drug Cost
      - Avg Cost / Prescription
  4. Source/disclaimer line in block:
      - Explicitly label as CMS Medicare Part D (2023) data to avoid interpretation as commercial negotiated price.

  ### 5. UX consistency

  Keep section style aligned with procedures card (same card/table visual language), but independent visibility rules:

  - procedures card shown only when procedures rows exist
  - prescriptions card shown only when prescription rows exist

  ## Public Interfaces / Types

  No public API contract changes.
  Internal additions:

  1. New PricingClient.provider_top_prescriptions(...)
  2. New service payload load_doctor_top_prescriptions(...)
  3. New template context key doctor_top_prescriptions_async

  ## Tests

  ### Unit tests

  1. tests/python/test_pricing_client.py
      - add test for provider_top_prescriptions endpoint path + params.
  2. tests/python/test_doctor_services.py
      - success case normalization and computed avg cost
      - fallback naming/code logic
      - empty response -> items=[] and reason
      - timeout/error -> safe empty payload

  ### Integration/view-level checks

  1. doctor page HTML includes prescriptions section when items present.
  2. section absent when no items.
  3. page still renders if prescription fetch fails.

  ## Assumptions / Defaults

  1. Claims source is Medicare Part D only.
  2. Year fixed at 2023 (per your decision).
  3. No additional fan-out calls per drug row (single upstream fetch for performance).
  4. Currency displayed as USD formatted numeric.



› Implement the plan.


• I’m implementing the prescriptions section now in the same async pattern as procedures: client method, service loader, view task wiring, template block, and tests. I’ll run targeted pytest afterward.

    237
    238 +def _base_prescription_item(row: dict) -> dict:
    239 +    total_prescriptions = _as_float(row.get('total_prescriptions'))
    240 +    if total_prescriptions is None:
    241 +        total_prescriptions = _as_float(row.get('total_claims'))
    242 +
    243 +    total_beneficiaries = _as_float(row.get('total_beneficiaries'))
    244 +    if total_beneficiaries is None:
    245 +        total_beneficiaries = _as_float(row.get('total_benes'))
    246 +
    247 +    total_drug_cost = _as_float(row.get('total_drug_cost'))
    248 +    avg_cost_per_prescription = None
    249 +    if total_drug_cost is not None and total_prescriptions and total_prescriptions > 0:
    250 +        avg_cost_per_prescription = total_drug_cost / total_prescriptions
    251 +
    252 +    prescription_name = (
    253 +        row.get('prescription_name')
    254 +        or row.get('rx_name')
    255 +        or row.get('generic_name')
    256 +        or row.get('brand_name')
    257 +        or 'Drug'
    258 +    )
    259 +    prescription_code = str(
    260 +        row.get('prescription_code')
    261 +        or row.get('rx_code')
    262 +        or ''
    263 +    ).strip()
    264 +    prescription_code_system = str(
    265 +        row.get('prescription_code_system')
    266 +        or row.get('rx_code_system')
    267 +        or ''
    268 +    ).strip().upper()
    269 +
    270 +    return {
    271 +        'drug_name': prescription_name,
    272 +        'drug_code': prescription_code,
    273 +        'code_system': prescription_code_system,
    274 +        'total_prescriptions': total_prescriptions or 0.0,
    275 +        'total_beneficiaries': total_beneficiaries or 0.0,
    276 +        'total_drug_cost': total_drug_cost,
    277 +        'avg_cost_per_prescription': avg_cost_per_prescription,
    278 +    }
    279 +
    280 +
    281 +async def load_doctor_top_prescriptions(
    282 +    pricing_client: PricingClient,
    283 +    npi_data: dict,
    284 +    *,
    285 +    year: int = _DEFAULT_PRESCRIPTION_YEAR,
    286 +    limit: int = _DEFAULT_PRESCRIPTION_LIMIT,
    287 +) -> dict:
    288 +    npi = npi_data.get('npi')
    289 +    response_payload = {
    290 +        'items': [],
    291 +        'meta': {
    292 +            'year_used': year,
    293 +            'source': 'healthcare-mrf-api/drug-claims',
    294 +        },
    295 +    }
    296 +    if not npi:
    297 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'missing_npi'
    298 +        return response_payload
    299 +
    300 +    try:
    301 +        top_rx_payload = await asyncio.wait_for(
    302 +            pricing_client.provider_top_prescriptions(int(npi), year=year, limit=limit),
    303 +            timeout=_PRESCRIPTION_LIST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    304 +        )
    305 +    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
    306 +        _logger.warning("Doctor prescription list timed out for npi=%s", npi)
    307 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'upstream_timeout'
    308 +        return response_payload
    309 +    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive logging path
    310 +        _logger.exception("Doctor prescription list failed for npi=%s", npi)
    311 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'upstream_error'
    312 +        return response_payload
    313 +
    314 +    rows = top_rx_payload.get('items') if isinstance(top_rx_payload, dict) else None
    315 +    if not rows:
    316 +        response_payload['meta']['reason'] = 'no_claims_data'
    317 +        return response_payload
    318 +
    319 +    filtered_rows = [row for row in rows if isinstance(row, dict)]
    320 +    filtered_rows.sort(
    321 +        key=lambda row: _as_float(row.get('total_prescriptions')) or _as_float(row.get('total_claims')) or 0.0,
    322 +        reverse=True,
    323 +    )
    324 +    response_payload['items'] = [_base_prescription_item(row) for row in filtered_rows[:limit]]
    325 +
    326 +    query_meta = top_rx_payload.get('query') if isinstance(top_rx_payload, dict) else None
    327 +    if isinstance(query_meta, dict):
    328 +        response_payload['meta']['year_used'] = query_meta.get('year_used') or query_meta.get('year') or year
    329 +    return response_payload
    330 +
    331 +
    332  async def load_doctor_context(npi: int, npi_client: NPIClient, redis_conn) -> Optional[dict]:

• Implemented.

  ### What I changed

  1. Added prescription endpoint wrapper to pricing client
     root/portal/clients/pricing.py:51

  - New method: provider_top_prescriptions(...)
  - Calls GET /pricing/providers/{npi}/prescriptions
  - Uses year=2023, limit=10, order_by=total_claims, order=desc

  2. Added doctor-side async loader for top prescribed drugs
     root/portal/doctor/services.py:281

  - New method: load_doctor_top_prescriptions(...)
  - Normalizes fields:
      - drug_name, drug_code, code_system
      - total_prescriptions, total_beneficiaries
      - total_drug_cost, avg_cost_per_prescription
  - Handles missing data/timeouts gracefully
  - Returns items + meta (with reason when empty/error)

  3. Wired async task at page start (same pattern as procedures)
     root/portal/doctor/views.py:124

  - Starts doctor_top_prescriptions_async immediately
  - Exposes in JSON response: top_prescriptions (root/portal/doctor/views.py:199)
  - Passes async task to template context (root/portal/doctor/views.py:214)

  4. Added new doctor-page section/table
     root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:574

  - Async-resolved with |return_if_async
  - Hidden entirely when no rows
  - Columns:
      - Drug
      - Code
      - Prescriptions
      - Beneficiaries
      - Total Drug Cost
      - Avg Cost / Prescription
  - Explicit source labeling as CMS Medicare Part D claims (root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:638)

  ### Tests

  1. Added pricing client test for new method
     tests/python/test_pricing_client.py:57
  2. Added service tests for new loader
     tests/python/test_doctor_services.py:339

  - normalization/computed average
  - empty result behavior (reason = no_claims_data)

  3. Ran tests:

  - ./venv314/bin/pytest -q tests/python/test_pricing_client.py tests/python/test_doctor_services.py
  - Result: 15 passed

     954 +    session,

     956 +) -> dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]]:
     957 +    normalized_codes = sorted({str(code or "").strip().upper() for code in internal_codes if str(code or "").strip()})
     958 +    if not normalized_codes:
     959 +        return {}
     960 +
     961 +    external_systems = tuple(RX_EXTERNAL_CODE_PRIORITY)
     962 +    query = select(code_crosswalk_table).where(
     963 +        or_(
     964 +            and_(
     965 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.from_system) == INTERNAL_RX_CODE_SYSTEM,
     966 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.from_code).in_(normalized_codes),
     967 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.to_system).in_(external_systems),
     968 +            ),
     969 +            and_(
     970 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.to_system) == INTERNAL_RX_CODE_SYSTEM,
     971 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.to_code).in_(normalized_codes),
     972 +                func.upper(code_crosswalk_table.c.from_system).in_(external_systems),
     973 +            ),
     974 +        )
     975 +    )
     976 +    result = await session.execute(query)
     977 +    rows = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in result]
     978 +
     979 +    mapping: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
     980 +    for row in rows:
     981 +        from_system = str(row.get("from_system") or "").strip().upper()
     982 +        from_code = str(row.get("from_code") or "").strip().upper()
     983 +        to_system = str(row.get("to_system") or "").strip().upper()
     984 +        to_code = str(row.get("to_code") or "").strip().upper()
     985 +
     986 +        internal_code: str | None = None
     987 +        external_system: str | None = None
     988 +        external_code: str | None = None
     989 +
     990 +        if from_system == INTERNAL_RX_CODE_SYSTEM and to_system in external_systems:
     991 +            internal_code = from_code
     992 +            external_system = to_system
     993 +            external_code = to_code
     994 +        elif to_system == INTERNAL_RX_CODE_SYSTEM and from_system in external_systems:
     995 +            internal_code = to_code
     996 +            external_system = from_system
     997 +            external_code = from_code
     998 +
     999 +        if not internal_code or not external_system or not external_code:
    1000 +            continue
    1001 +
    1002 +        target = mapping.setdefault(internal_code, {system: [] for system in external_systems})
    1003 +        if external_code not in target[external_system]:
    1004 +            target[external_system].append(external_code)
    1005 +    return mapping

    1008 +def _select_preferred_external_rx_code(
    1009 +    fallback_system: Any,
    1010 +    fallback_code: Any,
    1011 +    *,
    1012 +    ndc_codes: list[str] | None = None,
    1013 +    rxnorm_codes: list[str] | None = None,
    1014 +) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
    1015 +    for code in ndc_codes or []:
    1016 +        value = str(code or "").strip().upper()
    1017 +        if value:
    1018 +            return "NDC", value
    1019 +    for code in rxnorm_codes or []:
    1020 +        value = str(code or "").strip().upper()
    1021 +        if value:
    1022 +            return "RXNORM", value
    1023 +
    1024 +    fallback_system_text = str(fallback_system or "").strip().upper() or None
    1025 +    fallback_code_text = str(fallback_code or "").strip().upper() or None
    1026 +    return fallback_system_text, fallback_code_text

    1029 +def _apply_prescription_code_preferences(
    1030 +    items: list[dict[str, Any]],
    1031 +    external_codes_by_internal: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] | None = None,
    1032 +) -> None:
    1033 +    external_codes_by_internal = external_codes_by_internal or {}
    1034 +    for item in items:
    1035 +        internal_code = str(item.get("rx_code") or item.get("prescription_code") or "").strip().upper()
    1036 +        mapped = external_codes_by_internal.get(internal_code, {})
    1037 +        ndc_codes = mapped.get("NDC") if isinstance(mapped, dict) else None
    1038 +        rxnorm_codes = mapped.get("RXNORM") if isinstance(mapped, dict) else None
    1039 +
    1040 +        if ndc_codes:
    1041 +            item["ndc_code"] = ndc_codes[0]
    1042 +        if rxnorm_codes:
    1043 +            item["rxnorm_id"] = rxnorm_codes[0]
    1044 +
    1045 +        preferred_system, preferred_code = _select_preferred_external_rx_code(
    1046 +            item.get("prescription_code_system"),
    1047 +            item.get("prescription_code"),
    1048 +            ndc_codes=ndc_codes,
    1049 +            rxnorm_codes=rxnorm_codes,
    1050 +        )
    1051 +        item["preferred_prescription_code_system"] = preferred_system
    1052 +        item["preferred_prescription_code"] = preferred_code

    116 +def _select_preferred_prescription_code(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str]:
    117 +    def _first_nonblank(*values: Any) -> str:
    118 +        for value in values:
    119 +            text = str(value or '').strip().upper()
    120 +            if text:
    121 +                return text
    122 +        return ''
    123 +
    124 +    preferred_system = _first_nonblank(
    125 +        row.get('preferred_prescription_code_system'),
    126 +        row.get('preferred_code_system'),
    127 +    )
    128 +    preferred_code = _first_nonblank(
    129 +        row.get('preferred_prescription_code'),
    130 +        row.get('preferred_code'),
    131 +    )
    132 +    if preferred_system in {'NDC', 'RXNORM'} and preferred_code:
    133 +        return preferred_system, preferred_code

    135 +    ndc_codes = row.get('ndc_codes')
    136 +    if isinstance(ndc_codes, list):
    137 +        ndc_code = _first_nonblank(*ndc_codes)
    138 +        if ndc_code:
    139 +            return 'NDC', ndc_code
    140 +    ndc_code = _first_nonblank(row.get('ndc_code'), row.get('ndc'))
    141 +    if ndc_code:
    142 +        return 'NDC', ndc_code

    146 +        rxnorm_code = _first_nonblank(*rxnorm_codes)
    147 +        if rxnorm_code:
    148 +            return 'RXNORM', rxnorm_code

    151 +        return 'RXNORM', rxnorm_code

    154 +    fallback_system = _first_nonblank(row.get('prescription_code_system'), row.get('rx_code_system'))
    155 +    return fallback_system, fallback_code

› what is the command to recover postgres on 5440?


• I’ll quickly inspect how Postgres is managed on your machine (brew services/pg_ctl) so I can give you the exact recovery command for the 5440 instance.

• Explored
  └ List var
    Search postgres|pgsql

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I can see your main postgresql@18 is up on default data dir. I’ll check the external-drive cluster paths so I can give you the exact start command for the 5440 instance.

• Explored
  └ List postgres
    Read postmaster.pid
    Search ^port\s*=|^listen_addresses\s*=|^unix_socket_directories\s*= in postgresql.conf

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes. For your custom cluster on 5440, use this:

  PGDATA=/Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440
  /opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@18/bin/pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" status

  If it says not running (and especially if postmaster.pid is stale), run:

  PGDATA=/Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440
  rm -f "$PGDATA/postmaster.pid"
  /opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@18/bin/pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" -l "$PGDATA/logfile" start
  pg_isready -h 127.0.0.1 -p 5440

  Stop command (if needed later):

  PGDATA=/Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440
  /opt/homebrew/opt/postgresql@18/bin/pg_ctl -D "$PGDATA" stop


› where is it's config?


• 5440 cluster config is here:

  - postgresql.conf: /Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440/postgresql.conf
  - pg_hba.conf: /Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440/pg_hba.conf
  - pg_ident.conf: /Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440/pg_ident.conf

  (From your current cluster state, PGDATA for this instance is /Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440.)


› can we make it be available via brew services?


• Ran brew services --help

• Explored
  └ Read homebrew.mxcl.postgresql@18.plist

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Not cleanly for two postgresql@18 instances.

  brew services supports one managed service per formula (postgresql@18), so it can’t natively manage both 5432 and 5440 as separate Homebrew services.

  Best setup:

  1. Keep 5432 on brew services (postgresql@18).
  2. Run 5440 as a separate launchctl agent using your custom PGDATA (/Volumes/Data/postgres/postgresql@18_5440).

  If you want, I can set up that launchctl plist now so 5440 auto-starts on login and is easy to start/stop.

• Ran a quick perf check. Short answer: the new drugs/procedures blocks are not the main slowdown.

  MRF Endpoint Speed (8085)

  - /api/v1/pricing/providers/1992153167/procedures?year=2023&limit=10
      - single request: ~21ms
      - wrk (2 threads, 8 conn, 15s): p50 16.65ms, p99 39.49ms, ~461 req/s
  - /api/v1/pricing/providers/1992153167/prescriptions?year=2023&limit=10
      - single request: ~40ms
      - wrk (2 threads, 8 conn, 15s): p50 20.14ms, p99 44.21ms, ~380 req/s

  Doctor Page Speed (5000)

  - Claims-heavy page (canonical URL, no redirect):
      - 20 sequential requests: p50 87ms, p95 97ms, max 104ms
  - Under concurrent load:
      - claims-heavy page (wrk, 2 threads, 6 conn, 10s): p50 ~1.11s, ~5.25 req/s
      - no-claims page (wrk, same settings): p50 ~1.11s, ~5.35 req/s

  Conclusion

  - New claims blocks themselves are fast (MRF calls are tens of milliseconds).
  - Page slowdown under concurrency is in the app/render path and is similar with or without claims blocks.
  - You still see occasional long-tail spikes on some pages; that looks unrelated to these new MRF calls (likely other dependencies in page rendering flow).

• I confirmed the current import only writes HP_RX_CODE -> HP_RX_CODE mappings, so richer external-code coverage requires a dedicated crosswalk enrichment stage. I need one product decision before I lock the
  plan shape.

• Questions 1/1 answered
  • Which source strategy should we implement for HP_RX_CODE -> NDC/RXNORM enrichment?
    answer: Hybrid (Recommended)

• Proposed Plan


  # Enrich HP_RX_CODE -> NDC/RXNORM Crosswalk in healthcare-mrf-api

  ## Summary

  Add a deterministic enrichment stage to the drug-claims import so mrf.code_crosswalk contains high-coverage mappings from internal HP_RX_CODE to external RXNORM and NDC codes.
  Chosen strategy: Hybrid (snapshot/export primary, optional live fallback for misses).
  Goal: provider top-prescriptions and related endpoints show external codes much more often, without slowing normal API reads.

  ## Public API / Interface Changes

  1. No breaking endpoint changes.
  2. Data quality change only: existing pricing/prescription responses will populate external code fields more frequently.
  3. Add import env toggles (new):
      - HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_SOURCE=hybrid|snapshot|live (default hybrid)
      - HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_FALLBACK=true|false (default true in hybrid)
      - HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_CONFIDENCE_MIN (default 0.85)
      - HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_BATCH_SIZE (default 5000)
  4. Add import metrics/log counters (rows scanned, mapped, unresolved, fallback hits).

  ## Implementation Plan (Decision Complete)

  ### 1. Build canonical matching key for HP_RX_CODE

  1. Reuse current canonicalization logic that creates HP_RX_CODE (generic+brand normalization path in process/drug_claims.py).
  2. Materialize a staging table with:
      - hp_rx_code
      - normalized generic token string
      - normalized brand token string
      - source year and counts
  3. Ensure deterministic normalization (casefold, punctuation strip, whitespace collapse).

  ### 2. Add snapshot-based crosswalk source

  1. Define a snapshot contract (CSV or DB read) from drug-api containing at minimum:
      - normalized generic/brand key (or fields needed to derive it)
      - rxnorm_id
      - ndc11 (and source granularity package/product)
  2. Build loader in import flow:
      - read snapshot into temporary staging table
      - index temp table by normalized key
  3. Resolve mappings with SQL joins from HP_RX_CODE staging to snapshot staging.

  ### 3. Add optional live fallback for misses

  1. For unresolved HP_RX_CODE rows after snapshot join:
      - query drug-api lookup endpoints in bounded async batches
      - cache per canonical key during run to avoid repeated network calls
  2. Only accept fallback mapping when confidence threshold passes.
  3. Record source tag:
      - drug_api_snapshot
      - drug_api_live
      - cms_partd_provider_drug (legacy/self)

  ### 4. Write richer rows into mrf.code_crosswalk

  1. Insert directed edges:
      - HP_RX_CODE -> RXNORM
      - HP_RX_CODE -> NDC (store canonical NDC11)
  2. Keep reverse edges too for lookup symmetry:
      - RXNORM -> HP_RX_CODE
      - NDC -> HP_RX_CODE
  3. match_type rules:
      - exact for deterministic key matches
      - normalized for relaxed token matches
  4. confidence rules:
      - 1.0 exact snapshot key
      - 0.9 normalized snapshot
      - 0.85+ live fallback per resolver score
  5. Upsert on (from_system, from_code, to_system, to_code).

  ### 5. Improve de-duplication and ranking

  1. Prefer mappings by:
      - higher confidence
      - newer source year
      - snapshot over live for equal confidence
  2. For multiple NDCs per HP_RX_CODE, keep top-N (default 5) by frequency/recency.
  3. Always keep all RxNorm mappings (usually small cardinality).

  ### 6. Hook into existing drug-claims finalize path

  1. Extend _materialize_prescription_and_code_rows to call enrichment pipeline after base prescription aggregation.
  2. Keep current self-edge insertion as fallback baseline.
  3. Ensure idempotency: rerun import won’t duplicate rows.

  ### 7. Performance safeguards

  1. Run enrichment in chunked SQL batches.
  2. Index requirements:
      - code_crosswalk(from_system, from_code)
      - code_crosswalk(to_system, to_code)
      - checksums already present; keep them updated
  3. Hard timeout and retry policy for live fallback batches.

  ### 8. Documentation updates

  1. Add section in healthcare-mrf-api/specs:
      - source hierarchy
      - confidence model
      - mapping directionality
      - troubleshooting unresolved codes
  2. Add sample SQL verification queries and expected coverage targets.

  ## Tests and Scenarios

  ### Unit tests

  1. Canonicalization parity test: same input strings produce same HP_RX_CODE.
  2. Mapping ranking test (confidence/source precedence).
  3. Deduplication test (no duplicate crosswalk edges).
  4. Fallback gating test (disabled/enabled and threshold behavior).

  ### Import/integration tests

  1. Snapshot-only run produces non-zero HP_RX_CODE -> RXNORM/NDC.
  2. Hybrid run increases mapped count vs snapshot-only with controlled mock misses.
  3. Idempotency run: second import does not inflate counts incorrectly.
  4. Regression: existing endpoints still return valid responses.

  ### API regression checks

  1. Top-prescriptions endpoint for known doctors returns external codes more often.
  2. Reverse lookup endpoints (code -> related) include HP_RX_CODE, RXNORM, NDC.
  3. Response latency unchanged on read endpoints (within current SLO).

  ### Acceptance criteria

  1. At least 3x increase in HP_RX_CODE rows that have >=1 external mapping.
  2. At least 60% of top-prescription rows in sampled doctors include external code(s).
  3. No read-path regression >10% p95 on pricing/prescription endpoints.
  4. Import runtime increase capped (target +15% max in hybrid mode).

  ## Rollout and Monitoring

  1. Phase 1: deploy snapshot-only in dev, validate coverage.
  2. Phase 2: enable hybrid fallback with low concurrency.
  3. Add import log summary:
      - total HP_RX_CODE
      - mapped to RxNorm
      - mapped to NDC
      - unresolved
      - fallback success ratio
  4. Alert if mapped coverage drops >20% from previous successful run.

  ## Assumptions / Defaults

  1. drug-api snapshot/export is available per import run (or via mounted path).
  2. NDC stored as normalized NDC11.
  3. RXNORM stored as numeric string.
  4. Hybrid mode default enabled.
  5. No API contract changes required; this is data enrichment plus import controls.

    582 +        return f"{base}{path}"

    584 +        return f"{base}/drug{path}"

    588 +def _extract_product_ndcs(payload: Any) -> list[str]:
    589 +    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
    590 +        return []
    591 +    candidates: list[str] = []
    592 +    for key in ("generic", "brand"):
    593 +        rows = payload.get(key)
    594 +        if not isinstance(rows, list):
    595 +            continue
    596 +        for row in rows:
    597 +            if not isinstance(row, dict):
    598 +                continue
    599 +            product_ndc = str(row.get("product_ndc") or "").strip()
    600 +            if product_ndc:
    601 +                candidates.append(product_ndc)
    602 +    # preserve order while deduping
    603 +    return list(dict.fromkeys(candidates))

    607 +    value = await db.scalar(
    608 +        "SELECT to_regclass(:qualified_name) IS NOT NULL;",
    609 +        qualified_name=f"{schema}.{table}",
    610 +    )
    611 +    return bool(value)
