    1416 +    await db.status(
    1417 +        f"""
    1418 +        CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_hp_rx_codes ON COMMIT DROP AS
    1419 +        SELECT
    1420 +            rx_code,
    1421 +            COALESCE(total_claims, 0)::double precision AS total_claims,
    1422 +            NULLIF(UPPER(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(generic_name, ''), '[^A-Za-z0-9]+', '', 'g')), '') AS generic_key,
    1423 +            NULLIF(UPPER(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(brand_name, ''), '[^A-Za-z0-9]+', '', 'g')), '') AS brand_key,
    1424 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(generic_name), '') AS generic_name,
    1425 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(brand_name), '') AS brand_name,
    1426 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(rx_name), '') AS rx_name
    1427 +        FROM {schema}.{prescription_table}
    1428 +        WHERE UPPER(COALESCE(rx_code_system, '')) = '{RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM}'
    1429 +          AND (COALESCE(BTRIM(generic_name), '') <> '' OR COALESCE(BTRIM(brand_name), '') <> '');
    1430 +        """
    1431 +    )
    1432 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_hp_rx_codes_generic_idx ON tmp_hp_rx_codes (generic_key);")
    1433 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_hp_rx_codes_brand_idx ON tmp_hp_rx_codes (brand_key);")
    1434 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_hp_rx_codes_code_idx ON tmp_hp_rx_codes (rx_code);")
    1435 +
    1436 +    await db.status(
    1437 +        f"""
    1438 +        CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_rx_snapshot_codes ON COMMIT DROP AS
    1439 +        SELECT
    1440 +            NULLIF(UPPER(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(p.generic_name, ''), '[^A-Za-z0-9]+', '', 'g')), '') AS generic_key,
    1441 +            NULLIF(UPPER(REGEXP_REPLACE(COALESCE(p.brand_name, ''), '[^A-Za-z0-9]+', '', 'g')), '') AS brand_key,
    1442 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(p.generic_name), '') AS generic_name,
    1443 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(p.brand_name), '') AS brand_name,
    1444 +            NULLIF(_DIGITS_ONLY_RE.sub('', COALESCE(BTRIM(rx.rxnorm_id), '')), '') AS rxnorm_code,
    1445 +            NULLIF(_DIGITS_ONLY_RE.sub('', COALESCE(pkg.ndc11, pkg.package_ndc, p.product_ndc, '')), '') AS ndc_code
    1446 +        FROM {snapshot_schema}.{product_table} p
    1447 +        LEFT JOIN LATERAL unnest(COALESCE(p.rxnorm_ids, ARRAY[]::varchar[])) AS rx(rxnorm_id) ON TRUE
    1448 +        LEFT JOIN {snapshot_schema}.{package_table} pkg
    1449 +            ON pkg.product_ndc = p.product_ndc
    1450 +        WHERE COALESCE(BTRIM(p.generic_name), '') <> '' OR COALESCE(BTRIM(p.brand_name), '') <> '';
    1451 +        """
    1452 +    )
    1453 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_snapshot_codes_generic_idx ON tmp_rx_snapshot_codes (generic_key);")
    1454 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_snapshot_codes_brand_idx ON tmp_rx_snapshot_codes (brand_key);")
    1455 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_snapshot_codes_rxnorm_idx ON tmp_rx_snapshot_codes (rxnorm_code);")
    1456 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_snapshot_codes_ndc_idx ON tmp_rx_snapshot_codes (ndc_code);")
    1457 +
    1458 +    await db.status(
    1459 +        """
    1460 +        CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates ON COMMIT DROP AS
    1461 +        WITH generic_matches AS (
    1462 +            SELECT
    1463 +                hp.rx_code,
    1464 +                hp.generic_name AS hp_generic_name,
    1465 +                hp.brand_name AS hp_brand_name,
    1466 +                hp.rx_name AS hp_rx_name,
    1467 +                snapshot.generic_name AS ext_generic_name,
    1468 +                snapshot.brand_name AS ext_brand_name,
    1469 +                snapshot.rxnorm_code,
    1470 +                snapshot.ndc_code,
    1471 +                CASE
    1472 +                    WHEN hp.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1473 +                         AND snapshot.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1474 +                         AND hp.generic_key = snapshot.generic_key
    1475 +                         AND hp.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1476 +                         AND snapshot.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1477 +                         AND hp.brand_key = snapshot.brand_key
    1478 +                        THEN 'exact'
    1479 +                    ELSE 'normalized'
    1480 +                END AS match_type,
    1481 +                CASE
    1482 +                    WHEN hp.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1483 +                         AND snapshot.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1484 +                         AND hp.generic_key = snapshot.generic_key
    1485 +                         AND hp.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1486 +                         AND snapshot.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1487 +                         AND hp.brand_key = snapshot.brand_key
    1488 +                        THEN 1.0
    1489 +                    ELSE 0.95
    1490 +                END AS confidence
    1491 +            FROM tmp_hp_rx_codes hp
    1492 +            INNER JOIN tmp_rx_snapshot_codes snapshot
    1493 +                ON hp.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1494 +               AND snapshot.generic_key = hp.generic_key
    1495 +        ),
    1496 +        brand_matches AS (
    1497 +            SELECT
    1498 +                hp.rx_code,
    1499 +                hp.generic_name AS hp_generic_name,
    1500 +                hp.brand_name AS hp_brand_name,
    1501 +                hp.rx_name AS hp_rx_name,
    1502 +                snapshot.generic_name AS ext_generic_name,
    1503 +                snapshot.brand_name AS ext_brand_name,
    1504 +                snapshot.rxnorm_code,
    1505 +                snapshot.ndc_code,
    1506 +                CASE
    1507 +                    WHEN hp.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1508 +                         AND snapshot.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1509 +                         AND hp.generic_key = snapshot.generic_key
    1510 +                         AND hp.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1511 +                         AND snapshot.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1512 +                         AND hp.brand_key = snapshot.brand_key
    1513 +                        THEN 'exact'
    1514 +                    ELSE 'normalized'
    1515 +                END AS match_type,
    1516 +                CASE
    1517 +                    WHEN hp.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1518 +                         AND snapshot.generic_key IS NOT NULL
    1519 +                         AND hp.generic_key = snapshot.generic_key
    1520 +                         AND hp.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1521 +                         AND snapshot.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1522 +                         AND hp.brand_key = snapshot.brand_key
    1523 +                        THEN 1.0
    1524 +                    ELSE 0.90
    1525 +                END AS confidence
    1526 +            FROM tmp_hp_rx_codes hp
    1527 +            INNER JOIN tmp_rx_snapshot_codes snapshot
    1528 +                ON hp.brand_key IS NOT NULL
    1529 +               AND snapshot.brand_key = hp.brand_key
    1530 +        ),
    1531 +        combined AS (
    1532 +            SELECT * FROM generic_matches
    1533 +            UNION ALL
    1534 +            SELECT * FROM brand_matches
    1535 +        ),
    1536 +        normalized AS (
    1537 +            SELECT
    1538 +                rx_code,
    1539 +                'RXNORM'::varchar AS to_system,
    1540 +                rxnorm_code AS to_code,
    1541 +                match_type,
    1542 +                confidence,
    1543 +                COALESCE(hp_generic_name, ext_generic_name, hp_brand_name, ext_brand_name, hp_rx_name) AS display_name,
    1544 +                COALESCE(hp_generic_name, ext_generic_name) AS generic_name,
    1545 +                COALESCE(hp_brand_name, ext_brand_name) AS brand_name
    1546 +            FROM combined
    1547 +            WHERE rxnorm_code ~ '^[0-9]+$'
    1548 +            UNION ALL
    1549 +            SELECT
    1550 +                rx_code,
    1551 +                'NDC'::varchar AS to_system,
    1552 +                ndc_code AS to_code,
    1553 +                match_type,
    1554 +                confidence,
    1555 +                COALESCE(hp_generic_name, ext_generic_name, hp_brand_name, ext_brand_name, hp_rx_name) AS display_name,
    1556 +                COALESCE(hp_generic_name, ext_generic_name) AS generic_name,
    1557 +                COALESCE(hp_brand_name, ext_brand_name) AS brand_name
    1558 +            FROM combined
    1559 +            WHERE ndc_code ~ '^[0-9]{11}$'
    1560 +        ),
    1561 +        dedup AS (
    1562 +            SELECT
    1563 +                rx_code,
    1564 +                to_system,
    1565 +                to_code,
    1566 +                CASE WHEN bool_or(match_type = 'exact') THEN 'exact' ELSE 'normalized' END AS match_type,
    1567 +                MAX(confidence) AS confidence,
    1568 +                MAX(display_name) AS display_name,
    1569 +                MAX(generic_name) AS generic_name,
    1570 +                MAX(brand_name) AS brand_name
    1571 +            FROM normalized
    1572 +            GROUP BY rx_code, to_system, to_code
    1573 +        ),
    1574 +        ranked AS (
    1575 +            SELECT
    1576 +                dedup.*,
    1577 +                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
    1578 +                    PARTITION BY dedup.rx_code, dedup.to_system
    1579 +                    ORDER BY
    1580 +                        dedup.confidence DESC,
    1581 +                        CASE WHEN dedup.match_type = 'exact' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END DESC,
    1582 +                        dedup.to_code ASC
    1583 +                ) AS rank_in_system
    1584 +            FROM dedup
    1585 +            WHERE dedup.confidence >= :confidence_min
    1586 +        )
    1587 +        SELECT
    1588 +            rx_code,
    1589 +            to_system,
    1590 +            to_code,
    1591 +            match_type,
    1592 +            confidence,
    1593 +            display_name,
    1594 +            generic_name,
    1595 +            brand_name
    1596 +        FROM ranked
    1597 +        WHERE (to_system = 'NDC' AND rank_in_system <= :max_ndc_per_code)
    1598 +           OR (to_system = 'RXNORM' AND rank_in_system <= :max_rxnorm_per_code);
    1599 +        """,
    1600 +        confidence_min=RX_CROSSWALK_CONFIDENCE_MIN,
    1601 +        max_ndc_per_code=RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_NDC_PER_CODE,
    1602 +        max_rxnorm_per_code=RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_RXNORM_PER_CODE,
    1603 +    )
    1604 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates_rx_code_idx ON tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates (rx_code);")
    1605 +    await db.status("CREATE INDEX tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates_to_idx ON tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates (to_system, to_code);")
    1606 +
    1607 +    mapped_codes = _safe_int(await db.scalar("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT rx_code) FROM tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates;"), 0)
    1608 +    if mapped_codes <= 0:
    1609 +        return {"mapped_codes": 0, "edges": 0}
    1610 +
    1611 +    await db.status(
    1612 +        f"""
    1613 +        INSERT INTO {schema}.{code_catalog_table}
    1614 +            (code_system, code, code_checksum, display_name, short_description, long_description, is_active, source, updated_at)
    1615 +        SELECT
    1616 +            to_system,
    1617 +            to_code,
    1618 +            hashtext(UPPER(to_system) || '|' || UPPER(to_code)),
    1619 +            COALESCE(NULLIF(BTRIM(display_name), ''), to_code),
    1620 +            NULLIF(BTRIM(COALESCE(generic_name, brand_name)), ''),
    1621 +            NULL,
    1622 +            TRUE,
    1623 +            'drug_api_snapshot',
    1624 +            NOW()
    1625 +        FROM tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates
    1626 +        ON CONFLICT (code_system, code) DO UPDATE
    1627 +        SET
    1628 +            code_checksum = excluded.code_checksum,
    1629 +            display_name = excluded.display_name,
    1630 +            short_description = excluded.short_description,
    1631 +            is_active = excluded.is_active,
    1632 +            source = excluded.source,
    1633 +            updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
    1634 +        """
    1635 +    )
    1636 +
    1637 +    edge_rows = await db.status(
    1638 +        f"""
    1639 +        WITH edges AS (
    1640 +            SELECT
    1641 +                '{RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM}'::varchar AS from_system,
    1642 +                rx_code AS from_code,
    1643 +                to_system,
    1644 +                to_code,
    1645 +                match_type,
    1646 +                confidence
    1647 +            FROM tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates
    1648 +            UNION ALL
    1649 +            SELECT
    1650 +                to_system AS from_system,
    1651 +                to_code AS from_code,
    1652 +                '{RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM}'::varchar AS to_system,
    1653 +                rx_code AS to_code,
    1654 +                match_type,
    1655 +                confidence
    1656 +            FROM tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates
    1657 +        )
    1658 +        INSERT INTO {schema}.{code_crosswalk_table}
    1659 +            (
    1660 +                from_system,
    1661 +                from_code,
    1662 +                from_checksum,
    1663 +                to_system,
    1664 +                to_code,
    1665 +                to_checksum,
    1666 +                match_type,
    1667 +                confidence,
    1668 +                source,
    1669 +                updated_at
    1670 +            )
    1671 +        SELECT
    1672 +            edges.from_system,
    1673 +            edges.from_code,
    1674 +            hashtext(UPPER(edges.from_system) || '|' || UPPER(edges.from_code)),
    1675 +            edges.to_system,
    1676 +            edges.to_code,
    1677 +            hashtext(UPPER(edges.to_system) || '|' || UPPER(edges.to_code)),
    1678 +            edges.match_type,
    1679 +            edges.confidence,
    1680 +            'drug_api_snapshot',
    1681 +            NOW()
    1682 +        FROM edges
    1683 +        ON CONFLICT (from_system, from_code, to_system, to_code) DO UPDATE
    1684 +        SET
    1685 +            from_checksum = excluded.from_checksum,
    1686 +            to_checksum = excluded.to_checksum,
    1687 +            match_type = excluded.match_type,
    1688 +            confidence = excluded.confidence,
    1689 +            source = excluded.source,
    1690 +            updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
    1691 +        """
    1692 +    )
    1693 +    return {"mapped_codes": mapped_codes, "edges": _safe_int(edge_rows, 0)}
    1694 +
    1695 +
    1696 +async def _collect_unresolved_hp_rx_codes(
    1697 +    *,
    1698 +    schema: str,
    1699 +    prescription_table: str,
    1700 +    code_crosswalk_table: str,
    1701 +    limit: int,
    1702 +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    1703 +    if limit <= 0:
    1704 +        return []
    1705 +    rows = await db.all(
    1706 +        f"""
    1707 +        SELECT
    1708 +            p.rx_code,
    1709 +            p.rx_name,
    1710 +            p.generic_name,
    1711 +            p.brand_name,
    1712 +            p.total_claims
    1713 +        FROM {schema}.{prescription_table} p
    1714 +        WHERE UPPER(COALESCE(p.rx_code_system, '')) = '{RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM}'
    1715 +          AND NOT EXISTS (
    1716 +              SELECT 1
    1717 +              FROM {schema}.{code_crosswalk_table} cw
    1718 +              WHERE UPPER(COALESCE(cw.from_system, '')) = '{RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM}'
    1719 +                AND cw.from_code = p.rx_code
    1720 +                AND UPPER(COALESCE(cw.to_system, '')) IN ('NDC', 'RXNORM')
    1721 +          )
    1722 +        ORDER BY COALESCE(p.total_claims, 0) DESC, p.rx_code ASC
    1723 +        LIMIT :limit;
    1724 +        """,
    1725 +        limit=limit,
    1726 +    )
    1727 +    return [_row_to_dict(row) for row in rows or []]
    1728 +
    1729 +
    1730 +async def _live_get_json(client: aiohttp.ClientSession, url: str, timeout: aiohttp.ClientTimeout) -> Any | None:
    1731 +    try:
    1732 +        async with client.get(url, timeout=timeout) as response:
    1733 +            if response.status == 404:
    1734 +                return None
    1735 +            if response.status >= 400:
    1736 +                logger.warning("Live RX crosswalk call failed status=%s url=%s", response.status, url)
    1737 +                return None
    1738 +            return await response.json(content_type=None)
    1739 +    except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught
    1740 +        logger.warning("Live RX crosswalk call failed url=%s error=%r", url, exc)
    1741 +        return None
    1742 +
    1743 +
    1744 +async def _resolve_live_external_codes_for_entry(
    1745 +    client: aiohttp.ClientSession,
    1746 +    timeout: aiohttp.ClientTimeout,
    1747 +    entry: dict[str, Any],
    1748 +) -> dict[str, Any]:
    1749 +    rx_code = str(entry.get("rx_code") or "").strip()
    1750 +    if not rx_code:
    1751 +        return {"rx_code": "", "rxnorm_codes": [], "ndc_codes": [], "display_name": None}
    1752 +
    1753 +    terms: list[str] = []
    1754 +    for candidate in (entry.get("generic_name"), entry.get("brand_name"), entry.get("rx_name")):
    1755 +        text = str(candidate or "").strip()
    1756 +        if not text:
    1757 +            continue
    1758 +        if _normalize_rx_name_key(text) and text not in terms:
    1759 +            terms.append(text)
    1760 +
    1761 +    product_ndcs: list[str] = []
    1762 +    for term in terms:
    1763 +        payload = await _live_get_json(client, _drug_api_url(f"/name/{quote(term, safe='')}/products"), timeout)
    1764 +        product_ndcs.extend(_extract_product_ndcs(payload))
    1765 +        if len(product_ndcs) >= RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_CODE:
    1766 +            break
    1767 +    product_ndcs = list(dict.fromkeys(product_ndcs))[:RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_CODE]
    1768 +
    1769 +    rxnorm_codes: set[str] = set()
    1770 +    ndc_codes: set[str] = set()
    1771 +    display_name = str(entry.get("generic_name") or entry.get("brand_name") or entry.get("rx_name") or "").strip() or None
    1772 +
    1773 +    for product_ndc in product_ndcs:
    1774 +        encoded_ndc = quote(str(product_ndc), safe="")
    1775 +        product_payload = await _live_get_json(client, _drug_api_url(f"/ndc/{encoded_ndc}"), timeout)
    1776 +        if isinstance(product_payload, dict):
    1777 +            display_name = (
    1778 +                str(
    1779 +                    product_payload.get("generic_name")
    1780 +                    or product_payload.get("brand_name")
    1781 +                    or display_name
    1782 +                    or ""
    1783 +                ).strip()
    1784 +                or display_name
    1785 +            )
    1786 +            for raw_rxnorm in product_payload.get("rxnorm_ids") or []:
    1787 +                normalized_rxnorm = _normalize_rxnorm_code(raw_rxnorm)
    1788 +                if normalized_rxnorm:
    1789 +                    rxnorm_codes.add(normalized_rxnorm)
    1790 +            normalized_product_ndc = _normalize_ndc11_code(product_payload.get("product_ndc"))
    1791 +            if normalized_product_ndc:
    1792 +                ndc_codes.add(normalized_product_ndc)
    1793 +
    1794 +        packages_payload = await _live_get_json(client, _drug_api_url(f"/ndc/{encoded_ndc}/packages"), timeout)
    1795 +        if isinstance(packages_payload, list):
    1796 +            for package in packages_payload:
    1797 +                if not isinstance(package, dict):
    1798 +                    continue
    1799 +                normalized_ndc = _normalize_ndc11_code(package.get("ndc11")) or _normalize_ndc11_code(package.get("package_ndc"))
    1800 +                if normalized_ndc:
    1801 +                    ndc_codes.add(normalized_ndc)
    1802 +
    1803 +    return {
    1804 +        "rx_code": rx_code,
    1805 +        "display_name": display_name,
    1806 +        "rxnorm_codes": sorted(rxnorm_codes)[:RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_RXNORM_PER_CODE],
    1807 +        "ndc_codes": sorted(ndc_codes)[:RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_NDC_PER_CODE],
    1808 +    }
    1809 +
    1810 +
    1811 +async def _upsert_external_code_and_edges(
    1812 +    *,
    1813 +    schema: str,
    1814 +    code_catalog_table: str,
    1815 +    code_crosswalk_table: str,
    1816 +    hp_code: str,
    1817 +    to_system: str,
    1818 +    to_code: str,
    1819 +    display_name: str | None,
    1820 +    confidence: float,
    1821 +    source: str,
    1822 +) -> int:
    1823 +    if not hp_code or not to_system or not to_code:
    1824 +        return 0
    1825 +
    1826 +    await db.status(
    1827 +        f"""
    1828 +        INSERT INTO {schema}.{code_catalog_table}
    1829 +            (code_system, code, code_checksum, display_name, short_description, long_description, is_active, source, updated_at)
    1830 +        VALUES (
    1831 +            :code_system,
    1832 +            :code,
    1833 +            hashtext(UPPER(:code_system) || '|' || UPPER(:code)),
    1834 +            :display_name,
    1835 +            NULL,
    1836 +            NULL,
    1837 +            TRUE,
    1838 +            :source,
    1839 +            NOW()
    1840 +        )
    1841 +        ON CONFLICT (code_system, code) DO UPDATE
    1842 +        SET
    1843 +            code_checksum = excluded.code_checksum,
    1844 +            display_name = excluded.display_name,
    1845 +            is_active = excluded.is_active,
    1846 +            source = excluded.source,
    1847 +            updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
    1848 +        """,
    1849 +        code_system=to_system,
    1850 +        code=to_code,
    1851 +        display_name=display_name or to_code,
    1852 +        source=source,
    1853 +    )
    1854 +
    1855 +    rowcount = await db.status(
    1856 +        f"""
    1857 +        INSERT INTO {schema}.{code_crosswalk_table}
    1858 +            (
    1859 +                from_system,
    1860 +                from_code,
    1861 +                from_checksum,
    1862 +                to_system,
    1863 +                to_code,
    1864 +                to_checksum,
    1865 +                match_type,
    1866 +                confidence,
    1867 +                source,
    1868 +                updated_at
    1869 +            )
    1870 +        VALUES (
    1871 +            :from_system,
    1872 +            :from_code,
    1873 +            hashtext(UPPER(:from_system) || '|' || UPPER(:from_code)),
    1874 +            :to_system,
    1875 +            :to_code,
    1876 +            hashtext(UPPER(:to_system) || '|' || UPPER(:to_code)),
    1877 +            :match_type,
    1878 +            :confidence,
    1879 +            :source,
    1880 +            NOW()
    1881 +        )
    1882 +        ON CONFLICT (from_system, from_code, to_system, to_code) DO UPDATE
    1883 +        SET
    1884 +            from_checksum = excluded.from_checksum,
    1885 +            to_checksum = excluded.to_checksum,
    1886 +            match_type = excluded.match_type,
    1887 +            confidence = excluded.confidence,
    1888 +            source = excluded.source,
    1889 +            updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
    1890 +        """,
    1891 +        from_system=RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM,
    1892 +        from_code=hp_code,
    1893 +        to_system=to_system,
    1894 +        to_code=to_code,
    1895 +        match_type="normalized",
    1896 +        confidence=confidence,
    1897 +        source=source,
    1898 +    )
    1899 +
    1900 +    reverse_rowcount = await db.status(
    1901 +        f"""
    1902 +        INSERT INTO {schema}.{code_crosswalk_table}
    1903 +            (
    1904 +                from_system,
    1905 +                from_code,
    1906 +                from_checksum,
    1907 +                to_system,
    1908 +                to_code,
    1909 +                to_checksum,
    1910 +                match_type,
    1911 +                confidence,
    1912 +                source,
    1913 +                updated_at
    1914 +            )
    1915 +        VALUES (
    1916 +            :from_system,
    1917 +            :from_code,
    1918 +            hashtext(UPPER(:from_system) || '|' || UPPER(:from_code)),
    1919 +            :to_system,
    1920 +            :to_code,
    1921 +            hashtext(UPPER(:to_system) || '|' || UPPER(:to_code)),
    1922 +            :match_type,
    1923 +            :confidence,
    1924 +            :source,
    1925 +            NOW()
    1926 +        )
    1927 +        ON CONFLICT (from_system, from_code, to_system, to_code) DO UPDATE
    1928 +        SET
    1929 +            from_checksum = excluded.from_checksum,
    1930 +            to_checksum = excluded.to_checksum,
    1931 +            match_type = excluded.match_type,
    1932 +            confidence = excluded.confidence,
    1933 +            source = excluded.source,
    1934 +            updated_at = excluded.updated_at;
    1935 +        """,
    1936 +        from_system=to_system,
    1937 +        from_code=to_code,
    1938 +        to_system=RX_INTERNAL_CODE_SYSTEM,
    1939 +        to_code=hp_code,
    1940 +        match_type="normalized",
    1941 +        confidence=confidence,
    1942 +        source=source,
    1943 +    )
    1944 +    return _safe_int(rowcount, 0) + _safe_int(reverse_rowcount, 0)
    1945 +
    1946 +
    1947 +async def _enrich_rx_crosswalk_from_live(
    1948 +    *,
    1949 +    schema: str,
    1950 +    prescription_table: str,
    1951 +    code_catalog_table: str,
    1952 +    code_crosswalk_table: str,
    1953 +) -> dict[str, int]:
    1954 +    unresolved_rows = await _collect_unresolved_hp_rx_codes(
    1955 +        schema=schema,
    1956 +        prescription_table=prescription_table,
    1957 +        code_crosswalk_table=code_crosswalk_table,
    1958 +        limit=RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_MAX_CODES,
    1959 +    )
    1960 +    attempted = len(unresolved_rows)
    1961 +    if attempted <= 0:
    1962 +        return {"attempted": 0, "mapped_codes": 0, "edges": 0}
    1963 +
    1964 +    client = await get_http_client(use_proxy=DRUG_CLAIMS_USE_PROXY)
    1965 +    timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(
    1966 +        total=RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    1967 +        connect=min(5.0, RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS),
    1968 +        sock_read=RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    1969 +    )
    1970 +
    1971 +    async with client:
    1972 +        healthcheck = await _live_get_json(client, _drug_api_url("/"), timeout)
    1973 +        if not isinstance(healthcheck, dict):
    1974 +            logger.warning(
    1975 +                "Skipping live RX crosswalk enrichment; drug-api unavailable at %s",
    1976 +                _drug_api_url("/"),
    1977 +            )
    1978 +            return {"attempted": attempted, "mapped_codes": 0, "edges": 0}
    1979 +
    1980 +        semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(RX_CROSSWALK_LIVE_CONCURRENCY)
    1981 +
    1982 +        async def _resolve_one(entry: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    1983 +            async with semaphore:
    1984 +                return await _resolve_live_external_codes_for_entry(client, timeout, entry)
    1985 +
    1986 +        resolved = await asyncio.gather(*[_resolve_one(entry) for entry in unresolved_rows], return_exceptions=True)
    1987 +
    1988 +    mapped_codes = 0
    1989 +    total_edges = 0
    1990 +    source = "drug_api_live"
    1991 +    default_confidence = max(RX_CROSSWALK_CONFIDENCE_MIN, 0.88)
    1992 +
    1993 +    for result in resolved:
    1994 +        if isinstance(result, Exception):
    1995 +            logger.warning("Live RX crosswalk resolver task failed: %r", result)
    1996 +            continue
    1997 +        hp_code = str(result.get("rx_code") or "").strip()
    1998 +        rxnorm_codes = result.get("rxnorm_codes") or []
    1999 +        ndc_codes = result.get("ndc_codes") or []
    2000 +        if not hp_code or (not rxnorm_codes and not ndc_codes):
    2001 +            continue
    2002 +
    2003 +        mapped_codes += 1
    2004 +        display_name = str(result.get("display_name") or "").strip() or None
    2005 +
    2006 +        for rxnorm_code in rxnorm_codes:
    2007 +            normalized = _normalize_rxnorm_code(rxnorm_code)
    2008 +            if not normalized:
    2009 +                continue
    2010 +            total_edges += await _upsert_external_code_and_edges(
    2011 +                schema=schema,
    2012 +                code_catalog_table=code_catalog_table,
    2013 +                code_crosswalk_table=code_crosswalk_table,
    2014 +                hp_code=hp_code,
    2015 +                to_system="RXNORM",
    2016 +                to_code=normalized,
    2017 +                display_name=display_name,
    2018 +                confidence=default_confidence,
    2019 +                source=source,
    2020 +            )
    2021 +
    2022 +        for ndc_code in ndc_codes:
    2023 +            normalized = _normalize_ndc11_code(ndc_code)
    2024 +            if not normalized:
    2025 +                continue
    2026 +            total_edges += await _upsert_external_code_and_edges(
    2027 +                schema=schema,
    2028 +                code_catalog_table=code_catalog_table,
    2029 +                code_crosswalk_table=code_crosswalk_table,
    2030 +                hp_code=hp_code,
    2031 +                to_system="NDC",
    2032 +                to_code=normalized,
    2033 +                display_name=display_name,
    2034 +                confidence=default_confidence,
    2035 +                source=source,
    2036 +            )
    2037 +
    2038 +    return {"attempted": attempted, "mapped_codes": mapped_codes, "edges": total_edges}
    2039 +
    2040 +

• Edited healthcare-mrf-api/doc/specs/drug_claims_v1.md (+19 -0)
    40
    41 +## HP_RX_CODE External Crosswalk Enrichment
    42 +
    43 +`drug-claims` finalize stage enriches `code_crosswalk` with external medication codes:
    44 +
    45 +- `HP_RX_CODE <-> RXNORM`
    46 +- `HP_RX_CODE <-> NDC` (normalized NDC11 when available)
    47 +
    48 +Source strategy is env-driven:
    49 +
    50 +- `HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_SOURCE=hybrid|snapshot|live` (default `hybrid`)
    51 +- Snapshot mode reads normalized source rows from drug-api tables (`rx_data.product` + `rx_data.package` by default).
    52 +- Hybrid mode optionally performs bounded live fallback against drug-api for unresolved internal codes.
    53 +
    54 +Confidence and volume controls:
    55 +
    56 +- `HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_CONFIDENCE_MIN` (default `0.85`)
    57 +- `HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_NDC_PER_CODE` (default `5`)
    58 +- `HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_MAX_RXNORM_PER_CODE` (default `5`)
    59 +
    60  ## API Endpoints

• Implemented in healthcare-mrf-api with snapshot + live-fallback crosswalk enrichment for HP_RX_CODE -> NDC/RXNORM.

  ### What I changed

  - Added crosswalk enrichment pipeline into drug-claims finalize flow in process/drug_claims.py:1332.
  - Added new env-driven strategy/config in process/drug_claims.py:146:
      - HLTHPRT_RX_CROSSWALK_SOURCE=hybrid|snapshot|live
      - snapshot source table config (rx_data.product, rx_data.package by default)
      - live fallback controls (base URL, limits, concurrency, timeout, confidence).
  - Implemented snapshot enrichment (primary source) in process/drug_claims.py:1393:
      - normalizes names
      - builds candidate mappings
      - writes code_catalog + bidirectional code_crosswalk edges.
  - Implemented bounded live fallback for unresolved codes in process/drug_claims.py:1947:
      - calls drug-api /api/v1/drug/name/.../products, /ndc/..., /ndc/.../packages
      - inserts external codes + bidirectional edges.
  - Added helper normalization/parsing utilities in process/drug_claims.py:557 and process/drug_claims.py:588.
  - Added/updated docs and config:
      - .env.example:49

      - doc/specs/drug_claims_v1.md:41

• Done. I tested via live calls to http://127.0.0.1:8085 with real pharmacy NPIs across states.

  Discovery endpoint used

  - GET /api/v1/pricing/providers?state={STATE}&year=2023&q=pharmacy&limit=5&order_by=total_services&order=desc

  State samples (real pharmacy NPIs)

  1. CA 1013998921 (Caremark, L.L.C.)

  - Procedures: GET /api/v1/pricing/providers/1013998921/procedures?year=2023&limit=5 → total=2 (e.g. HCPCS J7170, J7192)
  - Prescriptions: GET /api/v1/pricing/providers/1013998921/prescriptions?year=2023&limit=5 → total=0

  2. TX 1275032948 (Trifecta Pharmacy LLC)

  - Procedures: .../procedures?... → total=1 (HCPCS K1034)
  - Prescriptions: .../prescriptions?... → total=0

  3. FL 1619479243 (All Family Pharmacy LLC)

  - Procedures: .../procedures?... → total=1 (HCPCS K1034)
  - Prescriptions: .../prescriptions?... → total=0

  4. NY 1104923036 (Pharmakon Pharmacy Inc)

  - Procedures: .../procedures?... → total=1 (HCPCS K1034)
  - Prescriptions: .../prescriptions?... → total=0

  5. IL 1073078325 (Staywell Pharmacy Corp)

  - Procedures: .../procedures?... → total=3 (includes HCPCS K1034, CPT 91320)
  - Prescriptions: .../prescriptions?... → total=0

  Broader check

  - Sampled 40 pharmacy-like NPIs (national query q=pharmacy):
  - with_procedures_gt0 = 22
  - with_prescriptions_gt0 = 0

  Control check (endpoint health)

  - Non-pharmacy prescriber 1164432506:
      - Prescriptions endpoint returns data (total>0)
      - Procedures endpoint returns data (total>0)
  - So pharmacy prescriptions=0 appears to be data-shape/content, not endpoint failure.

  If you want, I can now run the same validation for a larger sample (e.g. 300 pharmacy NPIs) and export a CSV report with state,npi,procedure_total,prescription_total.

• Understood. I’m checking process/drug_claims.py around the crosswalk snapshot step now; this error pattern usually means the temp table is created on one DB session and indexed on another, so I’ll patch it
  to use one durable staging table lifecycle and retry-safe cleanup.

─ Worked for 3m 51s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I found the root cause and patched it: snapshot enrichment was using CREATE TEMP TABLE across separate DB sessions (db.status), which guarantees “relation does not exist.” I’m adding a focused unit test now
  so this regression is locked down.

• Patch is validated in your project venv (8 passed in tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py). I’ll now summarize the exact fix and what to rerun for your failed finalize job.

• Explored
  └ Read test_process_drug_claims_unit.py, drug_claims.py

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Fixed. Root cause was session-scoped temp tables.

  db.status(...) opens a new DB session each call, so this sequence broke:

  1. CREATE TEMP TABLE tmp_hp_rx_codes ...
  2. next call CREATE INDEX ... ON tmp_hp_rx_codes (new session, table gone)

  ### What I changed

  - Reworked snapshot crosswalk enrichment to use run-scoped UNLOGGED staging tables (not TEMP) and explicit cleanup:
      - process/drug_claims.py:1393
      - Creates:
          - mrf.tmp_hp_rx_codes_<token>
          - mrf.tmp_rx_snapshot_codes_<token>
          - mrf.tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates_<token>
      - Drops them before and in finally after processing.
  - Added regression test to lock behavior:
      - tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py:127
      - Verifies no CREATE TEMP TABLE, and that staging tables are dropped.

  ### Validation

  - ./venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py
  - Result: 8 passed

  ### What to run now

  Restart worker with updated code, then re-run finalize for the failed run:

  ./venv/bin/python main.py finish drug-claims --import-id <import_id> --run-id <run_id>

  (Use the same run_id from the failed job, e.g. 20260217142329_3d1261f0.)

• Yes, production code looks fine. Local was a build/runtime mismatch.

  Root cause: local static_root/dist/manifest.json was stale (it had base.* entries), while templates load main.css/main.js. That makes CSS/JS 404 locally and page looks unstyled.

  git reset --hard does not remove untracked/generated build artifacts, so this can persist.

  Run this from healthporta:

  cd /Users/nick/project/healthporta
  source "$HOME/.nvm/nvm.sh"
  nvm use 23
  rm -rf static_root/dist/*
  cd fe_build
  npm ci
  npm run build
  cd ..
  ./venv313/bin/python main.py server start --host 127.0.0.1 --port 9000 --workers 1

  Quick verify:

  curl -s http://127.0.0.1:9000/ | rg "assets/css/main|assets/js/main"

  You should see hashed main.* asset URLs and no CSS/JS 404s.
  (There are two favicon 404s for android-icon-*, but that does not affect styling.)

• Yes. I implemented it for both doctor and pharmacy pages.

  What changed:

  - root/templates/web/doctor/doctor_details.html:602
  - root/templates/web/pharmacy/pharmacy_details.html:642

  Behavior now:

  - Drug name is clickable and goes to:
      - /drug-list/<drug-name-slug>/
  - Code column is clickable:
      - If code is NDC, it goes to a prefilled exact lookup:
          - /drug-prices/?drug_name=<name>&ndc_num=<ndc>&auto_submit=true
      - If code is RXNORM (or other non-NDC), it links to the same drug-name page:
          - /drug-list/<drug-name-slug>/

  So for rows like your screenshot (NDC 71610051530), users now get a direct code-based price lookup link.

  first and return a normal response (no broken stream), plus normalize letter input.

─ Worked for 2m 19s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Fixed.

  Root causes:

  - /drug-sitemap/<letter> was yielding a redirect() object from inside a stream generator, which can break response streaming.
  - Local config was pointing DRUGS_API_URL to 127.0.0.1:5003 (down), while your active drug-api is on 127.0.0.1:8087.

  Changes made:

  - root/portal/drug/views.py:458
      - Preloads sitemap data before streaming.
      - Returns proper 404 when empty instead of yielding redirect in-stream.
      - Normalizes letter usage for template rendering.
  - root/portal/drug/services.py:66
      - Normalizes sitemap letter to lowercase and validates it (single alpha char).
  - local.cfg:7
      - Switched DRUGS_API_URL to http://127.0.0.1:8087/api/v1.

  Verification:

  - https://local.pharmacy-near-me.com/drug-sitemap/O now returns 200 and renders drug links correctly.

─ Worked for 1m 17s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Fixed in healthcare-mrf-api.

  Cause:

  - Snapshot enrichment assumed rx_data.product.rxnorm_ids always exists.
  - Your environment has rx_data.product without that column, so finalize crashed at snapshot stage.

  What I changed:

  1. Added schema introspection for snapshot columns:

  - process/drug_claims.py:614
  - New helper: _column_exists(schema, table, column)

  2. Made snapshot SQL adaptive:

  - process/drug_claims.py:1434
  - Uses:
      - rxnorm_ids array if present
      - else scalar fallback from rxnorm_id / rxnorm / rxcui
      - else disables snapshot RXNORM mapping safely (does not crash)
  - Also made package NDC source selection column-aware (ndc11, package_ndc, product_ndc).

  3. Added regression test:

  - tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py:167
  - Verifies fallback path uses p.rxnorm_id::varchar and does not use unnest(p.rxnorm_ids).

  4. Updated existing snapshot test to mock column checks:

  - tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py:128

  Validation:

  - Ran ./venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py
  - Result: 10 passed

  Next:

  - Deploy this patch, then re-run finalize for your failed import/run (20260217160038_41be7ace).
  - If you want, I can also give you a one-liner to requeue that exact finalize job.

• Root cause is exactly the path in your cron command: it uses venv/bin/activate, but this image uses venv313 under /opt.

  Use this production-safe cron command now (no source, no cwd assumptions):

  15 5 * * 1 cid="$(docker ps -qf 'name=docker-drugs_api' | head -n1)"; [ -n "$cid" ] && docker exec "$cid" /bin/sh -lc 'cd /opt && /opt/venv313/bin/python /opt/main.py start ndc && /opt/venv313/bin/python /
  opt/main.py worker process.NDC --burst && /opt/venv313/bin/python /opt/main.py start label && /opt/venv313/bin/python /opt/main.py worker process.Labeling --burst' > /dev/null 2>&1

  I also hardened the repo so this doesn’t regress:

  - drug-api/service/run_import.sh
  - drug-api/service/start_api.sh
  - drug-api/README.md

  docker exec "$cid" /usr/local/bin/run_import.sh

› the ndc-rxnorm crosswalk - how do you build NDC /rxnorms - seems IDs are quite long (possibly you connect to packages directly?)


• I’ll inspect the exact crosswalk code paths and answer with the precise mapping logic (including why some codes look long). I’m pulling the relevant functions now.

• Explored
  └ Search normalize_ndc11|normalize_rxnorm|code_crosswalk|rxnorm|ndc|HP_RX_CODE|_enrich_rx_crosswalk in drug_claims.py
    Read drug_claims.py

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, and yes: package data is used.

  Current crosswalk logic in healthcare-mrf-api:

  1. Internal code is generated first (HP_RX_CODE)

  - Built from generic_name + brand_name checksum, not from CMS directly.
  - That’s why you see values like -519044211.
  - Source: process/drug_claims.py:534

  2. Snapshot enrichment (fast path)

  - Reads rx_data.product + rx_data.package.
  - RXNORM comes from product.rxnorm_ids (or fallback scalar rxnorm_id/rxnorm/rxcui).
  - NDC comes from package.ndc11, package.package_ndc, fallback product.product_ndc.
  - Source: process/drug_claims.py:1411, process/drug_claims.py:1467, process/drug_claims.py:1531

  3. Live fallback (for unresolved HP_RX_CODEs)

  - Calls drug-api /name/{term}/products, then /ndc/{product_ndc} and /ndc/{product_ndc}/packages.
  - Collects rxnorm_ids and package NDCs.
  - Source: process/drug_claims.py:1835, process/drug_claims.py:1864, process/drug_claims.py:1885

  Validation/normalization:

  - RXNORM: digits only (variable length).
  - NDC: digits only and must be exactly 11 digits.
  - Source: process/drug_claims.py:547, process/drug_claims.py:557

  So if IDs look “too long/weird”, those are usually HP_RX_CODE internal IDs, not external NDC/RXNORM.

• Root cause confirmed: python main.py worker ... in drug-api hits Python 3.13 + uvloop behavior where no default loop exists for ARQ startup.

  I fixed it in drug-api/main.py by creating a default event loop before cli().

  Changed:

  - drug-api/main.py:24 add _ensure_default_event_loop()
  - drug-api/main.py:86 call it in if __name__ == '__main__':

  Also aligned production venv naming to venv (as requested):

  - drug-api/Dockerfile:7 builds /opt/venv
  - drug-api/service/start_api.sh uses /opt/venv/bin/python
  - drug-api/service/run_import.sh uses /opt/venv/bin/python
  - compatibility symlink kept: /opt/venv313 -> /opt/venv

  Immediate prod workaround (before deploy):

  docker exec "$(docker ps -qf 'name=docker-drugs_api' | head -n1)" /bin/bash -lc '
  cd /opt &&
  source /opt/venv/bin/activate &&
  python main.py start ndc &&
  python -m arq process.NDC --burst &&
  python main.py start label &&
  python -m arq process.Labeling --burst
  '

  └ Read drug_claims.py
    Search rx_code_system|HP_RX_CODE|prescription in drug_claims.py
    Read drug_claims.py

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• We connect to both.

  - Snapshot crosswalk uses product as the anchor and joins package by product_ndc: healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1467 and healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1469.
  - NDC candidate source is COALESCE(pkg.ndc11, pkg.package_ndc, p.product_ndc) when package fields exist: healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1472 and healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1491.
  - Live fallback also reads both:
      - product endpoint /ndc/{product_ndc} for RXNORM + product NDC
      - package endpoint /ndc/{product_ndc}/packages for package NDC11s
        (healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1864 to healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1891).

  How we know the “exact package” today: we generally do not.
  Current claims pipeline maps HP_RX_CODE (name-based aggregate) to likely external codes, not a guaranteed dispensed package. Without package-level claim fields (or dosage/form/quantity details), exact
  package selection is ambiguous.

• Edited ~/project/pharmacy/site/root/portal/drug/views.py (+45 -6)
    488
    489 +def _product_ndc_candidates_from_token(token: str) -> list[str]:
    490 +    text = str(token or '').strip()
    491 +    if not text:
    492 +        return []
    493 +
    494 +    if '-' in text:
    495 +        left, right = text.split('-', 1)
    496 +        if left.isdigit() and right.isdigit():
    497 +            return [f'{left}-{right}']
    498 +        return []
    499 +
    500 +    digits = ''.join(ch for ch in text if ch.isdigit())
    501 +    if not digits:
    502 +        return []
    503 +
    504 +    candidates: list[str] = []
    505 +    if len(digits) == 11:
    506 +        # NDC11 can map to multiple product NDC segmentations; try common candidates.
    507 +        candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[5:9]}')
    508 +        if digits[5] == '0':
    509 +            candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[6:9]}')
    510 +        if digits[0] == '0':
    511 +            candidates.append(f'{digits[1:5]}-{digits[5:9]}')
    512 +    elif len(digits) in {8, 9, 10}:
    513 +        candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[5:]}')
    514 +
    515 +    return list(dict.fromkeys(candidates))
    516 +
    517 +

    180
    181 +def _ndc_lookup_candidates(value: Any) -> list[str]:
    182 +    raw_value = str(value or '').strip()
    183 +    if not raw_value:
    184 +        return []
    185 +
    186 +    if '-' in raw_value:
    187 +        left, right = raw_value.split('-', 1)
    188 +        if left.isdigit() and right.isdigit():
    189 +            return [f'{left}-{right}']
    190 +        return []
    191 +
    192 +    digits = ''.join(ch for ch in raw_value if ch.isdigit())
    193 +    if not digits:
    194 +        return []
    195 +
    196 +    candidates: list[str] = []
    197 +    if len(digits) == 11:
    198 +        candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[5:9]}')
    199 +        if digits[5] == '0':
    200 +            candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[6:9]}')
    201 +        if digits[0] == '0':
    202 +            candidates.append(f'{digits[1:5]}-{digits[5:9]}')
    203 +    elif len(digits) in {8, 9, 10}:
    204 +        candidates.append(f'{digits[:5]}-{digits[5:]}')
    205 +    return list(dict.fromkeys(candidates))
    206 +
    207 +
    208  def _resolve_ndc_for_drug_page(row: dict, code_system: str, code: str) -> str:
        ⋮

    229 +def _collect_ndc_candidates_for_lookup(row: dict, code_system: str, code: str) -> list[str]:
    230 +    values: list[Any] = []
    231 +    if code_system == 'NDC' and code:
    232 +        values.append(code)
    233 +
    234 +    ndc_codes = row.get('ndc_codes')
    235 +    if isinstance(ndc_codes, list):
    236 +        values.extend(ndc_codes)
    237 +
    238 +    values.extend((row.get('ndc_code'), row.get('ndc')))
    239 +
    240 +    candidates: list[str] = []
    241 +    for value in values:
    242 +        candidates.extend(_ndc_lookup_candidates(value))
    243 +    return list(dict.fromkeys(candidates))

    267 +def _extract_product_ndc_from_payload(payload: Any) -> str:
    268 +    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
    269 +        return ''
    270 +    product_ndc = str(payload.get('product_ndc') or '').strip()
    271 +    if not product_ndc:
    272 +        return ''
    273 +    left, _, right = product_ndc.partition('-')
    274 +    if left.isdigit() and right.isdigit():
    275 +        return product_ndc

    278 +
    279 +def _extract_product_ndc_from_rxnorm_products(payload: Any) -> str:
    280 +    if not isinstance(payload, list):
    281 +        return ''
    282 +    for row in payload:
    283 +        if not isinstance(row, dict):
    284 +            continue
    285 +        product_ndc = _extract_product_ndc_from_payload(row)
    286 +        if product_ndc:
    287 +            return product_ndc
    288 +    return ''

    291 +async def _resolve_canonical_drug_product_url(
    292 +    *,
    293 +    item: dict,
    294 +    row: dict,
    295 +    drug_client: DrugClient,
    296 +    semaphore: asyncio.Semaphore,
    297 +    ndc_cache: dict[str, str],
    298 +    rxnorm_cache: dict[str, str],
    299 +) -> Optional[str]:
    300 +    name_slug = slugify(str(item.get('drug_name') or ''), only_ascii=True)
    301 +    if not name_slug:
    302 +        return None
    303 +
    304 +    code_system = str(item.get('code_system') or '').strip().upper()
    305 +    code = str(item.get('drug_code') or '').strip()
    306 +
    307 +    for ndc_candidate in _collect_ndc_candidates_for_lookup(row, code_system, code):
    308 +        cached_ndc = ndc_cache.get(ndc_candidate)
    309 +        if cached_ndc:
    310 +            return f'/drug/{name_slug}-{cached_ndc}/'
    311 +        if ndc_candidate in ndc_cache:
    312 +            continue
    313 +
    314 +        product_ndc = ''
    315 +        try:
    316 +            async with semaphore:
    317 +                payload = await asyncio.wait_for(
    318 +                    drug_client.ndc_detail(ndc_candidate),
    319 +                    timeout=_PRESCRIPTION_CANONICAL_LOOKUP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    320 +                )
    321 +            product_ndc = _extract_product_ndc_from_payload(payload)
    322 +        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive timeout/network path
    323 +            product_ndc = ''
    324 +
    325 +        ndc_cache[ndc_candidate] = product_ndc
    326 +        if product_ndc:
    327 +            return f'/drug/{name_slug}-{product_ndc}/'
    328 +
    329 +    rxnorm_id = _resolve_rxnorm_for_drug_page(row, code_system, code)
    330 +    if rxnorm_id:
    331 +        cached_ndc = rxnorm_cache.get(rxnorm_id)
    332 +        if cached_ndc:
    333 +            return f'/drug/{name_slug}-{cached_ndc}/'
    334 +        if rxnorm_id not in rxnorm_cache:
    335 +            product_ndc = ''
    336 +            try:
    337 +                async with semaphore:
    338 +                    payload = await asyncio.wait_for(
    339 +                        drug_client.products_by_rxnorm(rxnorm_id),
    340 +                        timeout=_PRESCRIPTION_CANONICAL_LOOKUP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    341 +                    )
    342 +                product_ndc = _extract_product_ndc_from_rxnorm_products(payload)
    343 +            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive timeout/network path
    344 +                product_ndc = ''
    345 +            rxnorm_cache[rxnorm_id] = product_ndc
    346 +            cached_ndc = product_ndc
    347 +        if cached_ndc:
    348 +            return f'/drug/{name_slug}-{cached_ndc}/'
    349 +
    350 +    return None
    351 +

• Yes. I implemented canonical drug links for doctor/pharmacy top-prescribed tables.

  ### What it does now

  - For each drug row, it resolves to a real product_ndc via drug API (/drug/ndc/... and RXNORM fallback), then builds:
      - /drug/<drug-slug>-<product_ndc>/
  - If canonical product_ndc can’t be resolved, no link is shown (name stays plain text).
  - Code column remains plain text (no link), per your earlier requirement.

  ### Key changes

  - Canonical resolver in prescription service:
      - root/portal/doctor/services.py:291
      - root/portal/doctor/services.py:578
  - Doctor page now passes DrugClient for canonical lookup:
      - root/portal/doctor/views.py:43
      - root/portal/doctor/views.py:126
  - Pharmacy page now passes DrugClient too:
      - root/portal/pharmacy/views.py:72

› hhmm. whats wrong

  MacBook-Pro-8:~ nick$ ssh-agent
  SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/var/folders/b8/tskwngks71xfzlcc23m98dfw0000gn/T//ssh-EcsXVcCPz8Ns/agent.47769; export SSH_AUTH_SOCK;
  SSH_AGENT_PID=47770; export SSH_AGENT_PID;
  echo Agent pid 47770;
  MacBook-Pro-8:~ nick$ ssh-add
  Error connecting to agent: No such file or directory
  MacBook-Pro-8:~ nick$


• ssh-agent printed the env exports, but your shell didn’t apply them.
  So ssh-add couldn’t find the agent socket.

  Run this in the same shell:

  unset SSH_AUTH_SOCK SSH_AGENT_PID
  eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
  ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519   # or your key path
  ssh-add -l

  On macOS, if you want keychain persistence:

  ssh-add --apple-use-keychain ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

  If ssh-add -l then shows keys, you’re good.


› something made wrong

  MacBook-Pro-8:~ nick$ ssh-add
  Error connecting to agent: No such file or directory
  MacBook-Pro-8:~ nick$ ssh-add -l
  Error connecting to agent: No such file or directory


• Your shell still has stale SSH_AUTH_SOCK / SSH_AGENT_PID.

  Run exactly this in the same terminal:

  unset SSH_AUTH_SOCK SSH_AGENT_PID
  eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
  echo "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK"
  [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && echo "agent socket OK"
  ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519   # use your real key path
  ssh-add -l

  Why it broke: you ran ssh-agent (prints exports) but those vars were not active in that shell before ssh-add.

  If ssh-add still fails, share output of:

  env | grep SSH
  ls -l "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK"

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Checked 5434 directly. DB tables are fine; the failure is from crosswalk candidate duplication plus old runtime SQL.

  What’s wrong

  1. 5434 has expected source schema/data:

  - rx_data.product has rxnorm_ids column.
  - rx_data.package exists and is populated.
  - mrf.pricing_prescription has 3778 HP_RX_CODE rows.

  2. The snapshot candidate set has many duplicate external targets:

  - candidate_rows: 24654
  - duplicate (to_system,to_code) targets: 4795
  - max duplicate size for one target: 67 (example RXNORM 748797)

  With old SQL (INSERT ... SELECT FROM tmp_rx_crosswalk_candidates ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE), Postgres throws:
  ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE command cannot affect row a second time.

  3. Your error SQL text shows old code path (no dedup CTE), so production runtime is not using the fixed query yet.

  Fix status in code

  - Dedup fix is present in healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1702 (WITH dedup_catalog AS (SELECT DISTINCT ON (to_system, to_code) ...) before catalog upsert).
  - Live upsert type-cast fix is present in healthcare-mrf-api/process/drug_claims.py:1935 (explicit CAST(:code_system AS varchar) etc).
  - Unit test passes: tests/test_process_drug_claims_unit.py (10 passed).

  What to do now

  1. Redeploy healthcare-mrf-api with current process/drug_claims.py.
  2. Re-run drug_claims_finalize.
  3. Verify enrichment loaded:

  - SELECT source, count(*) FROM mrf.code_crosswalk WHERE from_system='HP_RX_CODE' GROUP BY source;
    You should see drug_api_snapshot (and/or drug_api_live fallback), not only cms_partd_provider_drug.

• Yes. If you want your drug page to feel closer to DailyMed quality, these are the best upgrades:

  1. Add a sticky section navigator (TOC) with anchors (Description, Indications, Warnings, Interactions, Adverse Reactions, Packaging).
  2. Add View all / Collapse all for long label sections (DailyMed pattern).
  3. Add a clear provenance strip at top: Last updated, Data source, Label version, and links to machine-readable docs (PDF/XML equivalent).
  4. Split audience mode: Patient summary vs Professional detail (short plain-language summary first).
  5. Add safety quick actions block near top: Report adverse event, Recalls, and major contraindication highlights.
  6. Improve package table UX: sortable rows, copyable NDC, and compact badges for dosage/route/type.
  7. Add inline definitions/tooltips for regulatory/clinical terms (e.g., RxCUI, NDA, contraindication).
  8. Keep schema + SEO but reduce cognitive load by turning very long content into collapsible cards.

  DailyMed reference you shared:
  https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=6660b616-a82d-9109-19ce-2bdf9747acea

  If you want, I can implement items 1-4 directly on your template first (highest impact).

    780 +            (function () {
    781 +                function escapeHtml(value) {
    782 +                    return value
    783 +                        .replaceAll('&', '&amp;')
    784 +                        .replaceAll('<', '&lt;')
    785 +                        .replaceAll('>', '&gt;')
    786 +                        .replaceAll('"', '&quot;')
    787 +                        .replaceAll("'", '&#39;');
    788 +                }
    789 +
    790 +                function formatLongDrugLabelText() {
    791 +                    var nodes = document.querySelectorAll('#package-data-container .drug-label-text');
    792 +                    nodes.forEach(function (node) {
    793 +                        var raw = (node.textContent || '').trim();
    794 +                        if (!raw || raw.length < 700) {
    795 +                            return;
    796 +                        }
    797 +
    798 +                        var normalized = raw.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    799 +                        normalized = normalized
    800 +                            .replace(/(?=\bTable\s+\d+\s*:)/g, '\n\n')
    801 +                            .replace(/(?=\b\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\s+[A-Z])/g, '\n\n')
    802 +                            .replace(/(?=\b\d{1,2}\s+[A-Z][A-Z][A-Z])/g, '\n\n');
    803 +
    804 +                        var chunks = normalized
    805 +                            .split(/\n{2,}/)
    806 +                            .map(function (chunk) {
    807 +                                return chunk.trim();
    808 +                            })
    809 +                            .filter(Boolean);
    810 +
    811 +                        if (chunks.length < 2) {
    812 +                            return;
    813 +                        }
    814 +
    815 +                        node.innerHTML = chunks.map(function (chunk) {
    816 +                            return '<p>' + escapeHtml(chunk) + '</p>';
    817 +                        }).join('');
    818 +                    });
    819 +                }
    820 +
    821 +                if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    822 +                    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', formatLongDrugLabelText);
    823 +                } else {
    824 +                    formatLongDrugLabelText();
    825 +                }
    826 +            })();
    827 +        </script>
    828  {% endblock %}

    882 +                        var minExpandedHeight = 330;
    883 +                        if (textLength < minTextLength || node.scrollHeight <= minExpandedHeight) {
    884 +                            return;
    885 +                        }
    886 +
    887 +                        node.classList.add('is-collapsed');

    895 +                        toggle.addEventListener('click', function () {
    896 +                            var expanded = toggle.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';

    898 +                                node.classList.add('is-collapsed');
    899 +                                toggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

    902 +                                node.classList.remove('is-collapsed');

    905 +                            }

• Yes, now this case is fixed more robustly.

  I updated the formatter so table dumps inside long text blocks are handled correctly:

  1. If a section already has a structured table (*_table), text from Table N:/Table N. onward is removed from the text block.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, this was a real mismatch.

  Root cause:

  - api-mcp sent ?limit=5 for getMedicationProductsByName.
  - api-layer medication routes rejected all query params, so limit triggered 400 invalid_request.

  Implemented fix in api-layer:

  1. Added validated optional limit (1..50) for medication search/list routes.
  2. Applied limit to returned payloads:

  - truncates top-level arrays
  - truncates generic/brand arrays for name-based responses

  3. Kept label routes strict (still reject unknown query params).
  4. Updated OpenAPI so these endpoints officially accept limit.

  Changed files:

  - api-layer/src/routes/medications.rs:111
  - api-layer/src/routes/medications.rs:265
  - api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:258

  Validation:

  - Ran: cargo test --manifest-path /Users/nick/project/endurantdevs/api-layer/Cargo.toml medications
  - Result: 7/7 tests passed.

  After deploy/restart of api-layer, the MCP call with query.limit should stop failing.

• I found the bug: listProviderServiceClaimProviders doesn’t currently allow code/code_system, so MCP gets 400 invalid_request. I’m patching this route to accept those params (plus procedure aliases) and auto-
  switch to service-search upstream when code filters are present.

─ Worked for 1m 11s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Confirmed. This was a real api-layer validator gap on listProviderServiceClaimProviders.

  ### What was wrong

  /api/v1/provider-services-claims/providers rejected code / code_system (and procedure_* aliases), so MCP calls like your screenshot returned 400 invalid_request.

  ### Fix implemented

  - Added support for:
  - code, code_system, zip5, expand_codes
  - aliases: procedure_code -> code, procedure_code_system -> code_system, procedure -> q
  - Added compatibility routing:
  - If service-specific filters are present (code, code_system, etc.), this endpoint now proxies to /api/v1/pricing/providers/by-service
  - Otherwise it keeps existing behavior (/api/v1/pricing/providers)
  - Added guardrail:
  - service-filter mode still requires at least one of q or code

  ### Files changed

  - api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:357
  - api-layer/src/routes/claims_ratings.rs:579
  - api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml:1257
  - api-layer/tests_e2e/test_claims_ratings_and_npi_contracts.py:32

  ### Validation

  - Ran: cargo test --manifest-path api-layer/Cargo.toml claims_ratings -- --nocapture
  - Result: 13 passed

  After deploying/restarting api-layer, your exact call should stop failing with 400 and return data (or 404 if no matching rows).

  If you also want the new prescription autocomplete enrichment (ndc_code / rxnorm_id), redeploy healthcare-mrf-api too.

• Proposed Plan


  ## Add /procedure-search Page in healthporta (Live, mrf-api-backed)

  ### Summary

  Build a new public page at /procedure-search in /Users/nick/project/healthporta that matches your mock layout and is fully wired to mrf-api (:8085) for live procedure/provider search.
  The page will support sharable URL filters, insurance-aware narrowing via plan-network + NPI intersection, server pagination, and MapTiler map rendering (with list-only fallback when key is missing).

  ### Public Interfaces / Config Changes

  1. New page route
      - GET /procedure-search
      - Added under app/endpoint/root.py blueprint (url_prefix='/').
  2. URL query contract (deep-linkable)
      - state, city, zip5
      - q (free-text procedure)
      - code, code_system (CPT/HCPCS)
      - insurance (issuer query text)
      - provider_name
      - year (default 2023)
      - sort (default cost_low)
      - limit (default 25)
      - page (default 1)
  3. New env config
      - HLTHPRT_MAPTILER_KEY in .env.example.
      - Behavior when missing: page still works (provider list), map panel shows “Map unavailable” fallback.

  ### Implementation Plan

  1. Backend route + orchestration
      - File: app/endpoint/root.py
      - Add handler procedure_search(request):
          - Parse/normalize query params.
          - Render empty state when no search intent.
          - Execute upstream fetch flow when filters exist.
      - Keep logic in small helper functions (same file or new app/procedure_search.py) to avoid bloating root endpoint module.
  2. Procedure/provider upstream flow (mrf-api)
      - Primary search endpoint:
          - GET /api/v1/pricing/providers/by-service
          - Use code+code_system when code provided; otherwise pass q.
          - Pass location (zip5 preferred, else state+city), year, limit, page.
      - Normalize response item fields for template:
          - npi, provider_name, provider_type, city, state, zip5
          - avg_submitted_charge, avg_allowed_amount, cost_index, estimated_cost_level
          - computed display values (currency, badges, distance placeholder if none).
  3. Insurance filter via NPI intersection (best-effort)
      - Resolve insurance candidates:
          - GET /api/v1/plan/search?q=<insurance>&state=<state>&year=<year>&limit=<N>
          - collect checksum values from returned plans.
      - For each checksum, query insured providers:
          - GET /api/v1/npi/all?plan_network_checksum=<checksum>&has_insurance=1&state/city/zip&limit=<M>
      - Build union set of allowed NPIs.
      - Filter by-service result items to NPIs in that set.
      - If insurance filter yields no intersection, show explicit “no in-network matches” notice.
  4. Provider enrichment for map/cards
      - For visible page items, fetch details concurrently:
          - GET /api/v1/npi/id/<npi>
      - Extract from address_list (primary):
          - formatted_address, telephone_number, lat, long.
      - Merge into result cards.
      - Map markers created only for providers with coordinates.
  5. Sorting / pricing semantics
      - Default sort: full price low-to-high using:
          - primary: avg_submitted_charge
          - fallback: avg_allowed_amount
      - Additional sorts (implemented but optional UI exposure): cost_high, name.
      - Keep Medicare-oriented fields visible but secondary; full price is primary ranking/display value.
  6. Template/UI build
      - New template: templates/index/procedure_search.html
      - Extend main_web.html.
      - Layout mirrors mock:
          - Top filter bar: location, insurance, procedure, provider-name search.
          - Left: provider count, price range bar, provider cards.
          - Right: interactive map.
      - Include inline page-scoped CSS for this page (consistent with repo pattern).
      - Include pagination controls and empty/error states.
  7. MapTiler integration
      - Client-side map init only when HLTHPRT_MAPTILER_KEY exists.
      - Use MapTiler SDK/style from env key.
      - Marker popups include: provider name, address, price snippet.
      - When key missing: keep right panel with non-breaking fallback message.
  8. No navigation entry (per decision)
      - Do not add header/footer link yet; route is URL-accessible only.
  9. SEO/meta for the page
      - Add title/description/canonical blocks in template:
          - “Procedure Cost Search | HealthPorta”
      - Canonical preserves filtered URL shape where appropriate.

  ### Test Plan

  1. Route rendering
      - GET /procedure-search returns 200 with empty-state UI.
  2. Query parsing + defaults
      - Missing params -> defaults (year=2023, sort=cost_low, page=1, limit=25).
  3. Procedure search mode
      - code+code_system path calls by-service correctly.
      - free-text q path calls by-service with q.
  4. Insurance intersection
      - Mock plan search + npi/all + by-service:
          - verifies NPI filtering works.
          - verifies explicit no-match notice when intersection empty.
  5. Sorting correctness
      - cost_low sorts by submitted charge then allowed amount fallback.
  6. Map enrichment
      - With HLTHPRT_MAPTILER_KEY: map bootstrap data present.
      - Without key: list renders and fallback map message shown.
  7. Pagination
      - URL page/limit propagates to upstream and pager UI.

  ### Assumptions / Defaults Locked

  - Live data source is mrf-api on configured HLTHPRT_HEALTHCARE_MRF_API (:8085 locally).
  - Default claims year is 2023.
  - Map provider pins require coordinates; no geocoding fallback in v1.
  - Insurance behavior is “best-effort” via plan checksum + NPI intersection.
  - Launch is URL-only (no nav link yet).

    227 +def _pick_preferred_code(codes: list[Mapping[str, Any]], preferred_system: str | None = None) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:
    228 +    ordered_codes: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    229 +    for code_obj in codes:
    230 +        if not isinstance(code_obj, Mapping):
    231 +            continue
    232 +        code_system = _clean_text(code_obj.get("code_system"), max_len=24).upper()
    233 +        code = _normalize_code(code_obj.get("code"))
    234 +        if not code_system or not code:
    235 +            continue
    236 +        ordered_codes.append((code_system, code))
    237 +
    238 +    if not ordered_codes:
    239 +        return None, None
    240 +

    242 +    if preferred_system:

    244 +    preferred_order.extend(["CPT", "HCPCS"])

    248 +            if code_system == system:
    249 +                return code, code_system
