    259 +    first_name = _clean_text(row.get("provider_first_name"), max_len=80)
    260 +    last_name = _clean_text(row.get("provider_last_name"), max_len=80)
    261 +    combined = " ".join(part for part in [first_name, last_name] if part)
    262 +    return combined or "Unknown provider"
    263 +
    264 +
    265 +async def _autocomplete_procedures(
    266 +    *,
    267 +    query: str,
    268 +    year: int,
    269 +    limit: int,
    270 +    code_system: str,
    271 +    prefer_code_values: bool,
    272 +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    273 +    if len(query) < 2:
    274 +        return []
    275 +
    276 +    payload = await make_api_request(
    277 +        "/pricing/procedures/autocomplete",
    278 +        api_type="plan",
    279 +        params={
    280 +            "q": query,
    281 +            "year": year,
    282 +            "limit": limit,
    283 +            "code_system": code_system,
    284 +            "dedupe_terms": 1,
    285 +        },
    286 +    )
    287 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    288 +        return []
    289 +
    290 +    result_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    291 +    seen: set[tuple[str, str, str]] = set()
    292 +
    293 +    for item in payload.get("items") or []:
    294 +        if not isinstance(item, Mapping):
    295 +            continue
    296 +        term = _clean_text(item.get("term"), max_len=180)
    297 +        if not term:
    298 +            continue
    299 +
    300 +        codes = item.get("codes")
    301 +        codes_list = codes if isinstance(codes, list) else []
    302 +        code_value, code_system_value = _pick_preferred_code(codes_list, preferred_system=code_system)
    303 +
    304 +        if prefer_code_values:
    305 +            if not code_value or not code_system_value:
    306 +                continue
    307 +            if code_system_value not in CODE_SYSTEMS:
    308 +                continue
    309 +            key = (code_value, code_system_value, term.lower())
    310 +            if key in seen:
    311 +                continue
    312 +            seen.add(key)
    313 +            result_items.append(
    314 +                {
    315 +                    "value": code_value,
    316 +                    "label": f"{code_system_value} {code_value} — {term}",
    317 +                    "term": term,
    318 +                    "code": code_value,
    319 +                    "code_system": code_system_value,
    320 +                }
    321 +            )
    322 +        else:
    323 +            key = (term.lower(), code_system_value or "", code_value or "")
    324 +            if key in seen:
    325 +                continue
    326 +            seen.add(key)
    327 +            label = term
    328 +            if code_value and code_system_value:
    329 +                label = f"{term} ({code_system_value} {code_value})"
    330 +            result_items.append(
    331 +                {
    332 +                    "value": term,
    333 +                    "label": label,
    334 +                    "term": term,
    335 +                    "code": code_value,
    336 +                    "code_system": code_system_value,
    337 +                }
    338 +            )
    339 +
    340 +        if len(result_items) >= limit:
    341 +            break
    342 +
    343 +    return result_items
    344 +
    345 +
    346 +async def _autocomplete_provider_names(
    347 +    *,
    348 +    query: str,
    349 +    state: str,
    350 +    city: str,
    351 +    zip5: str,
    352 +    limit: int,
    353 +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    354 +    if len(query) < 2:
    355 +        return []
    356 +
    357 +    params: dict[str, Any] = {
    358 +        "name_like": query,
    359 +        "has_insurance": 1,
    360 +        "limit": limit,
    361 +        "page": 1,
    362 +    }
    363 +    if zip5:
    364 +        params["zip_code"] = zip5
    365 +    else:
    366 +        if state:
    367 +            params["state"] = state
    368 +        if city:
    369 +            params["city"] = city
    370 +
    371 +    payload = await make_api_request("/npi/all", api_type="plan", params=params)
    372 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    373 +        return []
    374 +
    375 +    result_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    376 +    seen: set[str] = set()
    377 +    for row in payload.get("rows") or []:
    378 +        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
    379 +            continue
    380 +        provider_name = _provider_autocomplete_name(row)
    381 +        npi = row.get("npi")
    382 +        key = provider_name.lower()
    383 +        if key in seen:
    384 +            continue
    385 +        seen.add(key)
    386 +        label = provider_name
    387 +        if npi:
    388 +            label = f"{provider_name} (NPI {npi})"
    389 +        result_items.append(
    390 +            {
    391 +                "value": provider_name,
    392 +                "label": label,
    393 +                "npi": npi,
    394 +            }
    395 +        )
    396 +        if len(result_items) >= limit:
    397 +            break
    398 +
    399 +    return result_items
    400 +
    401 +
    402 +async def _autocomplete_insurance(
    403 +    *,
    404 +    query: str,
    405 +    year: int,
    406 +    state: str,
    407 +    zip5: str,
    408 +    limit: int,
    409 +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    410 +    if len(query) < 2:
    411 +        return []
    412 +
    413 +    params: dict[str, Any] = {
    414 +        "q": query,
    415 +        "year": year,
    416 +        "limit": max(limit * 3, 20),
    417 +    }
    418 +    if state:
    419 +        params["state"] = state
    420 +    elif zip5:
    421 +        params["zip_code"] = zip5
    422 +
    423 +    payload = await make_api_request("/plan/search", api_type="plan", params=params)
    424 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    425 +        return []
    426 +
    427 +    result_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    428 +    seen: set[str] = set()
    429 +    for row in payload.get("results") or []:
    430 +        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
    431 +            continue
    432 +        issuer = _clean_text(row.get("issuer_name"), max_len=140)
    433 +        marketing_name = _clean_text(row.get("marketing_name"), max_len=160)
    434 +        plan_id = _clean_text(row.get("plan_id"), max_len=32)
    435 +
    436 +        value = issuer or marketing_name
    437 +        if not value:
    438 +            continue
    439 +        value_key = value.lower()
    440 +        if value_key in seen:
    441 +            continue
    442 +        seen.add(value_key)
    443 +
    444 +        label = value
    445 +        if marketing_name and marketing_name.lower() != value.lower():
    446 +            label = f"{value} — {marketing_name}"
    447 +        elif plan_id:
    448 +            label = f"{value} ({plan_id})"
    449 +
    450 +        result_items.append(
    451 +            {
    452 +                "value": value,
    453 +                "label": label,
    454 +                "plan_id": plan_id or None,
    455 +            }
    456 +        )
    457 +        if len(result_items) >= limit:
    458 +            break
    459 +
    460 +    return result_items
    461 +
    462 +
    463 +async def _autocomplete_city(
    464 +    *,
    465 +    query: str,
    466 +    state: str,
    467 +    limit: int,
    468 +) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    469 +    if len(query) < 2 or not state:
    470 +        return []
    471 +
    472 +    payload = await make_api_request(
    473 +        f"/geo/state/{state}/cities",
    474 +        api_type="plan",
    475 +        params={"limit": 150, "page": 1},
    476 +    )
    477 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    478 +        return []
    479 +
    480 +    q_lower = query.lower()
    481 +    result_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    482 +    seen: set[str] = set()
    483 +
    484 +    for row in payload.get("items") or []:
    485 +        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
    486 +            continue
    487 +        city = _clean_text(row.get("city"), max_len=100)
    488 +        if not city:
    489 +            continue
    490 +        city_lower = city.lower()
    491 +        if q_lower not in city_lower:
    492 +            continue
    493 +        if city_lower in seen:
    494 +            continue
    495 +        seen.add(city_lower)
    496 +        result_items.append(
    497 +            {
    498 +                "value": city,
    499 +                "label": f"{city}, {state}",
    500 +                "state": state,
    501 +            }
    502 +        )
    503 +        if len(result_items) >= limit:
    504 +            break
    505 +
    506 +    return result_items
    507 +
    508 +
    509 +async def build_procedure_autocomplete_payload(args) -> dict[str, Any]:
    510 +    field = _clean_text(args.get("field"), max_len=32).lower()
    511 +    query = _normalize_query_text(args.get("q"), max_len=120)
    512 +    limit = _autocomplete_limit(args.get("limit"))
    513 +    year = _parse_int(args.get("year"), DEFAULT_YEAR, 2018, 2100)
    514 +    code_system = _normalize_code_system(args.get("code_system"))
    515 +    state = _parse_state(args.get("state"))
    516 +    city = _clean_text(args.get("city"), max_len=80)
    517 +    zip5 = _parse_zip5(args.get("zip5"))
    518 +
    519 +    items: list[dict[str, Any]]
    520 +    if field == "procedure":
    521 +        items = await _autocomplete_procedures(
    522 +            query=query,
    523 +            year=year,
    524 +            limit=limit,
    525 +            code_system=code_system,
    526 +            prefer_code_values=False,
    527 +        )
    528 +    elif field == "code":
    529 +        items = await _autocomplete_procedures(
    530 +            query=query,
    531 +            year=year,
    532 +            limit=limit,
    533 +            code_system=code_system,
    534 +            prefer_code_values=True,
    535 +        )
    536 +    elif field == "provider":
    537 +        items = await _autocomplete_provider_names(
    538 +            query=query,
    539 +            state=state,
    540 +            city=city,
    541 +            zip5=zip5,
    542 +            limit=limit,
    543 +        )
    544 +    elif field == "insurance":
    545 +        items = await _autocomplete_insurance(
    546 +            query=query,
    547 +            year=year,
    548 +            state=state,
    549 +            zip5=zip5,
    550 +            limit=limit,
    551 +        )
    552 +    elif field == "city":
    553 +        items = await _autocomplete_city(
    554 +            query=query,
    555 +            state=state,
    556 +            limit=limit,
    557 +        )
    558 +    else:
    559 +        items = []
    560 +
    561 +    return {
    562 +        "field": field,
    563 +        "query": query,
    564 +        "items": items[:limit],
    565 +    }
    566 +
    567 +
    568  def _build_procedure_params(filters: ProcedureSearchFilters) -> dict[str, Any]:

• Edited ~/project/healthporta/templates/index/procedure_search.html (+188 -0)
    365
    366 +<script>
    367 +(function () {
    368 +    const endpoint = '/procedure-search/autocomplete';
    369 +    const inputs = {
    370 +        city: document.getElementById('city'),
    371 +        state: document.getElementById('state'),
    372 +        zip5: document.getElementById('zip5'),
    373 +        insurance: document.getElementById('insurance'),
    374 +        procedure: document.getElementById('q'),
    375 +        code: document.getElementById('code'),
    376 +        codeSystem: document.getElementById('code_system'),
    377 +        provider: document.getElementById('provider_name'),
    378 +        year: document.getElementById('year')
    379 +    };
    380 +
    381 +    const listConfig = [
    382 +        { field: 'city', input: inputs.city, listId: 'city-suggestions' },
    383 +        { field: 'insurance', input: inputs.insurance, listId: 'insurance-suggestions' },
    384 +        { field: 'procedure', input: inputs.procedure, listId: 'procedure-suggestions' },
    385 +        { field: 'code', input: inputs.code, listId: 'code-suggestions' },
    386 +        { field: 'provider', input: inputs.provider, listId: 'provider-suggestions' }
    387 +    ];
    388 +
    389 +    const suggestionStore = {};
    390 +    const requestControllers = {};
    391 +    listConfig.forEach((cfg) => {
    392 +        suggestionStore[cfg.field] = new Map();
    393 +    });
    394 +
    395 +    function clearList(listId) {
    396 +        const list = document.getElementById(listId);
    397 +        if (list) {
    398 +            list.innerHTML = '';
    399 +        }
    400 +    }
    401 +
    402 +    function debounce(fn, delayMs) {
    403 +        let timer = null;
    404 +        return function debounced(...args) {
    405 +            if (timer) {
    406 +                window.clearTimeout(timer);
    407 +            }
    408 +            timer = window.setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delayMs);
    409 +        };
    410 +    }
    411 +
    412 +    function currentParams(field, query) {
    413 +        const params = new URLSearchParams();
    414 +        params.set('field', field);
    415 +        params.set('q', query);
    416 +        params.set('limit', '8');
    417 +        if (inputs.state && inputs.state.value) {
    418 +            params.set('state', inputs.state.value.trim());
    419 +        }
    420 +        if (inputs.city && inputs.city.value) {
    421 +            params.set('city', inputs.city.value.trim());
    422 +        }
    423 +        if (inputs.zip5 && inputs.zip5.value) {
    424 +            params.set('zip5', inputs.zip5.value.trim());
    425 +        }
    426 +        if (inputs.codeSystem && inputs.codeSystem.value) {
    427 +            params.set('code_system', inputs.codeSystem.value.trim());
    428 +        }
    429 +        if (inputs.year && inputs.year.value) {
    430 +            params.set('year', inputs.year.value.trim());
    431 +        }
    432 +        return params;
    433 +    }
    434 +
    435 +    async function fetchSuggestions(field, query) {
    436 +        if (!query || query.length < 2) {
    437 +            return [];
    438 +        }
    439 +        if (requestControllers[field]) {
    440 +            requestControllers[field].abort();
    441 +        }
    442 +        const controller = new AbortController();
    443 +        requestControllers[field] = controller;
    444 +
    445 +        try {
    446 +            const params = currentParams(field, query);
    447 +            const resp = await fetch(`${endpoint}?${params.toString()}`, {
    448 +                method: 'GET',
    449 +                headers: { 'Accept': 'application/json' },
    450 +                signal: controller.signal
    451 +            });
    452 +            if (!resp.ok) {
    453 +                return [];
    454 +            }
    455 +            const payload = await resp.json();
    456 +            return Array.isArray(payload.items) ? payload.items : [];
    457 +        } catch (err) {
    458 +            if (err && err.name === 'AbortError') {
    459 +                return [];
    460 +            }
    461 +            return [];
    462 +        }
    463 +    }
    464 +
    465 +    function renderSuggestions(field, listId, items) {
    466 +        const list = document.getElementById(listId);
    467 +        if (!list) {
    468 +            return;
    469 +        }
    470 +
    471 +        list.innerHTML = '';
    472 +        const store = new Map();
    473 +        items.forEach((item) => {
    474 +            if (!item || typeof item !== 'object') {
    475 +                return;
    476 +            }
    477 +            const value = String(item.value || '').trim();
    478 +            if (!value) {
    479 +                return;
    480 +            }
    481 +            const key = value.toLowerCase();
    482 +            if (store.has(key)) {
    483 +                return;
    484 +            }
    485 +
    486 +            const option = document.createElement('option');
    487 +            option.value = value;
    488 +            if (item.label && String(item.label).trim() !== value) {
    489 +                option.label = String(item.label).trim();
    490 +            }
    491 +            list.appendChild(option);
    492 +            store.set(key, item);
    493 +        });
    494 +        suggestionStore[field] = store;
    495 +    }
    496 +
    497 +    function handleSelection(field, value) {
    498 +        const key = String(value || '').trim().toLowerCase();
    499 +        if (!key) {
    500 +            return;
    501 +        }
    502 +        const item = suggestionStore[field].get(key);
    503 +        if (!item) {
    504 +            return;
    505 +        }
    506 +
    507 +        if (field === 'procedure') {
    508 +            if (inputs.code && item.code && !inputs.code.value.trim()) {
    509 +                inputs.code.value = item.code;
    510 +            }
    511 +            if (inputs.codeSystem && item.code_system) {
    512 +                inputs.codeSystem.value = item.code_system;
    513 +            }
    514 +        } else if (field === 'code') {
    515 +            if (inputs.procedure && item.term && !inputs.procedure.value.trim()) {
    516 +                inputs.procedure.value = item.term;
    517 +            }
    518 +            if (inputs.codeSystem && item.code_system) {
    519 +                inputs.codeSystem.value = item.code_system;
    520 +            }
    521 +        } else if (field === 'city') {
    522 +            if (inputs.state && item.state && !inputs.state.value.trim()) {
    523 +                inputs.state.value = item.state;
    524 +            }
    525 +        }
    526 +    }
    527 +
    528 +    listConfig.forEach((cfg) => {
    529 +        if (!cfg.input) {
    530 +            return;
    531 +        }
    532 +        const run = debounce(async () => {
    533 +            const query = cfg.input.value.trim();
    534 +            if (query.length < 2) {
    535 +                clearList(cfg.listId);
    536 +                suggestionStore[cfg.field] = new Map();
    537 +                return;
    538 +            }
    539 +            const items = await fetchSuggestions(cfg.field, query);
    540 +            renderSuggestions(cfg.field, cfg.listId, items);
    541 +        }, 220);
    542 +
    543 +        cfg.input.addEventListener('input', run);
    544 +        cfg.input.addEventListener('change', () => {
    545 +            handleSelection(cfg.field, cfg.input.value);
    546 +        });
    547 +        cfg.input.addEventListener('blur', () => {
    548 +            handleSelection(cfg.field, cfg.input.value);
    549 +        });
    550 +    });
    551 +})();
    552 +</script>

• Fixed. Marker placement is now robust for /procedure-search.

  What I changed:

  - Coerce NPI values from strings to ints before detail lookup, so provider detail fetch doesn’t get skipped (app/procedure_search.py:97, app/procedure_search.py:880).
  - Read coordinates from multiple possible fields (lat/long, latitude/longitude, lng) and validate ranges before plotting (app/procedure_search.py:106, app/procedure_search.py:919).
  - In map JS, coerce marker coords with Number(...) and validate before setLngLat, preventing silent marker drops when coords are strings (templates/index/procedure_search.html:580, templates/index/
    procedure_search.html:599).
  - Added regression test for string NPI + alternate coordinate fields (tests/test_procedure_search.py:182).

  Also already in place from previous step:

  - Autocomplete API route + wiring (app/endpoint/root.py:223, templates/index/procedure_search.html:368).

    159 +def _cost_level_from_price_band(
    160 +    value: float | None,
    161 +    minimum: float | None,
    162 +    maximum: float | None,
    163 +) -> str | None:
    164 +    if value is None or minimum is None or maximum is None:
    165 +        return None
    166 +    if maximum <= minimum:
    167 +        return "$$$"
    168 +    ratio = (value - minimum) / (maximum - minimum)

    184 +    if score < 0:
    185 +        return None

    109
    110 +def _parse_state_token(value: Any) -> str:
    111 +    token = _clean_text(value, max_len=64)
    112 +    if not token:
    113 +        return ""
    114 +
    115 +    short = _parse_state(token)
    116 +    if short and short in SHORT_STATES:
    117 +        return short
    118 +
    119 +    normalized = re.sub(r"[^a-z\s-]+", " ", token.lower())
    120 +    normalized = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", normalized).strip()
    121 +    if not normalized:
    122 +        return ""
    123 +
    124 +    return (
    125 +        LONG_STATES.get(normalized)
    126 +        or LONG_STATES.get(normalized.replace(" ", "-"))
    127 +        or ""
    128 +    )
    129 +
    130 +
    131 +def _compose_location_text(city: str, state: str, zip5: str) -> str:
    132 +    if zip5 and not city and not state:
    133 +        return zip5
    134 +    if city and state and zip5:
    135 +        return f"{city}, {state} {zip5}"
    136 +    if city and state:
    137 +        return f"{city}, {state}"
    138 +    if city:
    139 +        return city
    140 +    if state and zip5:
    141 +        return f"{state} {zip5}"
    142 +    if state:
    143 +        return state
    144 +    return zip5
    145 +
    146 +
    147 +def _parse_location_input(
    148 +    raw_location: Any,
    149 +    *,
    150 +    fallback_state: str = "",
    151 +    fallback_city: str = "",
    152 +    fallback_zip5: str = "",
    153 +) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
    154 +    location = _clean_text(raw_location, max_len=120)
    155 +    state = _parse_state(fallback_state)
    156 +    city = _clean_text(fallback_city, max_len=80)
    157 +    zip5 = _parse_zip5(fallback_zip5)
    158 +
    159 +    if not location:
    160 +        return state, city, zip5
    161 +
    162 +    zip_match = ZIP_IN_TEXT_PATTERN.search(location)
    163 +    if zip_match:
    164 +        zip5 = zip_match.group(1)
    165 +
    166 +    parts = [part.strip() for part in location.split(",") if part.strip()]
    167 +
    168 +    if len(parts) >= 2:
    169 +        maybe_state = _parse_state_token(parts[-1])
    170 +        if maybe_state:
    171 +            state = maybe_state
    172 +        elif len(parts) >= 3:
    173 +            maybe_state = _parse_state_token(parts[-2])
    174 +            if maybe_state:
    175 +                state = maybe_state
    176 +        first_part = parts[0]
    177 +        if first_part and not _parse_state_token(first_part) and not first_part.isdigit():
    178 +            city = _clean_text(first_part, max_len=80)
    179 +    else:
    180 +        maybe_state = _parse_state_token(location)
    181 +        if maybe_state and not zip_match:
    182 +            state = maybe_state
    183 +            city = ""
    184 +            zip5 = ""
    185 +        else:
    186 +            cleaned = location.strip()
    187 +            if cleaned and not cleaned.isdigit():
    188 +                tokens = [token for token in cleaned.replace(",", " ").split() if token]
    189 +                trailing_state = _parse_state_token(tokens[-1]) if tokens else ""
    190 +                if trailing_state and len(tokens) > 1:
    191 +                    state = trailing_state
    192 +                    city = _clean_text(" ".join(tokens[:-1]), max_len=80)
    193 +                elif not zip_match:
    194 +                    city = _clean_text(cleaned, max_len=80)
    195 +                elif zip_match:
    196 +                    prefix = cleaned[: zip_match.start()].strip(" ,")
    197 +                    if prefix:
    198 +                        tokens = [token for token in prefix.replace(",", " ").split() if token]
    199 +                        trailing_state = _parse_state_token(tokens[-1]) if tokens else ""
    200 +                        if trailing_state and len(tokens) > 1:
    201 +                            state = trailing_state
    202 +                            city = _clean_text(" ".join(tokens[:-1]), max_len=80)
    203 +                        elif not _parse_state_token(prefix):
    204 +                            city = _clean_text(prefix, max_len=80)
    205 +
    206 +    return state, city, zip5
    207 +
    208 +
    209  def _normalize_query_text(value: Any, max_len: int = 120) -> str:

─ Worked for 1m 03s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Implemented. /procedure-search now uses a single location field with autocomplete for city, ZIP, or state.

  What changed:

  - Replaced city + state + zip5 form inputs with one location input.
  - location parsing supports:
      - ZIP (02110)
      - state (NY / New York)
      - city+state (Chicago, IL)
      - mixed forms with ZIP (Boston, MA 02110)
  - Autocomplete now supports field=location and returns state, ZIP, and popular city/state suggestions.
  - Kept backward compatibility: old city/state/zip5 query params still parse.

  Key files:

  - app/procedure_search.py:35
  - app/procedure_search.py:103
  - app/procedure_search.py:248
  - app/procedure_search.py:285
  - app/procedure_search.py:703

  - templates/index/procedure_search.html:498
  - tests/test_procedure_search.py:122
  - tests/test_procedure_search.py:142
  - tests/test_procedure_search.py:302

  Validation:

  - Test suite passed: 16 passed.

  If you still see old fields in browser, do a hard refresh once.

• Implemented.

  When a user picks a procedure from autocomplete, /procedure-search now submits the selected procedure code (with code system) instead of only the display text.

  What changed:

  - Updated autocomplete handling in templates/index/procedure_search.html.

      - selectedCodeSystem
      - selectedValue

      - CPT 70553 / HCPCS ... (based on selected suggestion)

    1005 +            specialty_key = _clean_text(provider_obj.get("specialty_key"), max_len=120)
    1006 +            if specialty_key:
    1007 +                return specialty_key.title()
    1008 +
    1009 +    taxonomy_list = row.get("taxonomy_list")
    1010 +    if isinstance(taxonomy_list, list):
    1011 +        for taxonomy in taxonomy_list:
    1012 +            if not isinstance(taxonomy, Mapping):
    1013 +                continue
    1014 +            code = _clean_text(taxonomy.get("healthcare_provider_taxonomy_code"), max_len=24)
    1015 +            if code:
    1016 +                return f"Taxonomy {code}"
    1017 +
    1018 +    credential = _clean_text(row.get("provider_credential_text"), max_len=32)
    1019 +    if credential:
    1020 +        return credential
    1021 +    return "Provider"
    1022 +
    1023 +
    1024 +async def _resolve_procedure_code_for_near_search(filters: ProcedureSearchFilters) -> tuple[str, str]:
    1025 +    if filters.code:
    1026 +        return filters.code, filters.code_system
    1027 +    if not filters.q:
    1028 +        return "", filters.code_system
    1029 +
    1030 +    payload = await make_api_request(
    1031 +        "/pricing/procedures/autocomplete",
    1032 +        api_type="plan",
    1033 +        params={
    1034 +            "q": filters.q,
    1035 +            "year": filters.year,
    1036 +            "limit": 5,
    1037 +            "code_system": filters.code_system,
    1038 +            "dedupe_terms": 1,
    1039 +        },
    1040 +    )
    1041 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1042 +        return "", filters.code_system
    1043 +
    1044 +    for item in payload.get("items") or []:
    1045 +        if not isinstance(item, Mapping):
    1046 +            continue
    1047 +        codes_obj = item.get("codes")
    1048 +        codes = codes_obj if isinstance(codes_obj, list) else []
    1049 +        code_value, code_system_value = _pick_preferred_code(
    1050 +            codes,
    1051 +            preferred_system=filters.code_system,
    1052 +        )
    1053 +        if code_value and code_system_value:
    1054 +            return code_value, code_system_value
    1055 +
    1056 +    return "", filters.code_system
    1057 +
    1058 +
    1059 +async def _resolve_near_anchor(filters: ProcedureSearchFilters) -> dict[str, Any]:
    1060 +    if filters.zip5:
    1061 +        return {"zip5": filters.zip5}
    1062 +
    1063 +    if filters.city:
    1064 +        params: dict[str, Any] = {"city": filters.city}
    1065 +        if filters.state:
    1066 +            params["state"] = filters.state
    1067 +        payload = await make_api_request("/geo/city", api_type="plan", params=params)
    1068 +        if isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1069 +            items_obj = payload.get("items")
    1070 +            items = items_obj if isinstance(items_obj, list) else []
    1071 +            for item in items:
    1072 +                if not isinstance(item, Mapping):
    1073 +                    continue
    1074 +                lat = _to_float(item.get("lat") or item.get("latitude"))
    1075 +                lng = _to_float(item.get("long") or item.get("longitude"))
    1076 +                if not _valid_coordinate_pair(lat, lng):
    1077 +                    continue
    1078 +                return {
    1079 +                    "lat": lat,
    1080 +                    "long": lng,
    1081 +                    "zip5": _parse_zip5(item.get("zip_code")),
    1082 +                }
    1083 +
    1084 +    if filters.state:
    1085 +        payload = await make_api_request(
    1086 +            f"/geo/state/{filters.state}/cities",
    1087 +            api_type="plan",
    1088 +            params={"limit": 1},
    1089 +        )
    1090 +        if isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1091 +            items_obj = payload.get("items")
    1092 +            items = items_obj if isinstance(items_obj, list) else []
    1093 +            if items and isinstance(items[0], Mapping):
    1094 +                lat = _to_float(items[0].get("avg_lat") or items[0].get("lat"))
    1095 +                lng = _to_float(items[0].get("avg_long") or items[0].get("long"))
    1096 +                if _valid_coordinate_pair(lat, lng):
    1097 +                    return {"lat": lat, "long": lng}
    1098 +
    1099 +    return {}
    1100 +
    1101 +
    1102 +async def _fetch_insurance_plan_networks(
    1103 +    filters: ProcedureSearchFilters,
    1104 +) -> tuple[list[int], list[dict[str, Any]], list[str]]:
    1105 +    if not filters.insurance:
    1106 +        return [], [], []
    1107 +
    1108 +    plan_params: dict[str, Any] = {
    1109 +        "q": filters.insurance,
    1110 +        "year": filters.year,
    1111 +        "limit": INSURANCE_PLAN_LIMIT,
    1112 +    }
    1113 +    if filters.state:
    1114 +        plan_params["state"] = filters.state
    1115 +    elif filters.zip5:
    1116 +        plan_params["zip_code"] = filters.zip5
    1117 +
    1118 +    plans_payload = await make_api_request("/plan/search", api_type="plan", params=plan_params)
    1119 +    if not isinstance(plans_payload, Mapping):
    1120 +        return [], [], ["Insurance lookup is temporarily unavailable."]
    1121 +
    1122 +    checksums: list[int] = []
    1123 +    issuer_candidates: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    1124 +    seen_checksums: set[int] = set()
    1125 +
    1126 +    for plan in plans_payload.get("results") or []:
    1127 +        if not isinstance(plan, Mapping):
    1128 +            continue
    1129 +        checksum = plan.get("checksum")
    1130 +        if not isinstance(checksum, int):
    1131 +            continue
    1132 +        issuer_name = _safe_display(
    1133 +            plan.get("issuer_name") or plan.get("plan_contact") or plan.get("marketing_name"),
    1134 +            default="Issuer",
    1135 +        )
    1136 +        if checksum not in seen_checksums:
    1137 +            seen_checksums.add(checksum)
    1138 +            checksums.append(checksum)
    1139 +        issuer_candidates.append(
    1140 +            {
    1141 +                "issuer": issuer_name,
    1142 +                "plan_id": plan.get("plan_id"),
    1143 +                "checksum": checksum,
    1144 +            }
    1145 +        )
    1146 +
    1147 +    if not checksums:
    1148 +        return [], issuer_candidates[:5], ["No matching plan networks were found for this insurance filter."]
    1149 +
    1150 +    return checksums[:MAX_INSURANCE_CHECKSUMS], issuer_candidates[:5], []
    1151 +
    1152 +
    1153 +async def _fetch_provider_cost_levels_for_code(
    1154 +    near_rows: list[Mapping[str, Any]],
    1155 +    *,
    1156 +    code: str,
    1157 +    code_system: str,
    1158 +    filters: ProcedureSearchFilters,
    1159 +) -> dict[int, dict[str, Any]]:
    1160 +    if not near_rows:
    1161 +        return {}
    1162 +
    1163 +    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(NEARBY_DETAIL_CONCURRENCY)
    1164 +    params_base: dict[str, Any] = {"year": filters.year}
    1165 +    if filters.zip5:
    1166 +        params_base["zip5"] = filters.zip5
    1167 +    else:
    1168 +        if filters.state:
    1169 +            params_base["state"] = filters.state
    1170 +        if filters.city:
    1171 +            params_base["city"] = filters.city
    1172 +
    1173 +    async def _load(row: Mapping[str, Any]):
    1174 +        npi = _to_int(row.get("npi"))
    1175 +        if npi is None or npi <= 0:
    1176 +            return None
    1177 +        async with semaphore:
    1178 +            payload = await make_api_request(
    1179 +                f"/pricing/providers/{npi}/procedures/{code_system}/{code}/estimated-cost-level",
    1180 +                api_type="plan",
    1181 +                params=params_base.copy(),
    1182 +                return_error=True,
    1183 +            )
    1184 +            if isinstance(payload, Mapping) and payload.get("fuckup"):
    1185 +                return npi, None
    1186 +            if isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1187 +                return npi, payload
    1188 +            return npi, None
    1189 +
    1190 +    tasks = [asyncio.create_task(_load(row)) for row in near_rows]
    1191 +    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
    1192 +
    1193 +    profiles: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}
    1194 +    for result in results:
    1195 +        if isinstance(result, Exception) or result is None:
    1196 +            continue
    1197 +        npi, payload = result
    1198 +        if isinstance(npi, int) and isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1199 +            profiles[npi] = dict(payload)
    1200 +    return profiles
    1201 +
    1202 +
    1203 +async def _populate_nearby_results(
    1204 +    context: dict[str, Any],
    1205 +    filters: ProcedureSearchFilters,
    1206 +) -> bool:
    1207 +    code, code_system = await _resolve_procedure_code_for_near_search(filters)
    1208 +    if not code:
    1209 +        return False
    1210 +
    1211 +    anchor = await _resolve_near_anchor(filters)
    1212 +    lat = _to_float(anchor.get("lat"))
    1213 +    lng = _to_float(anchor.get("long"))
    1214 +    has_coordinates = _valid_coordinate_pair(lat, lng)
    1215 +    if not filters.zip5 and not has_coordinates:
    1216 +        return False
    1217 +
    1218 +    insurance_task = None
    1219 +    if filters.insurance:
    1220 +        insurance_task = asyncio.create_task(_fetch_insurance_plan_networks(filters))
    1221 +
    1222 +    near_limit = min(
    1223 +        NEARBY_CANDIDATE_MAX_LIMIT,
    1224 +        max(filters.limit * max(filters.page, 1) * NEARBY_CANDIDATE_MULTIPLIER, filters.limit),
    1225 +    )
    1226 +
    1227 +    near_params: dict[str, Any] = {
    1228 +        "limit": near_limit,
    1229 +        "radius": NEARBY_RADIUS_MILES,
    1230 +        "procedure_codes": code,
    1231 +        "procedure_code_system": code_system,
    1232 +        "year": filters.year,
    1233 +    }
    1234 +    if filters.provider_name:
    1235 +        near_params["q"] = filters.provider_name
    1236 +    if filters.zip5:
    1237 +        near_params["zip_codes"] = filters.zip5
    1238 +    else:
    1239 +        near_params["lat"] = lat
    1240 +        near_params["long"] = lng
    1241 +
    1242 +    if insurance_task is not None:
    1243 +        checksums, issuer_candidates, warnings = await insurance_task
    1244 +        context["issuer_candidates"] = issuer_candidates
    1245 +        context["warnings"].extend(warnings)
    1246 +        if checksums:
    1247 +            near_params["plan_network"] = ",".join(str(checksum) for checksum in checksums)
    1248 +        else:
    1249 +            context["providers"] = []
    1250 +            context["markers"] = []
    1251 +            context["results_total"] = 0
    1252 +            context["result_start"] = 0
    1253 +            context["result_end"] = 0
    1254 +            context["pagination"].update(
    1255 +                {
    1256 +                    "total": 0,
    1257 +                    "has_prev": filters.page > 1,
    1258 +                    "has_next": False,
    1259 +                    "prev_url": _page_url(filters, filters.page - 1) if filters.page > 1 else None,
    1260 +                    "next_url": None,
    1261 +                }
    1262 +            )
    1263 +            return True
    1264 +
    1265 +    near_payload = await make_api_request(
    1266 +        "/npi/near/",
    1267 +        api_type="plan",
    1268 +        params=near_params,
    1269 +        return_error=True,
    1270 +    )
    1271 +    if isinstance(near_payload, Mapping) and near_payload.get("fuckup"):
    1272 +        context["warnings"].append(_decode_error(near_payload))
    1273 +        return False
    1274 +    if not isinstance(near_payload, list):
    1275 +        return False
    1276 +
    1277 +    near_rows = [row for row in near_payload if isinstance(row, Mapping)]
    1278 +    if filters.provider_name:
    1279 +        needle = filters.provider_name.lower()
    1280 +        near_rows = [row for row in near_rows if needle in _provider_name_from_near_row(row).lower()]
    1281 +
    1282 +    profiles = await _fetch_provider_cost_levels_for_code(
    1283 +        near_rows,
    1284 +        code=code,
    1285 +        code_system=code_system,
    1286 +        filters=filters,
    1287 +    )
    1288 +
    1289 +    merged_items: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    1290 +    for row in near_rows:
    1291 +        npi = _to_int(row.get("npi"))
    1292 +        if npi is None or npi <= 0:
    1293 +            continue
    1294 +        profile_payload = profiles.get(npi)
    1295 +        if not isinstance(profile_payload, Mapping):
    1296 +            continue
    1297 +
    1298 +        provider_obj = profile_payload.get("provider")
    1299 +        provider_details = provider_obj if isinstance(provider_obj, Mapping) else {}
    1300 +        avg_submitted_charge = _to_float(provider_details.get("avg_submitted_charge"))
    1301 +        if avg_submitted_charge is None:
    1302 +            total_submitted_charge = _to_float(provider_details.get("total_submitted_charge"))
    1303 +            claim_count = _to_float(provider_details.get("claim_count"))
    1304 +            if total_submitted_charge is not None and claim_count and claim_count > 0:
    1305 +                avg_submitted_charge = total_submitted_charge / claim_count
    1306 +
    1307 +        city_raw = _clean_text(row.get("city_name") or row.get("city"), max_len=80)
    1308 +        state_raw = _parse_state(row.get("state_name")) or _parse_state(row.get("state"))
    1309 +        zip5_raw = _parse_zip5(row.get("postal_code") or row.get("zip_code") or row.get("zip"))
    1310 +        lat_raw = _to_float(row.get("lat") or row.get("latitude"))
    1311 +        lng_raw = _to_float(row.get("long") or row.get("lng") or row.get("longitude"))
    1312 +        if not _valid_coordinate_pair(lat_raw, lng_raw):
    1313 +            lat_raw = None
    1314 +            lng_raw = None
    1315 +
    1316 +        address_text = _clean_text(row.get("formatted_address"), max_len=220)
    1317 +        if not address_text:
    1318 +            address_text = ", ".join(part for part in [city_raw, state_raw, zip5_raw] if part)
    1319 +
    1320 +        merged_items.append(
    1321 +            {
    1322 +                "npi": npi,
    1323 +                "provider_name": _provider_name_from_near_row(row),
    1324 +                "provider_type": _provider_type_from_near_row(row, profile_payload),
    1325 +                "city": city_raw,
    1326 +                "state": state_raw,
    1327 +                "zip5": zip5_raw,
    1328 +                "address": address_text,
    1329 +                "phone": _normalize_phone(row.get("telephone_number")),
    1330 +                "distance_miles": _to_float(row.get("distance")),
    1331 +                "avg_submitted_charge": avg_submitted_charge,
    1332 +                "avg_allowed_amount": None,
    1333 +                "estimated_cost_level": _clean_text(profile_payload.get("estimated_cost_level"), max_len=10),
    1334 +                "quality_proxy_score": None,
    1335 +                "lat": lat_raw,
    1336 +                "lng": lng_raw,
    1337 +            }
    1338 +        )
    1339 +
    1340 +    sorted_items = _apply_sort(merged_items, filters.sort)
    1341 +    total = len(sorted_items)
    1342 +    offset = max((filters.page - 1) * filters.limit, 0)
    1343 +    paged_items = sorted_items[offset: offset + filters.limit]
    1344 +
    1345 +    providers: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    1346 +    markers: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    1347 +    price_values: list[float] = []
    1348 +    for item in paged_items:
    1349 +        full_price = _to_float(item.get("avg_submitted_charge"))
    1350 +        if full_price is not None:
    1351 +            price_values.append(full_price)
    1352 +        lat_value = _to_float(item.get("lat"))
    1353 +        lng_value = _to_float(item.get("lng"))
    1354 +        maps_url = _google_maps_url(lat_value, lng_value, _clean_text(item.get("address"), max_len=220))
    1355 +
    1356 +        provider = {
    1357 +            "npi": item.get("npi"),
    1358 +            "provider_name": _safe_display(item.get("provider_name"), default="Unknown provider"),
    1359 +            "provider_type": _safe_display(item.get("provider_type")),
    1360 +            "city": _safe_display(item.get("city")),
    1361 +            "state": _safe_display(item.get("state")),
    1362 +            "zip5": _safe_display(item.get("zip5")),
    1363 +            "address": _safe_display(item.get("address")),
    1364 +            "phone": item.get("phone"),
    1365 +            "distance_label": _format_distance_miles(item.get("distance_miles")),
    1366 +            "price_full": full_price,
    1367 +            "price_full_label": _format_money(full_price),
    1368 +            "price_allowed": None,
    1369 +            "price_allowed_label": None,
    1370 +            "cost_index": _cost_index_display(item.get("estimated_cost_level"), item.get("estimated_cost_level")),
    1371 +            "quality_score": None,
    1372 +            "rating_label": _quality_rating(item.get("quality_proxy_score")),
    1373 +            "network_label": "In-network" if filters.insurance else None,
    1374 +            "matched_service_codes": 1,
    1375 +            "maps_url": maps_url,
    1376 +            "lat": lat_value,
    1377 +            "lng": lng_value,
    1378 +        }
    1379 +        providers.append(provider)
    1380 +
    1381 +        if _valid_coordinate_pair(lat_value, lng_value):
    1382 +            markers.append(
    1383 +                {
    1384 +                    "npi": provider["npi"],
    1385 +                    "name": provider["provider_name"],
    1386 +                    "lat": lat_value,
    1387 +                    "lng": lng_value,
    1388 +                    "price": provider["price_full_label"] or "N/A",
    1389 +                    "address": provider["address"],
    1390 +                    "maps_url": maps_url,
    1391 +                }
    1392 +            )
    1393 +
    1394 +    context["providers"] = providers
    1395 +    context["markers"] = markers
    1396 +    context["results_total"] = total
    1397 +    context["result_start"] = offset + 1 if providers else 0
    1398 +    context["result_end"] = offset + len(providers) if providers else 0
    1399 +    context["pagination"].update(
    1400 +        {
    1401 +            "total": total,
    1402 +            "has_prev": filters.page > 1,
    1403 +            "has_next": (offset + filters.limit) < total,
    1404 +            "prev_url": _page_url(filters, filters.page - 1) if filters.page > 1 else None,
    1405 +            "next_url": _page_url(filters, filters.page + 1) if (offset + filters.limit) < total else None,
    1406 +        }
    1407 +    )
    1408 +
    1409 +    if price_values:
    1410 +        minimum = min(price_values)
    1411 +        maximum = max(price_values)
    1412 +        context["price_min"] = minimum
    1413 +        context["price_max"] = maximum
    1414 +        context["price_min_label"] = _format_money(minimum)
    1415 +        context["price_max_label"] = _format_money(maximum)
    1416 +        for provider in providers:
    1417 +            if not _is_cost_level(provider.get("cost_index")):
    1418 +                provider["cost_index"] = _cost_level_from_price_band(
    1419 +                    provider.get("price_full"),
    1420 +                    minimum,
    1421 +                    maximum,
    1422 +                )
    1423 +
    1424 +    for provider in providers:
    1425 +        if not _is_cost_level(provider.get("cost_index")):
    1426 +            provider["cost_index"] = "—"
    1427 +
    1428 +    if filters.insurance and providers:
    1429 +        context["filtered_scope_notice"] = "Insurance filtering is applied to nearby provider candidates."
    1430 +
    1431 +    return True

    441 +@pytest.mark.asyncio

─ Worked for 3m 25s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Fixed. I checked the pharmacy-locator approach and applied the same geo-aware insurance behavior to /procedure-search.

  ### What I changed

  - Updated insurance autocomplete to:
      - Resolve location to state (including ZIP -> state via /geo/zip/{zip}),
      - Query local issuers first (/issuer/state/{state}),
      - Fallback to global issuer search (/issuer/) when needed,
      - Supplement with plan matches from /plan/search for richer suggestions.
  - Removed the hard year=2023 filter from insurance lookups (this was the main reason Aet returned nothing).
  - Updated insurance filter resolution used during search to avoid year-locked empty matches and added broader fallback when geo-scoped search returns none.

    1553 +def _extract_network_checksums(plan: Mapping[str, Any]) -> list[int]:
    1554 +    collected: list[int] = []
    1555 +    seen_local: set[int] = set()
    1556 +
    1557 +    def _add(value: Any):
    1558 +        parsed = _to_int(value)
    1559 +        if parsed is None or parsed in seen_local:
    1560 +            return
    1561 +        seen_local.add(parsed)
    1562 +        collected.append(parsed)
    1563 +
    1564 +    raw = plan.get("network_checksum")
    1565 +    if isinstance(raw, Mapping):
    1566 +        for key in raw.keys():
    1567 +            _add(key)
    1568 +    elif isinstance(raw, list):
    1569 +        for item in raw:
    1570 +            if isinstance(item, Mapping):
    1571 +                _add(item.get("checksum"))
    1572 +                _add(item.get("network_checksum"))
    1573 +            else:
    1574 +                _add(item)

    1585 +def _plan_rows_from_payload(payload: Any) -> list[Mapping[str, Any]]:
    1586 +    if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):
    1587 +        return []
    1588 +    rows = payload.get("results") or []
    1589 +    if not isinstance(rows, list):
    1590 +        return []
    1591 +    return [row for row in rows if isinstance(row, Mapping)]

    1594 +def _issuer_rows_from_payload(payload: Any) -> list[Mapping[str, Any]]:

    1599 +        if isinstance(rows, list):
    1600 +            return [row for row in rows if isinstance(row, Mapping)]
    1601 +    return []

    1604 +async def _resolve_insurance_state(filters: ProcedureSearchFilters) -> str:
    1605 +    if filters.state:
    1606 +        return filters.state
    1607 +    if not filters.zip5:
    1608 +        return ""
    1609 +    geo_payload = await make_api_request(f"/geo/zip/{filters.zip5}", api_type="plan")

    1611 +        return _parse_state(geo_payload.get("state"))

    1615 +async def _search_insurance_plans(filters: ProcedureSearchFilters) -> tuple[list[Mapping[str, Any]], bool]:
    1616 +    if not filters.insurance:
    1617 +        return [], True
    1618 +
    1619 +    plan_params: dict[str, Any] = {
    1620 +        "q": filters.insurance,
    1621 +        "limit": INSURANCE_PLAN_LIMIT,
    1622 +    }
    1623 +    if filters.state:
    1624 +        plan_params["state"] = filters.state
    1625 +    elif filters.zip5:
    1626 +        plan_params["zip_code"] = filters.zip5
    1627 +
    1628 +    primary_payload = await make_api_request("/plan/search", api_type="plan", params=plan_params)
    1629 +    if not isinstance(primary_payload, Mapping):
    1630 +        return [], False
    1631 +
    1632 +    plans = _plan_rows_from_payload(primary_payload)
    1633 +    if plans:
    1634 +        return plans, True
    1635 +
    1636 +    if filters.state or filters.zip5:
    1637 +        fallback_payload = await make_api_request(
    1638 +            "/plan/search",
    1639 +            api_type="plan",
    1640 +            params={"q": filters.insurance, "limit": INSURANCE_PLAN_LIMIT},
    1641 +        )
    1642 +        if not isinstance(fallback_payload, Mapping):
    1643 +            return [], False
    1644 +        plans = _plan_rows_from_payload(fallback_payload)
    1645 +        if plans:
    1646 +            return plans, True
    1647 +
    1648 +    resolved_state = await _resolve_insurance_state(filters)
    1649 +    issuer_params = {"q": filters.insurance, "limit": INSURANCE_ISSUER_LIMIT}
    1650 +    issuer_url = f"/issuer/state/{resolved_state}" if resolved_state else "/issuer/"
    1651 +    issuer_payload = await make_api_request(issuer_url, api_type="plan", params=issuer_params)
    1652 +    issuer_rows = _issuer_rows_from_payload(issuer_payload)
    1653 +
    1654 +    if not issuer_rows:
    1655 +        return [], True
    1656 +
    1657 +    query = filters.insurance.lower()
    1658 +    scored_issuers: list[tuple[int, int, int]] = []
    1659 +    for row in issuer_rows:
    1660 +        issuer_id = _to_int(row.get("issuer_id"))
    1661 +        if issuer_id is None or issuer_id <= 0:
    1662 +            continue
    1663 +        issuer_name = _clean_text(row.get("issuer_name") or row.get("issuer_marketing_name"), max_len=160).lower()
    1664 +        score = 0
    1665 +        if issuer_name == query:
    1666 +            score = 3
    1667 +        elif issuer_name.startswith(query):
    1668 +            score = 2
    1669 +        elif query in issuer_name:
    1670 +            score = 1
    1671 +        plan_count = _to_int(row.get("plan_count")) or 0
    1672 +        scored_issuers.append((score, plan_count, issuer_id))
    1673 +
    1674 +    if not scored_issuers:
    1675 +        return [], True
    1676 +
    1677 +    scored_issuers.sort(reverse=True)
    1678 +    issuer_ids: list[int] = []
    1679 +    seen_issuer_ids: set[int] = set()
    1680 +    for _score, _plan_count, issuer_id in scored_issuers:
    1681 +        if issuer_id in seen_issuer_ids:
    1682 +            continue
    1683 +        seen_issuer_ids.add(issuer_id)
    1684 +        issuer_ids.append(issuer_id)
    1685 +        if len(issuer_ids) >= INSURANCE_ISSUER_PLAN_LOOKUP_LIMIT:
    1686 +            break
    1687 +
    1688 +    by_id_params_base: dict[str, Any] = {"limit": INSURANCE_PLAN_LIMIT}
    1689 +    if filters.state:
    1690 +        by_id_params_base["state"] = filters.state
    1691 +    elif filters.zip5:
    1692 +        by_id_params_base["zip_code"] = filters.zip5
    1693 +    elif resolved_state:
    1694 +        by_id_params_base["state"] = resolved_state
    1695 +
    1696 +    combined: list[Mapping[str, Any]] = []
    1697 +    seen_plan_ids: set[str] = set()
    1698 +    for issuer_id in issuer_ids:
    1699 +        params = dict(by_id_params_base)
    1700 +        params["issuer_id"] = issuer_id
    1701 +        issuer_plan_payload = await make_api_request("/plan/search", api_type="plan", params=params)
    1702 +        if not isinstance(issuer_plan_payload, Mapping):
    1703 +            continue
    1704 +        for plan in _plan_rows_from_payload(issuer_plan_payload):
    1705 +            plan_id = _clean_text(plan.get("plan_id"), max_len=64)
    1706 +            dedupe_key = plan_id or f"issuer:{issuer_id}:{len(combined)}"
    1707 +            if dedupe_key in seen_plan_ids:
    1708 +                continue
    1709 +            seen_plan_ids.add(dedupe_key)
    1710 +            combined.append(plan)
    1711 +
    1712 +    return combined, True

• Edited ~/project/healthporta/app/procedure_search.py (+67 -0)
    344
    345 +def _percentile_linear(values: list[float], quantile: float) -> float | None:
    346 +    if not values:
    347 +        return None
    348 +    ordered = sorted(values)
    349 +    if len(ordered) == 1:
    350 +        return ordered[0]
    351 +    q = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(quantile)))
    352 +    position = (len(ordered) - 1) * q
    353 +    lower = int(position)
    354 +    upper = min(lower + 1, len(ordered) - 1)
    355 +    fraction = position - lower
    356 +    if upper == lower:
    357 +        return ordered[lower]
    358 +    return ordered[lower] + (ordered[upper] - ordered[lower]) * fraction
    359 +
    360 +
    361 +def _cost_level_from_percentile_band(
    362 +    value: float | None,
    363 +    p10: float | None,
    364 +    p90: float | None,
    365 +) -> str | None:
    366 +    if value is None or p10 is None or p90 is None:
    367 +        return None
    368 +    if p90 <= p10:
    369 +        return "$$$"
    370 +
    371 +    clamped = min(max(value, p10), p90)
    372 +    ratio = (clamped - p10) / (p90 - p10)
    373 +    bucket = min(4, max(0, int(ratio * 5.0)))
    374 +    return "$" * (bucket + 1)
    375 +
    376 +
    377 +def _apply_local_cost_index_bands(
    378 +    providers: list[dict[str, Any]],
    379 +    markers: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
    380 +) -> None:
    381 +    priced: list[float] = []
    382 +    for provider in providers:
    383 +        price = _to_float(provider.get("price_full"))
    384 +        if price is not None:
    385 +            priced.append(price)
    386 +
    387 +    if not priced:
    388 +        for provider in providers:
    389 +            provider["cost_index"] = "—"
    390 +        if markers:
    391 +            for marker in markers:
    392 +                marker["cost_index"] = "—"
    393 +        return
    394 +
    395 +    p10 = _percentile_linear(priced, 0.10)
    396 +    p90 = _percentile_linear(priced, 0.90)
    397 +
    398 +    by_npi: dict[int, str] = {}
    399 +    for provider in providers:
    400 +        npi = _to_int(provider.get("npi"))
    401 +        level = _cost_level_from_percentile_band(_to_float(provider.get("price_full")), p10, p90) or "—"
    402 +        provider["cost_index"] = level
    403 +        if npi is not None:
    404 +            by_npi[npi] = level
    405 +
    406 +    if markers:
    407 +        for marker in markers:
    408 +            marker_npi = _to_int(marker.get("npi"))
    409 +            marker["cost_index"] = by_npi.get(marker_npi, "—")
    410 +
    411 +
    412  def _quality_rating(value: Any) -> float | None:

  └ Read mcp_server.py, test_mcp_server_helpers.py

─ Worked for 2m 02s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Root cause found: call_api_read_batch was passing full variant plan IDs in path (e.g. 11718TX0140019-06) directly to getPlanById, but upstream expects:

  - path plan_id = base HIOS ID (11718TX0140019)
  - variant passed separately (full variant ID) with year

  So requests became /api/v1/plans/<full-variant>?year=2026 and returned 404.

  Implemented fix in api-mcp:

  - Added plan-id normalization for getPlanById, getPlanPrices, getPlanDrugCoverage in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:4855
  - For getPlanById with year present, auto-adds query.variant from full variant ID in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:4895
  - Added regression tests in:
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:367
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:386
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1055

  Validation:

  - PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py passed (93 passed).

  What to deploy:

  1. Redeploy api-mcp (this fix is in MCP batch request normalization).
  2. No API contract change required for clients.

  Quick manual request shape (already normalized automatically now):

  - path_params.plan_id = "11718TX0140019"
  - query.year = 2026
  - query.variant = "11718TX0140019-06"

  Also note: in my local 8085 data, two of your sample IDs become valid after splitting; one (33602TX0510204-01) appears genuinely missing for 2026 (still 404).

• Same root cause, different path: this batch is calling MCP tool find_providers_by_plan, and inside that flow getPlanById was still being called with full variant plan IDs in the path (...-03), which upstream
  returns as 404.

  I fixed it globally in MCP tool calls:

  - Added plan call normalization helper in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:4882
  - Wired normalization into _authorized_call so all tool-internal OpenAPI calls get normalized in api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1081
  - Added tests in:
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:407
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:421
      - (plus existing batch normalization tests)

  Now this transforms automatically:

  - /api/v1/plans/11718TX0140021-03?year=2026
  - into /api/v1/plans/11718TX0140021?year=2026&variant=11718TX0140021-03

  I also verified on local upstream (8085) that all 3 sample IDs you posted resolve 200 with that normalized format.

  You only need to redeploy api-mcp for this fix.

• I’m implementing a concrete fallback for this exact failure mode: when provider plan rows are empty, MCP will try provider profile/network checksum routes (including npi-style network arrays) to recover plan
  options instead of stopping at no_plan_ids_available.

• Edited api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py (+29 -0)
    4296
    4297 +    checksum_keys = {
    4298 +        "network_checksum",
    4299 +        "network_checksums",
    4300 +        "plans_network_array",
    4301 +        "plan_network_checksum",
    4302 +        "plan_network_checksums",
    4303 +        "checksum_network",
    4304 +    }
    4305 +
    4306 +    def _walk(node: Any) -> None:
    4307 +        if isinstance(node, dict):
    4308 +            for key, value in node.items():
    4309 +                if key in checksum_keys:
    4310 +                    if isinstance(value, dict):
    4311 +                        for nested_key in value.keys():
    4312 +                            _add(nested_key)
    4313 +                    elif isinstance(value, list):
    4314 +                        for item in value:
    4315 +                            _add(item)
    4316 +                    else:
    4317 +                        _add(value)
    4318 +                _walk(value)
    4319 +            return
    4320 +        if isinstance(node, list):
    4321 +            for item in node:
    4322 +                _walk(item)
    4323 +
    4324 +    _walk(data)
    4325 +
    4326      return out

• I confirmed root cause: read_resource is not registered as an MCP read-tool handler, so batch preprocessing reports it as unknown OpenAPI operation. I’m adding a native read_resource MCP tool (URI
  dispatcher) and wiring it into batch handlers, then adding tests for metadata://... reads through call_api_read_batch.

    2159 +    @server.tool(description="Read a registered MCP resource by URI.", annotations=readonly_tool)
    2160 +    async def read_resource(uri: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    2161 +        normalized_uri = str(uri or "").strip()
    2162 +        if not normalized_uri:
    2163 +            return _tool_input_error_payload("uri is required", operation_id="read_resource")
    2164 +
    2165 +        handler = resource_read_handlers.get(normalized_uri)
    2166 +        if handler is None:
    2167 +            return {
    2168 +                "ok": False,
    2169 +                "status": 404,
    2170 +                "operation_id": "read_resource",
    2171 +                "error": f"unknown resource uri: {normalized_uri}",
    2172 +                "available_uris": sorted(resource_read_handlers.keys()),
    2173 +            }
    2174 +
    2175 +        try:
    2176 +            content = await handler()
    2177 +        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
    2178 +            return _tool_runtime_error_payload(
    2179 +                str(exc),
    2180 +                operation_id="read_resource",
    2181 +                uri=normalized_uri,
    2182 +            )
    2183 +
    2184 +        parsed_content: Any = content
    2185 +        if isinstance(content, str):
    2186 +            stripped = content.strip()
    2187 +            if stripped:
    2188 +                try:
    2189 +                    parsed_content = json.loads(stripped)
    2190 +                except json.JSONDecodeError:
    2191 +                    parsed_content = content
    2192 +
    2193 +        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
    2194 +            "ok": True,
    2195 +            "operation_id": "read_resource",
    2196 +            "uri": normalized_uri,
    2197 +        }
    2198 +        if isinstance(parsed_content, (dict, list)):
    2199 +            payload["data"] = parsed_content
    2200 +        else:
    2201 +            payload["text"] = str(parsed_content)
    2202 +        return payload

• Fixed in api-mcp.

  Root cause:

  - call_api_read_batch only recognized OpenAPI operation_ids unless the ID was in MCP read-tool handler map.
  - read_resource was not registered there, so Gemini got unknown operation_id: read_resource.

  Changes made:

  - Added MCP tool read_resource(uri) with URI dispatch for:
      - openapi://healthporta-api-layer
      - instructions://healthporta
      - all metadata://... resources
      - File: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:2160
  - Registered read_resource in batch MCP handlers:
      - File: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:3663
  - Added read_resource to known MCP tool IDs + examples (so fix-it/suggestions are correct):
      - File: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:96
      - File: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:859
  - Added instruction hint:
      - File: api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:979
  - Added tests:
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1471
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1663
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1678
      - api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py:1682

  Validation:

  - PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py passed.
  - python3 -m py_compile healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py passed.

  After redeploy/restart api-mcp, this batch pattern should work:

    24 +{% macro compose_address(address_obj, include_locality=True, fallback='') -%}
    25 +    {%- set fallback_value = (fallback or '')|string|trim -%}
    26 +    {%- if not address_obj -%}
    27 +        {{- fallback_value -}}
    28 +    {%- else -%}
    29 +        {%- set line1 = (address_obj.street_address or address_obj.first_line or '')|string|trim -%}
    30 +        {%- set line2 = (address_obj.street_address2 or address_obj.second_line or '')|string|trim -%}
    31 +        {%- set street = line1 -%}
    32 +        {%- if line2 -%}
    33 +            {%- set street = street ~ (', ' if street else '') ~ line2 -%}
    34 +        {%- endif -%}
    35 +        {%- if not include_locality -%}
    36 +            {{- street or fallback_value -}}
    37 +        {%- else -%}
    38 +            {%- set city = (address_obj.city_name or address_obj.city or '')|string|trim -%}
    39 +            {%- set state = (address_obj.state_name or address_obj.state or '')|string|trim -%}
    40 +            {%- set zip_code = (address_obj.zip_code or address_obj.postal_code or '')|string|trim -%}
    41 +            {%- set locality = city -%}
    42 +            {%- if state -%}
    43 +                {%- set locality = locality ~ (', ' if locality else '') ~ state -%}
    44 +            {%- endif -%}
    45 +            {%- if zip_code -%}
    46 +                {%- set locality = locality ~ (' ' if locality else '') ~ zip_code -%}
    47 +            {%- endif -%}
    48 +            {%- set full_address = street -%}
    49 +            {%- if locality -%}
    50 +                {%- set full_address = full_address ~ (', ' if full_address else '') ~ locality -%}
    51 +            {%- endif -%}
    52 +            {{- full_address or fallback_value -}}
    53 +        {%- endif -%}
    54 +    {%- endif -%}
    55 +{%- endmacro %}

› I asked mcp-client:

   ok, if you get instructions to perform such operations from MCP server(healthporta) - what would you get to improve the efficiency of operation?
  ▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀▀
  ▀▀▀
  ✦ To improve the efficiency of healthcare operations using the HealthPorta MCP server, I would implement the following "Fast-Path" strategies based on the server's specific operating protocols:


    1. Batching & Parallelism (Reduction of Round-Trips)
    Instead of sequential calls, I would use call_api_read_batch or openapi_batch to execute independent discovery and data-retrieval steps in a single turn.
     * Example: Fetching a provider's profile, their plan network evidence, and their recent claims data in one request instead of three.


    2. Situation-First Routing
    The server instructions include a protocol for classifying queries into pre-defined "Patient Scenarios" (via metadata://patient-scenarios).
     * By matching your request (e.g., "Find a PCP") to a known scenario, I can skip "Discovery" phases and jump directly to the most efficient tool chain (e.g., resolve_specialty ->
       find_providers_by_plan).


    3. Smart Geographic Anchoring
    Instead of broad city searches which can be sparse, I would use listGeoCityZipCodes to resolve specific latitude/longitude anchors for "Downtown" or "City Center."
     * I then use getNpiNear with a precise 2-5 mile radius. This is significantly faster and more relevant than a city-wide listProviders call.


    4. Plan-Provider Handshake (Checksum Matching)
    The most efficient way to verify "In-Network" status is the Checksum Handshake:
     * I retrieve the plan_network_checksum from the plan details once.
     * I pass that checksum directly into the searchProvidersByGeo filter.
     * This ensures the API only returns providers who are guaranteed matches for your specific insurance, eliminating the need to verify coverage for each provider individually.


    5. Discovery Reuse
    I utilize the server_status and describe_mcp_tool results to "memoize" the API structure for the session. This prevents me from having to re-read documentation for every sub-task, allowing me to
    generate complex JSON payloads (like CPT/RxNorm filters) more accurately on the first attempt.


    6. Token-Efficient Summarization
    I default to summary=compact for all API calls. This reduces the "chatter" between the server and the LLM, keeping the context window clean and ensuring faster processing of the final
  recommendation.


• Good catch. That output still contains stale strategy names (searchProvidersByGeo, listGeoCityZipCodes) that don’t match the current fast path. I’m going to clean MCP guidance so clients consistently
  route through canonical calls (getNpiNear, geo ZIP resolver, checksum handshake fields).

• I found the right normalization hook. I’m adding a generic “move missing path params from query when names match” rule, which will cover code_system here and similar path-param mistakes in batch
  calls.

• Edited api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py (+21 -0)
    5377
    5378 +    # Compatibility: if a required path parameter is missing but present in query,
    5379 +    # lift it into path_params so batch calls with misplaced fields still execute.
    5380 +    path_param_names = _extract_path_param_names(operation.path)
    5381 +    for path_name in path_param_names:
    5382 +        if str(params.get(path_name) or "").strip():
    5383 +            continue
    5384 +        if path_name not in normalized_query:
    5385 +            continue
    5386 +        raw_value = normalized_query.get(path_name)
    5387 +        if raw_value is None or isinstance(raw_value, bool):
    5388 +            continue
    5389 +        candidate_value = str(raw_value).strip()
    5390 +        if not candidate_value:
    5391 +            continue
    5392 +        params[path_name] = candidate_value
    5393 +        del normalized_query[path_name]
    5394 +        context = str(operation_id or operation.path or "operation")
    5395 +        warnings.append(
    5396 +            f"Moved query.{path_name} to path_params.{path_name} for {context}."
    5397 +        )
    5398 +
    5399      return params, normalized_query, warnings

    5855 +def _normalize_openapi_operation_id_alias(
    5856 +    operation_id: str,
    5857 +    *,
    5858 +    operations: dict[str, Operation],
    5859 +) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    5860 +    normalized = str(operation_id or "").strip()
    5861 +    if not normalized:
    5862 +        return "", None
    5863 +    if normalized in operations:
    5864 +        return normalized, None
    5865 +    if normalized in _KNOWN_MCP_TOOL_IDS:
    5866 +        return normalized, None
    5867 +
    5868 +    alias = _OPENAPI_OPERATION_ID_ALIASES.get(normalized.lower())
    5869 +    if alias and alias in operations:
    5870 +        return alias, f"Normalized legacy operation_id '{normalized}' to '{alias}'."
    5871 +
    5872 +    compact = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", normalized.lower())
    5873 +    if compact:
    5874 +        matches = [
    5875 +            candidate
    5876 +            for candidate in operations.keys()
    5877 +            if re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", str(candidate).lower()) == compact
    5878 +        ]
    5879 +        if len(matches) == 1:
    5880 +            resolved = str(matches[0])
    5881 +            return (
    5882 +                resolved,
    5883 +                f"Normalized operation_id '{normalized}' to canonical '{resolved}'.",
    5884 +            )
    5885 +
    5886 +    return normalized, None
