• Proposed Plan


  # MCP Reliability & Workflow Standardization Plan (MCP-First)

  ## Summary

  Stabilize api-mcp as a strict logic-broker so clients stop issuing malformed/fragmented calls, while keeping upstream APIs unchanged for now.
  This phase focuses on:

  1. deterministic request normalization + guardrails,
  2. composite MCP workflow tools for plan/provider pricing tasks,
  3. explicit non-fallback year behavior (strict-year),
  4. stronger recipes/instructions so Gemini/OpenAI clients follow canonical operation shapes.

  ## Scope (Locked)

  - In scope: api-mcp only (api-mcp/healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py, tests, MCP metadata/instructions).
  - Out of scope for this phase: new api-layer endpoints (/api/v1/...) and DB/schema changes.
  - Behavior policy locked from your choice:
      - year_fallback: strict year only (no automatic prior-year retry).
      - batch mode: auto-correct known-safe mistakes + warnings.

  ## Public Interface Changes (MCP-facing)

  1. Add MCP composite tools (new read tools, no upstream API changes):

  - get_plan_full_detail(plan_id, year?, zip_code?, age?, summary?)
      - Aggregates: getPlanById + getPlanPrices + normalized “essential benefits” projection.
  - calculate_family_premium(plan_id, zip_code, ages[], year, summary?)
      - Resolves available premium rows and returns per-age selected rows + total + math trace.
  - get_plan_quick_summary(plan_id, year, zip_code?, age?, summary?)
      - Returns “Essential 10” normalized fields (PCP/specialist/ER/imaging/drug tier/deductible/MOOP).

  2. Tighten existing tool contracts and canonical routing:

  - find_providers_by_plan remains anchored to /api/v1/npi/near path (no /providers/geo detours).
  - Batch wrappers continue accepting OpenAPI operation_id + MCP tool IDs, but with stricter canonicalization warnings and deterministic argument lifting.

  3. Add machine-readable warnings/diagnostics envelope fields (where absent):

  - warnings[] (normalization actions applied)
  - strict_year_applied: true
  - normalization_trace[] (optional compact trace for debugging client agents)

  ## Implementation Design

  ### A) Batch and Operation Hardening

  1. Expand safe alias normalization allowlist:

  - Provider geo aliases (nucc_classification -> classification, etc.) already present; enforce across all call paths (call_operation, call_api_read_batch, call_api_batch) and add regression tests
    for each.
  - Plan-search aliases (marketing_name_like -> q) preserved with warnings.
  - Required path params auto-lift from query (already present) expanded with explicit per-operation tests.

  2. Enforce deterministic operation resolution:

  - Keep operation-id alias map (snake_case -> canonical OpenAPI IDs).
  - If unknown:
      - if it matches MCP tool ID, route directly (batch MCP handler path),
      - else return 400 with fix_it and closest canonical IDs.

  3. Preserve strict-year policy:

  - No automatic fallback to prior year in plan/provider composite tools.
  - Return explicit no_data_for_year decision + next_actions when empty.

  ### B) New Composite MCP Tools (Logic-Broker Layer)

  1. get_plan_full_detail

  - Inputs: plan_id, year, optional zip_code, optional age.
  - Calls:
      - getPlanById (required),
      - getPlanPrices (if year/zip context supports it),
      - local extractor for key benefit fields from returned plan payload.
  - Output sections:
      - identity (plan core),
      - pricing_rows,
      - benefit_summary (normalized),
      - completeness (what was missing).

  2. calculate_family_premium

  - Inputs: plan_id, zip_code, year, ages[].
  - Calls:
      - getPlanPrices with strict year.
  - Logic:
      - resolve matching rows per age band,
      - return selected rows per member, subtotal list, total, unresolved members.
  - Output includes exact arithmetic trace to remove client-side manual math.

  3. get_plan_quick_summary

  - Inputs: plan_id, year, optional zip_code, optional age.
  - Extract only “Essential 10” and normalized keys:
      - pcp_copay, specialist_copay, er_cost_share, imaging_cost_share,
      - tier1_drug_cost_share,
      - deductible_individual, deductible_family,
      - moop_individual, moop_family,
      - metal_level/plan_type if available.
  - Include source_fields map so provenance is explicit.

  ### C) Instructions, Recipes, and Metadata Upgrades

  1. Update instructions://healthporta:

  - Add strict canonical examples for:
      - getProviderServiceClaimForProvider path params (code_system in path),
      - listIssuers (no year),
      - searchProvidersByGeo accepted taxonomy params,
      - when to use getNpiNear vs listProviders.
  - Explicit “do not invent operation_id names”.

  2. Add/refresh metadata://tool-recipes fast-paths:

  - “Cost-Quality Provider Search” (procedure code -> provider list -> ratings batch).
  - “Plan 360 Review” (single composite tool usage).
  - “Family Premium Calculation” (single composite tool usage).
  - All examples marked placeholder-only for IDs/ZIP.

  3. Improve corrective guidance:

  - For invalid params, return one corrected payload in fix_it.corrected_call with canonical parameter names.

  ### D) Observability & Guardrails

  1. Add per-tool counters/timers in MCP layer:

  - success/failure by operation,
  - normalization-applied count,
  - unknown-operation count,
  - strict-year-empty count.

  2. Add structured error taxonomy:

  - invalid_request, unknown_operation, missing_path_param, strict_year_no_data, upstream_api_error.

  ## Test Plan

  ### Unit tests (api-mcp/tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py)

  1. call_api_read_batch routes MCP read_resource items successfully via batch.
  2. searchProvidersByGeo alias normalization:

  - nucc_classification accepted and rewritten.

  3. getProviderServiceClaimForProvider:

  - lifts code_system from query to path_params.

  4. listIssuers:

  - never injects year.

  5. unknown operation handling:

  - snake_case alias resolves to canonical,
  - truly unknown returns 400 + fix_it.

  6. strict-year behavior:

  - composite tools do not auto-fallback; return explicit empty-year decision.

  ### Integration-style MCP tests

  1. Mixed batch: OpenAPI operations + MCP tool IDs in one request list.
  2. Composite get_plan_full_detail with valid plan/year returns stitched payload.
  3. calculate_family_premium returns deterministic total + per-member trace.
  4. get_plan_quick_summary returns normalized essential keys even with sparse upstream fields.

  ### Regression scenarios (from observed failures)

  1. listIssuers with state=IL succeeds without year injection.
  2. Provider plan search via find_providers_by_plan uses getNpiNear only.
  3. Batch requests with legacy params (marketing_name_like, nucc_classification) execute with warnings, not hard failure.
  4. Missing-path-parameter cases produce executable fix_it payload.

  ## Rollout Plan

  1. Ship behind MCP feature flags:

  - ENABLE_PLAN_COMPOSITES
  - ENABLE_STRICT_NORMALIZATION_TRACE

  2. Deploy api-mcp only.
  3. Validate with scripted smoke set (real operations used by Gemini/OpenAI flows).
  4. Enable flags progressively in production after error-rate comparison.

  ## Assumptions and Defaults

  - No api-layer contract changes in this phase.
  - Strict year behavior is intentional and user-visible.
  - Auto-correction applies only to allowlisted safe mappings; all corrections emit warnings.
  - Composite tools are additive and backward-compatible with existing MCP toolset.


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  run pytest for helper/regression coverage.

    4234 +def _normalize_field_key(value: Any) -> str:
    4235 +    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", str(value or "").lower())
    4236 +
    4237 +
    4238 +def _build_normalized_field_map(source: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, tuple[Any, str]]:
    4239 +    out: dict[str, tuple[Any, str]] = {}
    4240 +    if not isinstance(source, dict):
    4241 +        return out
    4242 +    for key, value in source.items():
    4243 +        if value in (None, "", [], {}):
    4244 +            continue
    4245 +        normalized_key = _normalize_field_key(key)
    4246 +        if not normalized_key or normalized_key in out:
    4247 +            continue
    4248 +        out[normalized_key] = (value, str(key))
    4249 +    return out
    4250 +
    4251 +
    4252 +def _extract_plan_quick_summary(
    4253 +    plan_payload: dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | str | None,
    4254 +    *,
    4255 +    prices_payload: dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | str | None = None,
    4256 +) -> dict[str, Any]:
    4257 +    data = _extract_data_object(plan_payload)
    4258 +    if not data:
    4259 +        return {}
    4260 +
    4261 +    source_dicts: list[dict[str, Any]] = [data]
    4262 +    for key in ("attributes", "plan_benefits", "variant_attributes", "variant_benefits"):
    4263 +        value = data.get(key)
    4264 +        if isinstance(value, dict):
    4265 +            source_dicts.append(value)
    4266 +
    4267 +    source_maps = [_build_normalized_field_map(source) for source in source_dicts]
    4268 +
    4269 +    def _extract_value(*aliases: str) -> tuple[Any, str | None]:
    4270 +        for alias in aliases:
    4271 +            normalized_alias = _normalize_field_key(alias)
    4272 +            if not normalized_alias:
    4273 +                continue
    4274 +            for source_map in source_maps:
    4275 +                value_entry = source_map.get(normalized_alias)
    4276 +                if value_entry is None:
    4277 +                    continue
    4278 +                value, original_key = value_entry
    4279 +                if value in (None, "", [], {}):
    4280 +                    continue
    4281 +                return value, original_key
    4282 +        return None, None
    4283 +
    4284 +    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
    4285 +    source_fields: dict[str, str] = {}
    4286 +
    4287 +    for key in ("plan_id", "marketing_name", "issuer_name", "state", "year", "plan_type", "metal_level"):
    4288 +        value = data.get(key)
    4289 +        if value not in (None, "", [], {}):
    4290 +            out[key] = value
    4291 +
    4292 +    for field, aliases in _PLAN_QUICK_SUMMARY_FIELD_ALIASES.items():
    4293 +        value, source_key = _extract_value(*aliases)
    4294 +        if value in (None, "", [], {}):
    4295 +            continue
    4296 +        out[field] = value
    4297 +        if source_key:
    4298 +            source_fields[field] = source_key
    4299 +
    4300 +    if prices_payload is not None:

    4303 +            value = price_summary.get(key)
    4304 +            if value not in (None, "", [], {}):
    4305 +                out[key] = value
    4306 +
    4307 +    if source_fields:
    4308 +        out["source_fields"] = source_fields
    4309 +    return out
    4310 +

    4312 +def _extract_plan_price_rows(payload: dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | str | None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    4313 +    rows = _extract_items_from_payload(payload)
    4314 +    return [row for row in rows if isinstance(row, dict)]
    4315 +
    4316 +
    4317 +def _extract_plan_price_row_rate(row: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[float | None, str | None]:
    4318 +    if not isinstance(row, dict):
    4319 +        return None, None
    4320 +    for key in ("individual_rate", "rate", "premium", "monthly_premium"):
    4321 +        value = _coerce_float(row.get(key))
    4322 +        if value is not None:
    4323 +            return value, key
    4324 +    return None, None
    4325 +
    4326 +
    4327 +def _select_plan_price_row_for_age(rows: list[dict[str, Any]], age: int) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
    4328 +    candidates: list[tuple[float, int, str, dict[str, Any], int | None, int | None, str | None]] = []
    4329 +    for row in rows:
    4330 +        rate, rate_key = _extract_plan_price_row_rate(row)
    4331 +        if rate is None:
    4332 +            continue
    4333 +        min_age = _coerce_int(row.get("min_age"))
    4334 +        max_age = _coerce_int(row.get("max_age"))
    4335 +        if min_age is not None and age < min_age:
    4336 +            continue
    4337 +        if max_age is not None and age > max_age:
    4338 +            continue
    4339 +        age_span = 10_000
    4340 +        if min_age is not None and max_age is not None and max_age >= min_age:
    4341 +            age_span = max_age - min_age
    4342 +        checksum = str(row.get("checksum") or "")
    4343 +        candidates.append((rate, age_span, checksum, row, min_age, max_age, rate_key))
    4344 +
    4345 +    if not candidates:
    4346 +        return None
    4347 +
    4348 +    rate, _, _, row, min_age, max_age, rate_key = min(
    4349 +        candidates,
    4350 +        key=lambda item: (item[0], item[1], item[2]),
    4351 +    )
    4352 +    return {
    4353 +        "age": age,
    4354 +        "monthly_premium": round(rate, 2),
    4355 +        "rate_source_field": rate_key,
    4356 +        "min_age": min_age,
    4357 +        "max_age": max_age,
    4358 +        "rating_area_id": row.get("rating_area_id"),
    4359 +        "state": row.get("state"),
    4360 +        "rate_effective_date": row.get("rate_effective_date"),
    4361 +        "rate_expiration_date": row.get("rate_expiration_date"),
    4362 +        "checksum": row.get("checksum"),
    4363 +    }
    4364 +
    4365 +
    4366 +def _calculate_family_premium_from_rows(rows: list[dict[str, Any]], ages: list[int]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    4367 +    members: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    4368 +    unresolved_ages: list[int] = []
    4369 +    total_monthly_premium = 0.0
    4370 +
    4371 +    for age in ages:
    4372 +        selected = _select_plan_price_row_for_age(rows, age)
    4373 +        if selected is None:
    4374 +            unresolved_ages.append(age)
    4375 +            continue
    4376 +        members.append(selected)
    4377 +        total_monthly_premium += float(selected.get("monthly_premium") or 0.0)
    4378 +
    4379 +    return {
    4380 +        "member_count": len(ages),
    4381 +        "priced_member_count": len(members),
    4382 +        "unresolved_ages": unresolved_ages,
    4383 +        "all_ages_priced": len(unresolved_ages) == 0,
    4384 +        "selection_strategy": "lowest_rate_for_age_band",
    4385 +        "total_monthly_premium": round(total_monthly_premium, 2),
    4386 +        "members": members,
    4387 +    }

    2834 +                "Use calculate_family_premium to estimate monthly household total from age bands.",
    2835 +                "Use get_plan_quick_summary for Essential-10 style fields only.",
    2836 +            ],

    2843 +        if include_prices and prices_error is None:

    2847 +
    2848 +    @server.tool(
    2849 +        description=(
    2850 +            "Return normalized 'Essential 10' plan summary fields (copays, deductible, MOOP, and premium range) "
    2851 +            "for one plan/year."
    2852 +        ),
    2853 +        annotations=readonly_tool,
    2854 +    )
    2855 +    async def get_plan_quick_summary(
    2856 +        ctx: Context,
    2857 +        plan_id: str,
    2858 +        year: int | None = None,
    2859 +        zip_code: str | None = None,
    2860 +        age: int | None = None,
    2861 +        include_price_summary: bool = True,
    2862 +        summary: bool | str = "compact",
    2863 +    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
    2864 +        normalized_plan_id = str(plan_id or "").strip()
    2865 +        if not normalized_plan_id:
    2866 +            return _tool_input_error_payload("plan_id is required", operation_id="get_plan_quick_summary")
    2867 +        try:
    2868 +            _normalize_summary_mode(summary)

    2871 +
    2872 +        effective_year = _default_provider_search_year(year)

    2874 +        normalized_age = _coerce_int(age)

    2876 +            return _tool_input_error_payload("age must be an integer", operation_id="get_plan_quick_summary")

    2881 +            path=f"/api/v1/plans/{normalized_plan_id}",
    2882 +            query={"year": effective_year},

    2885 +            cache_key_parts={"plan_id": normalized_plan_id, "year": effective_year},
    2886 +        )
    2887 +        if plan_error is not None:
    2888 +            return plan_error
    2889 +
    2890 +        if normalized_zip:
    2891 +            list_query: dict[str, Any] = {
    2892 +                "plan_id": normalized_plan_id,
    2893 +                "zip_code": normalized_zip,
    2894 +                "year": effective_year,
    2895 +                "limit": 1,
    2896 +            }
    2897 +            if normalized_age is not None:

    2899 +            list_payload, list_error = await _authorized_call(
    2900 +                ctx,
    2901 +                method="GET",
    2902 +                path="/api/v1/plans",
    2903 +                query=list_query,
    2904 +                operation_id="listPlans",
    2905 +                cache_namespace="plan-zip-year-check",
    2906 +                cache_key_parts=list_query,
    2907 +            )
    2908 +            if list_error is not None:
    2909 +                return list_error
    2910 +            if not _extract_items_from_payload(list_payload):
    2911 +                return {
    2912 +                    "ok": True,
    2913 +                    "operation_id": "get_plan_quick_summary",
    2914 +                    "decision": "no_data_for_year",

    2924 +                        "Retry with a year known to have plan rows for this ZIP.",
    2925 +                    ],
    2926 +                }

    2928 +        prices_payload: dict[str, Any] | list[Any] | str | None = None

    2941 +

    2962 +    @server.tool(
    2963 +        description=(
    2964 +            "Estimate monthly family premium from one plan and member ages. Strict-year behavior: "
    2965 +            "does not auto-fallback to prior years."
    2966 +        ),
    2967 +        annotations=readonly_tool,
    2968 +    )
    2969 +    async def calculate_family_premium(
    2970 +        ctx: Context,
    2971 +        plan_id: str,
    2972 +        zip_code: str,
    2973 +        ages: list[int],
    2974 +        year: int | None = None,
    2975 +        summary: bool | str = "standard",
    2976 +    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
    2977 +        normalized_plan_id = str(plan_id or "").strip()
    2978 +        if not normalized_plan_id:
    2979 +            return _tool_input_error_payload("plan_id is required", operation_id="calculate_family_premium")
    2980 +        normalized_zip = _normalize_zip_code(zip_code)
    2981 +        if not normalized_zip:
    2982 +            return _tool_input_error_payload(
    2983 +                "zip_code must be a valid 5-digit ZIP code",
    2984 +                operation_id="calculate_family_premium",
    2985 +            )
    2986 +        if not isinstance(ages, list) or not ages:
    2987 +            return _tool_input_error_payload(
    2988 +                "ages must be a non-empty list of integers",
    2989 +                operation_id="calculate_family_premium",
    2990 +            )
    2991 +        if len(ages) > 20:
    2992 +            return _tool_input_error_payload(
    2993 +                "ages supports up to 20 values per call",
    2994 +                operation_id="calculate_family_premium",
    2995 +            )
    2996 +        try:
    2997 +            summary_mode = _normalize_summary_mode(summary)
    2998 +        except ValueError as exc:
    2999 +            return _tool_input_error_payload(str(exc), operation_id="calculate_family_premium")
    3000 +
    3001 +        normalized_ages: list[int] = []
    3002 +        for age in ages:
    3003 +            parsed_age = _coerce_int(age)
    3004 +            if parsed_age is None or parsed_age < 0 or parsed_age > 120:
    3005 +                return _tool_input_error_payload(
    3006 +                    "ages must contain integers in range 0..120",
    3007 +                    operation_id="calculate_family_premium",
    3008 +                )
    3009 +            normalized_ages.append(parsed_age)
    3010 +
    3011 +        effective_year = _default_provider_search_year(year)
    3012 +
    3013 +        list_payload, list_error = await _authorized_call(
    3014 +            ctx,
    3015 +            method="GET",
    3016 +            path="/api/v1/plans",

› ✓  call_operation (healthporta MCP Server) {"operation_id":"listPlans","query":{"order_by":"premium_min","is_dental_only":false,"limit":10,"zip_code":"60601","year":2026}}                       │
  │                                                                                                                                                                                                   │
  │ {                                                                                                                                                                                                 │
  │   "ok": false,                                                                                                                                                                                    │
  │   "error_type": "runtime_error",                                                                                                                                                                  │
  │   "error": "Invalid variable type: value should be str, int or float, got False of type <class 'bool'>",                                                                                          │
  │   "operation_id": "listPlans",                                                                                                                                                                    │
  │   "method": "GET",                                                                                                                                                                                │
  │   "path": "/api/v1/plans"                                                                                                                                                                         │
  │ }                                                                                                                                                                                                 │
  ╰───────────────────────────────────────


• I reproduced the cause from your payload: is_dental_only: false is being passed as a raw Python bool into URL query encoding, which this transport rejects. I’m patching api-mcp to coerce query
  booleans to "true"/"false" before outbound API calls (single and batch).

    215 +def _normalize_query_params_for_transport(query: dict[str, Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any]:
    216 +    if not isinstance(query, dict):
    217 +        return {}
    218 +    out: dict[str, Any] = {}
    219 +    for key, value in query.items():
    220 +        normalized = _normalize_query_param_value(value)
    221 +        if normalized is None:
    222 +            continue
    223 +        out[str(key)] = normalized
    224 +    return out
    225 +
    226 +
    227 +def _normalize_query_param_value(value: Any) -> Any:
    228 +    if value is None:
    229 +        return None
    230 +    if isinstance(value, bool):
    231 +        return "true" if value else "false"
    232 +    if isinstance(value, (str, int, float)):
    233 +        return value
    234 +    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
    235 +        normalized_items: list[Any] = []
    236 +        for item in value:
    237 +            normalized_item = _normalize_query_param_value(item)
    238 +            if normalized_item is None:
    239 +                continue
    240 +            if isinstance(normalized_item, (str, int, float)):
    241 +                normalized_items.append(normalized_item)
    242 +            else:
    243 +                normalized_items.append(json.dumps(normalized_item, separators=(",", ":"), default=str))
    244 +        return normalized_items if normalized_items else None
    245 +    return json.dumps(value, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)

• Fixed.

  Your error came from query encoding rejecting raw Python booleans in URL params.
  is_dental_only: false was sent as False instead of "false".

  ### What I changed

  - Added transport-level query normalization in api-mcp:
      - healthporta_mcp/api_layer_client.py
      - _request() now normalizes query params before aiohttp call.
      - New helper converts:
          - True/False -> "true"/"false"
          - drops None values
          - keeps str/int/float
          - handles list values safely
          - stringifies nested objects if passed accidentally

  ### Tests added

  - tests/test_api_layer_client_helpers.py
      - bool coercion test
      - list coercion test
      - nested-object stringification test

─ Worked for 5m 05s ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Reviewed and hardened api-mcp against api-layer contracts, including MCP-client instructions and batch behavior.

  What I changed

  - Fixed batch handling so MCP tool IDs are no longer treated as unknown OpenAPI ops in prepare phase (read_resource, etc.):
      - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6976
  - Added stronger query normalization/compatibility mapping:
      - listIssuers: maps legacy issuer filters to q, removes unsupported year
      - getNpiNear: maps legacy/provider-geo style keys (Lat/Long, radius_miles, plan_network_checksum, nucc_*, zip aliases), strips unsupported issuer-like filters
      - listPlans / listProviders / searchProvidersByGeo / pharmacy contexts now have case-insensitive alias normalization for common keys
      - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6463
      - New context helpers:
          - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6745
          - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6761
  - Added explicit warning hooks for dropped unsupported params (listIssuers.year, getNpiNear.issuer_like*):
      - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6093
  - Improved MCP instruction text + examples to reduce client misuse of path params in batch calls:
      - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1171
      - Added concrete call_api_read_batch examples for:
          - getProviderServiceClaimForProvider
          - getMedicationProductsByName
          - getMedicationProductsByRxNorm
          - read_resource
      - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:1048
  - Kept/finalized transport normalization fix for query params (bool/list/object coercion before HTTP request):
      - healthporta_mcp/api_layer_client.py:73
      - healthporta_mcp/api_layer_client.py:215

  Validation

  - Static wrapper audit vs api-layer/openapi/openapi.yaml: 0 mismatches for internal _authorized_call usage.
  - Static metadata/examples audit (_MCP_TOOL_EXAMPLES, recipes/scenarios/intent hints): 0 OpenAPI contract mismatches.
  - Tests:
      - PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py tests/test_api_layer_client_helpers.py passed
      - PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q passed (full suite)

  Files updated

  - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py
  - healthporta_mcp/api_layer_client.py
  - tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py
  - tests/test_api_layer_client_helpers.py

  Next step is redeploy api-mcp so MCP clients pick up these fixes.

• The failure was due to missing plan_id in the extraction logic, which ignored path_params when a query was present. This is fixed by merging path_params correctly with args, supporting calls like
  {"operation_id":"find_providers_by_plan","args":{"plan_id":"..","zip_code":"60601","year":2025,...}}. Unit tests confirm the fix.

  To resolve the issue, redeploy the updated api-mcp server. As a workaround, explicitly passing plan_id in the args can help. Retest with the same call format after redeployment.

• Root cause found and fixed.

  - call_api_read_batch MCP-tool items could lose required args when request used mixed shapes (query + path_params), so find_providers_by_plan got called without plan_id.
  - This is now fixed by merging MCP-tool args from:
      - args / arguments / query
      - plus compatibility merge from path_params and object body

  Changes:

  - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:6981
  - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py:7013

  You should redeploy api-mcp only.

  Safe request shape going forward:

  {
    "operation_id": "find_providers_by_plan",
    "args": {
      "plan_id": "36096IL0810081",
      "zip_code": "60601",
      "year": 2025,
      "summary": "standard"
    }
  }

  path_params still works now as compatibility, but args is preferred.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• Yes, this happened because upstream rows mix two signals:

  - numeric claims-based cost_index
  - categorical estimated_cost_level ($...$$$$$)

  Some rows were sending the categorical value inside cost_index, so type drift appeared in one response.

  I fixed it in api-mcp normalization:

  - cost_index is now numeric only (float) when available.
  - $ / $$ / ... / $$$$$ is now only in estimated_cost_level.
  - If a row had symbolic cost_index, MCP moves that meaning to estimated_cost_level and removes cost_index.

  Updated:

  - healthporta_mcp/mcp_server.py
  - tests/test_mcp_server_helpers.py

  Tests:

  - targeted tests passed
  - full suite passed: PYTHONPATH=. pytest -q

  You only need to redeploy api-mcp to get consistent output.

  cost_index removed. I’ll patch code + tests now.

› we need to leave estimated_cost_level or cost_index - what is better?


• Better to keep estimated_cost_level as the primary field.

  Why:

  - It is explicit and unambiguous ($..$$$$$), so clients won’t treat it as numeric.
  - It matches what you want users/LLMs to reason on.
  - It avoids the exact mixed-type bug you just hit with cost_index.

  Recommended rollout:

  1. Make estimated_cost_level canonical everywhere.
  2. Keep cost_index only as temporary alias (same string value) for backward compatibility.
  3. Deprecate and remove cost_index after client migration.


› ok, remove duplicate string, just lets assume we remember the synonym as cost index

• Proposed Plan


  # Multi-Repo Base Architecture Review Prompt Rollout

  ## Summary

  Create a standardized base_arch_prompt.md in the specs folder of each in-scope project, adapted to that project’s architecture and risks (not a blind copy).
  Scope confirmed:

  1. api-mcp
  2. api-layer
  3. api-app
  4. healthcare-mrf-api (specs/ only)
  5. /Users/nick/project/healthporta
  6. /Users/nick/project/pharmacy/site

  For projects missing specs/, create it.

  ———

  ## What Will Be Added Per Project

  ### 1) Common base prompt structure (shared across all files)

  Each base_arch_prompt.md will include the same review protocol, adapted language, and deterministic output format:

  1. Review goals and engineering preferences
      - DRY enforcement
      - edge-case handling emphasis
      - anti-hacky implementation preference
      - explicitness over cleverness
  2. Four-stage review flow
      - Architecture
      - Code quality
      - Tests
      - Performance/scale
  3. Issue format contract
      - severity
      - concrete evidence (file:line)
      - options including “do nothing”
      - tradeoffs/impact
      - opinionated recommendation
  4. Interaction mode
      - section-by-section or full pass
      - max top-N issues per section
      - mandatory “AskUserQuestion” decisions when tradeoffs exist
  5. Output schema
      - numbered issues
      - option lettering per issue
      - final recommendation summary

  ### 2) Project-specific adaptations inside each file

  Each prompt will have a dedicated Project Context section so the reviewer prioritizes the correct boundaries and failure modes.

  - api-mcp/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: MCP tool contracts, operation-id alignment, path/query param normalization, batch orchestration robustness, metadata instruction quality, fallback/default-year logic, token-efficiency
        behavior.
  - api-layer/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: gateway boundary, auth/bundle enforcement, upstream error mapping, OpenAPI contract correctness, rate-limit behavior, request validation consistency, backward compatibility.
  - api-app/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: internal API + dashboard boundary, session/auth lifecycle, DB pool resilience, billing/stripe flows, docs/openapi integration, client key lifecycle and Redis sync.
  - healthcare-mrf-api/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: import pipeline correctness, staging/swap safety (_old backup policy), long-running jobs, index strategy, claims/drug/NPI API query performance, import failure recovery.
  - /Users/nick/project/healthporta/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: UI route architecture, async data loading patterns, map/search UX performance, template rendering constraints, API call orchestration and failure UX.
  - /Users/nick/project/pharmacy/site/specs/base_arch_prompt.md
      - focus: doctor/pharmacy/drug page data integrity, async fan-out safety, SEO/canonical correctness, address formatting normalization, page latency and template hydration patterns.

  ———

  ## File/Interface Changes

  No runtime API/interface behavior changes.
  Only docs/spec artifacts:

  1. api-mcp/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new dir + file)
  2. api-layer/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new file)
  3. api-app/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new file)
  4. healthcare-mrf-api/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new file)
  5. /Users/nick/project/healthporta/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new dir + file)
  6. /Users/nick/project/pharmacy/site/specs/base_arch_prompt.md (new dir + file)

  Optional index updates (recommended): add one bullet link to each repo’s specs index/README where present.

  ———

  ## Implementation Steps (decision-complete)

  1. Create/verify specs/ directories
      - create for api-mcp, healthporta, pharmacy/site if absent.
  2. Author shared core template text
      - convert screenshot intent into clean markdown prompt (same workflow, explicit constraints, deterministic issue format).
  3. Inject project context blocks
      - tailor “what to evaluate first” and “hotspots” per repo using current architecture docs/README realities.
  4. Standardize output contract in all six files
      - enforce numbered issues, option letters, recommendation + rationale, and AskUserQuestion gate before implementation.
  5. Add/adjust specs index links
      - where specs/README.md exists (api-layer, api-app), add “Base Architecture Review Prompt”.
      - do not create noisy extra docs elsewhere.
  6. Validation pass
      - verify paths, markdown formatting, and consistency of section headings.
      - ensure no repo-specific contradictions (e.g., wrong ports/components).

  ———

  ## Test/Verification Scenarios

  1. Presence check
      - each scoped repo has exactly one specs/base_arch_prompt.md.
  2. Consistency check
      - all six files share identical core review protocol sections.
  3. Specialization check
      - each file has project-specific hotspots and examples relevant to that repository only.
  4. Index discoverability
      - where specs/README.md exists, it links to base_arch_prompt.md.
  5. No behavioral drift
      - no code, config, or runtime files changed outside docs.

  ———

  ## Assumptions and Defaults Chosen

  1. “All projects in scope” = the 6 repositories you confirmed (Core 4 + frontends).
  2. For healthcare-mrf-api, canonical location is healthcare-mrf-api/specs/base_arch_prompt.md only.
  3. Repos without specs/ will get a new specs/ directory.
  4. Prompt wording will preserve strictness and decision structure from your screenshot, while normalizing tone for reusable engineering docs.


─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  # US Potential Clients Library (1000+ orgs) for HealthPorta APIs

  ## Summary

  Create a scalable, auto-generated library of 1000+ potential US client organizations (healthtech vendors + startups, with public-source provenance) mapped to what HealthPorta can deliver today (via
  api-layer + api-mcp) and what we can build quickly. Deliverables live in api-app/specs/ as Markdown: one index (potential_clients.md) plus one file per company under a structured folder, generated
  by a script.

  This is feasible without paid datasets by using public vendor registries (primary: ONC CHPL developers/vendors) and optionally augmenting with public lists for payers/providers later. The
  “proposal” content is templated + type-aware so it’s useful at scale.

  ———

  ## Goals and Success Criteria

  - Generate >= 1000 unique organizations with deterministic output.
  - Each org gets a “what to pitch” page (medium depth) with:
      - org name + category + source references
      - likely needs/use-cases
      - which HealthPorta bundles/endpoints solve it
      - “quick builds” we can deliver in days/weeks
      - risks/unknowns + next steps
  - A top-level index that:
      - explains capabilities (grounded in current api-layer + api-mcp)
      - provides search/navigation by category/state/type
      - includes data provenance + regeneration instructions

  ———

  ## Location and Repo Layout (api-app)

  Add a new docs subtree:

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients.md
    Executive index + how to use + regeneration commands.
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/README.md
    “Operating manual” for the library (sources, scoring, templates).
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/companies/<slug>.md
    One file per company (recommended; avoids 1000 folders).
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/areas.md
    Our “offerings matrix”: use-cases mapped to api-layer bundles/endpoints.
  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/
    Generator code.
  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/.cache/ (gitignored)
    Downloaded datasets / intermediate files.

  Update api-app/specs/README.md to link potential_clients.md.

  ———

  ## Capability Analysis (What We Sell)

  Codify current capabilities into a reusable matrix (used by the generator):

  ### Core Offerings (today)

  - Provider directory + geo search: providers/pharmacies, near/geo filtering, insurance networks
  - Plan + formulary intelligence: plans, issuers, prices, formularies, coverage checks
  - Claims/pricing analytics: provider-services-claims, drug-claims, ratings (cost level + quality proxy)
  - MCP-native AI workflows: tool recipes, batched calls, validated request shaping

  ### “Build Quickly” Add-ons (days/weeks)

  - Customer-specific “vertical endpoints” in api-layer (guardrailed filters + stable shapes)
  - Precomputed benchmarks and peer groups for specific specialties/procedures/metros
  - Higher-level composite endpoints (e.g., “best value providers for procedure in area”)
  - Usage analytics dashboards + per-route latency/error monitoring and alerts
  - Data exports/snapshots for internal BI (rate-limited + auditable)

  ———

  ## Data Sources (Public, No Paid Dependencies)

  Primary (guarantees 1000+ vendor orgs):

  1. ONC CHPL (Certified Health IT Product List) developer/vendor list
     Use developer/company names as candidates; store provenance (download timestamp + source URL).

  Optional enrichers (later phases, still public):
  2. CMS marketplace issuers (already in our system) for payer prospects.
  3. NPPES org NPIs for provider org prospects (if we decide to broaden mix).
  4. Public hospital datasets (data.cms.gov) for hospital system prospects.

  Given your preference (“healthtech vendors + startups”), CHPL is the backbone; optional sources are additive.

  ———

  ## Company Classification and Pitch Mapping

  ### Company categories (rule-based)

  - ehr_vendor
  - rcm_billing
  - telehealth
  - benefits_navigation
  - pharmacy_tech
  - provider_directory_data
  - price_transparency_analytics
  - other_health_it

  Classification heuristics (deterministic):

  - keyword match on company name/products (from CHPL export fields)
  - optionally “product type” tags from the dataset

  ### Proposal template (medium depth) per company

  Each companies/<slug>.md includes:

  - Summary
      - What they do (from source field(s))
      - Why we think they’re a fit (capability match)
  - Likely Needs
      - 3–6 bullet needs aligned to their category
  - What We Offer Now
      - bundles + example endpoints relevant to them
  - Fast Builds (1–3 weeks)
      - 3–8 concrete add-ons we can deliver quickly
  - Integration Notes
      - auth model, bundles, rate limits, MCP option
  - Risks / Unknowns
      - what we’d need to confirm in discovery
  - Next Step
      - 2–3 discovery questions to ask them (org-level, not personal)

  No personal contact info is stored.

  ———

  ## Generator Implementation

  ### Scripts

  Add api-app/scripts/potential_clients/generate.py with commands:

  - python generate.py fetch
    Downloads datasets into .cache/ (idempotent).
  - python generate.py build --min 1000
    Produces markdown files and the index (deterministic sorting).
  - python generate.py validate
    Ensures counts, unique slugs, required fields present.

  ### Determinism and Dedupe

  - Normalize company name:
      - uppercase/lowercase fold
      - strip punctuation + corporate suffixes (INC/LLC/LTD/CO/CORP)
  - Dedupe key:
      - normalized_name + (optional) headquarters state if present
  - Stable slug:
      - slugify(name) + short hash if collision

  ### Output Generation

  - Writes/overwrites:
      - specs/potential_clients.md
      - specs/potential_clients/companies/*.md
      - specs/potential_clients/areas.md (from a maintained template + endpoint references)

  ———

  ## Tests and Validation

  Add unit tests (fast, no network):

  - tests/test_potential_clients_normalize.py
      - name normalization + dedupe behavior
      - slug collision handling
  - tests/test_potential_clients_render.py
      - generated markdown contains required sections
  - tests/test_potential_clients_counts.py
      - fixture input generates expected counts
      - validation fails if < 1000 (when --min 1000 specified)

  Add a CI-safe mode:

  - generator supports --offline using a small fixture dataset in-repo.

  ———

  ## Operational Notes

  - Add .gitignore entry for api-app/scripts/potential_clients/.cache/.
  - Keep generated markdown reasonably sized:
      - medium depth but capped (avoid dumping raw dataset rows).
  - Regeneration cadence:
      - manual for now; later optional cron/job outside repo if desired.

  ———

  ## Deliverables Checklist

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients.md
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/README.md
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/areas.md
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/companies/ with >= 1000 pages
  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/generate.py
  - api-app/tests/ generator unit tests
  - api-app/specs/README.md updated to link the new doc

  ———

  ## Assumptions (Locked)

  - Docs live in api-app/specs/.
  - One markdown file per company (not one folder).
  - We prioritize publicly-derived healthtech vendor prospects to reach 1000 without paid sources.
  - We avoid storing personal contact data; proposals are org-level.

  # Update: Per-Company Recommended Plan + Why (1000+ Potential Clients)

  ## Summary

  Extend the potential-clients docs generator to output a recommended subscription plan (starter|growth|scale) and rationale per company, grounded in our actual plan tiers and rate-limit windows.

  Defaults you chose (locked in):

  - Bias: Conservative (default starter, upgrade only on clear signals)
  - Primary driver: Expected request volume
  - Do not recommend demo_100 in sales docs

  ———

  ## Source of Truth (Current Plan Tiers)

  Use these as the basis for recommendations:

  From api-app/specs/billing.md:

  - starter: 10k req/mo, 2 seats, 40/min, 1k/hr, 5k/day, 10k/mo
  - growth: 100k req/mo, 5 seats, 120/min, 5k/hr, 35k/day, 100k/mo
  - scale: 1M req/mo, 10 seats, 1k/min, 30k/hr, 300k/day, 1M/mo

  Bundles: for paid plans, the docs say all core bundles are included; api-layer/config/plans.toml also shows all bundles enabled for all tiers.

  Important consistency note:

  - api-app/specs/billing.md lists overage pricing tiers ($75/$50/$40 per 1000), but api-layer/config/plans.toml currently shows overage_price_per_1000_usd = 75 for all tiers. Before publishing plan
    pricing claims in client docs, reconcile these two sources (or have the generator not mention overage prices at all).

  ———

  ## New Outputs (Per Company)

  ### 1) Add these fields to every company page

  In api-app/specs/potential_clients/companies/<slug>.md, add a new section:

  Recommended Plan

  - recommended_plan: starter|growth|scale
  - why: short bullet list with deterministic reasons
  - assumptions: explicit call-volume assumptions used (so it’s honest and auditable)

  Example structure (template):

  - Recommended plan: growth
  - Why:
      - Likely high QPS consumer-facing search (provider + pricing + map)
      - Needs predictable peak rate headroom (>=120/min)
      - Anticipated monthly usage > 10k (autocomplete + map + results pagination)
  - Assumptions:
      - Peak: ~3–10 RPS during business hours
      - Monthly: ~30k–80k requests depending on traffic

  ### 2) Add the fields to the index

  In api-app/specs/potential_clients.md, include a summary table per category:

  - category
  - default recommended plan
  - most common upgrade triggers

  ———

  ## Deterministic Recommendation Rules (Conservative)

  Because we won’t have reliable revenue/traffic data from public sources, we use a ruleset + assumptions based on company category and product shape. The generator emits “assumptions” so the
  recommendation is transparent.

  ### Inputs available (public datasets)

  - company category (heuristic)
  - vendor/product count (if available)
  - product types / keywords (if available)
  - optional: “enterprise signal” keywords (EHR, clearinghouse, payer, nationwide, claims, RCM)

  ### Derived signals (computed)

  - enterprise_signal: boolean
  - consumer_traffic_signal: boolean
  - expected_monthly_bucket: one of <=10k, 10k-100k, >=100k
  - peak_rate_bucket: one of <=40/min, <=120/min, <=1000/min

  ### Plan assignment

  - Recommend scale when:
      - enterprise_signal AND (consumer_traffic_signal OR “embedded into many tenants” signal)
      - OR category is ehr_vendor / clearinghouse / “large navigation platform”
      - OR expected monthly bucket >=100k with high peak needs
  - Recommend growth when:
      - consumer_traffic_signal is true (patient-facing search, directories, pricing pages)
      - OR category is RCM/billing/benefits navigation where batch lookups + internal dashboards likely push beyond 10k/mo
      - OR expected monthly bucket 10k-100k
  - Otherwise recommend starter

  ### Why text generation (always include)

  Generate 3–6 bullets chosen from a fixed library keyed by:

  - plan thresholds (10k vs 100k vs 1M monthly)
  - peak rate windows (40/min vs 120/min vs 1000/min)
  - seat needs (2 vs 5 vs 10) only as secondary “possible fit” text (don’t claim certainty)

  ———

  ## Category Defaults (Quick Reference)

  These are the generator defaults before signals override:

  - price_transparency_analytics, provider_directory_data, telehealth marketplace, consumer pharmacy locator: default growth
  - ehr_vendor, clearinghouse, national RCM: default scale
  - pharmacy_tech (B2B), small RCM, small benefits tool, startup general: default starter

  ———

  ## Changes to Generator (Implementation Spec)

  Update generator to:

  1. Build a company_profile object (normalized fields + category + signals)
  2. Compute plan_recommendation = {plan, why[], assumptions[]}
  3. Render into each <slug>.md
  4. Render category rollups into potential_clients.md

  Do not mention overage pricing unless we reconcile the config/spec discrepancy; recommendations should focus on limits + monthly included.

  ———

  ## Tests (Non-Negotiable)

  Add unit tests for:

  - category → default plan mapping
  - signal overrides (enterprise forces scale; consumer traffic bumps growth)
  - deterministic “why” bullets (stable output given same inputs)
  - guardrail: never output demo_100

  ———

  ## Acceptance Criteria

  - 1000+ company pages each contain a Recommended Plan section.
  - Recommendations are deterministic and explain assumptions.
  - Index includes category defaults and upgrade triggers.
  - No references to demo_100.
  - No overage pricing references unless sources reconciled.

  ———

  ## Open Items (Explicit)

  - Decide whether to reconcile overage pricing config vs docs now, or simply exclude overage pricing from prospect docs. (Recommended: exclude until reconciled.)

  accessible CHPL JSON seems easiest. However, I need to ensure we filter developers to only include those based in the US since the user specifically asked for that. Let’s check if the CHPL dataset
  includes US addresses to do that effectively.

  help ensure they're all from the US. Using CMS NPPES organizational NPIs for provider organizations and pharmacies is a solid approach since that's definitely US-based. The user's preference seems to

  was focused on vendors and startups.

  are focused on US-based entities. To accommodate the user’s request for working with subfolders company by company, I’ll implement a structure under potential_clients/companies/<slug>.md with one
  file per company. Additionally, I’ll include signals and category mapping to derive plan recommendations in the markdown. It's important to maintain a manageable repo size, but the user has specified
  they want this comprehensive output. So, I’ll proceed with creating these files, aiming for medium depth to keep things organized and effective!

  endpoint list in api-layer/specs/interfaces.md. Each company file should have a "Quick Pitch" and "Implementation Roadmap" to help propose to clients quickly.

  the URL using web.run to get it right.

     1 +# Potential Clients (US) - Target List and Pitches
     2 +
     3 +This directory is a structured prospecting library: organizations that are likely to benefit from HealthPorta APIs (and short-build add-ons).
     4 +
     5 +## How To Use
     6 +
     7 +- Start with `specs/potential_clients/areas.md` to understand what we sell (capabilities mapped to bundles/endpoints).
     8 +- Use the generated company pages in `specs/potential_clients/companies/` for outreach notes:
     9 +  - what they likely need,
    10 +  - what we can provide now,
    11 +  - what we can build quickly,
    12 +  - recommended plan tier and why (with explicit assumptions).
    13 +
    14 +## Plan Recommendation Policy (Heuristic)
    15 +
    16 +Per-company plan recommendations are conservative and deterministic:
    17 +
    18 +- Default to `starter` unless there are strong signals for higher usage.
    19 +- Primary driver: expected request volume + peak rate (not seats).
    20 +- We do not recommend `demo_100` in sales docs.
    21 +
    22 +Plan tiers (source of truth for limits/units): `api-layer/config/plans.toml` and `api-app/specs/billing.md`.
    23 +

    26 +The initial 1000+ list is generated from a public HealthIT.gov dataset of app marketplace listings (2019-2025):
    27 +- Dataset page: https://www.healthit.gov/data/data-sets/dataset-apps-and-software-applications-discovered-app-marketplaces
    28 +- CSV download is fetched by the generator and cached locally.
    29 +
    30 +This yields a mix of healthtech vendors, startups, and healthcare organizations operating in the US healthcare market.

    37 +- `api-app/scripts/potential_clients/generate.py`
    38 +

    40 +

• Added api-app/specs/potential_clients/README.md (+68 -0)
     1 +# Potential Clients Library - Operating Manual
     2 +
     3 +## Purpose
     4 +
     5 +Maintain a structured, repeatable list of US healthcare-market prospects with specific pitches mapped to HealthPorta capabilities.
     6 +
     7 +This is designed to scale to thousands of orgs without becoming unmaintainable:
     8 +- deterministic generation,
     9 +- explicit provenance,
    10 +- consistent template structure,
    11 +- conservative assumptions documented per recommendation.
    12 +
    13 +## What We Store (and What We Don't)
    14 +
    15 +Stored:
    16 +- organization name (company/developer name from source data)
    17 +- high-level category tags (heuristic)
    18 +- example products (app names) and marketplaces (Epic/Cerner/etc.)
    19 +- likely needs and mapped HealthPorta offerings
    20 +- recommended plan tier (`starter|growth|scale`) with explicit assumptions
    21 +
    22 +Not stored:
    23 +- personal contact info
    24 +- scraped emails/phones
    25 +- any private data
    26 +
    27 +## Output Structure
    28 +
    29 +- `specs/potential_clients.md`: top-level index and workflow.
    30 +- `specs/potential_clients/areas.md`: what we can sell / build quickly (mapped to bundles and endpoints).
    31 +- `specs/potential_clients/companies/<slug>.md`: one page per company.
    32 +
    33 +## Generator
    34 +
    35 +Script:
    36 +- `api-app/scripts/potential_clients/generate.py`
    37 +
    38 +Commands:
    39 +- `fetch`: download/update cached dataset(s)
    40 +- `build`: generate the company pages + indexes
    41 +- `validate`: verify counts and required sections
    42 +
    43 +Design requirements:
    44 +- deterministic sorting and stable slugs
    45 +- name normalization and dedupe
    46 +- no network required for tests (`--offline` uses fixtures)
    47 +
    48 +## Plan Tier Semantics
    49 +
    50 +The generator recommends the paid tiers only:
    51 +- `starter`: 10k requests/month (40/min)
    52 +- `growth`: 100k requests/month (120/min)
    53 +- `scale`: 1M requests/month (1000/min)
    54 +
    55 +Recommendations focus on limits/units, not overage pricing (overage policy sources must be reconciled before we sell on it).
    56 +
    57 +## Adding New Sources (Later)
    58 +
    59 +This design supports adding more public sources:
    60 +- payers/issuers (CMS marketplace / existing plan data)
    61 +- provider organizations (NPPES org NPIs, hospital systems)
    62 +- other healthtech vendor lists (public registries)
    63 +
    64 +Any new source must:
    65 +- include provenance in docs
    66 +- be cacheable
    67 +- be usable offline for tests via a fixture slice
    68 +

• Added api-app/specs/potential_clients/areas.md (+90 -0)
     1 +# Offerings Matrix (Capabilities -> Bundles -> Pitches)
     2 +
     3 +This file maps HealthPorta capabilities to the `api-layer` public API surface so prospect pages can stay grounded in what we actually ship.
     4 +
     5 +## Bundles (api-layer)
     6 +
     7 +All paid tiers currently include these bundles (see `api-layer/config/plans.toml`):
     8 +- `plan` (plans + prices + issuers)
     9 +- `geo` (states/cities/zip lookup)
    10 +- `npi` (providers directory + taxonomy)
    11 +- `pharmacy` (pharmacies directory + geo/nearby)
    12 +- `formulary` (formularies + plan drug coverage)
    13 +- `medication` (drug lookup endpoints)
    14 +- `provider_services_claims` (procedure/service claims search)
    15 +- `drug_claims` (prescription claims search)
    16 +- `ratings` (quality proxy + estimated cost level)
    17 +
    18 +## Areas We Can Sell Today
    19 +
    20 +### 1) Provider and Facility Search
    21 +Target clients:
    22 +- patient experience apps, scheduling apps, telehealth marketplaces, navigation tools, provider directories
    23 +
    24 +What we provide:
    25 +- provider directory search (`/api/v1/providers`, `/api/v1/providers/geo`, `/api/v1/providers/{npi}`)
    26 +- nearby provider discovery (`/api/v1/providers/{npi}/nearby`)
    27 +- taxonomy resolution (`/api/v1/nucc/taxonomies`)
    28 +
    29 +Quick builds:
    30 +- specialty-first “doctor finder” endpoint with opinionated guardrails and faster defaults
    31 +- “provider card” composite response (address + phone + maps url + rating/cost bucket)
    32 +
    33 +### 2) Pharmacy Search and Coverage-Aware Pharmacy Locator
    34 +Target clients:
    35 +- pharmacy tech, medication adherence, navigation apps
    36 +
    37 +What we provide:
    38 +- pharmacy directory (`/api/v1/pharmacies`, `/api/v1/pharmacies/{npi}`)
    39 +- pharmacy geo search (`/api/v1/pharmacies/geo`)
    40 +- pharmacy profile (`/api/v1/pharmacies/{npi}/profile`)
    41 +
    42 +Quick builds:
    43 +- plan-aware “covered pharmacies for Rx” search and ranking
    44 +
    45 +### 3) Plan and Network Intelligence
    46 +Target clients:
    47 +- benefits navigation, payer-facing tooling, enrollment decision support, broker tooling
    48 +
    49 +What we provide:
    50 +- plan search + issuer lookups (`/api/v1/plans`, `/api/v1/issuers`)
    51 +- premium rows (`/api/v1/plans/{plan_id}/prices`)
    52 +- insurance network locator (`/api/v1/insurance-networks`, `/api/v1/insurance-networks/{checksum}/locator`)
    53 +
    54 +Quick builds:
    55 +- “Plan Quick Summary” composite: essential benefits + premium min/max for a ZIP
    56 +
    57 +### 4) Formulary and Drug Coverage
    58 +Target clients:
    59 +- medication apps, pharmacy programs, benefits navigation tools
    60 +
    61 +What we provide:
    62 +- formularies and tiers (`/api/v1/formularies/*`)
    63 +- plan drug coverage (`/api/v1/plans/{plan_id}/drugs/{rxnorm_id}/coverage`)
    64 +- medication lookup (`/api/v1/medications/*`)
    65 +
    66 +Quick builds:
    67 +- “best plans for a drug” (coverage + tier + restrictions summary)
    68 +
    69 +### 5) Claims-Based Pricing and Benchmarks (Medicare-derived)
    70 +Target clients:
    71 +- cost transparency, analytics vendors, navigation apps, value-based care tooling
    72 +
    73 +What we provide:
    74 +- procedure/provider aggregates and search (`/api/v1/provider-services-claims/*`)
    75 +- prescription/provider aggregates (`/api/v1/drug-claims/*`)
    76 +- ratings (`/api/v1/ratings/*`) including estimated cost level and quality proxy
    77 +
    78 +Quick builds:
    79 +- “best value providers for procedure near ZIP” (search + ratings join + rank)
    80 +- guardrailed sorting by cost bucket only when code + location are present
    81 +
    82 +## Plan Tier Guidance (How We Sell)
    83 +
    84 +We recommend plans based on expected traffic and peak rates:
    85 +- `starter`: pilots, internal tools, low-volume integration (<=10k/mo, <=40/min)
    86 +- `growth`: production SaaS workflows, patient-facing search (10k-100k/mo, <=120/min)
    87 +- `scale`: large platforms, multi-tenant marketplaces, high peak traffic (>=100k/mo, <=1000/min)
    88 +
    89 +Company pages include assumptions so these recommendations stay honest and debuggable.
    90 +

• I’m thinking about how to categorize our information. First, if categories include 'billing' or related terms, it goes under rcm_billing. Next, 'telehealth' or 'virtual' will be labeled telehealth,
  while 'pharmacy' or related terms get the pharmacy_tech tag. For 'price' or 'cost' phrases, we’ll use price_transparency_analytics. If 'provider' is mentioned along with 'directory', it indicates
  provider_directory_data. Additionally, 'insurance' terms will fall under benefits_navigation, otherwise into other_health_it. I’m considering some technical points here too!

  Defining upgrade criteria

  I’m exploring how to determine upgrades based on sources and signals. If we have at least two unique sources or the sources include specific ones like Epic or Cerner, that's a positive signal. I’ll
  look at expected monthly buckets and peak rates using assumptions since real numbers aren’t available. For plan selection, we'll default to a starter plan but can upgrade to growth under various
  conditions, such as certain category signals or app counts. Careful scaling will ensure it’s not too frequent; better to be conservative!

  Defining usage assumptions

  I'm thinking about why we need to outline specific reasons for bullets in our triggers. For example, patient-facing workflows create high read traffic due to searches, maps, and pagination. Multi-
  marketplace EHR integrations indicate multi-tenant usage, and a large catalog of marketplace apps points to a broader customer base. When it comes to estimating API usage, I’ll present assumptions
  for different plans: starter (≤ 10k req/month), growth (10k-100k), and scale (≥ 100k). If the consumer traffic signal is high, I’ll also estimate for patient-facing search!

  Mapping offerings to categories

  I'm focused on how to map our offerings to the appropriate categories. For example, if we're looking at rcm_billing, we’ll cover areas like Plan and Network Intelligence, claims-based pricing, and
  formulary coverage. For telehealth, the relevant areas are provider search and pharmacy search, while pharmacy_tech will include medication lookup. Each category ties into specific offerings like
  benefits_navigation or provider_directory_data as well. I’ll also create a concise bullet list for relevant bundles to keep things organized and manageable!

  Company file template generation

  I’m working on a company file template which includes a snapshot with details from the HealthIT.gov app marketplace dataset. The sections will cover the company's marketplace footprint, app count,
  and example products, among other things. I need to generate up to 1,000 files while ensuring file safety and paths are correct.

  I’ll implement a generator using pseudocode and make sure to validate output files, ensuring they contain necessary sections. I also want to create markers in the summary markdown for easier
  integration later!

     44 +

     66 +def _utc_now_iso() -> str:
     67 +    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat()
     68 +
     69 +
     70 +def _safe_write_text(path: Path, content: str) -> None:
     71 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
     72 +    path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")

     74 +
     75 +def _normalize_name(value: str) -> str:
     76 +    lowered = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", " ", (value or "").strip().lower()).strip()
     77 +    parts = [p for p in lowered.split() if p and p not in _CORP_SUFFIXES]
     78 +    return " ".join(parts)
     79 +
     80 +
     81 +def _slugify(value: str) -> str:
     82 +    lowered = (value or "").strip().lower()
     83 +    lowered = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lowered).strip("-")
     84 +    lowered = re.sub(r"-{2,}", "-", lowered)
     85 +    return lowered or "company"
     86 +
     87 +
     88 +def _short_hash(value: str) -> str:
     89 +    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:8]
     90 +
     91 +
     92 +def _pick_first_non_empty(values: Iterable[str]) -> str | None:
     93 +    for v in values:
     94 +        if v:
     95 +            s = str(v).strip()
     96 +            if s:
     97 +                return s
     98 +    return None
     99 +
    100 +
    101 +def _split_csv_list(value: str) -> list[str]:
    102 +    raw = (value or "").strip()
    103 +    if not raw:
    104 +        return []
    105 +    # Data uses comma-separated lists; normalize whitespace.
    106 +    return [item.strip().lower() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]
    107 +
    108 +
    109 +def _format_bullets(items: list[str], indent: int = 0) -> str:
    110 +    pad = " " * indent
    111 +    return "\n".join(f"{pad}- {item}" for item in items)
    112 +
    113 +
    114 +@dataclass
    115 +class CompanyAgg:
    116 +    name: str
    117 +    normalized_name: str
    118 +    rows: int = 0
    119 +    app_names: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)
    120 +    sources: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)
    121 +    categories: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)
    122 +    users: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)
    123 +    websites: Counter[str] = field(default_factory=Counter)
    124 +    descriptions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    125 +
    126 +    def add_row(self, row: dict[str, str]) -> None:
    127 +        self.rows += 1
    128 +        source = (row.get("source") or "").strip().lower()
    129 +        if source:
    130 +            self.sources[source] += 1
    131 +        cats = _split_csv_list(row.get("categories") or "")
    132 +        for c in cats:
    133 +            self.categories[c] += 1
    134 +        users = _split_csv_list(row.get("users") or "")
    135 +        for u in users:
    136 +            self.users[u] += 1
    137 +        website = (row.get("website") or "").strip()
    138 +        if website:
    139 +            self.websites[website] += 1
    140 +        app_name = _pick_first_non_empty(
    141 +            [
    142 +                (row.get("appName_clean") or "").strip(),
    143 +                (row.get("appName") or "").strip(),
    144 +            ]
    145 +        )
    146 +        if app_name:
    147 +            self.app_names[app_name] += 1
    148 +        desc = (row.get("description") or "").strip()
    149 +        if desc and len(self.descriptions) < 5:
    150 +            self.descriptions.append(desc)
    151 +
    152 +    @property
    153 +    def app_count(self) -> int:
    154 +        return self.rows
    155 +
    156 +    @property
    157 +    def marketplace_count(self) -> int:
    158 +        return len(self.sources)
    159 +
    160 +    def top_sources(self, n: int = 4) -> list[str]:
    161 +        return [k for k, _v in self.sources.most_common(n)]
    162 +
    163 +    def top_categories(self, n: int = 8) -> list[str]:
    164 +        return [k for k, _v in self.categories.most_common(n)]
    165 +
    166 +    def top_apps(self, n: int = 5) -> list[str]:
    167 +        return [k for k, _v in self.app_names.most_common(n)]
    168 +
    169 +    def best_website(self) -> str | None:
    170 +        return _pick_first_non_empty([k for k, _v in self.websites.most_common(5)])
    171 +
    172 +
    173 +def _detect_company_category(agg: CompanyAgg) -> str:
    174 +    cats = set(agg.top_categories(50))
    175 +    users = set(k for k, _v in agg.users.most_common(50))
    176 +    corpus = " ".join(agg.top_categories(20) + agg.top_apps(10) + agg.descriptions).lower()
    177 +
    178 +    def has(*terms: str) -> bool:
    179 +        return any(t in corpus for t in terms)
    180 +
    181 +    if "billing" in cats or has("billing", "rcm", "revenue cycle", "claims", "prior authorization", "copay"):
    182 +        return "rcm_billing"
    183 +    if has("telehealth", "virtual visit", "video visit", "remote visit"):
    184 +        return "telehealth"
    185 +    if "pharmacy" in cats or has("pharmacy", "rx", "prescription", "medication", "drug", "formulary"):
    186 +        return "pharmacy_tech"
    187 +    if has("insurance", "plan", "coverage", "benefit", "deductible", "network"):
    188 +        return "benefits_navigation"
    189 +    if has("price", "cost", "transparency", "estimate", "benchmark"):
    190 +        return "price_transparency_analytics"
    191 +    if has("provider directory", "find a doctor", "provider search", "doctor search", "directory"):
    192 +        return "provider_directory_data"
    193 +    if "patients" in users and has("patient", "experience", "engagement", "check-in", "intake"):
    194 +        return "patient_experience"
    195 +    return "other_health_it"
    196 +
    197 +
    198 +def _detect_signals(agg: CompanyAgg, category: str) -> dict[str, bool]:
    199 +    users = set(k for k, _v in agg.users.most_common(50))
    200 +    top_sources = set(agg.top_sources(10))
    201 +
    202 +    consumer_traffic = "patients" in users or category in {"patient_experience", "telehealth", "pharmacy_tech"}
    203 +    enterprise = agg.marketplace_count >= 2 or bool(top_sources.intersection({"epic", "cerner", "athena"}))
    204 +
    205 +    return {"consumer_traffic_signal": consumer_traffic, "enterprise_signal": enterprise}
    206 +
    207 +
    208 +def _recommended_plan(agg: CompanyAgg, category: str) -> tuple[str, list[str], list[str]]:
    209 +    signals = _detect_signals(agg, category)
    210 +    consumer = signals["consumer_traffic_signal"]
    211 +    enterprise = signals["enterprise_signal"]
    212 +
    213 +    plan = "starter"
    214 +    reasons: list[str] = []
    215 +    assumptions: list[str] = []
    216 +
    217 +    # Conservative: only move up when signals are clear.
    218 +    if consumer or agg.app_count >= 10 or category in {"price_transparency_analytics", "provider_directory_data"}:
    219 +        plan = "growth"
    220 +    if (enterprise and agg.app_count >= 20) or agg.app_count >= 50:
    221 +        plan = "scale"
    222 +
    223 +    if plan == "starter":
    224 +        reasons = [
    225 +            "Pilot/internal integration is the most likely initial usage profile.",
    226 +            "Starter supports <=10k requests/month with a 40/min peak window.",
    227 +        ]
    228 +        assumptions = [
    229 +            "Assume <=10k requests/month during early integration.",
    230 +            "Assume peak <=40 requests/minute (burst-friendly but not high-traffic consumer search).",
    231 +        ]
    232 +    elif plan == "growth":
    233 +        reasons = [
    234 +            "Signals suggest production SaaS usage (patient-facing workflows and/or higher catalog footprint).",
    235 +            "Growth supports 10k-100k requests/month with a 120/min peak window.",
    236 +        ]
    237 +        if consumer:
    238 +            reasons.insert(
    239 +                0, "Patient-facing search and navigation flows tend to generate high read traffic (search + pagination)."
    240 +            )
    241 +        if agg.app_count >= 10:
    242 +            reasons.append("Larger marketplace footprint suggests broader customer distribution and higher call volume.")
    243 +        assumptions = [
    244 +            "Assume 10k-100k requests/month for production usage (autocomplete + results + pagination).",
    245 +            "Assume peak <=120 requests/minute during business-hour bursts.",
    246 +        ]
    247 +    else:
    248 +        reasons = [
    249 +            "Enterprise integration signals plus large footprint suggest multi-tenant or high-traffic usage.",
    250 +            "Scale supports up to 1M requests/month with a 1000/min peak window.",
    251 +        ]
    252 +        if enterprise:
    253 +            reasons.insert(
    254 +                0, "Multi-marketplace EHR integrations suggest multi-tenant distribution and heavier aggregate traffic."
    255 +            )
    256 +        assumptions = [
    257 +            "Assume >=100k requests/month when embedded across many tenants or high-traffic consumer experiences.",
    258 +            "Assume peak bursts up to 1000 requests/minute.",
    259 +        ]
    260 +
    261 +    return plan, reasons, assumptions
    262 +
    263 +
    264 +def _areas_for_category(category: str) -> list[str]:
    265 +    mapping: dict[str, list[str]] = {
    266 +        "rcm_billing": ["Plan and Network Intelligence", "Claims-Based Pricing and Benchmarks", "Formulary and Drug Coverage"],
    267 +        "telehealth": ["Provider and Facility Search", "Plan and Network Intelligence", "Pharmacy Search"],
    268 +        "pharmacy_tech": ["Pharmacy Search", "Formulary and Drug Coverage", "Medication Lookup"],
    269 +        "benefits_navigation": ["Plan and Network Intelligence", "Formulary and Drug Coverage", "Provider and Facility Search"],
    270 +        "provider_directory_data": ["Provider and Facility Search", "Ratings (Cost Level + Quality Proxy)"],
    271 +        "price_transparency_analytics": ["Claims-Based Pricing and Benchmarks", "Ratings (Cost Level + Quality Proxy)"],
    272 +        "patient_experience": ["Provider and Facility Search", "Pharmacy Search", "Plan and Network Intelligence"],
    273 +        "other_health_it": ["Provider and Facility Search", "Plan and Network Intelligence"],
    274 +    }
    275 +    return mapping.get(category, mapping["other_health_it"])
    276 +

    278 +def _render_company_page(agg: CompanyAgg, source_label: str, fetched_at_iso: str) -> str:
    279 +    category = _detect_company_category(agg)
    280 +    plan, why, assumptions = _recommended_plan(agg, category)
    281 +
    282 +    top_sources = ", ".join(agg.top_sources(4)) if agg.sources else "unknown"
    283 +    top_categories = ", ".join(agg.top_categories(8)) if agg.categories else "unknown"
