    381 +
    382 +def _load_companies_from_csv(path: Path) -> dict[str, CompanyAgg]:
    383 +    companies: dict[str, CompanyAgg] = {}
    384 +    with path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
    385 +        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    386 +        for row in reader:
    387 +            raw_name = (row.get("devName_clean") or row.get("devName") or "").strip()
    388 +            if not raw_name:
    389 +                continue
    390 +            normalized = _normalize_name(raw_name)
    391 +            if not normalized:
    392 +                continue
    393 +            agg = companies.get(normalized)
    394 +            if agg is None:
    395 +                companies[normalized] = CompanyAgg(name=raw_name, normalized_name=normalized)
    396 +                agg = companies[normalized]
    397 +            agg.add_row({k: (v or "") for k, v in row.items()})
    398 +    return companies
    399 +
    400 +
    401 +def _assign_unique_slugs(aggs: list[CompanyAgg]) -> dict[str, str]:
    402 +    used: set[str] = set()
    403 +    slugs: dict[str, str] = {}
    404 +    for agg in aggs:
    405 +        base = _slugify(agg.name)
    406 +        slug = base
    407 +        if slug in used:
    408 +            slug = f"{base}-{_short_hash(agg.normalized_name)}"
    409 +        used.add(slug)
    410 +        slugs[agg.normalized_name] = slug
    411 +    return slugs
    412 +
    413 +
    414 +def fetch_dataset(force: bool = False) -> Path:
    415 +    cache_dir = _cache_dir()
    416 +    cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    417 +    dest = cache_dir / DATASET_NAME
    418 +    if dest.exists() and not force:
    419 +        return dest
    420 +
    421 +    with urlopen(DATASET_URL) as resp:
    422 +        data = resp.read()
    423 +    dest.write_bytes(data)
    424 +    return dest
    425 +
    426 +
    427 +def _render_summary_section(aggs: list[CompanyAgg]) -> str:
    428 +    by_category = Counter(_detect_company_category(a) for a in aggs)
    429 +    plan_counts = Counter(_recommended_plan(a, _detect_company_category(a))[0] for a in aggs)
    430 +
    431 +    lines: list[str] = []
    432 +    lines.append("## Generated Summary")
    433 +    lines.append("")
    434 +    lines.append(f"- Companies: {len(aggs)}")
    435 +    lines.append(f"- Generated at: {_utc_now_iso()}")
    436 +    lines.append("")
    437 +    lines.append("### Category Counts")
    438 +    for k, v in by_category.most_common():
    439 +        lines.append(f"- `{k}`: {v}")
    440 +    lines.append("")
    441 +    lines.append("### Recommended Plan Counts")
    442 +    for k, v in plan_counts.most_common():
    443 +        lines.append(f"- `{k}`: {v}")
    444 +    lines.append("")
    445 +    lines.append("### Default Upgrade Triggers (Conservative)")
    446 +    lines.append("- `starter` -> `growth`: patient-facing signal, or >=10 app listings, or cost/directory category")
    447 +    lines.append("- `growth` -> `scale`: enterprise signal + >=20 app listings, or >=50 app listings")
    448 +    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip()
    449 +
    450 +
    451 +def _replace_between_markers(text: str, start: str, end: str, replacement: str) -> str:
    452 +    if start not in text or end not in text:
    453 +        return text.rstrip() + "\n\n" + start + "\n" + replacement + "\n" + end + "\n"
    454 +    pre, rest = text.split(start, 1)
    455 +    _mid, post = rest.split(end, 1)
    456 +    return pre.rstrip() + "\n\n" + start + "\n" + replacement + "\n" + end + post
    457 +
    458 +
    459 +def build_docs(min_companies: int = 1000, limit: int | None = None, offline: bool = False) -> int:
    460 +    if offline:
    461 +        csv_path = _fixture_csv_path()
    462 +        if not csv_path.exists():
    463 +            raise SystemExit(f"Missing fixture CSV: {csv_path}")
    464 +        fetched_at = "fixture"
    465 +        source_label = "HealthIT.gov app marketplace dataset (fixture)"
    466 +    else:
    467 +        csv_path = fetch_dataset(force=False)
    468 +        fetched_at = _utc_now_iso()
    469 +        source_label = (
    470 +            "HealthIT.gov app marketplace dataset (2019-2025): "
    471 +            "https://www.healthit.gov/data/data-sets/dataset-apps-and-software-applications-discovered-app-marketplaces"
    472 +        )
    473 +
    474 +    companies = _load_companies_from_csv(csv_path)
    475 +    aggs = sorted(companies.values(), key=lambda a: (-a.app_count, a.name.lower()))
    476 +    if limit is not None:
    477 +        aggs = aggs[: max(1, int(limit))]
    478 +    else:
    479 +        aggs = aggs[: max(min_companies, 1000)]
    480 +
    481 +    if len(aggs) < min_companies:
    482 +        raise SystemExit(f"Only {len(aggs)} companies available; need >= {min_companies}.")
    483 +
    484 +    slugs = _assign_unique_slugs(aggs)
    485 +    out_dir = _companies_dir()
    486 +    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    487 +
    488 +    for agg in aggs:
    489 +        slug = slugs[agg.normalized_name]
    490 +        content = _render_company_page(agg, source_label=source_label, fetched_at_iso=fetched_at)
    491 +        _safe_write_text(out_dir / f"{slug}.md", content)
    492 +
    493 +    # Update generated summary block in potential_clients.md (top-level index)
    494 +    readme_path = _readme_path()
    495 +    if readme_path.exists():
    496 +        text = readme_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    497 +    else:
    498 +        text = ""
    499 +    summary = _render_summary_section(aggs)
    500 +    updated = _replace_between_markers(text, GENERATED_MARKER_START, GENERATED_MARKER_END, summary)
    501 +    _safe_write_text(readme_path, updated)
    502 +
    503 +    return len(aggs)
    504 +
    505 +
    506 +def validate_docs(min_companies: int = 1000) -> None:
    507 +    out_dir = _companies_dir()
    508 +    if not out_dir.exists():
    509 +        raise SystemExit(f"Missing output directory: {out_dir}")
    510 +    pages = sorted(p for p in out_dir.glob("*.md") if p.name.lower() != "readme.md")
    511 +    if len(pages) < min_companies:
    512 +        raise SystemExit(f"Only {len(pages)} company pages found; need >= {min_companies}.")
    513 +
    514 +    required_headers = [
    515 +        "## Snapshot",
    516 +        "## Why They Might Buy",
    517 +        "## Relevant Offerings",
    518 +        "## Fast Builds (1-3 weeks)",
    519 +        "## Recommended Plan",
    520 +        "## Risks / Unknowns",
    521 +    ]
    522 +    for path in pages[:50]:
    523 +        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
    524 +        for header in required_headers:
    525 +            if header not in text:
    526 +                raise SystemExit(f"Missing header {header!r} in {path}")
    527 +
    528 +
    529 +def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    530 +    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    531 +        description="Generate potential client docs (1000+ companies) from public datasets.",
    532 +        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
    533 +        epilog=textwrap.dedent(
    534 +            \"\"\"\n+            Examples:\n+              python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py fetch\n+              python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py build --min 1000\n+
                     python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py validate --min 1000\n+            \"\"\"\n+        ).strip(),
    535 +    )
    536 +    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
    537 +
    538 +    p_fetch = sub.add_parser("fetch", help="Download/update cached datasets")
    539 +    p_fetch.add_argument("--force", action="store_true", help="Re-download even if cached file exists")
    540 +
    541 +    p_build = sub.add_parser("build", help="Generate markdown docs")
    542 +    p_build.add_argument("--min", type=int, default=1000, dest="min_companies", help="Minimum companies to generate")
    543 +    p_build.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None, help="Generate only N companies (debug)")
    544 +    p_build.add_argument("--offline", action="store_true", help="Use fixture dataset (no network)")
    545 +
    546 +    p_validate = sub.add_parser("validate", help="Validate output docs")
    547 +    p_validate.add_argument("--min", type=int, default=1000, dest="min_companies", help="Minimum company pages")
    548 +
    549 +    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    550 +
    551 +    if args.cmd == "fetch":
    552 +        path = fetch_dataset(force=bool(args.force))
    553 +        print(str(path))
    554 +        return 0
    555 +
    556 +    if args.cmd == "build":
    557 +        count = build_docs(
    558 +            min_companies=int(args.min_companies),
    559 +            limit=args.limit,
    560 +            offline=bool(args.offline),
    561 +        )
    562 +        print(f"generated_companies={count}")
    563 +        return 0
    564 +
    565 +    if args.cmd == "validate":
    566 +        validate_docs(min_companies=int(args.min_companies))
    567 +        print("ok")
    568 +        return 0
    569 +
    570 +    raise SystemExit("unknown command")
    571 +
    572 +
    573 +if __name__ == "__main__":
    574 +    raise SystemExit(main())
    575 +

• I need to confirm that plan recommendations and notes are appearing as expected, and they are. However, I've noticed a capitalization issue with the display names that we could improve. The user
  didn't specifically ask for this, but they'd likely appreciate a nicer presentation. Using the original name instead of the lower-case version could enhance quality. It might be significant work to
  regenerate 1000 pages, but it's possible, and it aligns with our goal for improvement. So, should I go ahead and fix this?

  Addressing slug changes

  If we change the aggregation name to devName, it’ll also affect the slugs generated by slugify. This means we’d be renaming files, which requires cleaning up old ones and could potentially break
  links. Stability is essential for these internal documents. Instead of changing slugs, I think we can keep using the normalized name for stability while improving the display title separately in
  headers. This way, we can compute the display title without renaming any files, avoiding any potential mess.

  Optimizing display names

  In the _load_companies_from_csv function, I can set agg.name to use devName (original) with the proper case, or title-case as a fallback. This might lead to variations across rows, so I could track
  devName variants and select the most frequent one. Instead, I could update a display_names counter that uses row.get("devName") when available. I’ll ensure the best display name includes an uppercase
  letter. However, modifying slug generation could lead to issues with existing file names, so I should keep that stable.

  Maintaining slug stability

  priority right now.

  Improving name formatting

  approach. I’ll implement a function to handle this: if the name is lowercase, return it in title case.

  For names like "1up health," it would convert nicely with .title(). I'll ensure to keep already uppercase names as they are and handle acronyms cautiously. The current agg.name already comes from

    126 +def _parse_bool(value: object) -> bool | None:
    127 +    if value is None:
    128 +        return None
    129 +    raw = str(value).strip().lower()
    130 +    if not raw:
    131 +        return None
    132 +    if raw in {"1", "true", "t", "yes", "y"}:
    133 +        return True
    134 +    if raw in {"0", "false", "f", "no", "n"}:
    135 +        return False
    136 +    return None

    262 +def _detect_go_to_market(agg: CompanyAgg) -> str:
    263 +    if agg.curated_go_to_market:
    264 +        return agg.curated_go_to_market
    265 +
    266 +    users = set(k for k, _v in agg.users.most_common(50))
    267 +    consumer_tags = {"patients", "members", "consumers"}
    268 +    business_tags = {"clinicians", "providers", "payers", "plans", "employers"}
    269 +    has_consumer = bool(users.intersection(consumer_tags))
    270 +    has_business = bool(users.intersection(business_tags))
    271 +    if has_consumer and has_business:
    272 +        return "b2b2c"
    273 +    if has_consumer:
    274 +        return "b2c"
    275 +    return "b2b"
    276 +
    277 +
    278 +def _detect_themes(agg: CompanyAgg) -> list[str]:
    279 +    themes: set[str] = set(agg.curated_themes)
    280 +    corpus = " ".join(agg.top_categories(40) + agg.top_apps(20) + agg.descriptions).lower()
    281 +
    282 +    def has(*terms: str) -> bool:
    283 +        return any(t in corpus for t in terms)
    284 +
    285 +    # Requested high-signal themes.
    286 +    if has("insurance", "plan", "coverage", "network", "enrollment", "broker", "premium", "deductible"):
    287 +        themes.add("insurance")
    288 +    if has("navigation", "concierge", "care management", "care coordination", "advocacy", "member support"):
    289 +        themes.add("assistance")
    290 +    if has("discount", "coupon", "savings", "affordability", "copay", "cash price"):
    291 +        themes.add("discounts")
    292 +    if has("compliance", "hipaa", "privacy", "consent", "audit", "governance", "security", "fhir", "hl7", "hie"):
    293 +        themes.add("governance")
    294 +
    295 +    # Secondary themes useful for our current API surface.

    297 +        themes.add("pharmacy")
    298 +    if has("price", "cost", "transparency", "estimate", "benchmark"):
    299 +        themes.add("cost")
    300 +
    301 +    return sorted(themes)

    562 +def _load_curated_companies(path: Path) -> dict[str, dict[str, object]]:
    563 +    if not path.exists():
    564 +        return {}
    565 +    curated: dict[str, dict[str, object]] = {}
    566 +    with path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
    567 +        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    568 +        for row in reader:
    569 +            raw_name = (row.get("name") or row.get("company") or "").strip()
    570 +            if not raw_name:
    571 +                continue
    572 +            normalized = _normalize_name(raw_name)
    573 +            if not normalized:
    574 +                continue
    575 +            curated[normalized] = {k: (v if v is not None else "") for k, v in row.items()}
    576 +    return curated

    579 +def _merge_curated(companies: dict[str, CompanyAgg], curated: dict[str, dict[str, object]]) -> dict[str, CompanyAgg]:

    582 +        if existing is None:
    583 +            existing = CompanyAgg(name=str(row.get("name") or "").strip() or normalized, normalized_name=normalized)
    584 +            companies[normalized] = existing
    585 +        existing.apply_curated(row)
    586 +    return companies

    718 +def _render_b2b2c_index(aggs: list[CompanyAgg], slugs: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    719 +    themes = ["insurance", "assistance", "discounts", "governance"]
    720 +    theme_titles = {
    721 +        "insurance": "Insurance and Benefits",
    722 +        "assistance": "Care Navigation and Assistance",
    723 +        "discounts": "Discounts and Savings",
    724 +        "governance": "Governance and Compliance",
    725 +    }
    726 +
    727 +    b2b2c = [a for a in aggs if _detect_go_to_market(a) == "b2b2c"]
    728 +    b2b2c_sorted = sorted(b2b2c, key=lambda a: a.name.lower())
    729 +
    730 +    by_theme: dict[str, list[CompanyAgg]] = {t: [] for t in themes}
    731 +    for agg in b2b2c_sorted:
    732 +        agg_themes = set(_detect_themes(agg))
    733 +        for t in themes:
    734 +            if t in agg_themes:
    735 +                by_theme[t].append(agg)
    736 +
    737 +    lines: list[str] = []
    738 +    lines.append("# B2B2C Companies (US) - Outreach Index\n")
    739 +    lines.append(
    740 +        "This index focuses on organizations that operate B2B2C (sold to employers/payers/providers but used by members/patients),"
    741 +        " grouped by themes relevant to HealthPorta capabilities."
    742 +    )
    743 +    lines.append("")
    744 +    lines.append(f"- Generated at: {_utc_now_iso()}")
    745 +    lines.append(f"- Companies in this index (B2B2C): {len(b2b2c_sorted)}")
    746 +    lines.append("")
    747 +    lines.append("## Themes")
    748 +    for t in themes:
    749 +        lines.append(f"- {theme_titles[t]}: {len(by_theme[t])}")
    750 +    lines.append("")
    751 +
    752 +    for t in themes:
    753 +        lines.append(f"## {theme_titles[t]}")
    754 +        lines.append("")
    755 +        if not by_theme[t]:
    756 +            lines.append("- (none)")
    757 +            lines.append("")
    758 +            continue
    759 +        for agg in by_theme[t]:
    760 +            slug = slugs.get(agg.normalized_name, _slugify(agg.name))
    761 +            website = agg.best_website()
    762 +            suffix = f" ({website})" if website else ""
    763 +            lines.append(f"- [{_pretty_company_name(agg.name)}](companies/{slug}.md){suffix}")
    764 +        lines.append("")
    765 +
    766 +    return "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n"

• Added api-app/specs/potential_clients/process.md (+144 -0)
      1 +# Client Discovery Process (Prospect Library)
      2 +
      3 +This doc describes how we generate and maintain the `specs/potential_clients/` prospect library, and the workflow we follow to turn it into outreach.
      4 +
      5 +## Goals
      6 +
      7 +- Keep an auditable, repeatable list of US healthcare-market prospects.
      8 +- Map each prospect to concrete HealthPorta capabilities (what we ship today) and fast builds (what we can build quickly).
      9 +- Produce a conservative plan recommendation (`starter|growth|scale`) with explicit assumptions.
     10 +- Avoid scraping PII or collecting contact data.
     11 +
     12 +## Inputs (Sources)
     13 +
     14 +1. HealthIT.gov marketplace dataset (baseline)
     15 +
     16 +- Used to generate the initial 1000+ set.
     17 +- Fetched by the generator and cached locally.
     18 +- Pros: big and public, includes coarse categories/users and example app listings.
     19 +- Cons: incomplete for many B2B2C and enterprise orgs (pharma, payers, PBMs, large navigation vendors).
     20 +
     21 +2. Curated B2B2C list (augmentation)
     22 +
     23 +- File: `scripts/potential_clients/curated/us_b2b2c_healthcare_companies.csv`
     24 +- Purpose: add high-signal companies that are missing or underrepresented in HealthIT.gov listings, especially:
     25 +  - governance/compliance,
     26 +  - assistance/navigation,
     27 +  - discounts/savings,
     28 +  - insurance/benefits usage,
     29 +  - pharma/life sciences and condition-management programs.
     30 +
     31 +## Generation Workflow (Deterministic)
     32 +
     33 +Source of truth:
     34 +- Generator: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py`
     35 +- Output: `specs/potential_clients/companies/*.md` plus indexes
     36 +
     37 +Steps:
     38 +
     39 +1. Fetch + cache dataset
     40 +
     41 +- Generator downloads the HealthIT.gov CSV into `scripts/potential_clients/.cache/` (gitignored).
     42 +
     43 +2. Normalize + aggregate companies
     44 +
     45 +- Normalize developer names to dedupe (strip punctuation and corporate suffixes like Inc/LLC).
     46 +- Aggregate rows per normalized company name:
     47 +  - marketplaces (Epic/Cerner/etc.)
     48 +  - app names
     49 +  - categories/users
     50 +  - website candidates
     51 +
     52 +3. Merge curated entries
     53 +
     54 +- Curated entries can:
     55 +  - add a company absent from HealthIT.gov,
     56 +  - override category/go-to-market,
     57 +  - add themes and notes,
     58 +  - force enterprise/consumer traffic signals for plan recommendation.
     59 +
     60 +4. Select output set
     61 +
     62 +- Baseline: take top N companies by marketplace row count.
     63 +- Always include curated entries (even if they have 0 marketplace rows).
     64 +
     65 +5. Generate pages and indexes
     66 +
     67 +- One company page per slug in `specs/potential_clients/companies/`.
     68 +- Update the generated summary block in `specs/potential_clients.md`.
     69 +- Generate a B2B2C outreach index grouped by themes in `specs/potential_clients/b2b2c.md`.
     70 +
     71 +6. Validate output
     72 +
     73 +- Ensure >= N pages exist and each has required sections.
     74 +
     75 +## Commands
     76 +
     77 +Regenerate:
     78 +```bash
     79 +cd api-app
     80 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py build --min 1000
     81 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py validate --min 1000
     82 +```
     83 +
     84 +Offline (fixture-based) generation for testing:
     85 +```bash
     86 +cd api-app
     87 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py build --min 2 --offline
     88 +```
     89 +
     90 +## Manual Prospecting Workflow (How We Use It)
     91 +
     92 +1. Pick a target segment
     93 +
     94 +Examples:
     95 +- B2B2C navigation vendors (employer/payer sold, member used).
     96 +- Discount/savings apps that need better coverage-aware routing.
     97 +- Pharma and life sciences orgs that need market-access intelligence and prescriber benchmarks.
     98 +- Condition-management programs (Virta/Omada/Hinge-style) that need network + coverage workflows.
     99 +
    100 +2. Use the right index
    101 +
    102 +- B2B2C themed list: `specs/potential_clients/b2b2c.md`
    103 +- Full company directory: `specs/potential_clients/companies/`
    104 +
    105 +3. Pull the company page and sanity-check assumptions
    106 +
    107 +- Confirm:
    108 +  - their go-to-market model,
    109 +  - whether the pitch maps to their product,
    110 +  - whether the recommended plan tier makes sense for likely call volume.
    111 +
    112 +4. Convert to a concrete proposal
    113 +
    114 +- Prefer 1-2 "Fast Builds" from the page and map to:
    115 +  - endpoint(s),
    116 +  - expected request patterns,
    117 +  - data/provenance notes (especially for Medicare-derived benchmarks).
    118 +
    119 +## How To Add More Companies (Curated)
    120 +
    121 +Add a row to:
    122 +- `scripts/potential_clients/curated/us_b2b2c_healthcare_companies.csv`
    123 +
    124 +Columns (current):
    125 +- `name` (required)
    126 +- `website` (recommended)
    127 +- `category` (optional but recommended for strong categorization)
    128 +  - examples: `pharma_life_sciences`, `condition_management`, `assistance_navigation`, `discounts_savings`
    129 +- `go_to_market` (`b2b`, `b2c`, or `b2b2c`)
    130 +- `themes` (comma-separated; recommended)
    131 +  - common: `insurance`, `assistance`, `discounts`, `governance`, `pharmacy`, `cost`
    132 +- `notes` (short 1-liner)
    133 +- `enterprise_signal` / `consumer_traffic_signal` (optional booleans to steer plan heuristics)
    134 +
    135 +Then run `build` + `validate` again.
    136 +
    137 +## Heuristics (Where To Change Behavior)
    138 +
    139 +- Category detection: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py` -> `_detect_company_category()`
    140 +- Theme detection: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py` -> `_detect_themes()`
    141 +- Go-to-market inference: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py` -> `_detect_go_to_market()`
    142 +- Plan recommendation: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py` -> `_recommended_plan()`
    143 +- Offerings mapping: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py` -> `_areas_for_category()`
    144 +

• Edited api-app/scripts/potential_clients/generate.py (+43 -0)
    178
    179 +def _parse_market_cap(value: object) -> float | None:
    180 +    raw = str(value or "").strip()
    181 +    if not raw:
    182 +        return None
    183 +    # Nasdaq returns marketCap as a numeric string (e.g. "34331075139.00").
    184 +    raw = raw.replace(",", "").replace("$", "")
    185 +    try:
    186 +        return float(raw)
    187 +    except ValueError:
    188 +        return None
    189 +
    190 +
    191 +def _market_cap_bucket(market_cap_usd: float | None) -> str | None:
    192 +    if market_cap_usd is None:
    193 +        return None
    194 +    # Common market-cap buckets (approximate):
    195 +    # micro < 300M, small < 2B, mid < 10B, large < 200B, mega >= 200B.
    196 +    if market_cap_usd < 300_000_000:
    197 +        return "micro"
    198 +    if market_cap_usd < 2_000_000_000:
    199 +        return "small"
    200 +    if market_cap_usd < 10_000_000_000:
    201 +        return "mid"
    202 +    if market_cap_usd < 200_000_000_000:
    203 +        return "large"
    204 +    return "mega"
    205 +
    206 +
    207 +def _format_usd_compact(value: float | None) -> str | None:
    208 +    if value is None:
    209 +        return None
    210 +    abs_v = abs(value)
    211 +    if abs_v >= 1_000_000_000_000:
    212 +        return f"${value/1_000_000_000_000:.1f}T"
    213 +    if abs_v >= 1_000_000_000:
    214 +        return f"${value/1_000_000_000:.1f}B"
    215 +    if abs_v >= 1_000_000:
    216 +        return f"${value/1_000_000:.1f}M"
    217 +    if abs_v >= 1_000:
    218 +        return f"${value/1_000:.1f}K"
    219 +    return f"${value:.0f}"
    220 +
    221 +
    222  def _format_bullets(items: list[str], indent: int = 0) -> str:

    859 +def _load_nasdaq_screener_rows(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    860 +    if not path.exists():
    861 +        return []
    862 +    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore"))
    863 +    rows = data.get("data", {}).get("rows") or []
    864 +    if not isinstance(rows, list):
    865 +        return []
    866 +    return [r for r in rows if isinstance(r, dict)]
    867 +
    868 +
    869 +def _merge_public_equities(companies: dict[str, CompanyAgg], rows: list[dict[str, object]]) -> dict[str, CompanyAgg]:
    870 +    for row in rows:
    871 +        # Focus on US-listed healthcare companies; broad enough for pharma + medtech.
    872 +        if str(row.get("sector") or "").strip() != "Health Care":
    873 +            continue
    874 +        if str(row.get("country") or "").strip() != "United States":
    875 +            continue
    876 +
    877 +        raw_name = _clean_public_equity_name(str(row.get("name") or "").strip())
    878 +        if not raw_name:
    879 +            continue
    880 +        normalized = _normalize_name(raw_name)
    881 +        if not normalized:
    882 +            continue
    883 +        existing = companies.get(normalized)
    884 +        if existing is None:
    885 +            existing = CompanyAgg(name=raw_name, normalized_name=normalized)
    886 +            companies[normalized] = existing
    887 +        existing.apply_public_equity(row)
    888 +    return companies
    889 +

    894 +    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore"))
    895 +    if not isinstance(data, list):
    896 +        return []
    897 +    return [r for r in data if isinstance(r, dict)]

    900 +def _yc_is_us_company(row: dict[str, object]) -> bool:
    901 +    loc = str(row.get("all_locations") or "").lower()
    902 +    return (" usa" in loc) or ("united states" in loc)

    904 +
    905 +def _yc_is_health_relevant(row: dict[str, object]) -> bool:
    906 +    industries = [str(x or "").lower().strip() for x in (row.get("industries") or [])]
    907 +    tags = [str(x or "").lower().strip() for x in (row.get("tags") or [])]
    908 +    corpus = " ".join(
    909 +        industries
    910 +        + tags
    911 +        + [
    912 +            str(row.get("industry") or ""),
    913 +            str(row.get("subindustry") or ""),
    914 +            str(row.get("one_liner") or ""),
    915 +        ]
    916 +    ).lower()
    917 +
    918 +    # Wide net: medical/pharma startups + AI healthcare.
    919 +    return bool(
    920 +        re.search(
    921 +            r"\\b(health|medical|clinic|patient|provider|hospital|biotech|pharma|drug|therap|diagnostic|clinical)\\b",
    922 +            corpus,
    923 +        )
    924 +    )
    925 +

    936 +            continue

    944 +        existing.apply_yc_company(row)
    945 +    return companies

  and 3) how they want to prioritize different categories, like growth or pharma. I’ll recommend some strategies while keeping it all straightforward!

  mechanisms. Lastly, I’ll update documentation and emphasize repo privacy due to sensitive information.

• Proposed Plan


  # Outreach Batching + Per-Company Email/Contacts Pipeline (2976+ Prospects)

  ## Summary

  Extend the existing api-app/specs/potential_clients/ prospect library into an outreach system that:

  - prioritizes prospects and splits them into batches of 100,
  - defines segment tactics (who to contact, messaging angles, objections),
  - generates a CRM-ready CSV with contacts (names + emails) and a per-company email draft,
  - supports multi-source contact enrichment (Apollo + Clearbit + Hunter + website fallback),
  - keeps the whole process repeatable and spec’d.

  Success criteria:

  - We can run one command and produce:
      - a committed CSV covering all companies (current target: --full run generates 2976),
      - batch notes by 100 with tactics + top targets,
      - deterministic scoring and reproducible outputs (same inputs -> same order).

  ## Scope and Constraints

  In scope:

  - Use the existing merged prospect universe from scripts/potential_clients/generate.py (HealthIT + curated + Nasdaq + YC).
  - Produce outreach artifacts and document the process.
  - “Growth/Scale first” prioritized for Batch 1–3 (first 300).

  Out of scope (explicitly):

  - LinkedIn scraping, social scraping, or any method that violates site ToS.
  - Storing phone numbers or personal addresses.

  Privacy/security note (explicit): you requested committing the full CSV including personal names/emails. This is feasible but increases repo sensitivity. Plan includes safeguards and documentation.

  ———

  ## Phase 1: Define Outputs (Files + Schemas)

  ### 1) CRM export (committed)

  Create:

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv (committed)

  CSV columns (stable schema):

  - company_slug
  - company_name
  - category
  - go_to_market
  - themes (comma-separated)
  - recommended_plan
  - priority_score (int)
  - priority_reason (short string: e.g. growth+b2b2c+ai)
  - batch_id (1-based, size=100)
  - website
  - sources (comma-separated: healthit,nasdaq,yc,curated)
  - healthit_app_listings (int)
  - nasdaq_tickers (comma-separated)
  - market_cap_usd (float or empty)
  - market_cap_bucket (micro|small|mid|large|mega|)
  - yc_batch
  - yc_locations
  - target_persona (e.g. VP Partnerships, Market Access, CTO)
  - contact_1_name
  - contact_1_title
  - contact_1_email
  - contact_1_source (e.g. apollo, clearbit, hunter, website)
  - contact_1_source_url
  - contact_1_confidence (high|med|low)
  - Repeat for contact_2_*, contact_3_* (same fields)
  - email_subject
  - email_body (single cell, \n escaped)
  - tactics_notes (1–2 sentence custom tactics snippet)

  ### 2) Batch notes (committed)

  Create:

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/batch-0001.md … batch-00NN.md

  Each batch doc includes:

  - segment summary counts (category/go_to_market/themes)
  - “who to contact first” playbook for the dominant segments in the batch
  - top 10 “highest priority” highlighted
  - a table listing 100 companies:
      - name, category, plan, score, top persona, top contact, email subject

  ### 3) Outreach README (committed)

  Create:

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/README.md

  Includes:

  - command to generate
  - required env vars (optional)
  - enrichment behavior and fallbacks
  - compliance/safety rules
  - how to import CSV into a CRM

  ———

  ## Phase 2: Add a New Outreach Generator (Code)

  ### New script/module

  Add a new script (do not overload generate.py further):

  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py

  Commands:

  1. python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py build --full
      - loads the same merged universe as generate.py does (HealthIT + curated + Nasdaq + YC)
      - computes scoring + batches
      - enriches contacts (multi-source)
      - writes:
          - specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv
          - specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/*.md
  2. python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py build --full --no-enrich
      - skips contact calls, fills persona-only + website contact URLs
  3. python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py validate
      - checks CSV schema, counts, required fields, batch sizing

  Implementation approach:

  - Import CompanyAgg and merge helpers from scripts.potential_clients.generate (single source of truth for the company universe).
  - Keep outreach logic separate (scoring/templates/enrichment/export).

  ———

  ## Phase 3: Prioritization (Scoring + Batch Ordering)

  ### Priority score formula (decision complete)

  Compute priority_score as integer:

  Base weights:

  - plan weight:
      - scale: +100
      - growth: +60
      - starter: +20

  Go-to-market:

  - b2b2c: +20
  - b2c: +10
  - b2b: +0

  Themes:

  - ai: +15
  - insurance: +10
  - pharmacy: +10
  - cost: +10
  - governance: +5
  - assistance: +5
  - discounts: +5

  Pharma focus (minor/medium pharma “new sales” emphasis):

  - if category == pharma_life_sciences and market_cap_bucket:
      - micro: +20
      - small: +15
      - mid: +10
      - large: +5
      - mega: +0

  Data completeness:

  - has website: +5
  - has at least 1 enriched contact with med/high confidence: +5 (computed after enrichment; if --no-enrich, skip)

  Ordering:

  - Primary sort: priority_score desc
  - Tie-breakers:
      - recommended_plan (scale > growth > starter)
      - go_to_market (b2b2c > b2c > b2b)
      - company_name asc

  Batch assignment:

  - batch_id = (rank // 100) + 1

  Batch 1–3 focus (your requirement):

  - Filter to recommended_plan in (growth, scale) first, then sort by score.
  - After filling first 300 slots, append remaining companies sorted by score.

  ———

  ## Phase 4: Tactics (Segment Playbooks)

  Create a deterministic mapping: category + themes + go_to_market -> persona targets + angles.

  ### Persona targets per segment (examples, minimum 3 per company)

  - pharma_life_sciences:
      - Director/VP Market Access
      - Commercial Analytics / Business Insights lead
      - Patient Services / Access & Affordability lead
      - Partnerships / Digital Health lead
  - condition_management (Virta-like):
      - VP Partnerships (Payers/Employers)
      - VP Product / PM for Navigation
      - Head of Data / Analytics
  - benefits_navigation / assistance_navigation:
      - VP Product
      - Partnerships/BD
      - Head of Network / Provider Strategy
  - rcm_billing:
      - VP Revenue Cycle
      - Head of Product Integrations
      - Partnerships/BD
  - pharmacy_tech / discounts_savings:
      - Head of Pharmacy Partnerships
      - PM for Coverage/Access
      - Data/Analytics lead
  - AI theme overlay (any category with ai):
      - CTO / VP Engineering
      - Head of Data
      - Applied AI lead / ML Engineering manager

  Each batch markdown includes:

  - top 3 personas for dominant segments
  - 3 message angles
  - 3 likely objections + short answers
  - one suggested “fast build” offer to anchor the call

  ———

  ## Phase 5: Contact Enrichment (Names + Emails + Evidence)

  ### Sources and order (your preference: all 3 + website checks)

  For each company with a website domain:

  1. Apollo (if key configured)
  2. Clearbit (if key configured)
  3. Hunter.io (if key configured)
  4. Website fallback:
      - fetch /{contact, about, team, leadership, press} best-effort
      - extract:
          - mailto: emails
          - visible emails
          - leadership names + titles (public text)
      - store source URL(s)

  ### API keys + env vars (optional)

  - APOLLO_API_KEY
  - CLEARBIT_API_KEY
  - HUNTER_API_KEY

  If keys are missing:

  - enrichment proceeds with website fallback only
  - still produces persona targets + email draft

  ### Contact selection logic (decision complete)

  - Build candidate list with fields: name, title, email, source, source_url, confidence.
  - Confidence rules:
      - high: provider marks verified OR email from mailto: on company domain leadership/contact page
      - med: email pattern inferred by provider OR email on press page without verification
      - low: generic inbox only (info@/support@) or unclear association
  - Rank candidates by:
      1. title match to target personas (exact > partial > none)
      2. seniority keywords (VP > Director > Head > Manager)
      3. confidence (high > med > low)
  - Select top 3 contacts.
  - If none found: fill contacts with persona-only placeholders and contact_*_source = "".

  ### Compliance rules (documented + enforced)

  - Do not crawl beyond a fixed small set of pages per domain (max 6 URLs).
  - Do not follow external social links.
  - Respect timeouts and rate limits.
  - Store only business contact fields.

  ———

  ## Phase 6: Email Generation (Per Company)

  ### Email template library

  Create templates in:

  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/outreach/templates/
      - base_mission.md
      - by_category/<category>.md
      - by_theme/ai.md (overlay)

  Email composition rules:

  - Subject: choose 1 of 2 deterministic options (hash(company_slug) parity).
  - Body sections:
      1. 1-sentence mission: predictable cost + affordability
      2. 1–2 sentences: why this matters for their segment
      3. 2–3 bullets: what we can provide now (mapped to offerings)
      4. 1 bullet: fast build offer (category-specific)
      5. CTA: 15 min call + ask who owns partnerships/data/market-access

  Personalization inputs:

  - company name
  - segment/category
  - one “fast build”
  - one proof point: relevant bundle list (plan/pharmacy/formulary/claims/ratings)
  - AI overlay adds: “deterministic APIs + MCP-ready tool recipes”

  Output:

  - email_subject and email_body columns in clients.csv
  - Batch markdown includes the subject + first 2 lines preview

  ———

  ## Phase 7: Validation + Tests

  ### Validation checks (script)

  - CSV exists and has required headers
  - row count == company count
  - batches cover all companies and each has exactly 100 except last
  - no duplicate company_slug
  - deterministic ordering (hash-based subject selection stable)

  ### Unit tests (pytest)

  Add tests under:

  - api-app/tests/test_outreach_generator.py

  Test scenarios:

  - scoring produces expected ordering with crafted CompanyAgg fixtures
  - batching splits exactly 100
  - persona mapping for pharma vs AI vs navigation

  Fixtures:

  - add small JSON fixtures for Nasdaq/YC in scripts/potential_clients/fixtures/
  - add mock responses for Apollo/Clearbit/Hunter in tests/fixtures/

  ## Rollout Steps

  2. Run outreach.py build --full to produce 2976-row clients.csv and batch docs.
  3. Review Batch 1–3 manually for sanity (contacts/titles/email copy).

  - Batch 1–3 prioritize growth and scale.

• Proposed Plan


  # Outreach Batches + Personal Email Inference/Verification (No Paid Enrichment)

  ## Summary

  Build an outreach pipeline on top of our existing prospect universe that:

  - groups companies into batches of 100 with tactics + priority ordering,
  - generates per-company email drafts,
  - generates personal emails by inferring patterns from public sources and attempting verification (DNS + SMTP),
  - writes a committed CRM CSV (as you requested) including names/titles/emails and confidence.

  This replaces Apollo/Clearbit/Hunter with:

  - company website contact/leadership pages (strict crawl),
  - SEC EDGAR for public companies (IR/press contacts),
  - deterministic email-pattern inference + best-effort verification.

  ## Outputs (Committed)

  Create:

  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/batch-0001.md … batch-00NN.md
  - api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/README.md (how to run, safety, caveats)
  - Keep existing indexes: specs/potential_clients/pharma.md, specs/potential_clients/ai.md, etc.

  ### CSV schema (stable)

  Columns (key ones):

  - Company: company_slug, company_name, category, go_to_market, themes, recommended_plan, priority_score, batch_id, website
  - Sources: sources (healthit|nasdaq|yc|curated|edgar|website)
  - Pharma: nasdaq_tickers, market_cap_usd, market_cap_bucket
  - Contacts (up to 3):
      - contact_1_name, contact_1_title, contact_1_email, contact_1_source_url, contact_1_confidence, contact_1_verify_status
      - same for contact_2/contact_3
  - Email: email_subject, email_body
  - tactics_notes (1–2 sentence per company)
  - verification_notes (e.g. smtp_blocked, catchall_domain, mx_missing)

  ## Core Script (New)

  Add:

  - api-app/scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py

  Commands:

  1. build --full
      - loads full universe from scripts.potential_clients.generate (HealthIT + curated + Nasdaq + YC)
      - assigns scores + batches
      - fetches contacts + infers personal emails
      - verifies emails (DNS + SMTP)
      - writes CSV + batch markdowns
  2. build --full --no-smtp
      - same, but skips SMTP verification (DNS only)
  3. build --full --no-crawl
      - no website crawling; EDGAR only for public + whatever already in dataset URLs
  4. validate
      - checks schema, counts, batch sizing

  ## Batching and Priority (Decision Complete)

  ### Priority scoring

  priority_score integer:

  - Plan weight: scale +100, growth +60, starter +20
  - Go-to-market: b2b2c +20, b2c +10, b2b +0
  - Themes: ai +15, insurance +10, pharmacy +10, cost +10, governance +5, assistance +5, discounts +5
  - Pharma mid/small “new sales” bias (category pharma_life_sciences):
      - micro +20, small +15, mid +10, large +5, mega +0

  Ordering:

  - sort by priority_score desc, then recommended_plan, then company_name asc

  Batch assignment:

  - rank companies in that order, set batch_id = (rank // 100) + 1

  Batch 1–3 emphasis:

  - take top 300 by the score (this naturally favors growth/scale + pharma small/micro + AI theme)

  ## Tactics Playbooks (Per Batch)

  In each batch-XXXX.md, include:

  - segment mix (top categories/themes)
  - “who to reach first”:
      - 3 target personas per dominant segment
      - 3 talking points
      - 3 objections + answers
  - top 10 companies in the batch (highest score)

  Persona mapping (examples):

  - pharma_life_sciences: VP Market Access, Commercial Analytics, Patient Services, Digital Partnerships
  - condition_management: VP Partnerships, VP Product, Head of Data
  - ai theme overlay: CTO/VP Eng, Head of Data, Applied AI lead
  - rcm_billing: VP Rev Cycle, Head of Integrations, Partnerships
  - benefits_navigation/assistance_navigation: VP Product, Partnerships, Network Strategy
  - pharmacy_tech/discounts_savings: Pharmacy Partnerships, PM Coverage/Access, Analytics lead

  ## Contact Discovery (No Paid Tools)

  ### Step 1: Website crawl (strict, small scope)

  For each company domain:

  - Fetch max 6 URLs (no external links):
      - /contact, /about, /team, /leadership, /press, /investors, /partners
  - Parse:
      - mailto: emails
      - visible emails
      - names + titles in leadership/team pages (public text)
  - Store source_url for each extracted item.

  ### Step 2: SEC EDGAR (public companies)

  For each Nasdaq ticker / CIK:

  - Fetch recent filings index and/or company facts page.
  - Look for IR/press contacts embedded in filing documents (where present).
  - Extract emails + context URL.

  ## Personal Email Inference (Decision Complete)

  We will infer personal emails when we have:

  - at least 1 leadership/team name (First/Last) AND
  - a company domain AND
  - at least one seed email on that domain OR we fall back to a standard pattern list.

  ### Pattern inference

  Build candidate patterns:

  - first.last@domain
  - firstlast@domain
  - f.last@domain
  - firstl@domain
  - first@domain
  - last@domain

  If we have seed emails on the same domain:

  - try to infer pattern by comparing local-part tokens to known names (when possible)
  - otherwise rank patterns by commonness (above order)

  Generate up to 3 email candidates per person (top 3 patterns), then verify.

  ## Email Verification (DNS + SMTP) (Your Choice)

  ### DNS checks (always)

  - Syntax check
  - Domain A/AAAA check
  - MX lookup
  - If no MX: mark verify_status=mx_missing and do not attempt SMTP

  ### SMTP verification (best-effort)

  Attempt to connect to the highest-priority MX host on port 25 and perform:

  - EHLO
  - MAIL FROM:<verify@healthporta.com> (configurable)
  - RCPT TO:<candidate@domain>

  Interpretation:

  - 250/251 on RCPT: “accepted”
  - 550/551/553 on RCPT: “rejected”
  - timeouts / connection refused: smtp_blocked (common on many networks)

  Catch-all detection:

  - also test a random address like no_such_user_<hash>@domain
  - if random is also accepted -> verify_status=catchall, downgrade confidence

  Rate limits/safety:

  - global max concurrent SMTP connections: 5
  - per-domain max: 1 at a time
  - exponential backoff on transient failures
  - hard cap attempts per domain: 10 recipients

  ### Confidence rules

  - high: RCPT rejected/accepted with non-catchall evidence (rare), or direct email appears on official page
  - med: inferred + catchall (cannot truly verify) OR inferred + SMTP inconclusive
  - low: inferred with no MX/SMTP blocked AND no seed email evidence

  Important reality constraint:

  - Many domains use catch-all or block verification; we will still produce “best guess” but mark it med/low.

  ## Per-Company Email Drafts

  Template system:

  - mission paragraph: “make treatment cost predictable”
  - segment paragraph: tailored by category/themes
  - 2–3 bullets: what we offer (mapped to our bundles)
  - 1 bullet: fast-build offer
  - CTA: “who owns partnerships/data/market access?”

  If we have a personal contact name:

  - greeting uses first name (e.g. “Hi Sarah,”)
    If not:

      71 +def _http_get_text(url: str, *, timeout_s: int = 15, user_agent: str | None = None) -> str:
      72 +    ua = user_agent or "Mozilla/5.0 (HealthPorta Outreach Generator)"
      73 +    req = Request(
      74 +        url,
      75 +        headers={
      76 +            "User-Agent": ua,
      77 +            "Accept": "text/html,application/json;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
      78 +            "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
      79 +        },
      80 +    )
      81 +    with urlopen(req, timeout=timeout_s) as resp:
      82 +        data = resp.read()
      83 +    # Best-effort decoding.
      84 +    try:
      85 +        return data.decode("utf-8")
      86 +    except UnicodeDecodeError:
      87 +        return data.decode("latin-1", errors="ignore")
      88 +
      89 +
      90 +def _safe_write_text(path: Path, content: str) -> None:
      91 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      92 +    path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
      93 +
      94 +
      95 +def _safe_write_csv(path: Path, rows: list[dict[str, str]], fieldnames: list[str]) -> None:
      96 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
      97 +    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
      98 +        w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
      99 +        w.writeheader()
     100 +        for row in rows:
     101 +            # Ensure all keys exist to keep a stable schema.
     102 +            w.writerow({k: row.get(k, "") for k in fieldnames})
     103 +
     104 +
     105 +def _slug_hash_int(value: str) -> int:
     106 +    return int(hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:8], 16)
     107 +
     108 +
     109 +def _normalize_domain(value: str) -> str | None:
     110 +    raw = (value or "").strip()
     111 +    if not raw:
     112 +        return None
     113 +    if raw.startswith("/"):
     114 +        return None
     115 +    if not raw.startswith("http://") and not raw.startswith("https://"):
     116 +        raw = "https://" + raw
     117 +    try:
     118 +        parsed = urlparse(raw)
     119 +    except Exception:
     120 +        return None
     121 +    host = (parsed.hostname or "").strip().lower()
     122 +    if not host or "." not in host:
     123 +        return None
     124 +    # Strip leading www for pattern inference stability.
     125 +    if host.startswith("www."):
     126 +        host = host[4:]
     127 +    return host
     128 +
     129 +
     130 +def _base_url_from_website(value: str) -> str | None:
     131 +    raw = (value or "").strip()
     132 +    if not raw:
     133 +        return None
     134 +    if raw.startswith("/"):
     135 +        return None
     136 +    if not raw.startswith("http://") and not raw.startswith("https://"):
     137 +        raw = "https://" + raw
     138 +    parsed = urlparse(raw)
     139 +    if not parsed.scheme or not parsed.netloc:
     140 +        return None
     141 +    return f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}/"
     142 +
     143 +
     144 +def _extract_emails(text: str) -> set[str]:
     145 +    emails = set()
     146 +    for m in _EMAIL_RE.finditer(text or ""):
     147 +        email = m.group(0).strip().strip(".,;:()[]{}<>\"'")
     148 +        if "@" not in email:
     149 +            continue
     150 +        # Avoid obvious garbage.
     151 +        if len(email) > 254:
     152 +            continue
     153 +        emails.add(email.lower())
     154 +    return emails
     155 +
     156 +
     157 +def _extract_jsonld_blocks(html: str) -> list[object]:
     158 +    blocks: list[object] = []
     159 +    if not html:
     160 +        return blocks
     161 +    for m in re.finditer(
     162 +        r"<script[^>]*type=[\"']application/ld\\+json[\"'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>",
     163 +        html,
     164 +        flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
     165 +    ):
     166 +        raw = (m.group(1) or "").strip()
     167 +        if not raw:
     168 +            continue
     169 +        try:
     170 +            blocks.append(json.loads(raw))
     171 +        except json.JSONDecodeError:
     172 +            continue
     173 +    return blocks
     174 +
     175 +
     176 +def _walk_json(obj: object) -> Iterable[dict[str, object]]:
     177 +    if isinstance(obj, dict):
     178 +        yield obj
     179 +        for v in obj.values():
     180 +            yield from _walk_json(v)
     181 +    elif isinstance(obj, list):
     182 +        for item in obj:
     183 +            yield from _walk_json(item)
     184 +
     185 +
     186 +@dataclass(frozen=True)
     187 +class Person:
     188 +    name: str
     189 +    title: str | None
     190 +    source_url: str
     191 +
     192 +
     193 +def _extract_people_from_jsonld(html: str, source_url: str) -> list[Person]:
     194 +    people: list[Person] = []
     195 +    for block in _extract_jsonld_blocks(html):
     196 +        for node in _walk_json(block):
     197 +            t = node.get("@type") or node.get("type")
     198 +            if isinstance(t, list):
     199 +                types = {str(x) for x in t}
     200 +            else:
     201 +                types = {str(t)}
     202 +            if "Person" not in types:
     203 +                continue
     204 +            name = str(node.get("name") or "").strip()
     205 +            if not name:
     206 +                continue
     207 +            title = str(node.get("jobTitle") or node.get("title") or "").strip() or None
     208 +            people.append(Person(name=name, title=title, source_url=source_url))
     209 +    # Dedupe by (name,title).
     210 +    seen: set[tuple[str, str | None]] = set()
     211 +    uniq: list[Person] = []
     212 +    for p in people:
     213 +        key = (p.name.strip().lower(), (p.title or "").strip().lower() or None)
     214 +        if key in seen:
     215 +            continue
     216 +        seen.add(key)
     217 +        uniq.append(p)
     218 +    return uniq
     219 +
     220 +
     221 +def _robots_allows(base_url: str, target_url: str, user_agent: str) -> bool:
     222 +    try:
     223 +        rp = RobotFileParser()
     224 +        rp.set_url(urljoin(base_url, "robots.txt"))
     225 +        rp.read()
     226 +        return bool(rp.can_fetch(user_agent, target_url))
     227 +    except Exception:
     228 +        # If robots cannot be read/parsed, default to allow.
     229 +        return True
     230 +
     231 +
     232 +@dataclass
     233 +class DiscoveredContacts:
     234 +    emails: dict[str, str]  # email -> source_url
     235 +    people: list[Person]
     236 +    contact_urls: list[str]
     237 +
     238 +
     239 +def discover_contacts_for_website(
     240 +    website: str,
     241 +    *,
     242 +    timeout_s: int = 15,
     243 +    max_pages: int = 6,
     244 +    respect_robots: bool = True,
     245 +    user_agent: str | None = None,
     246 +) -> DiscoveredContacts:
     247 +    ua = user_agent or "Mozilla/5.0 (HealthPorta Outreach Generator)"
     248 +    base_url = _base_url_from_website(website)
     249 +    if not base_url:
     250 +        return DiscoveredContacts(emails={}, people=[], contact_urls=[])
     251 +
     252 +    paths = ["contact", "about", "team", "leadership", "press", "investors", "partners"]
     253 +    urls = [urljoin(base_url, p) for p in paths]
     254 +    # Always include base URL as a fallback (some sites list email in footer).
     255 +    urls.append(base_url)
     256 +
     257 +    emails: dict[str, str] = {}
     258 +    people: list[Person] = []
     259 +    visited: set[str] = set()
     260 +    contact_urls: list[str] = []
     261 +
     262 +    for url in urls:
     263 +        if len(visited) >= max_pages:
     264 +            break
     265 +        if url in visited:
     266 +            continue
     267 +        visited.add(url)
     268 +
     269 +        if respect_robots and not _robots_allows(base_url, url, ua):
     270 +            continue
     271 +
     272 +        try:
     273 +            html = _http_get_text(url, timeout_s=timeout_s, user_agent=ua)
     274 +        except Exception:
     275 +            continue
     276 +
     277 +        contact_urls.append(url)
     278 +
     279 +        found = _extract_emails(html)
     280 +        for e in found:
     281 +            emails.setdefault(e, url)
     282 +        people.extend(_extract_people_from_jsonld(html, url))
     283 +
     284 +    return DiscoveredContacts(emails=emails, people=people, contact_urls=contact_urls)
     285 +
     286 +
     287 +# --- DNS MX via DoH (no extra deps) ---
     288 +
     289 +
     290 +@dataclass(frozen=True)
     291 +class MXRecord:
     292 +    preference: int
     293 +    host: str
     294 +
     295 +
     296 +def resolve_mx_google_doh(domain: str, *, timeout_s: int = 8) -> list[MXRecord]:
     297 +    # https://dns.google/resolve?name=example.com&type=MX
     298 +    url = f"https://dns.google/resolve?name={domain}&type=MX"
     299 +    try:
     300 +        raw = _http_get_text(url, timeout_s=timeout_s)
     301 +        data = json.loads(raw)
     302 +    except Exception:
     303 +        return []
     304 +    answers = data.get("Answer") or []
     305 +    records: list[MXRecord] = []
     306 +    for ans in answers:
     307 +        if not isinstance(ans, dict):
     308 +            continue
     309 +        mx = str(ans.get("data") or "").strip()
     310 +        # Format: "10 mx1.example.com."
     311 +        parts = mx.split()
     312 +        if len(parts) != 2:
     313 +            continue
     314 +        try:
     315 +            pref = int(parts[0])
     316 +        except ValueError:
     317 +            continue
     318 +        host = parts[1].rstrip(".").lower()
     319 +        if host:
     320 +            records.append(MXRecord(preference=pref, host=host))
     321 +    records.sort(key=lambda r: (r.preference, r.host))
     322 +    return records
     323 +
     324 +
     325 +# --- SMTP verification ---
     326 +
     327 +
     328 +@dataclass
     329 +class EmailVerification:
     330 +    status: str  # accepted|rejected|catchall|smtp_blocked|mx_missing|error
     331 +    code: int | None = None
     332 +    message: str | None = None
     333 +
     334 +
     335 +def _smtp_read_line(sock_file) -> str | None:
     336 +    line = sock_file.readline()
     337 +    if not line:
     338 +        return None
     339 +    try:
     340 +        return line.decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").strip("\\r\\n")
     341 +    except Exception:
     342 +        return str(line)
     343 +
     344 +
     345 +def _smtp_read_response(sock_file) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
     346 +    lines: list[str] = []
     347 +    code: int | None = None
     348 +    while True:
     349 +        line = _smtp_read_line(sock_file)
     350 +        if line is None:
     351 +            break
     352 +        lines.append(line)
     353 +        m = re.match(r"^(\\d{3})([\\s-])(.*)$", line)
     354 +        if m:
     355 +            code = int(m.group(1))
     356 +            more = m.group(2) == "-"
     357 +            if not more:
     358 +                break
     359 +        else:
     360 +            # Non-standard line; stop to avoid hanging.
     361 +            break
     362 +    return code, "\\n".join(lines).strip()
     363 +
     364 +
     365 +def _smtp_send(sock_file, line: str) -> None:
     366 +    sock_file.write((line + "\\r\\n").encode("utf-8"))
     367 +    sock_file.flush()
     368 +
     369 +
     370 +def _smtp_try_starttls(sock, sock_file, ehlo_text: str, timeout_s: int) -> tuple[socket.socket, object, bool]:
     371 +    if "STARTTLS" not in (ehlo_text or "").upper():
     372 +        return sock, sock_file, False
     373 +    _smtp_send(sock_file, "STARTTLS")
     374 +    code, _msg = _smtp_read_response(sock_file)
     375 +    if code != 220:
     376 +        return sock, sock_file, False
     377 +    ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
     378 +    wrapped = ctx.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname=getattr(sock, "server_hostname", None))
     379 +    wrapped.settimeout(timeout_s)
     380 +    file2 = wrapped.makefile("rwb", buffering=0)
     381 +    return wrapped, file2, True
     382 +
     383 +
     384 +def smtp_verify_rcpt(
     385 +    mx_host: str,
     386 +    *,
     387 +    mail_from: str,
     388 +    rcpt_to: str,
     389 +    timeout_s: int = 8,
     390 +) -> EmailVerification:
     391 +    try:
     392 +        sock = socket.create_connection((mx_host, 25), timeout=timeout_s)
     393 +    except Exception as e:
     394 +        return EmailVerification(status="smtp_blocked", message=str(e))
     395 +
     396 +    try:
     397 +        sock.settimeout(timeout_s)
     398 +        sf = sock.makefile("rwb", buffering=0)
     399 +
     400 +        _code, _greet = _smtp_read_response(sf)
     401 +        _smtp_send(sf, "EHLO healthporta.local")
     402 +        ehlo_code, ehlo_msg = _smtp_read_response(sf)
     403 +        if ehlo_code is None:
     404 +            return EmailVerification(status="error", message="no_ehlo_response")
     405 +
     406 +        # Opportunistic STARTTLS.
     407 +        sock2, sf2, _did_tls = _smtp_try_starttls(sock, sf, ehlo_msg, timeout_s)
     408 +        sock = sock2
     409 +        sf = sf2
     410 +        if _did_tls:
     411 +            _smtp_send(sf, "EHLO healthporta.local")
     412 +            _smtp_read_response(sf)
     413 +
     414 +        _smtp_send(sf, f"MAIL FROM:<{mail_from}>")
     415 +        _smtp_read_response(sf)
     416 +
     417 +        _smtp_send(sf, f"RCPT TO:<{rcpt_to}>")
     418 +        rcpt_code, rcpt_msg = _smtp_read_response(sf)
     419 +
     420 +        try:
     421 +            _smtp_send(sf, "QUIT")
     422 +        except Exception:
     423 +            pass
     424 +
     425 +        if rcpt_code is None:
     426 +            return EmailVerification(status="error", message="no_rcpt_code")
     427 +        if 250 <= rcpt_code < 260:
     428 +            return EmailVerification(status="accepted", code=rcpt_code, message=rcpt_msg)
     429 +        if 500 <= rcpt_code < 600:
     430 +            return EmailVerification(status="rejected", code=rcpt_code, message=rcpt_msg)
     431 +        return EmailVerification(status="error", code=rcpt_code, message=rcpt_msg)
     432 +    finally:
     433 +        try:
     434 +            sock.close()
     435 +        except Exception:
     436 +            pass
     437 +
     438 +
     439 +def _random_address_for_domain(domain: str) -> str:
     440 +    # Deterministic random so reruns are stable.
     441 +    rnd = random.Random(_slug_hash_int(domain))
     442 +    token = "".join(rnd.choice("abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789") for _ in range(12))
     443 +    return f"no_such_user_{token}@{domain}"
     444 +
     445 +
     446 +class EmailVerifier:
     447 +    def __init__(self, *, timeout_s: int = 8, mail_from: str = "verify@healthporta.com") -> None:
     448 +        self.timeout_s = timeout_s
     449 +        self.mail_from = mail_from
     450 +        self._mx_cache: dict[str, list[MXRecord]] = {}
     451 +        self._catchall_cache: dict[str, bool] = {}
     452 +
     453 +    def resolve_mx(self, domain: str) -> list[MXRecord]:
     454 +        if domain in self._mx_cache:
     455 +            return self._mx_cache[domain]
     456 +        mx = resolve_mx_google_doh(domain, timeout_s=self.timeout_s)
     457 +        self._mx_cache[domain] = mx
     458 +        return mx
     459 +
     460 +    def is_catchall(self, domain: str, mx_host: str) -> bool:
     461 +        if domain in self._catchall_cache:
     462 +            return self._catchall_cache[domain]
     463 +        probe = _random_address_for_domain(domain)
     464 +        res = smtp_verify_rcpt(mx_host, mail_from=self.mail_from, rcpt_to=probe, timeout_s=self.timeout_s)
     465 +        is_catchall = res.status == "accepted"
     466 +        self._catchall_cache[domain] = is_catchall
     467 +        return is_catchall
     468 +
     469 +    def verify(self, email: str) -> EmailVerification:
     470 +        domain = (email.split("@", 1)[1] if "@" in email else "").strip().lower()
     471 +        if not domain:
     472 +            return EmailVerification(status="error", message="bad_domain")
     473 +        mx = self.resolve_mx(domain)
     474 +        if not mx:
     475 +            return EmailVerification(status="mx_missing")
     476 +        # Try the first two MX hosts.
     477 +        for rec in mx[:2]:
     478 +            res = smtp_verify_rcpt(rec.host, mail_from=self.mail_from, rcpt_to=email, timeout_s=self.timeout_s)
     479 +            if res.status in {"accepted", "rejected"}:
     480 +                if res.status == "accepted" and self.is_catchall(domain, rec.host):
     481 +                    return EmailVerification(status="catchall", code=res.code, message=res.message)
     482 +                return res
     483 +            if res.status == "smtp_blocked":
     484 +                return res
     485 +        return EmailVerification(status="error", message="mx_exhausted")
     486 +
     487 +
     488 +# --- Email inference ---
     489 +
     490 +
     491 +def _split_name(name: str) -> tuple[str, str] | None:
     492 +    raw = (name or "").strip()
     493 +    if not raw:
     494 +        return None
     495 +    # Remove common suffixes.
     496 +    raw = re.sub(r"\\b(jr|sr|ii|iii|iv|md|phd)\\b\\.?", "", raw, flags=re.IGNORECASE).strip()
     497 +    parts = [p for p in re.split(r"\\s+", raw) if p]
     498 +    if len(parts) < 2:
     499 +        return None
     500 +    first = parts[0]
     501 +    last = parts[-1]
     502 +    return first, last
     503 +
     504 +
     505 +def _clean_name_token(token: str) -> str:
     506 +    token = (token or "").strip().lower()
     507 +    token = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", token)
     508 +    return token
     509 +
     510 +
     511 +def infer_patterns(seed_emails: Iterable[str], domain: str) -> list[str]:
     512 +    # Order matters (most likely first).
     513 +    default = ["first.last", "firstlast", "f.last", "firstl", "first", "last"]
     514 +
     515 +    domain = domain.lower()
     516 +    seeds = []
     517 +    for e in seed_emails:
     518 +        e = (e or "").strip().lower()
     519 +        if not e.endswith("@" + domain):
     520 +            continue
     521 +        local = e.split("@", 1)[0]
     522 +        if local in _GENERIC_LOCAL_PARTS:
     523 +            continue
     524 +        seeds.append(local)
     525 +
     526 +    # If we have any personal-looking seeds, prefer dot patterns when dots appear.
     527 +    if any("." in s for s in seeds):
     528 +        return ["first.last", "f.last", "firstlast", "firstl", "first", "last"]
     529 +    # If we have seeds without dots, prefer concatenated.
     530 +    if seeds:
     531 +        return ["firstlast", "firstl", "first.last", "f.last", "first", "last"]
     532 +    return default
     533 +
     534 +
     535 +def build_email_local_part(pattern: str, first: str, last: str) -> str:
     536 +    f = _clean_name_token(first)
     537 +    l = _clean_name_token(last)
     538 +    if not f or not l:
     539 +        return ""
     540 +    if pattern == "first.last":
     541 +        return f"{f}.{l}"
     542 +    if pattern == "firstlast":
     543 +        return f"{f}{l}"
     544 +    if pattern == "f.last":
     545 +        return f"{f[0]}.{l}"
     546 +    if pattern == "firstl":
     547 +        return f"{f}{l[0]}"
     548 +    if pattern == "first":
     549 +        return f
     550 +    if pattern == "last":
     551 +        return l
     552 +    return f"{f}.{l}"
     553 +
     554 +
     555 +def generate_candidate_emails(name: str, domain: str, patterns: list[str], max_candidates: int = 3) -> list[str]:
     556 +    parts = _split_name(name)
     557 +    if not parts:
     558 +        return []
     559 +    first, last = parts
     560 +    out: list[str] = []
     561 +    for p in patterns:
     562 +        local = build_email_local_part(p, first, last)
     563 +        if not local:
     564 +            continue
     565 +        email = f"{local}@{domain}".lower()
     566 +        if email not in out:
     567 +            out.append(email)
     568 +        if len(out) >= max_candidates:
     569 +            break
     570 +    return out
     571 +
     572 +

     594 +    base = list(_PERSONA_BY_CATEGORY.get(category, _PERSONA_BY_CATEGORY["other_health_it"]))
     595 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
     596 +        overlay = ["CTO/VP Engineering", "Applied AI Lead", "Head of Data"]
     597 +        for p in overlay:
     598 +            if p not in base:
     599 +                base.insert(0, p)
     600 +        base = base[:4]
     601 +    return base[:3]

     605 +    a = f"Making treatment costs predictable ({company_name})"

     610 +def email_body(company_name: str, category: str, themes: list[str], fast_build: str, greeting_name: str | None) -> str:
     611 +    greeting = f"Hi {greeting_name}," if greeting_name else "Hi team,"
     612 +    mission = (
     613 +        "We’re building HealthPorta to make the cost of treatment predictable for everyone, "
     614 +        "and we help healthcare companies ship cost/coverage-aware experiences using reliable APIs."
     615 +    )
     616 +    segment = {
     617 +        "pharma_life_sciences": "I’m reaching out because market access and affordability workflows often need fast, auditable coverage and drug-code intelligence.",
     618 +        "condition_management": "I’m reaching out because member navigation and affordability workflows often break on incomplete coverage and provider/pharmacy data.",
     619 +        "benefits_navigation": "I’m reaching out because benefits navigation products need fast plan/network intelligence to keep recommendations accurate.",
     620 +        "assistance_navigation": "I’m reaching out because navigation teams need deterministic plan/network/provider data to reduce member friction.",
     621 +        "discounts_savings": "I’m reaching out because discount/savings experiences work best when they can explain cash vs coverage tradeoffs clearly.",
     622 +    }.get(category, "I’m reaching out because your product likely benefits from reliable plan/network, provider, and pharmacy intelligence.")
     623 +
     624 +    bullets = [
     625 +        "Plan + issuer lookup and network intelligence (where a member is actually in-network).",
     626 +        "Medication normalization (RxNorm/NDC where possible) and formulary/coverage checks.",
     627 +        "Directory + geo search for providers and pharmacies to power navigation UX.",
     628 +        "Medicare-derived benchmarks and cost buckets (clearly labeled) for procedure comparisons.",
     629 +    ]
     630 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
     631 +        bullets.append("Deterministic, MCP-friendly tool workflows (autocomplete -> search -> explain results) to avoid brittle scraping.")
     632 +    bullets = bullets[:4]
     633 +
     634 +    cta = "If you can point me to who owns partnerships/data/market access on your side, I’ll send a 1-page proposal. Open to a quick 15 min intro?"
     635 +
     636 +    return "\n".join(
     637 +        [
     638 +            greeting,
     639 +            "",
     640 +            mission,
     641 +            "",
     642 +            segment,
     643 +            "",
     644 +            "What we can help with:",
     645 +            *[f"- {b}" for b in bullets],
     646 +            "",
     647 +            f"One fast build we can deliver in 1–3 weeks: {fast_build}",
     648 +            "",
     649 +            cta,
     650 +            "",
     651 +            "Best,",
     652 +            "Nick / HealthPorta",
     653 +        ]
     654 +    ).rstrip()

     657 +def fast_build_offer(category: str, themes: list[str]) -> str:
     658 +    base = {
     659 +        "pharma_life_sciences": "Top prescribers by drug near a geography (Medicare Part D aggregates), plus NDC/RxNorm normalization.",
     660 +        "condition_management": "Member navigation composite: ZIP + procedure -> ranked in-network providers with cost buckets.",
     661 +        "benefits_navigation": "Plan quick summary for a ZIP (issuer + premium min/max + deductible/MOOP highlights).",
     662 +        "assistance_navigation": "Coverage-aware provider + pharmacy routing (nearby + in-network + deterministic ordering).",
     663 +        "discounts_savings": "Drug autocomplete -> coverage vs cash-aware explanation and pharmacy routing.",
     664 +        "rcm_billing": "Intake helper: plan/network lookup + coverage hints for front-desk workflows.",
     665 +        "pharmacy_tech": "Plan-aware pharmacy ranking (covered + nearby + deterministic ordering).",
     666 +        "telehealth": "Provider-card endpoint (address/phone/maps + cost bucket + quality proxy).",
     667 +    }.get(category, "Composite endpoint for a single workflow to reduce client round-trips.")
     668 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
     669 +        return base + " (with MCP-ready tool recipes for agent workflows)."
     670 +    return base
