     672 +
     673 +# --- Scoring / batching ---
     674 +
     675 +
     676 +def priority_score(*, plan: str, go_to_market: str, themes: list[str], category: str, market_cap_bucket: str | None) -> int:
     677 +    score = 0
     678 +    score += {"scale": 100, "growth": 60, "starter": 20}.get(plan, 0)
     679 +    score += {"b2b2c": 20, "b2c": 10, "b2b": 0}.get(go_to_market, 0)
     680 +    theme_weights = {
     681 +        "ai": 15,
     682 +        "insurance": 10,
     683 +        "pharmacy": 10,
     684 +        "cost": 10,
     685 +        "governance": 5,
     686 +        "assistance": 5,
     687 +        "discounts": 5,
     688 +    }
     689 +    for t in set(themes):
     690 +        score += theme_weights.get(t, 0)
     691 +    if category == "pharma_life_sciences" and market_cap_bucket:
     692 +        score += {"micro": 20, "small": 15, "mid": 10, "large": 5, "mega": 0}.get(market_cap_bucket, 0)
     693 +    return score
     694 +
     695 +
     696 +def _plan_rank(plan: str) -> int:
     697 +    return {"scale": 2, "growth": 1, "starter": 0}.get(plan, 0)
     698 +
     699 +
     700 +# --- Universe loading (from generate.py) ---
     701 +
     702 +
     703 +def _load_universe(*, full: bool, offline: bool, limit: int | None = None):
     704 +    from scripts.potential_clients import generate as gen
     705 +
     706 +    if offline:
     707 +        csv_path = gen._fixture_csv_path()
     708 +        nasdaq_path = gen._fixture_nasdaq_json_path()
     709 +        yc_path = gen._fixture_yc_json_path()
     710 +        fetched_at = "fixture"
     711 +    else:
     712 +        csv_path = gen.fetch_dataset(force=False)
     713 +        nasdaq_path = gen.fetch_nasdaq_screener(force=False)
     714 +        yc_path = gen.fetch_yc_companies(force=False)
     715 +        fetched_at = gen._utc_now_iso()
     716 +
     717 +    companies = gen._load_companies_from_csv(csv_path)
     718 +    companies = gen._merge_curated(companies, gen._load_curated_companies(gen._curated_csv_path()))
     719 +    companies = gen._merge_public_equities(companies, gen._load_nasdaq_screener_rows(nasdaq_path))
     720 +    companies = gen._merge_yc_companies(companies, gen._load_yc_companies_rows(yc_path))
     721 +
     722 +    aggs = list(companies.values())
     723 +    if not full:
     724 +        aggs = sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: (-a.app_count, a.name.lower()))[: max(1000, 100)]
     725 +    if limit is not None:
     726 +        aggs = sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: a.name.lower())[: max(1, int(limit))]
     727 +
     728 +    slugs = gen._assign_unique_slugs(sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: a.name.lower()))
     729 +    return gen, aggs, slugs, fetched_at
     730 +
     731 +
     732 +def _sec_user_agent() -> str | None:
     733 +    return os.environ.get("SEC_USER_AGENT")
     734 +
     735 +
     736 +def _fetch_sec_ticker_map(cache_dir: Path, *, timeout_s: int = 15) -> dict[str, int]:
     737 +    ua = _sec_user_agent()
     738 +    if not ua:
     739 +        return {}
     740 +    cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
     741 +    dest = cache_dir / "sec_company_tickers.json"
     742 +    if dest.exists():
     743 +        try:
     744 +            data = json.loads(dest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
     745 +        except Exception:
     746 +            data = None
     747 +    else:
     748 +        url = "https://www.sec.gov/files/company_tickers.json"
     749 +        try:
     750 +            raw = _http_get_text(url, timeout_s=timeout_s, user_agent=ua)
     751 +            dest.write_text(raw, encoding="utf-8")
     752 +            data = json.loads(raw)
     753 +        except Exception:
     754 +            data = None
     755 +    if not isinstance(data, dict):
     756 +        return {}
     757 +    out: dict[str, int] = {}
     758 +    for _k, v in data.items():
     759 +        if not isinstance(v, dict):
     760 +            continue
     761 +        ticker = str(v.get("ticker") or "").strip().lower()
     762 +        cik = v.get("cik_str")
     763 +        if not ticker or cik is None:
     764 +            continue
     765 +        try:
     766 +            out[ticker] = int(cik)
     767 +        except ValueError:
     768 +            continue
     769 +    return out
     770 +
     771 +
     772 +def _sec_extract_emails_for_cik(cik: int, *, timeout_s: int = 15, max_docs: int = 1) -> dict[str, str]:
     773 +    ua = _sec_user_agent()
     774 +    if not ua:
     775 +        return {}
     776 +    cik_str = str(int(cik)).zfill(10)
     777 +    url = f"https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik_str}.json"
     778 +    try:
     779 +        data = json.loads(_http_get_text(url, timeout_s=timeout_s, user_agent=ua))
     780 +    except Exception:
     781 +        return {}
     782 +    filings = (((data or {}).get("filings") or {}).get("recent") or {})
     783 +    forms = filings.get("form") or []
     784 +    acc = filings.get("accessionNumber") or []
     785 +    docs = filings.get("primaryDocument") or []
     786 +    if not (isinstance(forms, list) and isinstance(acc, list) and isinstance(docs, list)):
     787 +        return {}
     788 +
     789 +    wanted = {"8-k": 0, "10-k": 1, "10-q": 2}
     790 +    candidates: list[tuple[int, str, str]] = []
     791 +    for i in range(min(len(forms), len(acc), len(docs))):
     792 +        form = str(forms[i] or "").strip().lower()
     793 +        if form not in wanted:
     794 +            continue
     795 +        accession = str(acc[i] or "").strip()
     796 +        primary = str(docs[i] or "").strip()
     797 +        if not accession or not primary:
     798 +            continue
     799 +        rank = wanted[form]
     800 +        candidates.append((rank, accession, primary))
     801 +    candidates.sort(key=lambda t: (t[0], t[1]))
     802 +
     803 +    found: dict[str, str] = {}
     804 +    for _rank, accession, primary in candidates[: max_docs]:
     805 +        acc_nodash = accession.replace("-", "")
     806 +        doc_url = f"https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/{int(cik)}/{acc_nodash}/{primary}"
     807 +        try:
     808 +            text = _http_get_text(doc_url, timeout_s=timeout_s, user_agent=ua)
     809 +        except Exception:
     810 +            continue
     811 +        for e in _extract_emails(text):
     812 +            found.setdefault(e, doc_url)
     813 +    return found
     814 +
     815 +
     816 +def build(*, full: bool, offline: bool, no_crawl: bool, no_smtp: bool, limit: int | None, max_companies: int | None) -> int:
     817 +    gen, aggs, slugs, fetched_at = _load_universe(full=full, offline=offline, limit=limit)
     818 +
     819 +    # Compute company features.
     820 +    items = []
     821 +    for agg in aggs:
     822 +        slug = slugs.get(agg.normalized_name, gen._slugify(agg.name))
     823 +        category = gen._detect_company_category(agg)
     824 +        gtm = gen._detect_go_to_market(agg)
     825 +        themes = gen._detect_themes(agg)
     826 +        plan, _why, _assumptions = gen._recommended_plan(agg, category)
     827 +        mcap_bucket = gen._market_cap_bucket(agg.equity_market_cap_usd) if getattr(agg, "equity_market_cap_usd", None) else None
     828 +        score = priority_score(plan=plan, go_to_market=gtm, themes=themes, category=category, market_cap_bucket=mcap_bucket)
     829 +        items.append(
     830 +            {
     831 +                "agg": agg,
     832 +                "slug": slug,
     833 +                "category": category,
     834 +                "gtm": gtm,
     835 +                "themes": themes,
     836 +                "plan": plan,
     837 +                "score": score,
     838 +                "mcap_bucket": mcap_bucket or "",
     839 +            }
     840 +        )
     841 +
     842 +    # Growth/Scale first for first 300.
     843 +    hi = [x for x in items if x["plan"] in {"growth", "scale"}]
     844 +    lo = [x for x in items if x["plan"] not in {"growth", "scale"}]
     845 +    key = lambda x: (-int(x["score"]), -_plan_rank(x["plan"]), x["gtm"] != "b2b2c", x["slug"])
     846 +    hi.sort(key=key)
     847 +    lo.sort(key=key)
     848 +    ordered: list[dict[str, object]] = []
     849 +    ordered.extend(hi[:300])
     850 +    seen = {x["slug"] for x in ordered}
     851 +    for x in hi[300:] + lo:
     852 +        if x["slug"] in seen:
     853 +            continue
     854 +        ordered.append(x)
     855 +        seen.add(x["slug"])
     856 +
     857 +    if max_companies is not None:
     858 +        ordered = ordered[: max(1, int(max_companies))]
     859 +
     860 +    # Contact enrichment.
     861 +    verifier = EmailVerifier(timeout_s=int(os.environ.get("OUTREACH_SMTP_TIMEOUT_S", "8")), mail_from=os.environ.get("OUTREACH_VERIFY_FROM_EMAIL", "verify@healthporta.com"))
     862 +    respect_robots = os.environ.get("OUTREACH_RESPECT_ROBOTS", "true").strip().lower() not in {"0", "false", "no"}
     863 +    http_timeout = int(os.environ.get("OUTREACH_HTTP_TIMEOUT_S", "15"))
     864 +
     865 +    sec_cache_dir = _repo_root() / "scripts" / "potential_clients" / ".cache" / "sec"
     866 +    ticker_map = _fetch_sec_ticker_map(sec_cache_dir, timeout_s=http_timeout)
     867 +
     868 +    rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
     869 +
     870 +    # Stable CSV schema.
     871 +    fieldnames = [
     872 +        "company_slug",
     873 +        "company_name",
     874 +        "category",
     875 +        "go_to_market",
     876 +        "themes",

     890 +        # Contact 1-3.
     891 +        "contact_1_name",
     892 +        "contact_1_title",
     893 +        "contact_1_email",
     894 +        "contact_1_source",
     895 +        "contact_1_source_url",

     918 +

     920 +

     927 +        plan = str(item["plan"])
     928 +        score = int(item["score"])
     929 +        batch_id = (idx // 100) + 1
     930 +
     931 +        website = agg.best_website() if hasattr(agg, "best_website") else None
     932 +        website = website or ""
     933 +        domain = _normalize_domain(website) if website else None
     934 +
     935 +        sources = []
     936 +        if getattr(agg, "app_count", 0) > 0:
     937 +            sources.append("healthit")
     938 +        if getattr(agg, "curated", False):
     939 +            sources.append("curated")
     940 +        if getattr(agg, "public_equity", False):
     941 +            sources.append("nasdaq")
     942 +        if getattr(agg, "yc_company", False):
     943 +            sources.append("yc")
     944 +
     945 +        discovered = DiscoveredContacts(emails={}, people=[], contact_urls=[])
     946 +        if website and not no_crawl:
     947 +            discovered = discover_contacts_for_website(
     948 +                website,
     949 +                timeout_s=http_timeout,
     950 +                respect_robots=respect_robots,
     951 +            )
     952 +
     953 +        # EDGAR fallback for public equities when no emails from website.
     954 +        edgar_emails: dict[str, str] = {}
     955 +        if (not discovered.emails) and getattr(agg, "public_equity", False) and getattr(agg, "equity_symbols", None):
     956 +            tickers = sorted(getattr(agg, "equity_symbols"))
     957 +            for t in tickers[:1]:
     958 +                cik = ticker_map.get(str(t).lower())
     959 +                if not cik:
     960 +                    continue

     962 +                if edgar_emails:
     963 +                    break
     964 +
     965 +        all_emails: dict[str, str] = {}
     966 +        all_emails.update(discovered.emails)
     967 +        all_emails.update(edgar_emails)
     968 +
     969 +        # Pick people to attempt personal emails for.
     970 +        personas = target_personas(category, themes)
     971 +        target_persona = personas[0] if personas else ""
     972 +
     973 +        # Rank people by title relevance and seniority.
     974 +        def person_score(p: Person) -> int:
     975 +            t = (p.title or "").lower()
     976 +            s = 0
     977 +            for persona in personas:
     978 +                for token in persona.lower().split():
     979 +                    if token and token in t:
     980 +                        s += 3
     981 +            if any(k in t for k in ["chief", "cto", "cfo", "ceo", "president"]):
     982 +                s += 5
     983 +            if any(k in t for k in ["vp", "vice president"]):
     984 +                s += 4
     985 +            if "director" in t or "head" in t:
     986 +                s += 3
     987 +            return s
     988 +
     989 +        ranked_people = sorted(discovered.people, key=person_score, reverse=True)
     990 +
     991 +        contacts: list[dict[str, str]] = []
     992 +
     993 +        # Add inferred personal emails for up to 3 people.
     994 +        if domain and ranked_people:
     995 +            patterns = infer_patterns(all_emails.keys(), domain)
     996 +            for p in ranked_people[:3]:
     997 +                candidates = generate_candidate_emails(p.name, domain, patterns, max_candidates=3)
     998 +                chosen: str | None = None
     999 +                verify_status = ""
    1000 +                verification_notes = ""
    1001 +                if candidates:
    1002 +                    for cand in candidates:
    1003 +                        if no_smtp:
    1004 +                            chosen = cand
    1005 +                            verify_status = "not_checked"
    1006 +                            break
    1007 +                        res = verifier.verify(cand)
    1008 +                        verify_status = res.status
    1009 +                        if res.status in {"accepted", "catchall"}:
    1010 +                            chosen = cand
    1011 +                            break
    1012 +                        if res.status == "rejected":
    1013 +                            continue
    1014 +                        if res.status in {"smtp_blocked", "mx_missing"}:
    1015 +                            # Can't continue meaningfully.
    1016 +                            chosen = cand
    1017 +                            verification_notes = res.message or res.status
    1018 +                            break
    1019 +                if chosen:
    1020 +                    confidence = "med" if verify_status == "catchall" else ("high" if verify_status == "accepted" else "low")
    1021 +                    contacts.append(
    1022 +                        {
    1023 +                            "name": p.name,
    1024 +                            "title": p.title or "",
    1025 +                            "email": chosen,
    1026 +                            "source": "inferred",
    1027 +                            "source_url": p.source_url,
    1028 +                            "confidence": confidence,
    1029 +                            "verify_status": verify_status,
    1030 +                            "verification_notes": verification_notes,
    1031 +                        }
    1032 +                    )
    1033 +                if len(contacts) >= 3:
    1034 +                    break
    1035 +
    1036 +        # Fill remaining with public emails (generic or otherwise).
    1037 +        if len(contacts) < 3 and all_emails:
    1038 +            for e, src in sorted(all_emails.items()):
    1039 +                if any(c["email"] == e for c in contacts):
    1040 +                    continue
    1041 +                local = e.split("@", 1)[0]
    1042 +                confidence = "high" if local not in _GENERIC_LOCAL_PARTS else "med"
    1043 +                contacts.append(
    1044 +                    {
    1045 +                        "name": "",
    1046 +                        "title": "",
    1047 +                        "email": e,
    1048 +                        "source": "public",
    1049 +                        "source_url": src,
    1050 +                        "confidence": confidence,
    1051 +                        "verify_status": "public",
    1052 +                        "verification_notes": "",
    1053 +                    }
    1054 +                )
    1055 +                if len(contacts) >= 3:
    1056 +                    break
    1057 +
    1058 +        # Ensure 3 slots exist.
    1059 +        while len(contacts) < 3:
    1060 +            contacts.append(
    1061 +                {
    1062 +                    "name": "",
    1063 +                    "title": "",
    1064 +                    "email": "",
    1065 +                    "source": "",
    1066 +                    "source_url": (discovered.contact_urls[0] if discovered.contact_urls else ""),
    1067 +                    "confidence": "",
    1068 +                    "verify_status": "",
    1069 +                    "verification_notes": "",
    1070 +                }
    1071 +            )
    1072 +
    1073 +        greeting_name = (contacts[0]["name"].split(" ", 1)[0] if contacts[0]["name"] else None)
    1074 +        fb = fast_build_offer(category, themes)
    1075 +        subj = email_subject(agg.name, slug)
    1076 +        body = email_body(agg.name, category, themes, fb, greeting_name)
    1077 +
    1078 +        pr_reason = ",".join([plan, gtm] + sorted(set(themes))[:3])
    1079 +        tactics = f"Start with {target_persona}; lead with {category} fast-build and cost/coverage predictability mission."
    1080 +
    1081 +        verification_notes = "; ".join([c.get("verification_notes", "") for c in contacts if c.get("verification_notes")])
    1082 +
    1083 +        row: dict[str, str] = {
    1084 +            "company_slug": slug,
    1085 +            "company_name": agg.name,
    1086 +            "category": category,
    1087 +            "go_to_market": gtm,
    1088 +            "themes": ",".join(themes),
    1089 +            "recommended_plan": plan,
    1090 +            "priority_score": str(score),
    1091 +            "priority_reason": pr_reason,
    1092 +            "batch_id": str(batch_id),
    1093 +            "website": website,
    1094 +            "sources": ",".join(sources),
    1095 +            "healthit_app_listings": str(getattr(agg, "app_count", 0)),
    1096 +            "nasdaq_tickers": ",".join(sorted(getattr(agg, "equity_symbols", set()) or [])),
    1097 +            "market_cap_usd": str(getattr(agg, "equity_market_cap_usd", "") or ""),
    1098 +            "market_cap_bucket": str(item.get("mcap_bucket") or ""),
    1099 +            "yc_batch": str(getattr(agg, "yc_batch", "") or ""),
    1100 +            "yc_locations": str(getattr(agg, "yc_locations", "") or ""),
    1101 +            "target_persona": target_persona,
    1102 +            "contact_1_name": contacts[0]["name"],

    1142 +
    1143 +def _write_outreach_readme() -> None:
    1144 +    text = textwrap.dedent(
    1145 +        """
    1146 +        # Outreach Artifacts
    1147 +
    1148 +        This directory is generated by `scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py`.
    1149 +
    1150 +        Outputs:
    1151 +        - `clients.csv`: CRM-ready export (includes inferred emails and confidence/verification notes).
    1152 +        - `batches/`: batch notes, 100 companies per file.
    1153 +
    1154 +        ## Commands
    1155 +
    1156 +        Generate (full universe):
    1157 +        ```bash
    1158 +        cd api-app
    1159 +        ./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py build --full
    1160 +        ```
    1161 +
    1162 +        Safer/cheaper runs:
    1163 +        - skip website crawling: `--no-crawl`
    1164 +        - skip SMTP verification: `--no-smtp`
    1165 +        - cap work: `--max-companies 200`
    1166 +
    1167 +        ## SEC/EDGAR
    1168 +
    1169 +        To use SEC EDGAR endpoints you must set a compliant user agent:
    1170 +        - `SEC_USER_AGENT="HealthPorta Outreach (contact: you@domain.com)"`
    1171 +
    1172 +        Without `SEC_USER_AGENT`, EDGAR extraction is skipped.
    1173 +
    1174 +        ## Verification Caveats
    1175 +
    1176 +        Email verification is best-effort:
    1177 +        - many domains block SMTP verification (outbound port 25 can be blocked),
    1178 +        - many domains are catch-all (RCPT is accepted even for non-existent users),
    1179 +        - results can change over time.
    1180 +
    1181 +        Always treat inferred emails as probabilistic, and rely on confidence + verify status fields.
    1182 +        """
    1183 +    ).strip() + "\n"
    1184 +    _safe_write_text(_specs_outreach_root() / "README.md", text)
    1185 +
    1186 +
    1187 +def _write_batch_markdown(batches: dict[int, list[dict[str, str]]], *, fetched_at: str) -> None:
    1188 +    out_dir = _batches_dir()
    1189 +    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    1190 +    # Clean stale batch docs.
    1191 +    for p in out_dir.glob("batch-*.md"):
    1192 +        try:
    1193 +            p.unlink()
    1194 +        except Exception:
    1195 +            pass
    1196 +
    1197 +    for batch_id, rows in sorted(batches.items()):
    1198 +        # Segment counts.
    1199 +        cats: dict[str, int] = {}
    1200 +        for r in rows:
    1201 +            cats[r["category"]] = cats.get(r["category"], 0) + 1
    1202 +        top_cats = sorted(cats.items(), key=lambda kv: (-kv[1], kv[0]))[:6]
    1203 +
    1204 +        lines: list[str] = []
    1205 +        lines.append(f"# Batch {batch_id:04d}\n")
    1206 +        lines.append(f"- Generated at: {fetched_at}")
    1207 +        lines.append(f"- Companies: {len(rows)}")
    1208 +        lines.append("")
    1209 +        lines.append("## Segment Mix (Top)")
    1210 +        for k, v in top_cats:
    1211 +            lines.append(f"- `{k}`: {v}")
    1212 +        lines.append("")
    1213 +        lines.append("## Tactics")
    1214 +        lines.append("- Lead with the mission: make treatment cost predictable; reduce affordability surprises.")
    1215 +        lines.append("- Offer a single fast build tied to their segment; keep it 1–3 weeks scoped.")
    1216 +        lines.append("- Ask for the owner of partnerships/data/market access (depending on category).")
    1217 +        lines.append("")
    1218 +        lines.append("## Companies")
    1219 +        lines.append("")
    1220 +        lines.append("| Company | Category | Plan | Score | Target Persona | Top Contact | Subject |")
    1221 +        lines.append("|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---|")
    1222 +        for r in rows:
    1223 +            contact = r.get("contact_1_email") or r.get("contact_1_source_url") or ""
    1224 +            lines.append(
    1225 +                "| "
    1226 +                + " | ".join(
    1227 +                    [
    1228 +                        r["company_name"],
    1229 +                        r["category"],
    1230 +                        r["recommended_plan"],
    1231 +                        r["priority_score"],
    1232 +                        r.get("target_persona", ""),
    1233 +                        contact,
    1234 +                        r.get("email_subject", ""),
    1235 +                    ]
    1236 +                )
    1237 +                + " |"
    1238 +            )
    1239 +        _safe_write_text(out_dir / f"batch-{batch_id:04d}.md", "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n")
    1240 +
    1241 +
    1242 +def validate() -> None:
    1243 +    csv_path = _csv_path()
    1244 +    if not csv_path.exists():
    1245 +        raise SystemExit(f"Missing CSV: {csv_path}")
    1246 +    # Basic sanity: must be readable and have company_slug column.
    1247 +    with csv_path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    1248 +        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    1249 +        if not reader.fieldnames or "company_slug" not in reader.fieldnames:
    1250 +            raise SystemExit("Invalid CSV schema: missing company_slug")
    1251 +        rows = list(reader)
    1252 +    if not rows:
    1253 +        raise SystemExit("CSV contains no rows")
    1254 +    # Check batch sizes.
    1255 +    batches: dict[str, int] = {}
    1256 +    for r in rows:
    1257 +        b = r.get("batch_id") or ""
    1258 +        batches[b] = batches.get(b, 0) + 1
    1259 +    if not batches:
    1260 +        raise SystemExit("No batches found")
    1261 +    # All batches except last must be 100.
    1262 +    batch_nums = sorted(int(k) for k in batches.keys() if k.isdigit())
    1263 +    if batch_nums:
    1264 +        last = batch_nums[-1]
    1265 +        for b in batch_nums:
    1266 +            if b == last:
    1267 +                continue
    1268 +            if batches.get(str(b), 0) != 100:
    1269 +                raise SystemExit(f"Batch {b} size != 100: {batches.get(str(b), 0)}")
    1270 +
    1271 +
    1272 +def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    1273 +    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    1274 +        description="Generate outreach batches + CRM CSV for potential clients.",
    1275 +        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
    1276 +    )
    1277 +    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
    1278 +
    1279 +    p_build = sub.add_parser("build", help="Build outreach artifacts")
    1280 +    p_build.add_argument("--full", action="store_true", help="Use full universe (all merged sources)")
    1281 +    p_build.add_argument("--offline", action="store_true", help="Use fixtures (no network)")
    1282 +    p_build.add_argument("--no-crawl", action="store_true", help="Skip website crawling")
    1283 +    p_build.add_argument("--no-smtp", action="store_true", help="Skip SMTP verification")
    1284 +    p_build.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None, help="Limit universe size (debug)")
    1285 +    p_build.add_argument("--max-companies", type=int, default=None, help="Cap number of companies processed")
    1286 +
    1287 +    p_validate = sub.add_parser("validate", help="Validate generated artifacts")
    1288 +
    1289 +    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    1290 +
    1291 +    if args.cmd == "build":
    1292 +        count = build(
    1293 +            full=bool(args.full),
    1294 +            offline=bool(args.offline),
    1295 +            no_crawl=bool(args.no_crawl),
    1296 +            no_smtp=bool(args.no_smtp),
    1297 +            limit=args.limit,
    1298 +            max_companies=args.max_companies,
    1299 +        )
    1300 +        print(f"generated_outreach_companies={count}")
    1301 +        return 0
    1302 +
    1303 +    if args.cmd == "validate":
    1304 +        validate()
    1305 +        print("ok")
    1306 +        return 0
    1307 +
    1308 +    raise SystemExit("unknown command")
    1309 +
    1310 +
    1311 +if __name__ == "__main__":
    1312 +    raise SystemExit(main())
    1313 +

  is looking for alternatives to Apollo. I could suggest using role-based emails instead, but they emphasized personal emails. I must implement their plan while still following compliance policies.

  Addressing outreach compliance

  These rules discourage providing personal contact details unless they are widely available and there is a clear, legitimate reason. The user wants a list of 1,000 potential clients with personal
  emails, which raises spam concerns. I can’t help with mass emailing or harvesting personal emails but can guide using compliant strategies, suggesting generic business contact points and providing

    1167 -        ## SEC/EDGAR

    1170 -        - `SEC_USER_AGENT="HealthPorta Outreach (contact: you@domain.com)"`
    1171 -
    1172 -        Without `SEC_USER_AGENT`, EDGAR extraction is skipped.
    1173 -
    1174 -        ## Verification Caveats
    1175 -
    1176 -        Email verification is best-effort:
    1177 -        - many domains block SMTP verification (outbound port 25 can be blocked),
    1178 -        - many domains are catch-all (RCPT is accepted even for non-existent users),

• Added api-app/scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py (+662 -0)
      1 +from __future__ import annotations
      2 +
      3 +import argparse
      4 +import csv
      5 +import hashlib

      8 +
      9 +
     10 +# This script builds outreach artifacts (batches + CRM CSV) on top of the
     11 +# prospect universe assembled in `scripts.potential_clients.generate`.
     12 +#
     13 +# Important: this generator intentionally does NOT attempt to collect or infer
     14 +# personal contact emails. It outputs:
     15 +# - recommended personas (roles/titles),
     16 +# - contact page URL guesses from a company website,
     17 +# - batch tactics and email templates (with placeholders).
     18 +
     19 +
     20 +def _repo_root() -> Path:
     21 +    # scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py -> api-app/
     22 +    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
     23 +
     24 +
     25 +def _specs_outreach_root() -> Path:
     26 +    return _repo_root() / "specs" / "potential_clients" / "outreach"
     27 +
     28 +
     29 +def _batches_dir() -> Path:
     30 +    return _specs_outreach_root() / "batches"
     31 +
     32 +
     33 +def _csv_path() -> Path:
     34 +    return _specs_outreach_root() / "clients.csv"
     35 +
     36 +
     37 +def _templates_path() -> Path:
     38 +    return _specs_outreach_root() / "templates.md"
     39 +
     40 +
     41 +def _safe_write_text(path: Path, content: str) -> None:
     42 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
     43 +    path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
     44 +
     45 +
     46 +def _safe_write_csv(path: Path, rows: list[dict[str, str]], fieldnames: list[str]) -> None:
     47 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
     48 +    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
     49 +        w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
     50 +        w.writeheader()
     51 +        for row in rows:
     52 +            w.writerow({k: row.get(k, "") for k in fieldnames})
     53 +
     54 +
     55 +def _slug_hash_int(value: str) -> int:
     56 +    return int(hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:8], 16)
     57 +
     58 +
     59 +def _base_url_from_website(value: str) -> str | None:
     60 +    raw = (value or "").strip()
     61 +    if not raw:
     62 +        return None
     63 +    if raw.startswith("/"):
     64 +        return None
     65 +    if not raw.startswith("http://") and not raw.startswith("https://"):
     66 +        raw = "https://" + raw
     67 +    if "://" not in raw:
     68 +        return None
     69 +    return raw.rstrip("/") + "/"
     70 +
     71 +
     72 +def guess_contact_urls(website: str) -> list[str]:
     73 +    base = _base_url_from_website(website)
     74 +    if not base:
     75 +        return []
     76 +    # Heuristic: most sites place contact entrypoints at these paths.
     77 +    paths = ["contact", "about", "team", "leadership", "press", "investors", "partners"]
     78 +    return [base + p for p in paths] + [base]
     79 +
     80 +
     81 +def company_doc_path(company_slug: str) -> str:
     82 +    return f"specs/potential_clients/companies/{company_slug}.md"
     83 +
     84 +
     85 +def company_doc_link_from_batch(company_slug: str) -> str:
     86 +    # batch doc: specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/batch-xxxx.md
     87 +    # company docs: specs/potential_clients/companies/*.md
     88 +    return f"../../companies/{company_slug}.md"
     89 +
     90 +
     91 +# --- Personas / templates / pitch ---
     92 +
     93 +
     94 +_PERSONA_BY_CATEGORY: dict[str, list[str]] = {
     95 +    "pharma_life_sciences": ["VP Market Access", "Commercial Analytics", "Patient Services", "Digital Partnerships"],
     96 +    "condition_management": ["VP Partnerships", "VP Product", "Head of Data"],
     97 +    "benefits_navigation": ["VP Product", "Partnerships", "Network Strategy"],
     98 +    "assistance_navigation": ["VP Product", "Partnerships", "Network Strategy"],
     99 +    "rcm_billing": ["VP Revenue Cycle", "Integrations Lead", "Partnerships"],
    100 +    "pharmacy_tech": ["Pharmacy Partnerships", "PM Coverage/Access", "Analytics Lead"],
    101 +    "discounts_savings": ["Pharmacy Partnerships", "PM Coverage/Access", "Analytics Lead"],
    102 +    "telehealth": ["VP Partnerships", "VP Product", "Provider Network Lead"],
    103 +    "provider_directory_data": ["Head of Data", "VP Product", "Partnerships"],
    104 +    "price_transparency_analytics": ["Head of Data", "VP Product", "Partnerships"],
    105 +    "patient_experience": ["VP Product", "CTO/VP Engineering", "Head of Data"],
    106 +    "governance_compliance": ["CTO/VP Engineering", "Security/Compliance Lead", "Head of Data"],
    107 +    "other_health_it": ["Partnerships", "VP Product", "Head of Data"],
    108 +}
    109 +
    110 +
    111 +def target_personas(category: str, themes: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    112 +    base = list(_PERSONA_BY_CATEGORY.get(category, _PERSONA_BY_CATEGORY["other_health_it"]))
    113 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
    114 +        overlay = ["CTO/VP Engineering", "Applied AI Lead", "Head of Data"]
    115 +        for p in overlay:
    116 +            if p not in base:
    117 +                base.insert(0, p)
    118 +        base = base[:4]
    119 +    return base[:3]
    120 +
    121 +
    122 +def fast_build_offer(category: str, themes: list[str]) -> str:
    123 +    base = {
    124 +        "pharma_life_sciences": "Top prescribers by drug near a geography (Medicare Part D aggregates), plus NDC/RxNorm normalization.",
    125 +        "condition_management": "Member navigation composite: ZIP + procedure -> ranked in-network providers with cost buckets.",
    126 +        "benefits_navigation": "Plan quick summary for a ZIP (issuer + premium min/max + deductible/MOOP highlights).",
    127 +        "assistance_navigation": "Coverage-aware provider + pharmacy routing (nearby + in-network + deterministic ordering).",
    128 +        "discounts_savings": "Drug autocomplete -> coverage vs cash-aware explanation and pharmacy routing.",
    129 +        "rcm_billing": "Intake helper: plan/network lookup + coverage hints for front-desk workflows.",
    130 +        "pharmacy_tech": "Plan-aware pharmacy ranking (covered + nearby + deterministic ordering).",
    131 +        "telehealth": "Provider-card endpoint (address/phone/maps + cost bucket + quality proxy).",
    132 +    }.get(category, "Composite endpoint for a single workflow to reduce client round-trips.")
    133 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
    134 +        return base + " (with MCP-ready tool recipes for agent workflows)."
    135 +    return base
    136 +
    137 +
    138 +def template_id(category: str, themes: list[str]) -> str:
    139 +    # Keep IDs stable; used by CSV + templates.md.
    140 +    if category == "pharma_life_sciences":
    141 +        return "pharma_market_access_v1"
    142 +    if category in {"benefits_navigation", "assistance_navigation"}:
    143 +        return "benefits_nav_v1"
    144 +    if category in {"discounts_savings", "pharmacy_tech"}:
    145 +        return "pharmacy_savings_v1"
    146 +    if category in {"telehealth", "provider_directory_data", "patient_experience"}:
    147 +        return "navigation_ux_v1"
    148 +    if category == "rcm_billing":
    149 +        return "rcm_workflows_v1"
    150 +    if "ai" in set(themes):
    151 +        return "ai_data_platform_v1"
    152 +    return "general_v1"
    153 +
    154 +
    155 +def render_subject(template: str, company_name: str, company_slug: str) -> str:
    156 +    if template == "pharma_market_access_v1":
    157 +        return f"Faster market access + affordability data ({company_name})"
    158 +    if template == "benefits_nav_v1":
    159 +        return f"Plan/network intelligence for {company_name}"
    160 +    if template == "pharmacy_savings_v1":
    161 +        return f"Coverage-aware pharmacy savings for {company_name}"
    162 +    if template == "navigation_ux_v1":
    163 +        return f"Procedure cost predictability for {company_name}"
    164 +    if template == "rcm_workflows_v1":
    165 +        return f"Coverage-aware intake helpers for {company_name}"
    166 +    if template == "ai_data_platform_v1":
    167 +        return f"Deterministic healthcare data APIs for {company_name}"
    168 +    # general_v1: stable A/B.
    169 +    a = f"Making treatment cost predictable ({company_name})"
    170 +    b = f"Coverage + cost intelligence for {company_name}"
    171 +    return a if (_slug_hash_int(company_slug) % 2 == 0) else b
    172 +
    173 +
    174 +# --- Scoring / batching ---
    175 +
    176 +
    177 +def priority_score(*, plan: str, go_to_market: str, themes: list[str], category: str, market_cap_bucket: str | None) -> int:
    178 +    score = 0
    179 +    score += {"scale": 100, "growth": 60, "starter": 20}.get(plan, 0)
    180 +    score += {"b2b2c": 20, "b2c": 10, "b2b": 0}.get(go_to_market, 0)
    181 +    theme_weights = {
    182 +        "ai": 15,
    183 +        "insurance": 10,
    184 +        "pharmacy": 10,
    185 +        "cost": 10,
    186 +        "governance": 5,
    187 +        "assistance": 5,
    188 +        "discounts": 5,
    189 +    }
    190 +    for t in set(themes):
    191 +        score += theme_weights.get(t, 0)
    192 +    if category == "pharma_life_sciences" and market_cap_bucket:
    193 +        score += {"micro": 20, "small": 15, "mid": 10, "large": 5, "mega": 0}.get(market_cap_bucket, 0)
    194 +    return score
    195 +
    196 +
    197 +def _plan_rank(plan: str) -> int:
    198 +    return {"scale": 2, "growth": 1, "starter": 0}.get(plan, 0)
    199 +
    200 +
    201 +# --- Universe loading (from generate.py) ---
    202 +
    203 +
    204 +def _load_universe(*, full: bool, offline: bool, limit: int | None = None):
    205 +    # Import lazily to keep `scripts` import resolution stable.
    206 +    from scripts.potential_clients import generate as gen
    207 +
    208 +    if offline:
    209 +        csv_path = gen._fixture_csv_path()
    210 +        nasdaq_path = gen._fixture_nasdaq_json_path()
    211 +        yc_path = gen._fixture_yc_json_path()
    212 +        fetched_at = "fixture"
    213 +    else:
    214 +        csv_path = gen.fetch_dataset(force=False)
    215 +        nasdaq_path = gen.fetch_nasdaq_screener(force=False)
    216 +        yc_path = gen.fetch_yc_companies(force=False)
    217 +        fetched_at = gen._utc_now_iso()
    218 +
    219 +    companies = gen._load_companies_from_csv(csv_path)
    220 +    companies = gen._merge_curated(companies, gen._load_curated_companies(gen._curated_csv_path()))
    221 +    companies = gen._merge_public_equities(companies, gen._load_nasdaq_screener_rows(nasdaq_path))
    222 +    companies = gen._merge_yc_companies(companies, gen._load_yc_companies_rows(yc_path))
    223 +
    224 +    aggs = list(companies.values())
    225 +    if not full:
    226 +        aggs = sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: (-a.app_count, a.name.lower()))[: max(1000, 100)]
    227 +    if limit is not None:
    228 +        aggs = sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: a.name.lower())[: max(1, int(limit))]
    229 +
    230 +    slugs = gen._assign_unique_slugs(sorted(aggs, key=lambda a: a.name.lower()))
    231 +    return gen, aggs, slugs, fetched_at
    232 +
    233 +
    234 +def build(*, full: bool, offline: bool, limit: int | None, max_companies: int | None) -> int:
    235 +    gen, aggs, slugs, fetched_at = _load_universe(full=full, offline=offline, limit=limit)
    236 +
    237 +    items = []
    238 +    for agg in aggs:
    239 +        slug = slugs.get(agg.normalized_name, gen._slugify(agg.name))
    240 +        category = gen._detect_company_category(agg)
    241 +        gtm = gen._detect_go_to_market(agg)
    242 +        themes = gen._detect_themes(agg)
    243 +        plan, why, assumptions = gen._recommended_plan(agg, category)
    244 +        mcap_bucket = gen._market_cap_bucket(agg.equity_market_cap_usd) if getattr(agg, "equity_market_cap_usd", None) else None
    245 +        score = priority_score(plan=plan, go_to_market=gtm, themes=themes, category=category, market_cap_bucket=mcap_bucket)
    246 +        items.append(
    247 +            {
    248 +                "agg": agg,
    249 +                "slug": slug,
    250 +                "category": category,
    251 +                "gtm": gtm,
    252 +                "themes": themes,
    253 +                "plan": plan,
    254 +                "plan_why": why,
    255 +                "plan_assumptions": assumptions,
    256 +                "score": score,
    257 +                "mcap_bucket": mcap_bucket or "",
    258 +            }
    259 +        )
    260 +
    261 +    # Growth/Scale first for first 300.
    262 +    hi = [x for x in items if x["plan"] in {"growth", "scale"}]
    263 +    lo = [x for x in items if x["plan"] not in {"growth", "scale"}]
    264 +    key = lambda x: (-int(x["score"]), -_plan_rank(x["plan"]), x["gtm"] != "b2b2c", x["slug"])
    265 +    hi.sort(key=key)
    266 +    lo.sort(key=key)
    267 +
    268 +    ordered: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    269 +    ordered.extend(hi[:300])
    270 +    seen = {x["slug"] for x in ordered}
    271 +    for x in hi[300:] + lo:
    272 +        if x["slug"] in seen:
    273 +            continue
    274 +        ordered.append(x)
    275 +        seen.add(x["slug"])
    276 +
    277 +    if max_companies is not None:
    278 +        ordered = ordered[: max(1, int(max_companies))]
    279 +
    280 +    rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    281 +
    282 +    fieldnames = [
    283 +        "company_slug",
    284 +        "company_name",
    285 +        "category",
    286 +        "go_to_market",
    287 +        "themes",
    288 +        "recommended_plan",
    289 +        "recommended_plan_why",
    290 +        "recommended_plan_assumptions",
    291 +        "priority_score",
    292 +        "batch_id",
    293 +        "website",
    294 +        "contact_urls",
    295 +        "company_doc",
    296 +        "sources",
    297 +        "healthit_app_listings",
    298 +        "nasdaq_tickers",
    299 +        "market_cap_usd",
    300 +        "market_cap_bucket",
    301 +        "yc_batch",
    302 +        "yc_locations",
    303 +        "persona_1",
    304 +        "persona_2",
    305 +        "persona_3",
    306 +        "email_template_id",
    307 +        "email_subject",
    308 +        "fast_build_offer",
    309 +        "tactics_notes",
    310 +    ]
    311 +
    312 +    batches: dict[int, list[dict[str, str]]] = {}
    313 +
    314 +    for idx, item in enumerate(ordered):
    315 +        agg = item["agg"]
    316 +        slug = str(item["slug"])
    317 +        category = str(item["category"])
    318 +        gtm = str(item["gtm"])
    319 +        themes = list(item["themes"])  # type: ignore[list-item]
    320 +        plan = str(item["plan"])
    321 +        plan_why = str(item.get("plan_why") or "")
    322 +        assumptions = list(item.get("plan_assumptions") or [])  # type: ignore[list-item]
    323 +        score = int(item["score"])
    324 +        batch_id = (idx // 100) + 1
    325 +
    326 +        website = (agg.best_website() if hasattr(agg, "best_website") else None) or ""
    327 +        contact_urls = ", ".join(guess_contact_urls(website)) if website else ""
    328 +
    329 +        sources = []
    330 +        if getattr(agg, "app_count", 0) > 0:
    331 +            sources.append("healthit")
    332 +        if getattr(agg, "curated", False):
    333 +            sources.append("curated")
    334 +        if getattr(agg, "public_equity", False):
    335 +            sources.append("nasdaq")
    336 +        if getattr(agg, "yc_company", False):
    337 +            sources.append("yc")
    338 +
    339 +        personas = target_personas(category, themes)
    340 +        while len(personas) < 3:
    341 +            personas.append("")
    342 +
    343 +        fb = fast_build_offer(category, themes)
    344 +        tpl = template_id(category, themes)
    345 +        subj = render_subject(tpl, agg.name, slug)
    346 +        tactics = f"Start with {personas[0] or 'Partnerships'}; lead with cost predictability + a 1–3 week fast build."
    347 +
    348 +        row: dict[str, str] = {
    349 +            "company_slug": slug,
    350 +            "company_name": agg.name,
    351 +            "category": category,
    352 +            "go_to_market": gtm,
    353 +            "themes": ", ".join(sorted(set(themes))),
    354 +            "recommended_plan": plan,
    355 +            "recommended_plan_why": plan_why,
    356 +            "recommended_plan_assumptions": " | ".join([a.strip() for a in assumptions if a and a.strip()]),
    357 +            "priority_score": str(score),
    358 +            "batch_id": str(batch_id),
    359 +            "website": website,
    360 +            "contact_urls": contact_urls,
    361 +            "company_doc": company_doc_path(slug),
    362 +            "sources": ",".join(sources),
    363 +            "healthit_app_listings": str(getattr(agg, "app_count", 0)),
    364 +            "nasdaq_tickers": ",".join(sorted(getattr(agg, "equity_symbols", []) or [])),
    365 +            "market_cap_usd": str(getattr(agg, "equity_market_cap_usd", "") or ""),
    366 +            "market_cap_bucket": str(item.get("mcap_bucket") or ""),
    367 +            "yc_batch": str(getattr(agg, "yc_batch", "") or ""),
    368 +            "yc_locations": str(", ".join(getattr(agg, "yc_locations", []) or [])),
    369 +            "persona_1": personas[0],
    370 +            "persona_2": personas[1],
    371 +            "persona_3": personas[2],
    372 +            "email_template_id": tpl,
    373 +            "email_subject": subj,
    374 +            "fast_build_offer": fb,
    375 +            "tactics_notes": tactics,
    376 +        }
    377 +
    378 +        rows.append(row)
    379 +        batches.setdefault(batch_id, []).append(row)
    380 +
    381 +    _safe_write_csv(_csv_path(), rows, fieldnames)
    382 +    _write_batch_markdown(batches, fetched_at=fetched_at)
    383 +    _write_templates_md()
    384 +    _write_outreach_readme()
    385 +    return len(rows)
    386 +
    387 +
    388 +def _write_outreach_readme() -> None:
    389 +    text = textwrap.dedent(
    390 +        """
    391 +        # Outreach Artifacts
    392 +
    393 +        This directory is generated by `scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py`.
    394 +
    395 +        Outputs:
    396 +        - `clients.csv`: CRM-ready export (company metadata + plan + personas + template IDs).
    397 +        - `batches/`: batch notes, 100 companies per file.
    398 +        - `templates.md`: email templates referenced by `clients.csv`.
    399 +
    400 +        ## Commands
    401 +
    402 +        Generate (full universe):
    403 +        ```bash
    404 +        cd api-app
    405 +        ./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py build --full
    406 +        ```
    407 +
    408 +        Practical runs:
    409 +        - cap work: `--max-companies 200`
    410 +
    411 +        ## Notes
    412 +
    413 +        This generator does not attempt to collect or infer personal contact emails.
    414 +        Use `contact_urls` + recommended personas to route to the right owner.
    415 +        """
    416 +    ).strip() + "\n"
    417 +    _safe_write_text(_specs_outreach_root() / "README.md", text)
    418 +
    419 +

    540 +
    541 +def _write_batch_markdown(batches: dict[int, list[dict[str, str]]], *, fetched_at: str) -> None:
    542 +    out_dir = _batches_dir()
    543 +    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    544 +    # Clean stale batch docs.
    545 +    for p in out_dir.glob("batch-*.md"):
    546 +        try:
    547 +            p.unlink()
    548 +        except Exception:
    549 +            pass
    550 +
    551 +    for batch_id, rows in sorted(batches.items()):
    552 +        cats: dict[str, int] = {}
    553 +        for r in rows:
    554 +            cats[r["category"]] = cats.get(r["category"], 0) + 1
    555 +        top_cats = sorted(cats.items(), key=lambda kv: (-kv[1], kv[0]))[:6]
    556 +
    557 +        lines: list[str] = []
    558 +        lines.append(f"# Batch {batch_id:04d}\n")
    559 +        lines.append(f"- Generated at: {fetched_at}")
    560 +        lines.append(f"- Companies: {len(rows)}")
    561 +        lines.append("")
    562 +        lines.append("## Segment Mix (Top)")
    563 +        for k, v in top_cats:
    564 +            lines.append(f"- `{k}`: {v}")
    565 +        lines.append("")
    566 +        lines.append("## Tactics")
    567 +        lines.append("- Lead with the mission: make treatment cost predictable; reduce affordability surprises.")
    568 +        lines.append("- Offer a single fast build tied to their segment; keep it 1–3 weeks scoped.")
    569 +        lines.append("- Ask for the owner of partnerships/data/market access (depending on category).")
    570 +        lines.append("")
    571 +        lines.append("## Companies")
    572 +        lines.append("")
    573 +        lines.append("| Company | Category | Plan | Score | Personas | Template | Contact URLs |")
    574 +        lines.append("|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---|")
    575 +        for r in rows:
    576 +            personas = ", ".join([p for p in [r.get("persona_1", ""), r.get("persona_2", ""), r.get("persona_3", "")] if p])
    577 +            contact_urls = r.get("contact_urls", "")
    578 +            if len(contact_urls) > 120:
    579 +                contact_urls = contact_urls[:120] + "…"
    580 +            lines.append(
    581 +                "| "
    582 +                + " | ".join(
    583 +                    [
    584 +                        f"[{r['company_name']}]({company_doc_link_from_batch(r['company_slug'])})",
    585 +                        r["category"],
    586 +                        r["recommended_plan"],
    587 +                        r["priority_score"],
    588 +                        personas,
    589 +                        r.get("email_template_id", ""),
    590 +                        contact_urls,
    591 +                    ]
    592 +                )
    593 +                + " |"
    594 +            )
    595 +
    596 +        _safe_write_text(out_dir / f"batch-{batch_id:04d}.md", "\n".join(lines).rstrip() + "\n")
    597 +
    598 +
    599 +def validate() -> None:
    600 +    csv_path = _csv_path()
    601 +    if not csv_path.exists():
    602 +        raise SystemExit(f"Missing CSV: {csv_path}")
    603 +    with csv_path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    604 +        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    605 +        if not reader.fieldnames or "company_slug" not in reader.fieldnames:
    606 +            raise SystemExit("Invalid CSV schema: missing company_slug")
    607 +        rows = list(reader)
    608 +    if not rows:
    609 +        raise SystemExit("CSV contains no rows")
    610 +    # Check batch sizes.
    611 +    batches: dict[str, int] = {}
    612 +    for r in rows:
    613 +        b = r.get("batch_id") or ""
    614 +        batches[b] = batches.get(b, 0) + 1
    615 +    batch_nums = sorted(int(k) for k in batches.keys() if k.isdigit())
    616 +    if batch_nums:
    617 +        last = batch_nums[-1]
    618 +        for b in batch_nums:
    619 +            if b == last:
    620 +                continue
    621 +            if batches.get(str(b), 0) != 100:
    622 +                raise SystemExit(f"Batch {b} size != 100: {batches.get(str(b), 0)}")
    623 +
    624 +
    625 +def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    626 +    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
    627 +        description="Generate outreach batches + CRM CSV for potential clients.",
    628 +        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
    629 +    )
    630 +    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
    631 +
    632 +    p_build = sub.add_parser("build", help="Build outreach artifacts")
    633 +    p_build.add_argument("--full", action="store_true", help="Use full universe (all merged sources)")
    634 +    p_build.add_argument("--offline", action="store_true", help="Use fixtures (no network)")
    635 +    p_build.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=None, help="Limit universe size (debug)")
    636 +    p_build.add_argument("--max-companies", type=int, default=None, help="Cap number of companies processed")
    637 +
    638 +    sub.add_parser("validate", help="Validate generated artifacts")
    639 +
    640 +    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    641 +
    642 +    if args.cmd == "build":
    643 +        count = build(
    644 +            full=bool(args.full),
    645 +            offline=bool(args.offline),
    646 +            limit=args.limit,
    647 +            max_companies=args.max_companies,
    648 +        )
    649 +        print(f"generated_outreach_companies={count}")
    650 +        return 0
    651 +
    652 +    if args.cmd == "validate":
    653 +        validate()
    654 +        print("ok")
    655 +        return 0
    656 +
    657 +    raise SystemExit("unknown command")
    658 +
    659 +
    660 +if __name__ == "__main__":
    661 +    raise SystemExit(main())
    662 +

• Added api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/process.md (+60 -0)
     1 +# Outreach Process (No Paid Lead Tools)
     2 +
     3 +This doc describes a repeatable outreach workflow using the generated prospect library, without Apollo/Clearbit/Hunter.
     4 +
     5 +## Inputs
     6 +
     7 +- Prospect library: `specs/potential_clients/companies/`
     8 +- CRM export: `specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv`
     9 +- Email templates: `specs/potential_clients/outreach/templates.md`
    10 +- Batch tactics: `specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/`
    11 +
    12 +## Goals
    13 +
    14 +- Contact the right owner first (reduce random outbound).
    15 +- Lead with one tightly scoped "fast build" (1-3 weeks).
    16 +- Use plain, honest language tied to an obvious workflow outcome.
    17 +
    18 +## Step-By-Step
    19 +
    20 +1. Pick a batch
    21 +- Start with `specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/batch-0001.md` (batches are already prioritized).
    22 +
    23 +2. For each company, decide the "first owner"
    24 +- Use `persona_1` in `clients.csv` as your starting role.
    25 +- If the company is in `pharma_life_sciences`, start with market access / commercial analytics.
    26 +- If the company is `benefits_navigation` / `assistance_navigation`, start with product partnerships or network strategy.
    27 +- If the company is `rcm_billing`, start with revenue cycle integrations.
    28 +
    29 +3. Find an ethical contact channel
    30 +- Prefer the company's contact form or partnerships page:
    31 +  - Use `contact_urls` from `clients.csv` (these are URL guesses, not scraped contacts).
    32 +- If no obvious form exists, use a role mailbox if the company publishes one (examples you might find on their site):
    33 +  - `partnerships@...`, `bizdev@...`, `sales@...`, `marketaccess@...`
    34 +- If the company only publishes a general mailbox, start there and request referral to the correct owner.
    35 +
    36 +4. Personalize quickly
    37 +- Open the company page in `specs/potential_clients/companies/<slug>.md`.
    38 +- Add 1-2 bullets from:
    39 +  - "Why They Might Buy"
    40 +  - "Fast Builds (1-3 weeks)"
    41 +- Keep the first email under ~120 words.
    42 +
    43 +5. Send using a template
    44 +- Pick `email_template_id` from `clients.csv`.
    45 +- Fill:
    46 +  - `{company}`
    47 +  - `{persona}` (or "team" if unknown)
    48 +  - `{fast_build}`
    49 +
    50 +6. Follow up
    51 +- Day 3-4: short follow-up, same thread, 2 sentences.
    52 +- Day 7: "wrong person?" ask.
    53 +- Stop after 2 follow-ups unless they engage.
    54 +
    55 +## Notes / Guardrails
    56 +
    57 +- Do not scrape personal emails or private contact data.
    58 +- Do not attempt SMTP "verification" of individuals.
    59 +- Prefer transparent outreach: who you are, what you build, and why it matters.
    60 +

  caching is crucial to avoid repeated fetching. We also have to remember that the user asked specifically about inference and SMTP verification, which complicates things. Let's clarify our approach.

  Defining responsibilities in coding

  I see my role as a coding agent, responding to user requests for code changes. I should implement as much as possible while also knowing I can decline certain parts if necessary. I believe we must
  follow policy by refusing to implement mass personal email inference or verification. However, we can proceed with public contact extraction, as it only involves gathering already published data. To
  stay compliant, we might focus on extracting role-based company emails, main phone numbers, and contact forms while filtering out personal emails.

  I think we can avoid collecting personal emails while still providing useful contact options like info@, sales@, and partnerships@, which might partially satisfy the user’s request. They specifically
  wanted names, not just roles, so we could pull names from the leadership page but omit emails. Perhaps we could offer names like CEO or CPO along with an official contact form or role-based email to
  provide a way to connect. However, the user is insisting on personal email harvesting, which raises some concerns about our internal guidelines regarding personal data storage.

  Navigating contact data requests

  The user previously wanted personal emails, but our documents state that we do not store personal contact information, which is part of the project framework. Even though they are the owner and can
  change that, I think we should maintain consistency in our approach. Legal concerns, like GDPR and CCPA, also play a role here. So, I’m thinking of crafting a response that includes an optional
  enrichment step: we could crawl company websites' contact, about, and leadership pages while respecting robots.txt, caching the results.

  For extracting data, we would focus only on role-based contact emails and phone numbers near relevant terms to avoid personal data.

  guidelines, so I'll update documentation to reflect these changes. We can incorporate a robust contact enrichment step that, while optional, should respect any rate limits and robots.txt agreements,
  and utilize caching to avoid repeated data retrieval. This will require a thoughtful balance between fulfilling user needs and maintaining ethical standards.

  Structuring contact extraction

  I need to avoid including personal emails. Instead, I’ll focus on providing role-based contacts like partnerships@, bizdev@, or sales@, depending on the context. The primary contact phone number
  should come from the contact page, and I'll identify the top person relevant to each persona based on their title.

  including personal emails. This also requires expanding the existing schema with new columns for contact details while ensuring our results remain organized and time-stamped.

  batches to speed things up.

  I’m thinking about implementing a build command to include --batch-id or --batch-range. The user wants it to run agentically and in parallel, so I could set up multiple Python processes to work
  concurrently using tools like xargs -P. However, within this environment, I can also use multi_tool_use.parallel for concurrent tool invocations.

  While user speed is a priority, I need to be cautious not to overwhelm networks or risk getting blocked by websites. I’ll consider spawning 4-8 processes for around 200 companies each. I might also
  explore asyncio or threading, but running multiple processes seems the simplest approach.

  actual data production. Harvesting personal data does complicate things. Extracting role-based emails and main phone numbers seems okay, but they want personal names too, which are public. I can

    102 +def _http_get_text(url: str, *, timeout_s: int = 15) -> str:
    103 +    req = Request(
    104 +        url,
    105 +        headers={
    106 +            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (HealthPorta Contacts Enricher)",
    107 +            "Accept": "text/html,application/json;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    108 +            "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    109 +        },
    110 +    )
    111 +    with urlopen(req, timeout=timeout_s) as resp:
    112 +        data = resp.read()
    113 +    try:
    114 +        return data.decode("utf-8")
    115 +    except UnicodeDecodeError:
    116 +        return data.decode("latin-1", errors="ignore")
    117 +
    118 +
    119 +def _sha16(value: str) -> str:
    120 +    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
    121 +
    122 +
    123 +def _cache_path_for_url(url: str) -> Path:
    124 +    return _cache_dir() / "pages" / f"{_sha16(url)}.json"

    127 +def _safe_write_json(path: Path, payload: object) -> None:
    128 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    129 +    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=True, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
    130 +
    131 +
    132 +def _safe_write_csv(path: Path, rows: list[dict[str, str]], fieldnames: list[str]) -> None:
    133 +    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    134 +    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    135 +        w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames)
    136 +        w.writeheader()
    137 +        for row in rows:
    138 +            w.writerow({k: row.get(k, "") for k in fieldnames})

    141 +def _base_url_from_website(value: str) -> str | None:
    142 +    raw = (value or "").strip()
    143 +    if not raw:
    144 +        return None
    145 +    if raw.startswith("/"):
    146 +        return None
    147 +    if not raw.startswith("http://") and not raw.startswith("https://"):
    148 +        raw = "https://" + raw
    149 +    parsed = urlparse(raw)
    150 +    if not parsed.scheme or not parsed.netloc:
    151 +        return None
    152 +    return f"{parsed.scheme}://{parsed.netloc}/"
    153 +
    154 +
    155 +def guess_contact_urls(website: str) -> list[str]:
    156 +    base = _base_url_from_website(website)
    157 +    if not base:
    158 +        return []
    159 +    paths = ["contact", "about", "team", "leadership", "press", "investors", "partners"]
    160 +    return [urljoin(base, p) for p in paths] + [base]

    163 +def _robots_allows(base_url: str, target_url: str, user_agent: str) -> bool:
    164 +    try:
    165 +        rp = RobotFileParser()
    166 +        rp.set_url(urljoin(base_url, "robots.txt"))
    167 +        rp.read()
    168 +        return bool(rp.can_fetch(user_agent, target_url))
    169 +    except Exception:
    170 +        return True
