    173 +class _ContactParser(HTMLParser):
    174 +    def __init__(self) -> None:
    175 +        super().__init__()
    176 +        self.mailtos: set[str] = set()
    177 +        self.tels: set[str] = set()
    178 +        self._text: list[str] = []
    179 +
    180 +    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:
    181 +        if tag.lower() != "a":
    182 +            return
    183 +        href = ""
    184 +        for k, v in attrs:
    185 +            if k.lower() == "href" and v:
    186 +                href = v.strip()
    187 +                break
    188 +        if not href:
    189 +            return
    190 +        if href.lower().startswith("mailto:"):
    191 +            email = href.split(":", 1)[1].split("?", 1)[0].strip()
    192 +            if email:
    193 +                self.mailtos.add(email.lower())
    194 +        if href.lower().startswith("tel:"):
    195 +            tel = href.split(":", 1)[1].split("?", 1)[0].strip()
    196 +            if tel:
    197 +                self.tels.add(tel)
    198 +
    199 +    def handle_data(self, data: str) -> None:
    200 +        if data:
    201 +            self._text.append(data)
    202 +
    203 +    def text(self) -> str:
    204 +        return " ".join(self._text)
    205 +
    206 +
    207 +def _extract_emails_from_text(text: str) -> set[str]:
    208 +    emails = set()
    209 +    for m in _EMAIL_RE.finditer(text or ""):
    210 +        email = m.group(0).strip().strip(".,;:()[]{}<>\"'")
    211 +        if "@" not in email:
    212 +            continue
    213 +        if len(email) > 254:
    214 +            continue
    215 +        emails.add(email.lower())
    216 +    return emails
    217 +
    218 +
    219 +def _extract_phones_from_text(text: str) -> set[str]:
    220 +    phones = set()
    221 +    for m in _PHONE_RE.finditer(text or ""):
    222 +        phones.add(m.group(0).strip())
    223 +    return phones
    224 +
    225 +
    226 +def _extract_jsonld_blocks(html: str) -> list[object]:
    227 +    blocks: list[object] = []
    228 +    if not html:
    229 +        return blocks
    230 +    for m in re.finditer(
    231 +        r"<script[^>]*type=[\"']application/ld\\+json[\"'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>",
    232 +        html,
    233 +        flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
    234 +    ):
    235 +        raw = (m.group(1) or "").strip()
    236 +        if not raw:
    237 +            continue
    238 +        try:
    239 +            blocks.append(json.loads(raw))
    240 +        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    241 +            continue
    242 +    return blocks
    243 +
    244 +
    245 +def _walk_json(obj: object) -> Iterable[dict[str, object]]:
    246 +    if isinstance(obj, dict):
    247 +        yield obj
    248 +        for v in obj.values():
    249 +            yield from _walk_json(v)
    250 +    elif isinstance(obj, list):
    251 +        for item in obj:
    252 +            yield from _walk_json(item)
    253 +
    254 +
    255 +@dataclass(frozen=True)
    256 +class Person:
    257 +    name: str
    258 +    title: str | None
    259 +    source_url: str
    260 +
    261 +
    262 +def _extract_people_from_jsonld(html: str, source_url: str) -> list[Person]:
    263 +    people: list[Person] = []
    264 +    for block in _extract_jsonld_blocks(html):
    265 +        for node in _walk_json(block):
    266 +            t = node.get("@type") or node.get("type")
    267 +            if isinstance(t, list):
    268 +                types = {str(x) for x in t}
    269 +            else:
    270 +                types = {str(t)}
    271 +            if "Person" not in types:
    272 +                continue
    273 +            name = str(node.get("name") or "").strip()
    274 +            if not name:
    275 +                continue
    276 +            title = str(node.get("jobTitle") or node.get("title") or "").strip() or None
    277 +            people.append(Person(name=name, title=title, source_url=source_url))
    278 +    # Dedupe by (name,title).
    279 +    seen: set[tuple[str, str | None]] = set()
    280 +    uniq: list[Person] = []
    281 +    for p in people:
    282 +        key = (p.name.strip().lower(), (p.title or "").strip().lower() or None)
    283 +        if key in seen:
    284 +            continue
    285 +        seen.add(key)
    286 +        uniq.append(p)
    287 +    return uniq
    288 +
    289 +
    290 +def _role_email(email: str) -> bool:
    291 +    local = (email.split("@", 1)[0] if "@" in email else "").lower().strip()
    292 +    local = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", local)
    293 +    return bool(local) and (local in _ROLE_LOCAL_PARTS)
    294 +
    295 +
    296 +def _rank_role_emails(emails: Iterable[str], *, category: str) -> list[str]:
    297 +    prefs_by_category = {
    298 +        "pharma_life_sciences": ["marketaccess", "access", "commercial", "patientservices", "partnerships", "press", "media", "ir", "info"],
    299 +        "rcm_billing": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    300 +        "benefits_navigation": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    301 +        "assistance_navigation": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    302 +        "pharmacy_tech": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    303 +        "discounts_savings": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    304 +        "telehealth": ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"],
    305 +    }
    306 +    prefs = prefs_by_category.get(category, ["partnerships", "bizdev", "sales", "support", "info"])
    307 +
    308 +    def score(e: str) -> int:
    309 +        local = (e.split("@", 1)[0] if "@" in e else "").lower().strip()
    310 +        local = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", local)
    311 +        try:
    312 +            return 100 - prefs.index(local)
    313 +        except ValueError:
    314 +            return 0
    315 +
    316 +    uniq = sorted(set(emails))
    317 +    uniq.sort(key=lambda e: (-score(e), e))
    318 +    return uniq
    319 +
    320 +
    321 +def _person_score(p: Person, personas: list[str]) -> int:
    322 +    t = (p.title or "").lower()
    323 +    s = 0
    324 +    for persona in personas:
    325 +        for token in persona.lower().split():
    326 +            if token and token in t:
    327 +                s += 3
    328 +    if any(k in t for k in ["chief", "cto", "cfo", "ceo", "president"]):
    329 +        s += 5
    330 +    if any(k in t for k in ["vp", "vice president"]):
    331 +        s += 4
    332 +    if "director" in t or "head" in t:
    333 +        s += 3
    334 +    return s
    335 +
    336 +
    337 +@dataclass
    338 +class PageContacts:
    339 +    url: str
    340 +    fetched_at: str
    341 +    emails: list[str]
    342 +    phones: list[str]
    343 +    people: list[Person]
    344 +    error: str | None = None
    345 +
    346 +
    347 +def fetch_contacts_for_url(url: str, *, timeout_s: int, respect_robots: bool, sleep_s: float) -> PageContacts:
    348 +    cache_path = _cache_path_for_url(url)
    349 +    if cache_path.exists():
    350 +        try:
    351 +            cached = json.loads(cache_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    352 +            people = [
    353 +                Person(name=str(p.get("name") or ""), title=(str(p.get("title") or "") or None), source_url=str(p.get("source_url") or url))
    354 +                for p in (cached.get("people") or [])
    355 +                if isinstance(p, dict)
    356 +            ]
    357 +            return PageContacts(
    358 +                url=url,
    359 +                fetched_at=str(cached.get("fetched_at") or ""),
    360 +                emails=[str(x) for x in (cached.get("emails") or []) if x],
    361 +                phones=[str(x) for x in (cached.get("phones") or []) if x],
    362 +                people=people,
    363 +                error=str(cached.get("error") or "") or None,
    364 +            )
    365 +        except Exception:
    366 +            # Fall through to re-fetch.
    367 +            pass
    368 +
    369 +    fetched_at = _utc_now_iso()
    370 +    ua = "Mozilla/5.0 (HealthPorta Contacts Enricher)"
    371 +
    372 +    base = _base_url_from_website(url)
    373 +    if respect_robots and base and not _robots_allows(base, url, ua):
    374 +        pc = PageContacts(url=url, fetched_at=fetched_at, emails=[], phones=[], people=[], error="robots_disallow")
    375 +        _safe_write_json(
    376 +            cache_path,
    377 +            {
    378 +                "url": url,
    379 +                "fetched_at": fetched_at,
    380 +                "emails": [],
    381 +                "phones": [],
    382 +                "people": [],
    383 +                "error": pc.error,
    384 +            },
    385 +        )
    386 +        return pc
    387 +
    388 +    try:
    389 +        html = _http_get_text(url, timeout_s=timeout_s)
    390 +        parser = _ContactParser()
    391 +        parser.feed(html)
    392 +        text = parser.text()
    393 +        emails = set(parser.mailtos) | _extract_emails_from_text(html) | _extract_emails_from_text(text)
    394 +        phones = set(parser.tels) | _extract_phones_from_text(text)
    395 +        people = _extract_people_from_jsonld(html, url)
    396 +        # Only keep role-based emails to avoid collecting personal emails.
    397 +        role_emails = sorted({e for e in emails if _role_email(e)})
    398 +        role_phones = sorted({p for p in phones if p})
    399 +        pc = PageContacts(url=url, fetched_at=fetched_at, emails=role_emails, phones=role_phones, people=people, error=None)
    400 +    except Exception as e:
    401 +        pc = PageContacts(url=url, fetched_at=fetched_at, emails=[], phones=[], people=[], error=str(e))
    402 +
    403 +    _safe_write_json(
    404 +        cache_path,
    405 +        {
    406 +            "url": url,
    407 +            "fetched_at": fetched_at,
    408 +            "emails": pc.emails,
    409 +            "phones": pc.phones,
    410 +            "people": [{"name": p.name, "title": p.title, "source_url": p.source_url} for p in pc.people],
    411 +            "error": pc.error,
    412 +        },
    413 +    )
    414 +    if sleep_s > 0:
    415 +        time.sleep(sleep_s)
    416 +    return pc
    417 +
    418 +
    419 +def load_clients_rows() -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    420 +    path = _clients_csv_path()
    421 +    if not path.exists():
    422 +        raise SystemExit(f"Missing clients.csv: {path}. Run outreach build first.")
    423 +    with path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    424 +        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
    425 +        return [dict(r) for r in reader]
    426 +
    427 +
    428 +def build_batch_contacts(
    429 +    *,
    430 +    batch_id: int,
    431 +    timeout_s: int,
    432 +    respect_robots: bool,
    433 +    sleep_s: float,
    434 +    max_pages: int,
    435 +) -> int:
    436 +    from scripts.potential_clients import outreach as out
    437 +
    438 +    rows = load_clients_rows()
    439 +    batch_rows = [r for r in rows if str(r.get("batch_id") or "") == str(batch_id)]
    440 +    if not batch_rows:
    441 +        raise SystemExit(f"No rows for batch_id={batch_id}")
    442 +
    443 +    out_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    444 +    fieldnames = [
    445 +        "company_slug",
    446 +        "company_name",
    447 +        "batch_id",
    448 +        "website",
    449 +        "category",
    450 +        "persona_1",
    451 +        "persona_2",
    452 +        "persona_3",
    453 +        "contact_email_1",
    454 +        "contact_email_1_source_url",
    455 +        "contact_email_2",
    456 +        "contact_email_2_source_url",
    457 +        "contact_email_3",
    458 +        "contact_email_3_source_url",
    459 +        "contact_phone_1",
    460 +        "contact_phone_1_source_url",
    461 +        "person_1_name",
    462 +        "person_1_title",
    463 +        "person_1_source_url",
    464 +        "person_2_name",
    465 +        "person_2_title",
    466 +        "person_2_source_url",
    467 +        "person_3_name",
    468 +        "person_3_title",
    469 +        "person_3_source_url",
    470 +        "visited_urls",
    471 +        "fetched_at",
    472 +        "errors",
    473 +    ]
    474 +
    475 +    for r in batch_rows:
    476 +        website = (r.get("website") or "").strip()
    477 +        urls = out.guess_contact_urls(website) if website else []
    478 +        urls = urls[: max_pages]
    479 +
    480 +        pages: list[PageContacts] = []
    481 +        for url in urls:
    482 +            pages.append(
    483 +                fetch_contacts_for_url(url, timeout_s=timeout_s, respect_robots=respect_robots, sleep_s=sleep_s)
    484 +            )
    485 +
    486 +        emails: list[str] = []
    487 +        email_src: dict[str, str] = {}
    488 +        phones: list[str] = []
    489 +        phone_src: dict[str, str] = {}
    490 +        people: list[Person] = []
    491 +        errors: list[str] = []
    492 +
    493 +        for p in pages:
    494 +            if p.error:
    495 +                errors.append(f"{p.url} => {p.error}")
    496 +            for e in p.emails:
    497 +                if e not in email_src:
    498 +                    email_src[e] = p.url
    499 +                emails.append(e)
    500 +            for ph in p.phones:
    501 +                if ph not in phone_src:
    502 +                    phone_src[ph] = p.url
    503 +                phones.append(ph)
    504 +            people.extend(p.people)
    505 +
    506 +        category = (r.get("category") or "").strip()
    507 +        personas = [r.get("persona_1") or "", r.get("persona_2") or "", r.get("persona_3") or ""]
    508 +
    509 +        ranked_emails = _rank_role_emails(set(emails), category=category)
    510 +        ranked_people = sorted(people, key=lambda p: _person_score(p, personas), reverse=True)
    511 +
    512 +        def pick(lst: list[str], i: int) -> str:
    513 +            return lst[i] if i < len(lst) else ""
    514 +
    515 +        def pick_person(i: int) -> Person | None:
    516 +            return ranked_people[i] if i < len(ranked_people) else None
    517 +
    518 +        p1 = pick_person(0)
    519 +        p2 = pick_person(1)
    520 +        p3 = pick_person(2)
    521 +
    522 +        # Prefer tel: numbers if present; otherwise fall back to regex matches.
    523 +        phone = phones[0] if phones else ""
    524 +
    525 +        out_rows.append(
    526 +            {
    527 +                "company_slug": (r.get("company_slug") or "").strip(),
    528 +                "company_name": (r.get("company_name") or "").strip(),
    529 +                "batch_id": str(batch_id),
    530 +                "website": website,
    531 +                "category": category,
    532 +                "persona_1": personas[0],
    533 +                "persona_2": personas[1],
    534 +                "persona_3": personas[2],
    535 +                "contact_email_1": pick(ranked_emails, 0),
    536 +                "contact_email_1_source_url": email_src.get(pick(ranked_emails, 0), ""),
    537 +                "contact_email_2": pick(ranked_emails, 1),
    538 +                "contact_email_2_source_url": email_src.get(pick(ranked_emails, 1), ""),
    539 +                "contact_email_3": pick(ranked_emails, 2),
    540 +                "contact_email_3_source_url": email_src.get(pick(ranked_emails, 2), ""),
    541 +                "contact_phone_1": phone,
    542 +                "contact_phone_1_source_url": phone_src.get(phone, ""),
    543 +                "person_1_name": (p1.name if p1 else ""),
    544 +                "person_1_title": (p1.title or "" if p1 else ""),
    545 +                "person_1_source_url": (p1.source_url if p1 else ""),
    546 +                "person_2_name": (p2.name if p2 else ""),
    547 +                "person_2_title": (p2.title or "" if p2 else ""),
    548 +                "person_2_source_url": (p2.source_url if p2 else ""),
    549 +                "person_3_name": (p3.name if p3 else ""),
    550 +                "person_3_title": (p3.title or "" if p3 else ""),
    551 +                "person_3_source_url": (p3.source_url if p3 else ""),
    552 +                "visited_urls": ", ".join([p.url for p in pages]),
    553 +                "fetched_at": _utc_now_iso(),
    554 +                "errors": " | ".join(errors)[:2000],
    555 +            }
    556 +        )
    557 +
    558 +    _safe_write_csv(_batch_contacts_csv_path(batch_id), out_rows, fieldnames)
    559 +    return len(out_rows)
    560 +
    561 +
    562 +def merge_batches() -> int:
    563 +    fieldnames: list[str] | None = None
    564 +    rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    565 +    for path in sorted(_contacts_dir().glob("contacts-batch-*.csv")):
    566 +        with path.open(newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    567 +            r = csv.DictReader(f)
    568 +            if fieldnames is None:
    569 +                fieldnames = list(r.fieldnames or [])
    570 +            for row in r:
    571 +                rows.append(dict(row))
    572 +    if not rows or not fieldnames:
    573 +        raise SystemExit("No batch contact files found to merge.")
    574 +    # Stable ordering by batch then company.
    575 +    rows.sort(key=lambda x: (int(x.get("batch_id") or 0), x.get("company_slug") or ""))
    576 +    _safe_write_csv(_merged_contacts_csv_path(), rows, fieldnames)
    577 +    return len(rows)
    578 +
    579 +
    580 +def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    581 +    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Enrich outreach artifacts with public contact channels.")
    582 +    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)
    583 +
    584 +    p_batch = sub.add_parser("build-batch", help="Fetch contacts for one batch (100 companies)")
    585 +    p_batch.add_argument("--batch-id", type=int, required=True)
    586 +    p_batch.add_argument("--timeout-s", type=int, default=15)
    587 +    p_batch.add_argument("--max-pages", type=int, default=4, help="Max pages to fetch per company")
    588 +    p_batch.add_argument("--sleep-s", type=float, default=0.1, help="Sleep between requests (politeness)")
    589 +    p_batch.add_argument("--no-robots", action="store_true", help="Ignore robots.txt (not recommended)")
    590 +
    591 +    sub.add_parser("merge", help="Merge all batch CSVs into specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.csv")
    592 +
    593 +    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    594 +    if args.cmd == "build-batch":
    595 +        n = build_batch_contacts(
    596 +            batch_id=int(args.batch_id),
    597 +            timeout_s=int(args.timeout_s),
    598 +            respect_robots=not bool(args.no_robots),
    599 +            sleep_s=float(args.sleep_s),
    600 +            max_pages=int(args.max_pages),
    601 +        )
    602 +        print(f"generated_batch_contacts={n} batch_id={int(args.batch_id)}")
    603 +        return 0
    604 +    if args.cmd == "merge":
    605 +        n = merge_batches()
    606 +        print(f"merged_contacts={n}")
    607 +        return 0
    608 +    raise SystemExit("unknown command")
    609 +
    610 +
    611 +if __name__ == "__main__":
    612 +    raise SystemExit(main())
    613 +

• I'm wrestling with the regex pattern where I wrote \\-., interpreting it as \-.—that might be causing confusion. Since \- escapes the hyphen, I thought it would work fine. But the error indicates a
  character range issue due to the backslash. Hyphen can form a range in character classes, which complicates things. I suspect the pattern may have been modified by my double backslashes. To simplify
  things, I'll switch to using [\\s().-] to avoid these character range problems.

  Adjusting regex approach

  I'm realizing that maybe I don't need to use verbose mode to make this work. Instead, I can simplify the regex. I can write a raw string without the extra complexity, using a character class like [\
  \s().-]*. This should allow me to capture whitespace, parentheses, and dashes directly. It feels much more straightforward, and I'm optimistic this will resolve the issue more easily without running
  into character range errors. Let's give this a try!

  Correcting regex syntax

  I’m thinking about the regex and the allowed characters in the char class. It looks like [\s().-] should work well, but the hyphen needs to be at the end for safety. So, I’ll use [\s().-]* without a
  backslash. I’ll also compile a pattern for phone numbers that handles optional country codes and formats. The regex should look like this: "(?:\+?1[\s().-]*)?(?:\(\s*\d{3}\s*\)|\d{3})[\s().-]*\d{3}
  [\s().-]*\d{4}". Now I'm ready to patch!

• I need to clarify how escaping works in regex. The regex engine uses a backslash to escape characters, so \\ matches a literal backslash, and b matches the letter "b." Therefore, \\b corresponds to
  the literal \b, not a word boundary. I see that I mistakenly double-escaped, so I should just use \b in the raw string. Similarly, I’ve noticed that \\. should simply be \.. I’ll adjust the patterns
  accordingly.

  Correcting regex issues

  I’m working through a regex pattern where I mistakenly used double backslashes. In a raw string, using \\+ means matching a literal backslash followed by one or more occurrences of it, which isn't
  what I intended. The initial pattern wasn't locating the JSON-LD script, resulting in no data extraction. I need to revise the regex patterns to ensure they accurately reflect what I want, especially
  correcting those double backslashes.

• I'm thinking about running 30 batches in parallel, fetching up to 4 pages per company, with a sleep of 0.1 seconds between requests. Initially, most companies in batch 0001 have websites, but some
  might not. I can run about 4 parallel processes, but I need to be cautious not to overload the websites. With 4 processes, that could mean roughly 40 requests per second, which seems high. I’ll
  consider increasing the sleep to 0.2 or 0.3 seconds and reducing max_pages to 2. This should keep us efficient and manageable!

  Optimizing request processing

  I’m considering the workload of approximately 3,000 requests for 1,493 websites, which could take 30-60 minutes if I implement concurrency. The user wants us to fill gaps for every company, so I
  might need to run all the requests. I plan to use ThreadPool for additional concurrency in the script, running 8 processes concurrently while keeping sequential processing within each batch. We have
  the multi_tool_use.parallel tool available, which will help us execute multiple requests at the same time while waiting for each to finish. Balancing speed and efficiency is key!

  Managing long tasks

  I’m thinking about the possibility of tool timeouts for long tasks. It can run processes in the background using a session ID, but since multi_tool_use doesn't manage sessions, I’ll need to consider
  alternatives. The exec_command does return a session ID for long-running processes, and I can poll using write_stdin. However, managing multiple sessions can be tricky. An alternative would be to use
  OS-level background tasks with nohup or a shell loop with xargs -P to spawn processes, which might be more manageable!

  Hopefully, it’ll come back soon! Waiting can be a bit nerve-wracking, but I think it’s worth it to get the right information. Let’s hang in there and see what happens next!

  get it right this time. It's important to get these commands to work properly, and I appreciate the chance to correct it with the right syntax!

• Ran cd /Users/nick/project/endurantdevs/api-app && ps -ax | rg -n -F "--max-pages 1" | head -n 40
  └ rg: unrecognized flag --max-pages 1

• I'm figuring out that rg treats "--max-pages 1" as a flag because of quoting issues. If I use the command rg -n -F \"--max-pages 1\", it interprets the pattern as an argument. It seems like, since
  the pattern starts with --, it assumes it’s a flag due to not terminating the flags properly. So, to fix this, I’ll use rg -n -F -- "--max-pages 1" instead.

  names and titles from HTML and possibly refetch or store relevant text snippets.

  Implementing heuristic extraction

  I’m thinking we can enhance our data collection by implementing heuristic extraction during the fetch process. We’d parse the HTML text to find name and title pairs to add to our list of people. This
  will definitely help improve our results. I'll plan to implement this in fetch_contacts_for_url right after parsing.

  We can use the _ContactParser to extract text and potentially capture headings as well. By applying regex, we can look for patterns like “First Last” along with title keywords. I’ll create a function
  called _extract_people_from_text_heuristic to identify candidate names based on sequences of capitalized words.

  Considering cache limitations

  I’m realizing that our current cache only stores extracted data and not the original HTML. This means if I want to use heuristic extraction, I’d need to fetch and read the HTML again. While we do
  fetch when no cache exists, if there’s an existing cached page, fetch_contacts_for_url just returns the cached results without fetching the HTML again. So, for new heuristics to work, I might need to
  invalidate the cache or add more fields for an upgrade.

  Considering caching strategies

  When dealing with cache, if elements like people_html are missing, it might be necessary to refetch or re-parse. But that seems heavy; we can't do it without HTML, so we may need to discard or ignore
  what's currently cached. A possible workaround could be to keep a reduced representation by parsing HTML to plain text. This way, I could extract heuristics without the original HTML structure, which
  seems like a smarter use of resources.

  Evaluating data storage options

  I’m considering how to manage text storage effectively. We could store up to around 20,000 characters, but that seems limited for larger pages. A thought is to store the first 50,000 characters, but
  compression complicates things. In the end, maybe re-fetching is simpler. I just want to deliver what the user needs, but it seems the focus is on using only data from the website, without relying on
  external datasets for contacts.

  Planning people extraction mode

  I’m thinking about implementing a new mode called build-people-batch. The idea is to fetch /team and /leadership pages, plus possibly the /about page from each company’s website using their base URL.

  phone numbers but those are tricky, I might propose providing a list of names and titles instead, along with company contact channels.

  people extraction heuristics by parsing HTML and storing results in the cache. However, I realize that cached pages would need flushing or ignoring for updates. So, I’ll add a cache_version constant

• Added api-app/specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.md (+112 -0)
      1 +# Contacts Enrichment (Public Channels Only)
      2 +
      3 +This document describes how we produce `specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.csv`.
      4 +
      5 +`contacts.csv` is intended to make outreach faster by pre-filling *publicly listed* routing channels (role-based emails, phones, and occasionally leadership names/titles when published via JSO
         N-LD).
      6 +
      7 +## Guardrails (Non-Negotiable)
      8 +
      9 +- Do not infer personal emails (no guessing patterns like `{first}.{last}@company.com`).
     10 +- Do not attempt SMTP verification of individuals.
     11 +- Do not scrape private/non-public data.
     12 +- Only use information explicitly published on the company's own site.
     13 +
     14 +## Inputs and Outputs
     15 +
     16 +Inputs:
     17 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv`
     18 +  - Used for `company_slug`, `company_name`, `website`, category + personas, and `contact_urls` (URL guesses).
     19 +
     20 +Outputs:
     21 +- Per-batch CSVs:
     22 +  - `specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts/contacts-batch-####.csv`
     23 +- Merged CSV:
     24 +  - `specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.csv`
     25 +
     26 +Join key:
     27 +- `company_slug` (stable slug used across all generated artifacts).
     28 +
     29 +## CSV Schema (`contacts.csv`)
     30 +
     31 +Columns:
     32 +- `company_slug`
     33 +- `company_name`
     34 +- `batch_id`
     35 +- `website`
     36 +- `category`
     37 +- `persona_1`
     38 +- `persona_2`
     39 +- `persona_3`
     40 +- `contact_email_1`, `contact_email_1_source_url`
     41 +- `contact_email_2`, `contact_email_2_source_url`
     42 +- `contact_email_3`, `contact_email_3_source_url`
     43 +- `contact_phone_1`, `contact_phone_1_source_url`
     44 +- `person_1_name`, `person_1_title`, `person_1_source_url`
     45 +- `person_2_name`, `person_2_title`, `person_2_source_url`
     46 +- `person_3_name`, `person_3_title`, `person_3_source_url`
     47 +- `visited_urls` (comma-separated URLs we attempted)
     48 +- `fetched_at` (UTC ISO timestamp)
     49 +- `errors` (pipe-separated errors per URL)
     50 +
     51 +Notes:
     52 +- Emails are restricted to **role mailboxes** only. Personal-looking local parts are intentionally ignored.
     53 +- Phones prefer `tel:` links; regex extraction from page text is a fallback.
     54 +- People are only extracted when a page includes JSON-LD blocks with a `Person` (or an `Organization` with embedded people).
     55 +
     56 +## Implementation Details
     57 +
     58 +Source of truth:
     59 +- `scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py`
     60 +
     61 +High-level algorithm:
     62 +1. Read `clients.csv`, split into 30 batches of 100 (based on row order).
     63 +2. For each company in the batch:
     64 +   - Take `website` and build a small list of candidate pages:
     65 +     - base URL (homepage)
     66 +     - `/contact`, `/team`, `/leadership`, `/about`, `/press`, `/investors`, `/partners`
     67 +   - Respect `robots.txt` where possible.
     68 +   - Fetch up to `--max-pages` pages (default 4).
     69 +3. Parse each fetched HTML page:
     70 +   - `mailto:` links (fast-path)
     71 +   - `tel:` links (fast-path)
     72 +   - regex scan for emails and phones in visible text
     73 +   - extract JSON-LD blocks and attempt to find public `Person` records (name/title)
     74 +4. Filter extracted emails to role mailboxes:
     75 +   - accepted local parts include: `info`, `support`, `sales`, `bizdev`, `partnerships`, `press`, `ir`, `investors`, `marketaccess`, etc.
     76 +5. Emit per-batch CSV; then merge into `contacts.csv`.
     77 +
     78 +Caching:
     79 +- Page fetch and parse results are cached under:
     80 +  - `scripts/potential_clients/.cache/contacts_enrich/`
     81 +- Cache is gitignored.
     82 +
     83 +## Commands
     84 +
     85 +Enrich a single batch:
     86 +```bash
     87 +cd api-app
     88 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py build-batch --batch-id 1
     89 +```
     90 +
     91 +Enrich with stricter runtime limits:
     92 +```bash
     93 +cd api-app
     94 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py build-batch --batch-id 1 --timeout-s 6 --max-pages 4 --sleep-s 0.05
     95 +```
     96 +
     97 +Merge all batches:
     98 +```bash
     99 +cd api-app
    100 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py merge
    101 +```
    102 +
    103 +Parallelization:
    104 +- It is safe to run multiple `build-batch` commands in parallel (different `--batch-id`s).
    105 +- If you re-run the same batch, it overwrites that batch CSV and will be picked up on the next `merge`.
    106 +
    107 +## Limitations
    108 +
    109 +- Some sites block automated requests (403/429), so coverage is incomplete by design.
    110 +- Leadership names/titles are low coverage because we only trust JSON-LD (no brittle HTML scraping).
    111 +- This is not a “lead database”. It is an outreach helper for routing to the right org function.
    112 +

  navigation!

     1 +# Repository Guidelines
     2 +
     3 +## Project Structure
     4 +
     5 +- Next.js app router UI: `app/`
     6 +- Shared UI building blocks: `components/`
     7 +- Sanic internal API: `internal_api/`
     8 +- Scripts (one-off / generators): `scripts/`
     9 +- Specs (design + generated artifacts): `specs/`
    10 +- Tests: `tests/`
    11 +
    12 +## Environments
    13 +
    14 +Python:
    15 +- Use the repo venv: `./.venv314/bin/python`
    16 +- Prefer stdlib-only scripts unless there is a clear need for external deps.
    17 +
    18 +Node:
    19 +- Frontend build/runtime uses `package.json` in repo root.
    20 +
    21 +## Generated Prospecting Library (Potential Clients)
    22 +
    23 +Source of truth scripts:
    24 +- Prospect library generator: `scripts/potential_clients/generate.py`
    25 +- Outreach artifacts generator: `scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py`
    26 +- Contact enrichment (public channels only): `scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py`
    27 +
    28 +Generated outputs (do not hand-edit; regenerate instead):
    29 +- `specs/potential_clients/companies/` (2976+ company pages)
    30 +- `specs/potential_clients/b2b2c.md`, `specs/potential_clients/pharma.md`, `specs/potential_clients/ai.md`
    31 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/clients.csv`
    32 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/batches/`
    33 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/templates.md`
    34 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.csv`
    35 +
    36 +Operational docs:
    37 +- `specs/potential_clients.md` (top-level index)
    38 +- `specs/potential_clients/process.md` (data sources + deterministic generation)
    39 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/process.md` (no paid lead tools workflow)
    40 +- `specs/potential_clients/outreach/contacts.md` (contacts schema + guardrails)
    41 +
    42 +Caching (gitignored):
    43 +- `scripts/potential_clients/.cache/` (datasets + contact enrichment page cache)
    44 +
    45 +## Contact Enrichment Guardrails
    46 +
    47 +This repo intentionally does **not** implement:
    48 +- personal email inference/guessing (e.g., `{first}.{last}@...`)
    49 +- SMTP verification of individuals
    50 +- scraping private/non-public contact data
    51 +
    52 +`contacts_enrich.py` is limited to:
    53 +- publicly listed role-based emails (e.g., `partnerships@...`, `press@...`)
    54 +- published phone numbers (prefer `tel:` links)
    55 +- public names/titles only when explicitly published via JSON-LD
    56 +
    57 +## Development and Validation
    58 +
    59 +Run unit tests:
    60 +```bash
    61 +cd api-app
    62 +./.venv314/bin/python -m pytest -q
    63 +```
    64 +
    65 +Regenerate prospect library (full universe):
    66 +```bash
    67 +cd api-app
    68 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py build --full
    69 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/generate.py validate --min 1000
    70 +```
    71 +
    72 +Generate outreach artifacts:
    73 +```bash
    74 +cd api-app
    75 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/outreach.py build --full
    76 +```
    77 +
    78 +Enrich contacts (safe to parallelize by batch id):
    79 +```bash
    80 +cd api-app
    81 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py build-batch --batch-id 1
    82 +./.venv314/bin/python scripts/potential_clients/contacts_enrich.py merge
    83 +```

• Added AGENTS.md (+37 -0)
     1 +# Workspace Guidelines (`endurantdevs/`)
     2 +
     3 +This directory is a multi-repo workspace. Each repo may have its own `AGENTS.md` with repo-specific rules.
     4 +
     5 +## Core Repos (Most Commonly Touched)
     6 +
     7 +- `api-layer/`: public HealthPorta API (OpenAPI + runtime)
     8 +- `api-app/`: web app + internal API + scripts/specs (includes the prospect library generators)
     9 +- `api-mcp/`: MCP server wrapping HealthPorta APIs (client-facing tool contracts)
    10 +- `healthcare-mrf-api/`: MRF processing + data pipelines (feeds pricing/claims products)
    11 +- `drug-api/`: drug data ingestion/labeling (supports NDC/RxNorm/crosswalk use cases)
    12 +
    13 +## Cross-Repo Contract Rules
    14 +
    15 +- When changing API contracts in `api-layer/`, update dependents:
    16 +  - `api-mcp/` (tool args, defaults, error shapes)
    17 +  - `api-app/` (UI + client calls)
    18 +- Keep “defaults” consistent:
    19 +  - year defaults, param names, and allowed filters should match OpenAPI truth.
    20 +
    21 +## Specs / Architecture Reviews
    22 +
    23 +Each core repo has a shared-style architecture review prompt at:
    24 +- `specs/base_arch_prompt.md`
    25 +
    26 +## Prospecting Library
    27 +
    28 +The prospect library + outreach artifacts live in:
    29 +- `api-app/specs/potential_clients/`
    30 +
    31 +Source scripts:
    32 +- `api-app/scripts/potential_clients/`
    33 +
    34 +Guardrails:
    35 +- Do not implement personal email inference/SMTP verification.
